gmw_00011541.name: Object 627393
gmw_00011541.overview: Object 627393 is classified as a galaxy merger [arXiv:2002.00967]. The system features two distinct components that are separated by less than 0.5 arcseconds in space and by 200 kilometers per second in velocity [arXiv:2002.00967].

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 627393 as a merger, noting [CII] emission, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 627393 : this object is classified as a merger because two distinct components are present in the position velocity diagram (Le F evre et al. 2020). However, these components are separated by 200 km s ^ 1 in velocity and by less than 0.5" in space. The and [ CII ] spectra are extracted from the same physical region.

gmw_00011542.name: Object 665509
gmw_00011542.overview: Object 665509 is classified as a galaxy merger [arXiv:2002.00967]. This classification is based on the presence of a secondary [CII] emission peak located next to the main peak, which coincides with the brightest optical rest-frame emission [arXiv:2002.00967].

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 665509 as a merger, noting [CII] emission, UV/optical alignment, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 665509 : this object is classified as a merger because a secondary [ CII ] peak lies next to the main peak. The optical rest-frame emission is coincident with the brightest [ CII ] clump. We re-extracted the [ CII ] spectrum for this bright clump alone, and the position of the [ CII ] velocity peak is not affected.

gmw_00011543.name: Object 665626
gmw_00011543.overview: Object 665626 is a galaxy that exhibits [CII] emission aligned with its optical rest-frame light [arXiv:2002.00967]. However, the object is too faint in [CII] to perform a morpho-kinematic classification, and it shows no evidence of multiple components [arXiv:2002.00967].

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 665626 as too faint for morpho-kinematic classification, noting [CII] emission, UV/optical alignment, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 665626 : this object is too faint in [ CII ] for us to perform a morpho-kinematic classification. Its UV continuum is very faint, and therefore it is not detected in the optical band containing the line. However, the optical rest-frame light, traced by theUltraVISTA K_s image, is coincident with the peak of the [ CII ] emission. There is no evidence of multiple components.

gmw_00011544.name: Object 742174
gmw_00011544.overview: Object 742174 is a faint galaxy that exhibits [CII] emission [arXiv:2002.00967]. Its ultraviolet and optical emissions are offset by 0.7 arcseconds from the peak of this [CII] emission, and it shows no evidence of multiple components [arXiv:2002.00967].

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 742174 as too faint for morpho-kinematic classification, noting [CII] emission, UV/optical alignment, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 742174 : this object is too faint in [ CII ] for us to perform a morpho-kinematic classification. The and optical emissions are offset by 0.7" from the peak of the [ CII ] emission. There is no evidence of multiple components.

gmw_00011545.name: Object 773857
gmw_00011545.overview: Object 773857 is classified as a galaxy merger [arXiv:2002.00967]. It features a main component and a faint secondary clump detected in carbon II emission, with its optical rest-frame light originating near the peak of this emission [arXiv:2002.00967].

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 773857 as a merger, noting [CII] emission, UV/optical alignment, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 773857 : this object is classified as a merger because a faint [ CII ] clump is detected alongside the main clump. The UV rest-frame continuum of this object, which is selected as a narrow-band emitter, is very faint, therefore it is not detected in the optical image. However, the optical rest-frame light originates from a region close to the peak of the [ CII ] emission. We verified that the secondary [ CII ] clump does not affect the position of the [ CII ] velocity peak.

gmw_00011546.name: Object 803480
gmw_00011546.overview: Object 803480 is a dispersion-dominated galaxy [arXiv:2002.00967]. Its optical rest-frame light is well aligned with the peak of its [CII] emission, and it shows no evidence of multiple components [arXiv:2002.00967].

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 803480 as dispersion dominated, noting [CII] emission, UV/optical alignment, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 803480 : this object is classified as dispersion dominated. The optical rest-frame light is well aligned with the peak of the [ CII ] emission, and the velocity offset [ CII ] is also very small. There is no evidence of multiple components.

gmw_00011547.name: Object 859732
gmw_00011547.overview: Object 859732 is classified as a galaxy merger [arXiv:2002.00967]. This system contains multiple carbon II clumps, with the brightest clump located in the spectroscopic slit [arXiv:2002.00967].

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 859732 as a merger, noting [CII] emission, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 859732 : this object is classified as a merger because multiple [ CII ] clumps are present. However, only the brightest [ CII ] clump lies in the spectroscopic slit where the line has been extracted. The secondary [ CII ] clumps do not affect the position of the [ CII ] velocity peak.

gmw_00011548.name: 2MASX J17122774-2322108
gmw_00011548.overview: The galaxy 2MASX J17122774-2322108 is a cD galaxy located at the center of the Ophiuchus cluster [arXiv:2205.03424]. Its position is situated very close to the cluster's X-ray peak emission, with only a small offset of approximately 2 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2205.03424].

sources.summary: The paper treats 2MASX J17122774-2322108 as the Ophiuchus cD galaxy and cluster-center reference, noting its near-infrared role, X-ray-peak offset, coordinates, and tile location.

quotes.quote: Deep near-infrared images and photometry from the VISTA Variables in the Vía Láctea eXtended (VVVX) survey were used to detect galaxy member candidates of the Ophiuchus cluster up to 2 Mpc from the cD galaxy 2MASX J17122774-2322108 using criteria from a past paper to select the galaxies among the foreground sources. We also perform a morphological visual classification and generate color-magnitude diagrams and density profiles. We identify 537 candidate galaxy members of the Ophiuchus cluster up to 2 Mpc from the cD galaxy, increasing by a factor of seven the number of reported Ophiuchus galaxies. In addition, we performed a morphological classification of these galaxy candidates finding that the fraction of ellipticals reaches more than 60 in the central region of the cluster. On the other hand, the fraction of spirals fraction is lower than 20 , remaining almost constant throughout the cluster. Moreover, we study the red sequence of galaxy member candidates and use mock catalogs to explore the density profile of the cluster, finding that the value derived from the mock catalog toward an overdense region is in agreement with the galaxy excess of the central zone of the Ophiuchus cluster. Our investigation of the hidden population of Ophiuchus galaxies underscores the importance of this cluster as a prime target for future photometric and spectroscopic studies. Moreover the results of this work highlight the potential of the VVVX survey to study extragalactic objects in the Zone of Avoidance. survey -- Galaxy: bulge -- galaxies: clusters: individual: Ophiuchus -- Astrophysics of Galaxies The Ophiuchus cluster of galaxies is the second brightest galaxy cluster in the X-ray sky Million2010,werner16 and one of the hottest clusters with a cool core fujita08,perez-torres09 . Indeed, the new all-sky images released by the eROSITA (extended ROentgen Survey with an Imaging Telescope Array) mission Predehl2020 highlight this bright X-ray cluster of galaxies located at low latitudes behind the Milky Way, particularly at the edge of the bulge. Nevertheless, it has not been as well studied as the Virgo or Coma clusters due to its low Galactic latitude (b = 9.3^o ), which hampers optical observations. Ophiuchus has mostly been studied in X-rays Johnston1981,Matsu96,ebe02 . Moreover, based on Chandra observations, Million2010 have shown a small difference, 2 \ kpc , between the position of the X-ray peak and the cD galaxy 2MASX J17122774-2322108, as well as some strong features related to the existence of a merger in the cluster center.

The cD galaxy 2MASX J17122774-2322108 is taken as the cluster center position ( RA=258^ .1155 , Dec =-23^ .3698 , J2000.0 ), very close to the X-ray peak emission Million2010 . It is located in tile b506, 10 arcmin from the survey border (corresponding to 360 kpc considering the cluster redshift).

gmw_00011549.name: SDSS J114906.64 + 191006.3
gmw_00011549.overview: SDSS J114906.64 + 191006.3 is a host galaxy located at a heliocentric redshift of z=0.02080 [arXiv:1911.04564]. It exhibits a sub-solar metallicity [arXiv:1911.04564] and hosted the supernova LSQ13abf close to its core [arXiv:1911.04564].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J114906.64 + 191006.3 as LSQ13abf's host, places the supernova near its core, and uses SDSS spectroscopy to derive low extinction, sub-solar metallicity, redshift, and distance.

quotes.quote: The transient was located in SDSS J114906.64 + 191006.3 and has coordinates R.A.(J2000.0) = 11h49m06s.62 and Decl.(J2000.0) = +19^ 10 ' 10 71. A finding chart with the position of LSQ13abf in SDSS J114906.64 + 191006.3 is provided in Fig. FC . LSQ13abf was initially classified by the CSP-II as a SN Ic morrell13 , however as the object evolved, prevalent He i lines emerged in the spectra (see Sect. sec:spec ). Following the procedures of taddia13met and taddia15met , the de-projected distance of LSQ13abf from its nucleus is 0.33 times the galaxy radius ( _ 25 ), i.e., the supernova is close to the core of its host. Here we used the position angle, t-type, and the major and minor axes of the host galaxy from HyperLeda http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr . The host-galaxy center was spectroscopically observed by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS; DR13 albareti17 ).

The host-galaxy extinction appears minimal as the visual-wavelength spectra of LSQ13abf show no indications of significant Na i D absorption or diffuse-interstellar-band (DIB) features (see Sect. sec:spec ). After correcting the SDSS host-galaxy spectrum for MW extinction, we fit the emission lines of interest (H , [ O iii ] 5007, H , [ N ii ] 6584) with Gaussian functions, as shown in red in Fig. hostspec , to measure the metallicity of LSQ13abf from its SDSS host-galaxy spectrum. We obtain a N2 (and O3N2) pettini04 metallicity of 12+log(O/H) = 8.54(8.52) 0.18(0.16) dex. NED lists the heliocentric redshift of SDSS J114906.64 + 191006.3 to be z=0.02080 .

gmw_00011550.name: Az9
gmw_00011550.overview: Az9 is a strongly lensed, low-mass star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 4.274 [arXiv:2306.10450, arXiv:2405.04582]. It features a stable, rotation-dominated disk of gas [arXiv:2306.10450, arXiv:2405.04582] with approximately 80% of its star formation obscured by dust [arXiv:2306.10450, arXiv:2405.04582].

sources.summary: The paper presents Az9 as a strongly lensed z=4.274 low-mass dusty galaxy with ALMA [CII] and dust detections, a rotation-dominated disk, high obscured star formation, and unusual early dust enrichment.

quotes.quote: We report ALMA detections of [ CII ] and dust continuum in Az9, a multiply-imaged galaxy behind the Frontier Field cluster MACSJ0717.5+3745. The bright [ CII ] emission line provides a spectroscopic redshift of z=4.274 . This strongly lensed ( =7 1 ) galaxy has an intrinsic stellar mass of only 2 10^ 9 M_ and a total star formation rate of 26 M_ /yr ( 80 of which is dust obscured). Using public magnification maps, we reconstruct the [ CII ] emission in the source plane to reveal a stable, rotation-dominated disk with V/ =5.3 , which is >2 higher than predicted from simulations for similarly high-redshift, low-mass galaxies.

Az9 is not deficient in [ CII ] ; L_ [ CII ] /L_ IR =0.0027 consistent with local and high redshift normal star forming galaxies. While dust-obscured star formation is expected to dominate in higher mass galaxies, such a large reservoir of dust and gas in a lower mass disk galaxy 1.4 Gyr after the Big Bang challenges our picture of early galaxy evolution. Furthermore, the prevalence of such low-mass dusty galaxies has important implications for the selection of the highest redshift dropout galaxies with JWST. As one of the lowest stellar mass galaxies at z>4 to be detected in dust continuum and [ CII ] , Az9 is an excellent laboratory in which to study early dust enrichment in the interstellar medium.

sources.summary: The paper studies the lensed z=4.273 galaxy MACSJ0717 Az9, deriving molecular gas and PDR conditions, finding rotationally dominated CO-emitting gas and a long dust-obscured phase.

quotes.quote: We present the molecular gas content and ISM conditions of MACSJ0717 Az9, a strong gravitationally lensed z=4.273 , M_ * 2 10^9M_ star-forming galaxy with an unusually high ( 80 ) obscured star formation fraction. We detect CO(4-3) in two independent lensed images, as well as [ N II ] 205 m, with ALMA. We derive a molecular gas mass of log _ 10 [ M_ H_ 2 (M_ ) ] = 9.77 making it moderately deficient in molecular gas compared to the lower redshift gas fraction scaling relation. Leveraging photodissociation region (PDR) models, we combine our CO(4-3) measurements with existing measurements of the [ C II ] 158 m line and total infrared luminosity to model the PDR conditions. We find PDR conditions similar to local star-forming galaxies, with a mean hydrogen density log _ 10 [ n_H cm^ 3 ] = 4.80 0.39 and a mean radiation field strength log _ 10 [ G _0 Habing ] = 2.83 0.26 .

We use the 3D tilted-ring model fitting code 3D-Barolo to determine the kinematic properties of the CO(4-3) emitting gas. We find that it is rotationally dominated, with a V/ =4.6 1.7 , consistent with the kinematics of the [ C II ] . With PDR conditions remarkably similar to normal dusty star-forming galaxies at z 0.2 and a stable molecular disk, our observations of Az9 suggest that the dust-obscured phase for a low-mass galaxy at z 4 is relatively long.

gmw_00011551.name: F12D1
gmw_00011551.overview: F12D1 is a dwarf galaxy belonging to the M81 group [arXiv:1003.0861]. It exhibits a spatial variation in the centroids of its two stellar populations and possesses one of the lowest fractions of luminous asymptotic giant branch stars in the sample [arXiv:1003.0861].

sources.summary: F12D1 is included in the first-depth M81 dwarf sample, shows centroid variation between stellar populations, and has one of the lowest luminous AGB-star fractions.

quotes.quote: The first depth category contains KDG 61 , KDG 64 , DDO 71 , F6D1 and F12D1.

All of them show either a spatial variation of the centroids of the two stellar populations, as is the case of F12D1 and KDG 64, or that the metal-rich population is more centrally concentrated, as is the case of DDO 71, DDO 44 and F6D1.
The highest fraction of luminous AGB stars is observed in HS 117 and the lowest one in KDG 61 and F12D1.

gmw_00011552.name: HS 117
gmw_00011552.overview: HS 117 is classified as a transition-type dwarf galaxy that shares properties of both dwarf irregular and dwarf spheroidal galaxies [arXiv:1003.0861]. It contains associated neutral hydrogen gas and exhibits the highest fraction of luminous asymptotic giant branch stars in its dwarf sample [arXiv:1003.0861].

sources.summary: HS 117 is treated as a transition-type M81 dwarf with associated HI, dSph-like stellar populations, dIrr-like gas or star formation, and the highest luminous AGB fraction.

quotes.quote: The dwarfs in our sample are classified as dSphs (Karachentsev et al. ), with the exception of KDG 61, KDG 64, DDO 71, and HS 117 which are classified as transition-types (dIrr / dSph) as they have detectable HI content (Huchtmeier Skillman ; Boyce et al. ) or H emission (Karachentsev Kaisin ; Karachentsev et al. ).

Also among the dwarfs that coincide with the LG dSph locus, one dwarf is classified as transition-type, namely HS 117, with HI associated with it (Huchtmeier Skillman ; Karachentsev et al. ). Transition-type dwarfs are galaxies that share properties of both morphological types.
The highest fraction of luminous AGB stars is observed in HS 117 and the lowest one in KDG 61 and F12D1.

gmw_00011553.name: B0529+483
gmw_00011553.overview: B0529+483 is a flat-spectrum quasar [arXiv:1711.06713] and blazar [arXiv:1909.01043] located at a redshift of z = 1.16 [arXiv:1712.06314]. It features a triple radio structure consisting of a core and double lobes [arXiv:1712.06314], and it exhibits long-term flux variability [arXiv:1909.01043].

sources.summary: B0529+483 is modeled with RadioAstron data as a compact flat-spectrum quasar showing very high brightness temperatures and possible weak refractive scattering effects.

quotes.quote: The quasar 87GB 0529+483 (J0533+4822, hereafter B0529+483) is cataloged in the 87GB survey as having a flux density of 619 70 mJy at 4.9 GHz, with a spectral index between 80 cm and 6 cm of 0.1 (where S ^ ) 87GB,1991ApJS...75....1B . The flat spectrum implies compact structure which, combined with the source's location at a galactic latitude b=+8^ , raises the possibility that the source may be affected by scattering. It has been observed in 29 \ experiments and significant correlated flux density has been detected on projected baselines up to 19 Earth diameters. These two facts make it a suitable object to both measure its brightness temperature and examine refractive substructure effects. In this paper, we analyze correlated flux densities (i.e., visibility amplitudes) measured by \ for B0529+483 to extract the angular sizes and brightness temperatures of its most compact features.

The lower limit of the brightness temperature derived from the non-detections covers the range from 3.5 10^ 12 K to 2.3 10^ 13 K at 1.7 GHz, from 1.8 10^ 12 K to 1.9 10^ 13 K at 4.8 GHz and from 0.8 10^ 12 K to 2.4 10^ 13 K at 22 GHz. This may reflect the variability of B0529+483.
NE2001 predictions are inconsistent with our results for B0529+483: we detect structures at least 2--3 times smaller than the predicted broadening scale.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0529 + 483/J0533 + 4822 as a Fermi-detected z=1.16 quasar with a complex radio spectrum and a kpc-scale core plus double-lobe structure.

quotes.quote: 0529 + 483 (J0533 + 4822) This is a quasar at a redshift of z = 1.16 2012ApJ...748...49S . Fermi /LAT detected the source in the 100 MeV to 300 GeV energy band with a significance of 32.9 in the third Fermi source catalog (3FGL) 2015ApJS..218...23A . The source 0529+483 has a complex radio spectrum: a steep spectrum with a spectral index of = 0.95 between 74 MHz and 350 MHz (NED http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ ), a slightly inverted spectrum with = 0.04 between 8.1 and 15.4 GHz 2014AJ....147..143H , and a flat spectrum with = 0.1 shown in the WMAP data between 33 and 94 GHz 2011ApJS..192...15G .

The 1.4-GHz VLA image shows a core and a northeastern lobe 2007ApJS..171..376C . There is diffuse structure to the southwest of the core in the opposite direction of the northeast lobe. The kpc-scale structure can be described as a triple structure with a core and double lobes.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes blazar B0529+483 through Effelsberg scintillation monitoring, reporting repeated intra-day variability, long-term flux changes tied to compact-structure re-brightening, an intrinsic angular size of 27, and apparent brightness temperature in [0.35, 19.6] 10^13 K.

quotes.quote: At Effelsberg, significant IDV of the blazar B0529+483 was detected in three sessions out of four sessions (A14: no, A16: yes, A17: yes, A18: yes; see Table tab:obs_info ) in which it was observed. The source shows significant long-term flux variability. Following the flare in Oct. 2012 - May 2013 , its decay may coincide with the disappearance of the compact component, leading to no IDV detected in the low flux state in Jul. 2014, and possibly explaining the \ non-detections in Sep. 2013 and Nov. 2014. A subsequent rise seen in our data sees the return of IDV in late 2016, indicating re-brightening of the compact structure.

We analyzed the scintillation of blazar B0529+483, which showed IDV during Dec. 2016, Oct. 2017 and Apr. 2018. Our result suggests an intrinsic angular size of 27 \ and an apparent brightness temperature \ in the range [ 0.35, 19.6 ] 10^ 13 K in this source.

gmw_00011554.name: S212
gmw_00011554.overview: S212 is an infrared-faint radio source located at a redshift of z=2.76 [arXiv:1312.0046]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus indicated by broad emission lines, and it exhibits a high 1.4 GHz radio luminosity of 2.0e27 W/Hz [arXiv:1312.0046].

sources.summary: The paper identifies S212 as an infrared-faint radio source at z=2.76, with broad emission lines indicating an AGN and a high 1.4 GHz radio luminosity.

quotes.quote: S212 Three broad emission lines with full width at half maximum (FWHM) between 78  and 169  are visible in the spectrum of S212 which we identify as Ly , C iv , and C iii ] , at a redshift  z=2.76 0.05 (see Fig.  figure:all_spectra , upper left). Furthermore, a less distinct emission line can be associated with Si iv at around 5260  . The broad emission lines of a few thousand km s ^ 1 suggest the presence of an AGN in S212. Using the determined redshift and the measured radio flux density listed in Table  table:sample_IFRS , S212 has a 1.4 GHz luminosity of 2.0 10^ 27  W Hz ^ 1 (8.8 10^ 26 7.6 10^ 27  W Hz ^ 1 ) .

gmw_00011555.name: S265
gmw_00011555.overview: S265 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.84 [arXiv:1312.0046]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus, which is identified by broad carbon and magnesium emission lines in its spectrum [arXiv:1312.0046].

sources.summary: The paper finds S265 at z=1.84 with broad C iv, C iii], and Mg ii emission, arguing that the line widths indicate an AGN and deriving its radio luminosity.

quotes.quote: S265 We found three broad emission lines with FWHM between 57  and 223  in the spectrum of S265 in Fig.  figure:all_spectra (upper right) which we identify as C iv , C iii ] , and Mg ii , at a redshift  z=1.84 0.03 . These broad emission lines with line widths in the range of 4000 km s ^ 1 to 8000 km s ^ 1 clearly suggest the presence of an AGN in S265. We find a 1.4 GHz luminosity of 6.7 10^ 26  W Hz ^ 1 (3.2 10^ 26 1.2 10^ 27  W Hz ^ 1 ).

gmw_00011556.name: S713
gmw_00011556.overview: S713 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of approximately 2.13 [arXiv:1312.0046]. Its spectrum displays three broad emission lines that suggest the presence of an active nucleus, and it has a measured 1.4 GHz radio luminosity of 8.7e26 W/Hz [arXiv:1312.0046].

sources.summary: The paper describes S713 as a z=2.13 infrared-faint radio source whose broad emission lines imply an AGN and whose radio luminosity is measured from the spectrum.

quotes.quote: S713 Three broad emission lines are visible in the spectrum of S713, with FWHMs between 884  and 134  , corresponding to line widths about 5000  km   s ^ 1 and suggesting the presence of an AGN. The lines are associated with C iv , C iii ] , and Mg ii at a redshift  z=2.13 0.03 (see Fig.  figure:all_spectra , lower left). We find a 1.4 GHz luminosity of 8.7 10^ 26  W Hz ^ 1 (3.9 10^ 26 2.7 10^ 27  W Hz ^ 1 ) .

gmw_00011557.name: S539
gmw_00011557.overview: S539 is an infrared-faint radio galaxy [arXiv:1312.0046]. It is the optically faintest source in its observing program and has a tentative redshift of approximately 3.1 based on low signal-to-noise emission features [arXiv:1312.0046].

sources.summary: The paper describes S539 as the optically faintest IFRS in the program, with low signal-to-noise emission features tentatively interpreted as placing it near z=3.1.

quotes.quote: S539 The source S539 is the optically faintest IFRS in this observing programme. Unfortunately, it was only observed with 44 min on-source time, although 3 h had been requested. Therefore, the resulting spectrum does not provide the quality of the other spectra and we cannot use it to measure a redshift and line widths (see Fig.  figure:all_spectra , lower right). Despite the short integration time, emission features are visible, although with poor signal-to-noise.

We tentatively interpret the emission feature at around 4970  as Ly , indicated by the related break towards lower wavelengths, and a second emission feature at redder wavelengths as Si iv . We suggest S539 to be at redshift z 3.1 , although the low signal-to-noise ratio prevents a reliable determination.

gmw_00011558.name: Dwarf A
gmw_00011558.overview: Dwarf A is an optically faint companion galaxy located to the northwest of NGC 4631 [arXiv:2301.00937]. It has a relatively small angular size and possesses an HI mass of approximately 10^7.8 solar masses [arXiv:2301.00937].

sources.summary: The paper describes Dwarf A as an optically faint companion northwest of NGC 4631, measuring its HI mass and finding no FAST excess because of its small angular size.

quotes.quote: Apparent from the moment images are the main target NGC 4631, its major satellite NGC 4656 to the south-east, and a known optically faint companion Dwarf A to the north-west.

From the FAST cube, the masses of NGC 4631, its major satellite NGC 4656, and dwarf companion Dwarf A are 10^ 10.08 0.04 , 10^ 9.77 0.04 , and 10^ 7.77 0.04 M_ (all assuming the distance of NGC 4656) respectively. In comparison, the corresponding values from the PB-corrected WSRT cube are 10^ 9.90 0.04 , 10^ 9.38 0.04 , and 10^ 7.82 0.04 M_ .

There is no excess detected by FAST for Dwarf A, which is relatively small in angular size. Comparing the amplitude spectra One concern that arises when comparing the FAST and WSRT data is whether the flux calibrations are consistent. The consistent integral fluxes of Dwarf A support it

gmw_00011559.name: UGC 10420
gmw_00011559.overview: UGC 10420 is a face-on spiral galaxy located in the Abell 2199 cluster [arXiv:2302.01848, arXiv:2312.15694]. The galaxy is undergoing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2302.01848, arXiv:2312.15694], which has turned its outer spiral arms into a south-eastern wake of star-forming knots [arXiv:2302.01848].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 10420 as an Abell 2199 spiral undergoing ram-pressure stripping, with outer spiral arms becoming star-forming wake knots and X-ray data showing hot spots plus faint central emission.

quotes.quote: Analysis of archival X-ray data shows that UGC 10420 lies just at the edge of the X-ray emitting region of Abell 2199. Two bright X-ray sources, a few tens of kpc away from the centre of the galaxy without any optical counterpart are clearly visible, along with very faint emission from the central region of the galaxy. Such bright sources (hot spots, hereafter), are reminiscent of those generally observed around bright active galactic nuclei like Pictor A. Although such hot spots are often connected with a jet seen as faint diffuse emission hardcastle , such is not the case with .

In this paper we have presented the UVIT FUV data and ancillary data in other wavebands for the spiral galaxy , a member of the cluster Abell 2199. Our analysis shows that this galaxy, at a projected distance of 680 kpc from the cluster's centre is being ram-pressure stripped by the ICM of the cluster.
As a consequence of stripping, the outer spiral arms of the galaxy are being removed and turned into what looks like a wake of star-forming knots on the south-eastern side of the galaxy (Fig. uvit ).

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 10420 as a face-on spiral undergoing ram-pressure stripping, with an extended UV disk, possible past interaction with UGC 10429, and displaced radio emission.

quotes.quote: UGC 10420 is a face-on spiral galaxy undergoing ram-pressure stripping in (Figure ugc ). We have presented detailed multi-wavelength analysis of UGC 10420 in , where we showed that it is not only an RPS candidate, but the only known cluster galaxy with an extended UV disk.

UGC 10429 having M^* = 10^ 10.5 M_ salim16 , is 2100 km s ^ 1 away from the nearest large galaxy UGC 10420 in redshift space (Figure ugc ). The projected distance between UGC 10429 and UGC 10420 is 347 h^ 1 kpc, which diminishes a chance of ongoing interaction between them. These two galaxies however, may have had a close flyby interaction in the past. UGC 10429 is a low-ionization nuclear emission region galaxy LINER; toba14,zaw19 . Interestingly, while the low-frequency radio emission in UGC 10420 appears behind the galaxy relative to the optical disc see, for a detailed discussion mahajan23 , in UGC 10429 the radio emission is observed at the edge of the optical disc facing the cluster centre (Figure deformed (left)).

gmw_00011560.name: UGC 10429
gmw_00011560.overview: UGC 10429 is a cluster galaxy classified as a low-ionization nuclear emission region (LINER) [arXiv:2312.15694]. It is separated from the nearby galaxy UGC 10420, though the two may have experienced a past flyby interaction [arXiv:2312.15694].

sources.summary: The paper discusses UGC 10429 as a cluster galaxy and LINER, separated from UGC 10420 but possibly linked by a past flyby, with asymmetric radio and UV emission.

quotes.quote: One of the cluster galaxies from their sample observed by the UVIT is UGC 10429 (Figures ugc and deformed (left)). UGC 10429 having M^* = 10^ 10.5 M_ salim16 , is 2100 km s ^ 1 away from the nearest large galaxy UGC 10420 in redshift space (Figure ugc ). The projected distance between UGC 10429 and UGC 10420 is 347 h^ 1 kpc, which diminishes a chance of ongoing interaction between them. These two galaxies however, may have had a close flyby interaction in the past. UGC 10429 is a low-ionization nuclear emission region galaxy LINER; toba14,zaw19 .

Interestingly, while the low-frequency radio emission in UGC 10420 appears behind the galaxy relative to the optical disc see, for a detailed discussion mahajan23 , in UGC 10429 the radio emission is observed at the edge of the optical disc facing the cluster centre (Figure deformed (left)). On the other hand, the emission is more intense on the side of the disc away from the cluster centre.

gmw_00011561.name: Z224-65
gmw_00011561.overview: Z224-65 is a barred Seyfert II galaxy classified as a mid-infrared green-valley galaxy [arXiv:2312.15694]. Its ultraviolet emission is primarily constrained in an arch around the central bulge, while its low-frequency radio emission is scattered across the optical disk [arXiv:2312.15694].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Z224-65 as a barred Seyfert II and MIR green-valley galaxy, with UV emission arched around its bulge and low-frequency radio emission scattered across the disk.

quotes.quote: Z224-65 (Figure deformed (centre)) is a barred Seyfert type II galaxy toba14 . have classified Z224-65 as a mid-infrared (MIR) green valley Galaxies lying between the red sequence and blue cloud in a colour-magnitude diagram are popularly known as `green valley' galaxies in the literature. classified MIR green valley galaxies using a L_ /L_ (12 m ) vs [ 3.4 ] [ 12 ] WISE colour (see their fig. 3). galaxy based on its Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE) data.

The UVIT emission from Z224-65 is mostly constrained in an arch around the central bulge, in a direction opposite to the cluster centre. The low-frequency radio emission shows no preference, and is scattered around the optical disc. It would be interesting to obtain a high-frequency radio image of Z224-65 to throw more light on the interaction between the intra-cluster and the interstellar mediums, and confirm if the emission is linked to the turbulence caused by an environment related mechanism.

gmw_00011562.name: Z224-55
gmw_00011562.overview: Z224-55 is a LINER galaxy and a Type I active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:2312.15694]. The galaxy features an 80-arcsecond low-frequency radio tail, which suggests it may be undergoing ram-pressure stripping within its cluster [arXiv:2312.15694].

sources.summary: The paper describes Z224-55 as a LINER and Type I AGN candidate with an 80-arcsecond radio tail, suggesting ram-pressure stripping in the cluster potential.

quotes.quote: Z224-55 ( z=0.030 ; Figure deformed (right)) is classified as a LINER galaxy toba14 in . classified this galaxy as a Type I AGN based on the broad EW(H ) and other emission lines present in the spectra. Z224-55 shows an 80 long low-frequency radio tail in the direction opposite to the cluster centre. These multi-wavelength data suggests that Z224-55 may be undergoing ram-pressure stripping under the influence of the cluster's potential well, at a distance of 0.8 h^ 1 Mpc (almost 0.5R_ 200 ) from the cluster centre.

gmw_00011563.name: SDSS J163017.01 + 394924.3
gmw_00011563.overview: SDSS J163017.01 + 394924.3 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0701 [arXiv:2312.15694]. It features a faint, low-frequency radio tail extending to the northeast, suggesting it may be undergoing ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2312.15694].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J163017.01 + 394924.3 as a non-member spiral with faint tail-like radio emission and a possible ram-pressure-stripping connection to a higher-redshift structure.

quotes.quote: At redshift z = 0.0701 , SDSS J163017.01 + 394924.3 is not be a member of . The bright spot seen northward of the core in the optical image of this spiral galaxy is a foreground star with no emission associated with it (Figure spiral ). The low frequency radio emission outside the optical disk is faint, but assumes a tail-like morphology extending in the north-east direction. It is interesting to note that this galaxy will be close in redshift space ( 2000 km s ^ 1 ) to the large-scale structure which may be present in this field of view at a higher redshift (see Sec. bg-grps and Fig. z-hist below).
SDSS J163017.01 + 394924.3 also shows a low-frequency radio tail and could be a RPS candidate in a higher redshift group.

gmw_00011564.name: RGZ J123300.2+060325
gmw_00011564.overview: RGZ J123300.2+060325 is a Green Bean active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.304458 [arXiv:2405.19558]. It features extended jet-induced radio emission with a projected linear size of 250 kpc and radio lobes estimated to be over 6 million years old [arXiv:2405.19558].

sources.summary: The paper presents RGZ J123300.2+060325 as a Green Bean AGN host with extended jet-induced radio emission, old radio lobes, detached clouds, and fading ionization.

quotes.quote: Here, we present a new Green Bean galaxy at z=0.304458 0.000007 with large scale jet-induced radio emission. The Spectral Energy Distributions (SEDs) of the radio components show steep spectral indices ( =-0.85 to 0.92 for the extended regions, and =-1.02 for the faint radio core), and spectral age modeling of the extended radio emission indicates that the lobes are > 6 Myrs old. It is unclear whether the jet is active, or remnant with an off-time of 2-3 Myr.

The source, RGZ J123300.2+060325 (hereafter ) was found to meet 11 out of 12 of the color cuts used to identify the mostly non-jetted sample of GB galaxies, show no clear radio core in shallow FIRST imaging (potentially consistent with being ramped-down), and have extended radio morphology with a full projected linear size of 250 30 kpc Kapinska2017 . Although this source was not included in the original GB sample (due to missing one colorcut) the source does pass the updated GB galaxy color selection criteria of Schirmer2016 . In this paper, we use low-frequency, high resolution follow-up radio observations from the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) to confirm the association of the radio galaxy core with the spatially coincident optical host galaxy, constrain the spectral age of the extended radio components, and investigate its magnetic field in an attempt of disentangling its radio morphology.

gmw_00011565.name: ESO450-16
gmw_00011565.overview: ESO450-16 is classified as an unobscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.01273]. X-ray observations of the source require a power-law slope fitted between 1.0 and 2.0 for a statistically acceptable fit [arXiv:2004.01273].

sources.summary: The paper reports using both EPIC-pn and EPIC-MOS data for ESO450-16, fitting it with a freer power-law slope, and classifying it as an unobscured AGN.

quotes.quote: Only for ESO450-16 we used both EPIC-pn and EPIC-MOS data.
For ESO450-16 we limited the slope of the power-law between 1.0 and 2.0, for this source fixing to be 1.7 does not give a statistically acceptable fit.
As a result of our analysis we classify 2MASXJ08384815+0407340, IC-588, 2MASXJ16510578-0129258, MCG+00-58-028 as obscured AGN and ESO439-G009, NGC5940, ESO450-16, CGCG081-001 as unobscured AGN.

gmw_00011566.name: IC-588
gmw_00011566.overview: IC-588 is a galaxy classified as an obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.01273]. It exhibits persistent broadband X-ray emission [arXiv:2306.13639] with consistent luminosities observed between May 2015 and March 2019 [arXiv:2306.13639].

sources.summary: NSA 64286 has bright Chandra emission and an XMM catalog counterpart; its XMM and Chandra luminosities remain consistent between May 2015 and March 2019, supporting persistent AGN emission.

quotes.quote: As seen in Table tab:lum table , NSA 64145, NSA 64272, and NSA 64286 have both bright X-ray emission within archival CXO data and an associated source within the XMM Source Catalog. Thus, we can evaluate whether these sources have persistent or variable/transient X-ray emission. We present the GALEX near-UV light curves for these three objects from in Figure fig:light curves of 64145 64272 64286 . h We find consistent and persistent broadband X-ray emission for NSA 64145, NSA 64272, and NSA 64286.
NSA 64286 also has consistent X-ray luminosity between its XMM and CXO observations, taken in May 2015 and March 2019 respectively.
We consider the X-ray emission from the three sources above to be most likely due to an AGN as opposed to a variable source such as a tidal disruption event (TDE).

sources.summary: The paper models IC-588 with a fixed photon index and an added mekal component for soft X-rays, then classifies the source as an obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: Therefore, for 2MASXJ08384815+0407340, IC-588, ESO439-G009, and NGC5940 we fixed photon index to =1.7 or =1.8 .
For IC-588, ESO439-G009 and NGC5940 we used the additional mekal model Mewe1985 to model the soft X-rays below 1 keV.
As a result of our analysis we classify 2MASXJ08384815+0407340, IC-588, 2MASXJ16510578-0129258, MCG+00-58-028 as obscured AGN and ESO439-G009, NGC5940, ESO450-16, CGCG081-001 as unobscured AGN.

gmw_00011567.name: ESO439-G009
gmw_00011567.overview: ESO439-G009 is classified as an unobscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.01273]. Its soft X-ray emission below 1 keV has been modeled using an additional mekal component [arXiv:2004.01273].

sources.summary: The paper models ESO439-G009 with a fixed photon index and added soft-X-ray mekal component, then classifies it as an unobscured AGN.

quotes.quote: Therefore, for 2MASXJ08384815+0407340, IC-588, ESO439-G009, and NGC5940 we fixed photon index to =1.7 or =1.8 .
For IC-588, ESO439-G009 and NGC5940 we used the additional mekal model Mewe1985 to model the soft X-rays below 1 keV.
As a result of our analysis we classify 2MASXJ08384815+0407340, IC-588, 2MASXJ16510578-0129258, MCG+00-58-028 as obscured AGN and ESO439-G009, NGC5940, ESO450-16, CGCG081-001 as unobscured AGN.

gmw_00011568.name: NGC 5044-UCD1
gmw_00011568.overview: NGC 5044-UCD1 is an ultra-compact dwarf galaxy associated with the elliptical galaxy NGC 5044 [arXiv:1702.06472]. Spectroscopic analysis reveals that it hosts an old stellar population with an estimated age of 11.7 Gyr and a total metallicity of -0.79 dex [arXiv:1702.06472].

sources.summary: The paper confirms NGC 5044-UCD1 as an ultra-compact dwarf associated with NGC 5044, measuring its velocity, old stellar population, metallicity, and high stellar mass.

quotes.quote: In Gemini+GMOS deep images of several fields around NGC 5044 and in spectroscopic multi-object data of one of these fields, we detected an unresolved source with g' 20.6 mag, compatible with being an UCD. Its radial velocity was obtained with FXCOR and the penalized pixel-fitting (pPXF) code. To study its stellar population content, we measured the Lick/IDS indices and compared them with predictions of single stellar population models, and we used the full spectral fitting technique. The spectroscopic analysis of the UCD revealed a radial velocity that agrees with the velocity of the elliptical galaxy NGC 5044.

The age, metallicity, and elements ratio of NGC 5044-UCD1 were obtained through the ^2 minimization method of 2002MNRAS.333..517P and 2004MNRAS.355.1327P . We have used a smooth grid in age, [ /Fe ] , and [ Z/H ] by interpolating the single stellar population (SSP) models of 2003MNRAS.339..897T,2004MNRAS.351L..19T . As in similar works e.g. 2006MNRAS.367..815N , the indices H_ A , H_ F , H A G4300, Fe4383, H , Fe5015, Mgb, Fe5270, Fe5335, and Fe5406 gave the most reliable results. The analysis provides an age of 11.7^ +1.4 _ 1.2 Gyr, a total metallicity of [ Z/H ] =-0.79 0.04 dex, and an elements ratio of [ /Fe ] =0.30 0.06 .

gmw_00011569.name: SDSS J145057.28+530007.6
gmw_00011569.overview: SDSS J145057.28+530007.6 is a quasar located at an emission redshift of z = 0.9166 [arXiv:1806.03552]. It exhibits broad absorption lines of hydrogen Balmer and metastable helium, which suggest the presence of ultra-dense, broad-line region scale outflow gas near the nucleus [arXiv:1806.03552].

sources.summary: The quasar SDSS J145057.28+530007.6 is reported at z=0.9166 with broad Balmer and He absorption, implying ultra-dense BLR-scale outflow gas near the nucleus.

quotes.quote: In this work, we report on a quasar (SDSS J145057.28+530007.6, hereafter 1450) with an emission redshift of z_ emi =0.9166 0.0001 . 1450 \ shows the broad absorption troughs of hydrogen Balmer (from H to H ) and metastable helium ( He 1 * 3889, 10830) with the width of 1200 km s^ 1 , suggesting the ultra-dense broad-line region (BLR) scale outflow materials in the nuclear region.

Constrained mutually by the Balmer, He 1 * absorption lines and undetected Fe 2 * 5169 in the photoionization simulations, the physical properties of the outflow gases are derived as follows: ionization parameter 10^ 1.4 U 10^ 0.8 , density 10^ 8.2 0.4 n_ H 10^ 9.0 0.4 cm ^ 3 , and column density 10^ 22.0 0.2 N_ H 10^ 22.2-22.3 cm ^ 2 . We propose that the ultra-dense outflow gases appear in the vicinity of the surface of the BLR or are located at most 3.12 pc away from the engine.
Thus we are more inclined to believe that the absorption gases in 1450 \ attach to the BLR or appear in the vicinity of the surface of the BLR.

gmw_00011570.name: UDF11
gmw_00011570.overview: UDF11 is a dusty star-forming galaxy at redshift z ~ 2 [arXiv:2108.05596] with a complex, clumpy optical morphology [arXiv:2103.05011]. It hosts a radio-quiet active galactic nucleus that is still accreting material [arXiv:2108.05596], and its extended radio emission may indicate forthcoming feedback [arXiv:2103.05011].

sources.summary: The paper describes UDF11 as a z~2 DSFG resolved in ALMA and VLA, with radio morphology suggesting a radio-quiet AGN and a central nucleus still accreting material.

quotes.quote: UDF11 (J033240.06-274755.5) UDF11 is a DSFG at z_ spec 2 .

The galaxy is resolved both in ALMA and VLA maps. In the millimeter it results to be more extended ( _ ALMA 3.4 kpc; see Tab. tab:continuum ) than the bulk of high-z DSFGs (typical ALMA radius is found to be 1-2 kpc). The radio morphology is well reproduced by a two-components fit: one is spatially coincident with the central ALMA emission ( _ VLA 3.4 kpc); the other, more compact ( _ VLA 0.7 kpc) but shifted to the sides of the central millimeter emission, could indicate the presence of two radio lobes/hot spots, suggesting the presence of a central radio AGN Rujopakarn2016:2016ApJ...833...12R . The radio flux at 6 GHz is solely consistent with the ongoing star formation in the host galaxy, as it is confirmed by the SED analysis presented in Pantoni2021:2021MNRAS.504..928P . Basing on these results we can assert that the central nucleus is experiencing the Radio Quiet (RQ) phase, producing jets that are still not dominant in the radio band.

We expect the galaxy to host a RQ AGN, still accreting material. Also the HST/WFC3 H _ 160 radius is more extended ( _ H 4.5 kpc; see Tab. tab:galaxy_properties ) than the aforementioned ALMA and VLA sizes.

sources.summary: UDF11 has a multi-component radio counterpart, complex optical morphology, and radio emission more extended than its FIR emission, interpreted as a possible AGN-feedback signature.

quotes.quote: This is the case of UDF11 radio counterpart (see Tab. tab_sizes ) showing two main gaussian components: the first is centered on the ALMA source while the second extends around it cfr. Fig 1b in Rujopakarn2016:2016ApJ...833...12R .

Most of the sources show an optical isolated morphology, while 4 galaxies (UDF11, ALESS067.1, AzTEC.GS21, AzTEC.GS22; Pantoni et al. in preparation) have more complex (i.e., clumpy) morphologies, possibly indicating the presence of minor companions - that can enhance the formation of stars and prolong the star formation in the dominant galaxy by refuelling it with gas - or just being a signature of the ongoing dusty star formation.
For a few objects we found the radio emission to be more extended than the FIR one, possibly being a signature of the forthcoming AGN feedback e.g., UDF11; see Rujopakarn2016:2016ApJ...833...12R .

gmw_00011571.name: NGC 1490
gmw_00011571.overview: NGC 1490 is an elliptical galaxy surrounded by several large neutral hydrogen clouds that form a 500 kiloparsec arc [arXiv:2002.07312]. This arc-shaped ensemble likely has a tidal origin, and its largest cloud contains a very low surface brightness optical counterpart [arXiv:2002.07312].

sources.summary: The review reports several large H I clouds in a 500 kpc arc around NGC 1490, including a very faint optical counterpart and a favored tidal origin.

quotes.quote: NGC 1490 . --- Oosterloo et al. (2004) report the discovery of several large H i clouds along a 500 kpc long arc around the elliptical galaxy NGC 1490. The total H i mass of the clouds, as determined from Parkes and ATCA data, is 8 10^9 M _ . Deep optical images reveal a very low surface brightness optical counterpart in the core of the largest H i cloud, suggesting one of the highest known H i gas to light ratios. Oosterloo et al. favour a tidal origin for this arc-shaped cloud ensemble (HIPASS J0352--66), possibly similar to that of the Leo Ring (see Bekki et al. 2005a,b).

gmw_00011572.name: IC 2554
gmw_00011572.overview: IC 2554 is a peculiar galaxy that may be a merger remnant [arXiv:2002.07312]. It is connected to a large, arc-shaped neutral hydrogen plume containing a third of the system's total gas mass [arXiv:2002.07312].

sources.summary: The review describes IC 2554 as a peculiar galaxy with an attached arc-shaped H I plume, substantial gas in the cloud, and an uncertain interaction or merger-remnant origin.

quotes.quote: IC 2554 . --- Koribalski, Gordon Jones (2003) detect a large H i cloud between the peculiar galaxy IC 2554 and the elliptical galaxy NGC 3136B. The cloud is connected to the northern (approaching) end of IC 2554 and forms an arc-shaped plume to the east (see Fig. 1). The IC 2554 system (HIPASS J1008--67) contains a total H i mass of 2 10^9 M _ , a third of which resides in the cloud.

The role of NGC 3136B, situated just east of the 30 kpc sized H i cloud and undetected in H i , remains unclear as its systemic velocity is 500 km/s higher than that of IC 2554, suggesting it is not a likely interaction partner. Alternately, IC 2554 may be a merger remnant.

gmw_00011573.name: NGC 958
gmw_00011573.overview: NGC 958 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0312258]. Its observed far-infrared spectrum and model spectral energy distribution show strong agreement across ultraviolet, near-infrared, and submillimeter wavelengths [arXiv:astro-ph/0312258].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 958 as an example where its model SED and observed FIR spectrum agree well, including IRAS, SCUBA, UV, and near-infrared photometric points.

quotes.quote: As an example, we show in the upper panel of Fig. fig:goodbad the model SED (solid line) and the observed FIR spectrum of the galaxy NGC 958. Filled circles in the right hand part of the plot show the observed L_ 60 , L_ 100 , L_ 450 and L_ 850 measurements. The other two points in the UV and near-IR part of the spectrum show the observed U and K band luminosities. The dot-dashed line in the left hand part of the plot represents the intrinsic UV and optical SED. The dashed line represents the UV SED after the UV absorption in the HII regions has been subtracted.

gmw_00011574.name: AGN3
gmw_00011574.overview: AGN3 is a Type I active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1604.00102]. The object displays a spatial offset between its ultraviolet and Lyman-continuum emission, which is associated with a complex substructure that may indicate an ongoing merger [arXiv:1604.00102].

sources.summary: The paper treats AGN3 as a Type I Lyman-continuum AGN candidate with offset emission, likely contamination, and possible merger substructure if associated with the source.

quotes.quote: Among all 14 AGN four were also detected in the narrowband data within 0.8 of the band detection, so these consistitute our initial sample of AGN. Three of them are Type I (AGN3, AGN5, AGN11), and one is Type II (AGN4).

AGN3 and AGN4 are clearly morphologically different in from AGN5 and AGN11, with the former two showing offsets between UV and , while UV and are well aligned. If the detection is indeed it can be coming from stars in offset starbursting regions. One could imagine a scenario in which non-stellar could manifest an offset from the position of the AGN e.g. from dust scattering of the from the accretion disk toward the direction of the observer, or from bound-free emission from an ionized gas flow, though the latter would also scatter photons which we do not observe.

The emitting offset structure in AGN3 has =1.2 fixed aperture magnitudes =26.7 , =25.9 , =25.7 , =26.0 , =25.6 , =25.7 , and =27.2 , with uncertainties 0.1 . Similarly, for AGN4 the magnitudes of the offset structure are =26.6 , =25.6 , =25.4 , =25.7 , =25.6 , =24.2 , and =27.1 . All of these are well above the 3 limiting magnitude in the corresponding filter, except the detections, which are close to the limiting magnitude of (3 )=27.4^ m . The probability to have two or more foreground contaminants in our sample is low, P( 2)=5.5 , however the probability for one of them to be a contaminant is not negligible, P(=1)=26.7 . AGN3 is the most likely to be a contaminant because of its large spatial offset between and UV, =0.8 . In Figure fig:additional in the appendix we show additional available data of the two AGNs manifesting offsets. These images reveal a complicated substructure for AGN3, spatially coincident with the detection and visible across all of our multiwavelength data. If the substructure is at the same redshift as AGN3, it would be an indication of an ongoing merger.

gmw_00011575.name: AGN4
gmw_00011575.overview: AGN4 is a Type II active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1604.00102] and a spectroscopically confirmed Lyman-continuum leaking galaxy [arXiv:1806.01741]. A separate object to the northwest suggests the system may be undergoing a merger or an accretion event [arXiv:1604.00102].

sources.summary: The paper classifies AGN4 as a Type II Lyman-continuum candidate with narrow lines, possible stellar or foreground ionizing radiation, and nearby structure suggesting interaction or accretion.

quotes.quote: Three of them are Type I (AGN3, AGN5, AGN11), and one is Type II (AGN4). The spectrum of AGN4 in the appendix shows a rest-frame velocity width of both the and the CIV lines of 670 km s ^ 1 , and is thus clearly not Type I. 2009ApJ...692.1476C observe that only Xray sources with broad emission lines in their spectra have detectable ionizing flux. The ionizing radiation from AGN4 could be stellar in nature or this could be a foreground contaminant.

AGN3 and AGN4 are clearly morphologically different in from AGN5 and AGN11, with the former two showing offsets between UV and , while UV and are well aligned. If the detection is indeed it can be coming from stars in offset starbursting regions. One could imagine a scenario in which non-stellar could manifest an offset from the position of the AGN e.g. from dust scattering of the from the accretion disk toward the direction of the observer, or from bound-free emission from an ionized gas flow, though the latter would also scatter photons which we do not observe.

For AGN4 there is an additional distinctly separate object to the North-West (NW). The detection seemingly originates from the center and stretches NW towards the second object, with no visible counterpart to the bridge between the two objects in the available archival Hubble Space Telescope (HST) F814W image, although the data is not very deep. If these nearby objects truly associate with the AGN host galaxies their presence may be an indication of an ongoing merger or accretion of a smaller object which may have triggered the AGN in both AGN3 and AGN4. Hereafter we refer to AGNs 3 and 4 as stellar LyC AGNs for simplicity, although their emission may come from contamination of foreground objects.

sources.summary: The paper treats 86861 as a Gold LyC-leaking LAE-AGN, identifies it with AGN04, and finds a well constrained SED escape fraction near 0.46.

quotes.quote: Target IDs 86861, 93564, 90675 and 92616 are all spectroscopically confirmed at z 3.07 and are coincident with compact F160W regions, except for 92616 where there is no F160W imaging.
Objects 86861, 93564 and 94460 are reported respectively as AGN04, LBG01 and LAE06 in .

Whether the F336W or IRAC fluxes influence the stellar population parameters therefore depend on the detailed shape of the object SED. For instance, Gold Object 86861 has a model escape fraction of f_ esc = 0.46 0.05 when including all the photometric data. The rest-frame UV, F160W , and IRAC data for this object provide reasonably tight constraints on the object parameters, such that if the F336W data is removed from the fit, the star formation rate and age of the object only change less than 10 to A_ SFR = 8.3 M_ / yr and _ 10 t_ age = 8.9 . However, the IRAC data for this object does help resolve the age-SFR degeneracy and removing the IRAC data decreases the age to _ 10 t_ age = 8.3 , increases the star formation rate to A_ SFR = 9.6 M_ / yr , and decreases the inferred escape fraction to f_ esc = 0.38 .

gmw_00011576.name: AGN5
gmw_00011576.overview: AGN5 is classified as a Type I Lyman-continuum active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1604.00102]. The object exhibits strong variability across multiple bands, and its spectral energy distribution suggests an almost transparent line of sight [arXiv:1604.00102].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGN5 as a strongly variable Type I Lyman-continuum AGN whose corrected SED suggests a transparent line of sight and non-unity escape fraction.

quotes.quote: AGN5 is strongly variable, with the multiband observations taken at different epochs. In the inset figure we add in quadrature to the formal photometry error of each band the uncertainty due to variability, estimated from the dispersion of the light curve in Figure fig:varconfirmed and amounting to 0.65^ m .

We therefore apply a variability correction to AGN5 by assuming the band light curve in Figure fig:varconfirmed is representative of the light curves in all other filters. The correction brings all filter measurements to the level of the reference year 2008 . The image has no correction since it was taken in the year 2008 . The , , and bands were taken in the years 2000 and 2001 , which are not covered by the band light curve. The precise correction would be the amplitude between the magnitude in 2008 and the year of observation, however since we do not have band data in 2000 , 2001 we assign the average of the light curve m to these years and calculate their correction amplitude as A=m(2008)- m . The correction resulted in brightening the , , and fluxes by 0.60^ m .

Even with the variability correction the SED shows an excess in the band (blue circle) compared to the 2001AJ....122..549V spectrum and is instead more consistent with the intrinsic 2015MNRAS.449.4204L spectrum. The broad OVI emission line falls well inside the band filter (Figure fig:spectra ), however from a Gaussian fit to the line we estimate that it contributes 20 to the band flux. Although the B band suffers from IGM attenuation much less than the ( ) band, this may suggest an almost transparent line of sight to AGN5.

gmw_00011577.name: AGN11
gmw_00011577.overview: AGN11 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1604.00102]. It is classified as a broad-line Type I Lyman-continuum source that exhibits marginal variability in its survey photometry [arXiv:1604.00102].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGN11/SSA22a-D12 as a broad-line Type I Lyman-continuum AGN candidate with marginal variability in the survey photometry.

quotes.quote: The only matches with our catalog are two objects already classified as broad emission line AGN from our spectra, AGN8 (SSA22a-D13) and AGN11 (SSA22a-D12).
AGN11 has S 2.5 , which is fairly high, but the random match probability is not negligible, P 12 , and it is therefore not convincingly variable.
AGN11 is marginally variable, but we add the dispersion of its light curve, which is 0.12^m .

gmw_00011578.name: AGN10
gmw_00011578.overview: AGN10 is an active galactic nucleus classified by its X-ray emission [arXiv:1604.00102]. It features a red spectral energy distribution that may indicate it is obscured or host-dominated, and it exhibits extended Lyman-alpha blob emission [arXiv:1604.00102].

sources.summary: The paper discusses AGN10 as an X-ray-classified, high-equivalent-width AGN with a red, possibly obscured or host-dominated SED and extended Lyman-alpha blob emission.

quotes.quote: In panel C two AGNs show very high observed equivalent widths, AGN10 with 840 and AGN14 with 980 . These two objects were classified as AGNs in 2015MNRAS.450.2615S due to superimposed Xray emission. We examined the SEDs of these two objects (Figure fig:hostdominated in the appendix) and the imaging data in all filters. The SEDs reveal spectra redder than the typical quasar spectrum, which may indicate that they are obscured or even host-dominated. The continuum-subtracted images (Figure fig:mosaic_non_detect ) show extended emission, which is expected for AGN10, classified as a Lyman Blob (LAB) by 2005MNRAS.363.1398G .

gmw_00011579.name: AGN14
gmw_00011579.overview: AGN14 is an active galactic nucleus classified by its X-ray emission [arXiv:1604.00102]. It features a red spectral energy distribution that suggests it may be obscured or host-dominated, and it is surrounded by a very extended Lyman-alpha halo [arXiv:1604.00102].

sources.summary: The paper discusses AGN14 as an X-ray-classified, high-equivalent-width AGN with a red possible host-dominated SED and very extended emission resembling a large Lyman-alpha halo.

quotes.quote: In panel C two AGNs show very high observed equivalent widths, AGN10 with 840 and AGN14 with 980 . These two objects were classified as AGNs in 2015MNRAS.450.2615S due to superimposed Xray emission. We examined the SEDs of these two objects (Figure fig:hostdominated in the appendix) and the imaging data in all filters. The SEDs reveal spectra redder than the typical quasar spectrum, which may indicate that they are obscured or even host-dominated. The continuum-subtracted images (Figure fig:mosaic_non_detect ) show extended emission, which is expected for AGN10, classified as a Lyman Blob (LAB) by 2005MNRAS.363.1398G . AGN14 has not previously been identified as a LAB, however Figure fig:mosaic_non_detect shows it to be even more extended than AGN10, with a clealy visible large halo.

gmw_00011580.name: ASASSN-17gs
gmw_00011580.overview: ASASSN-17gs is an active galactic nucleus that was initially detected as an optical transient in May 2017 [arXiv:1811.01756]. It was subsequently classified as a BL Lacertae object and exhibits high Z-band polarization consistent with a non-thermal emission mechanism [arXiv:1811.01756].

sources.summary: The paper describes ASASSN-17gs as a transient later classified as a BL Lac object and reports high Z-band polarization consistent with non-thermal emission.

quotes.quote: ASASSN-17gs ASASSN-17gs (also known as AT2017egv) was an optical transient detected on 2017 May 25 Stanek2017b coincident with a new gamma-ray source, Fermi J1544-0649, initially detected by Fermi /LAT on 2017 May 15 Ciprini2017 . Initially the source was seen as a candidate relativistic tidal disruption event (TDEs with a relativistic jet, similar to Swift J1644 and Swift J2058). The source was later classified as a BL Lac object via follow-up multi-band radio observations Bruni2018 . We observed the source approximately ten weeks post-alert and measured a polarisation of P = 9.03( 0.52) in Z band.

We calculate E(B-V) = 0.14 at the source position corresponding to P_ Gal, dust 1.26 . The result agrees with previous observations of high levels of polarisation in BL Lac objects (i.e. Smith2007 ) and is consistent with a non-thermal emission mechanism.

gmw_00011581.name: Gaia16agw
gmw_00011581.overview: Gaia16agw is classified as a blazar candidate that was detected in February 2016 [arXiv:1811.01756]. The source exhibits periods of high variability on timescales of months, showing changes of 1 to 2 magnitudes [arXiv:1811.01756].

sources.summary: The paper classifies Gaia16agw as a blazar candidate, noting earlier ASASSN detection, multi-year variability, higher activity during observation, and a V-band polarization limit.

quotes.quote: Gaia16agw Gaia16agw (also known as AT2016dth) was detected on 2016 February 29 Delgado2016 . The source was coincident with a previous detection from ASASSN (ASASSN15mw; Shappee2014 ). Figure fig:Gaia16agw_lc shows the source exhibits periods of high variability variability since its most historic Gaia detection in 2015 - changes on timescales of months by 1-2 mag. It therefore was classified as a Blazar candidate. We observed the source approximately 16 weeks post- Gaia detection, as the source appeared to enter a period of higher activity and measured a polarisation of P 0.36 and a brightness of 17.58( 0.01) mag in V band.

gmw_00011582.name: Gaia16aob
gmw_00011582.overview: Gaia16aob is a candidate active galactic nucleus that was detected during a brightening event in May 2016 [arXiv:1811.01756]. Observations conducted six weeks post-alert during a period of high variability measured low polarization, which is consistent with an intrinsically unpolarized source [arXiv:1811.01756].

sources.summary: The paper links Gaia16aob to a candidate AGN brightening, observes it during high variability, and finds low polarization consistent with an intrinsically unpolarized source.

quotes.quote: Gaia16aob Gaia16aob (also known as AT2016eaa) was detected on 2016 May 10 as a brightening of candidate AGN 2MASX J11431053-2946384 Delgado2016 . We observed the source approximately six weeks post-alert, during a period of high variability (see Figure fig:Gaia16aob_lc ) and measured a polarisation of P = 0.37( 0.12) and a brightness of 17.13( 0.01) mag in V band. We calculate E(B-V) = 0.06 at the source position corresponding to P_ Gal, dust 0.54 . The polarisation measurement is therefore consistent with an intrinsically unpolarised source.

gmw_00011583.name: Cloud-9
gmw_00011583.overview: Cloud-9 is a starless neutral hydrogen cloud located near the galaxy M94 with no obvious luminous counterpart [arXiv:2309.03253]. It is modeled as a possible reionization-limited HI cloud inhabiting a dark matter halo [arXiv:2309.03253].

sources.summary: The paper models Cloud-9 as a possible RELHIC: an H I cloud near M94 in a dark matter halo, with no luminous counterpart and uncertain density profile.

quotes.quote: The system (hereafter Cloud-9) has round column density isocontours and a line width consistent with thermal broadening from gas at T 2 10^4 K . These properties are unlike those of previously detected dark HI clouds and similar to the expected properties of REionization-Limited-HI Cloud (RELHICs), namely, starless dark matter (DM) halos filled with gas in hydrostatic equilibrium and in thermal equilibrium with the cosmic ultraviolet background. At the distance of M94, d 4.7 Mpc, we find that Cloud-9 is consistent with being a RELHIC inhabiting a Navarro-Frenk-White (NFW) DM halo of mass, M_ 200 5 10^ 9 M_ , and concentration, c_ NFW 13 . Although the agreement between the model and observations is good, Cloud-9 appears to be slightly, but systematically, more extended than expected for CDM RELHICs. This may imply either that Cloud-9 is much closer than implied by its recessional velocity, v_ CL9 300 km s ^ 1 , or that its halo density profile is flatter than NFW, with a DM mass deficit greater than a factor of 10 at radii 1 kpc.

The system (termed Cloud-9; hereafter CL-9) was observed with the Five-Hundred-Meter Aperture Spherical Telescope (FAST), has no obvious luminous counterpart, and exhibits properties consistent with those expected for RELHICs. Using the models introduced by Benitez-Llambay2017 and Benitez-Llambay2020 , we address whether the Zhou2023 observations are consistent with CL-9 being a CDM RELHIC.

gmw_00011584.name: LAB1 J2143-4423
gmw_00011584.overview: LAB1 J2143-4423 is a Lyman-alpha galaxy that exhibits weak polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission [arXiv:1011.3556]. Its mid-infrared output is primarily driven by star formation, though it also contains an active galactic nucleus component [arXiv:1011.3556].

sources.summary: The paper reports real but weak PAH emission from LAB1 J2143-4423, interpreting its mid-infrared output as primarily star formation while noting a significant, uncertain AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: The weakest PAH detection is that of source LAB1 J2143-4423, with a 7.7 \ feature (defined as the combination of the 7.60 and 7.85 \ PAH lines) detection of only 2 significance. However, the whole 7-9 \ complex for the LAB1 J2143-4423 source is detected at 4 significance. Combined with their discovery at the appropriate wavelengths there is little doubt the PAH features for LAB1 J2143-4423 are real.

For LAB1 J2143-4423, its L/C (1.5 0.7), suggests a similar but opposite situation: the primary contribution is from star formation, but the AGN component is significant. The large error on the LAB1 J2143-4423 L/C does add some further confusion, as a one sigma deviation could easily change the likely power source.

gmw_00011585.name: LAB6a J2143-4423
gmw_00011585.overview: LAB6a J2143-4423 is a Lyman-alpha galaxy counterpart powered by an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1011.3556]. Its spectrum is featureless, lacking apparent polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon features, and the galaxy lies outside the typical locus of star formation [arXiv:1011.3556].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LAB6a J2143-4423 as an AGN-powered counterpart, outside the star-formation locus and lacking apparent PAH features in its spectrum.

quotes.quote: For the case of LAB6 J2143-4423, an examination of the full SED and IRS spectra see and below sca09 reveals that the counterpart LAB6a J2143-4423 is powered by an AGN while LAB6b J2143-4423 is powered by star formation, making it almost a certainty that the sub-mm flux identified at that position is coming from LAB6b J2143-4423 and all further analysis assumes that.

An additional six of the blob components are only upper limits, but only two of those (LAB6a J2143-4423 and LAB19 J1714+5015) are clearly outside and above the locus of star formation, consistent with IRS spectrum of LAB6a J2143-4423 see below, sca09 and the broad line nature of LAB19 J1714+5015 pas96,col06a .
These featureless sources are the counterpart LAB6a J2143-4423 previously reported in sca09 and LAB18 J1714+5015 kee99 .

gmw_00011586.name: LAB6b J2143-4423
gmw_00011586.overview: LAB6b J2143-4423 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.38 [arXiv:1011.3556]. The galaxy is powered by star formation and has a large stellar mass of 4e11 solar masses [arXiv:1011.3556].

sources.summary: The paper treats LAB6b J2143-4423 as a star-formation-powered LAB counterpart, assigns the submillimeter flux to it, and cites SED fitting that gives a large stellar mass.

quotes.quote: For the case of LAB6 J2143-4423, an examination of the full SED and IRS spectra see and below sca09 reveals that the counterpart LAB6a J2143-4423 is powered by an AGN while LAB6b J2143-4423 is powered by star formation, making it almost a certainty that the sub-mm flux identified at that position is coming from LAB6b J2143-4423 and all further analysis assumes that.

In the case of LAB6 J2143-4423 there are two MIPS 24 \ sources with confirmed z =2.38 redshifts sca09 ), so both sources (LAB6a and LAB6b) are included on the plot, but the submm detection has been assigned to LAB6b J2143-4423 (as described above).
In addition, both LAB1 J2143-4423 fra01 and LAB6b J2143-4423 sca09 have undergone a full SED fitting analysis, producing masses of 1.5 10 ^ 11 M _ and 4 10 ^ 11 M _ respectively.

gmw_00011587.name: LAB1 J1434+3317
gmw_00011587.overview: LAB1 J1434+3317 is a bright mid-infrared Lyman-alpha blob galaxy [arXiv:1011.3556]. Its mid-infrared emission is dominated by an active galactic nucleus, though it also hosts a non-negligible starburst component with strong polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon luminosity [arXiv:1011.3556].

sources.summary: The paper includes LAB1 J1434+3317 as a bright mid-infrared LAB source whose emission is AGN-dominated but still has substantial star formation and strong PAH luminosity.

quotes.quote: At the time of observation there was one more known mid-infrared bright (0.86 mJy at 24 \ ) LAB1 J1434+3317 discovered in the NOAO Deep Wide-Field Survey by for which IRS spectra had already been taken. We include its IRS data as part of our analysis.
With a L/C of 0.8 0.2, LAB1 J1434+3317 appears to have its main mid-IR contribution from an AGN, but the star formation component is far from negligible.
The most discrepant source is that of LAB1 J1434+3317, which despite having a low L/C ratio does have the brightest 7.7 \ luminosity of the sample.

gmw_00011588.name: NGC 6534
gmw_00011588.overview: NGC 6534 is an early-type lenticular galaxy featuring a round outer ring embedded within an exponential stellar disk [arXiv:1811.06602]. The ionized gas in this outer ring is excited by young stars, with a flat star formation history spanning the last 100 to 200 million years [arXiv:1811.06602].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6534 as an early-type ring galaxy whose outer ring has young-star excitation, near-solar metallicity, and roughly constant star formation over 100-200 Myr.

quotes.quote: Two early-type galaxies with outer rings, NGC 6534 and MCG 11-22-015, are selected to be studied. After inspecting the gas excitation in the rings using the Baldwin-Phillips-Terlevich method bpt , we estimate the star formation rates (SFR) in the two outer rings of our galaxies by using several SFR indicators derived from narrow-band photometry in the H emission line and archival GALEX ultraviolet images of the galaxies. The ionized gas is excited by young stars in the ring of NGC 6534 and partly by shocks -- in MCG 11-22-015.

The derived SFRs allow to qualitatively restore star formation histories (SFH) in the rings: in NGC 6534 the SFH is flat during the last 100-200 Myr, and in MCG 11-22-015 the star formation has started only a few Myr ago. We suggest that the rings in NGC 6534 and MCG 11-22-015 have different natures: the former is a resonant one supplied with gas perhaps through tidal effects, and the latter has been produced by a satellite accretion.

NGC 6534 is moderately inclined to our line of sight as demonstrated by the elongated outer isophotes; the outer ring matches the ellipticity of the outermost isophotes being evidently a round structure inside the exponential stellar disk. However, the inner isophotes, R 8 , are very round; taking in mind quasi-exponential surface-brightness profile of the inner part of NGC 6534 we can suspect a pseudobulge containing an inner stellar ring slightly elongated orthogonally with respect to the outer isophotes and to the outer ring.

gmw_00011589.name: MCG 11-22-015
gmw_00011589.overview: MCG 11-22-015 is an early-type galaxy featuring an elliptical outer ring [arXiv:1811.06602]. This ring structure likely originated from satellite accretion, with star formation beginning only a few million years ago and gas that is partly shock-excited [arXiv:1811.06602].

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG 11-22-015 as an early-type ring galaxy with partly shock-excited gas, recent ring star formation, and a likely satellite-accretion origin.

quotes.quote: Two early-type galaxies with outer rings, NGC 6534 and MCG 11-22-015, are selected to be studied. After inspecting the gas excitation in the rings using the Baldwin-Phillips-Terlevich method bpt , we estimate the star formation rates (SFR) in the two outer rings of our galaxies by using several SFR indicators derived from narrow-band photometry in the H emission line and archival GALEX ultraviolet images of the galaxies. The ionized gas is excited by young stars in the ring of NGC 6534 and partly by shocks -- in MCG 11-22-015.

The derived SFRs allow to qualitatively restore star formation histories (SFH) in the rings: in NGC 6534 the SFH is flat during the last 100-200 Myr, and in MCG 11-22-015 the star formation has started only a few Myr ago. We suggest that the rings in NGC 6534 and MCG 11-22-015 have different natures: the former is a resonant one supplied with gas perhaps through tidal effects, and the latter has been produced by a satellite accretion.

MCG 11-22-015, on the contrary, has rather round outer isophotes and two local ellipticity maxima at R=5 and at R=14 . The latter radius corresponds roughly to the outer ring, so if we assume that the galaxy is seen face-on we must recognize the elliptical shape of its outer ring. The surface-brightness profiles show the prominent ring in NGC 6534 overposed onto the regular exponential disk ( _ ,0 =19.4 , h=9.8 , or 5.1 kpc) and a Type-II disk, according to the freeman classification, in MCG 11-22-015, with the scalelength of 4 2 , or 2.2 kpc.

gmw_00011590.name: BC1
gmw_00011590.overview: BC1 is an isolated, low-mass star-forming region located toward the Virgo cluster of galaxies [arXiv:2206.13971]. It hosts multiple star-forming regions and likely originated from gas stripped from larger galaxies, as indicated by its high metallicity [arXiv:2206.13971].

sources.summary: The paper confirms BC1 as one of four isolated Virgo Blue Candidates with multiple star-forming regions, Virgo-compatible velocity, high metallicity, and likely origin in gas stripped from larger galaxies.

quotes.quote: We use panoramic optical spectroscopy obtained with MUSE@VLT to investigate the nature of five candidate extremely isolated low-mass star forming regions (Blue Candidates, BCs hereafter) toward the Virgo cluster of galaxies. Four of the five (BC1, BC3, BC4, BC5) are found to host several regions and to have radial velocities fully compatible with being part of the Virgo cluster. All the confirmed candidates have mean metallicity significantly in excess of that expected from their stellar mass, indicating that they originated from gas stripped from larger galaxies. In summary, these four candidates share the properties of the prototype system SECCO 1, suggesting the possible emergence of a new class of stellar systems, intimately linked to the complex duty cycle of gas within clusters of galaxies.

gmw_00011591.name: BC3
gmw_00011591.overview: BC3 is an isolated, low-mass star-forming region located toward the Virgo cluster of galaxies [arXiv:2206.13971]. It hosts multiple star-forming regions and has a high metallicity, suggesting it originated from gas stripped from larger galaxies [arXiv:2206.13971].

sources.summary: The paper confirms BC3 as one of four isolated Virgo Blue Candidates with multiple star-forming regions, Virgo-compatible velocity, high metallicity, and likely origin in gas stripped from larger galaxies.

quotes.quote: We use panoramic optical spectroscopy obtained with MUSE@VLT to investigate the nature of five candidate extremely isolated low-mass star forming regions (Blue Candidates, BCs hereafter) toward the Virgo cluster of galaxies. Four of the five (BC1, BC3, BC4, BC5) are found to host several regions and to have radial velocities fully compatible with being part of the Virgo cluster. All the confirmed candidates have mean metallicity significantly in excess of that expected from their stellar mass, indicating that they originated from gas stripped from larger galaxies. In summary, these four candidates share the properties of the prototype system SECCO 1, suggesting the possible emergence of a new class of stellar systems, intimately linked to the complex duty cycle of gas within clusters of galaxies.

gmw_00011592.name: BC4
gmw_00011592.overview: BC4 is an isolated, low-mass star-forming region located toward the Virgo cluster of galaxies [arXiv:2206.13971]. It hosts multiple star-forming regions and has a high metallicity, suggesting it originated from gas stripped from larger galaxies [arXiv:2206.13971].

sources.summary: The paper confirms BC4 as one of four isolated Virgo Blue Candidates with multiple star-forming regions, Virgo-compatible velocity, high metallicity, and likely origin in gas stripped from larger galaxies.

Four of the five (BC1, BC3, BC4, BC5) are found to host several regions and to have radial velocities fully compatible with being part of the Virgo cluster. gmw_00011593.name: BC5
gmw_00011593.overview: BC5 is an isolated, low-mass star-forming region located toward the Virgo cluster of galaxies [arXiv:2206.13971]. It hosts multiple star-forming regions and likely originated from gas stripped from larger galaxies, as indicated by its high metallicity [arXiv:2206.13971].

sources.summary: The paper confirms BC5 as one of four isolated Virgo Blue Candidates with multiple star-forming regions, Virgo-compatible velocity, high metallicity, and likely origin in gas stripped from larger galaxies.
