quotes.quote: We use panoramic optical spectroscopy obtained with MUSE@VLT to investigate the nature of five candidate extremely isolated low-mass star forming regions (Blue Candidates, BCs hereafter) toward the Virgo cluster of galaxies. Four of the five (BC1, BC3, BC4, BC5) are found to host several regions and to have radial velocities fully compatible with being part of the Virgo cluster. All the confirmed candidates have mean metallicity significantly in excess of that expected from their stellar mass, indicating that they originated from gas stripped from larger galaxies. In summary, these four candidates share the properties of the prototype system SECCO 1, suggesting the possible emergence of a new class of stellar systems, intimately linked to the complex duty cycle of gas within clusters of galaxies.

gmw_00011594.name: NGC 7113
gmw_00011594.overview: NGC 7113 is a bright early-type galaxy located in a low-density environment [arXiv:1611.03872]. It exhibits a lower dark matter halo content compared to similar early-type galaxies situated in high-density environments [arXiv:1611.03872].

sources.summary: The paper presents photometry and long-slit spectroscopy of NGC 7113, models its early-type structure and mass distribution, and finds a low dark-matter halo content for its low-density environment.

quotes.quote: New photometric and long-slit spectroscopic observations are presented for NGC 7113, PGC 1852, and PGC 67207 which are three bright galaxies residing in low-density environments. The surface-brightness distribution is analysed from the K_S band images taken with adaptive optics at the Gemini North Telescope and the ugriz band images from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey while the line-of-sight stellar velocity distribution and line-strength Lick indices inside the effective radius are measured along several position angles. The age, metallicity, and element abundance of the galaxies are estimated from single stellar-population models.

The structural parameters of the two early-type galaxies NGC 7113 and PGC 67207 are obtained from a two-dimensional photometric decomposition and the mass-to-light ratio of all the (luminous and dark) mass that follows the light is derived from orbit-based axisymmetric dynamical modelling together with the mass density of the dark matter halo. The dynamically derived mass that follows the light is about a factor of 2 larger than the stellar mass derived using stellar-population models with Kroupa initial mass function. Both galaxies have a lower content of halo dark matter with respect to early-type galaxies in high-density environments and in agreement with the predictions of semi-analytical models of galaxy formation.

gmw_00011595.name: PGC 1852
gmw_00011595.overview: PGC 1852 is a bright barred spiral galaxy located in a low-density environment [arXiv:1611.03872]. It features a stellar population with an estimated age of approximately 7 Gyr, solar metallicity, and a strong alpha-element overabundance [arXiv:1611.03872].

sources.summary: The paper includes PGC 1852 as a bright low-density galaxy, identifies it as a barred spiral rather than an elliptical, and reports solar metallicity, alpha overabundance, younger age, and photometric/spectroscopic data.

quotes.quote: New photometric and long-slit spectroscopic observations are presented for NGC 7113, PGC 1852, and PGC 67207 which are three bright galaxies residing in low-density environments. The surface-brightness distribution is analysed from the K_S band images taken with adaptive optics at the Gemini North Telescope and the ugriz band images from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey while the line-of-sight stellar velocity distribution and line-strength Lick indices inside the effective radius are measured along several position angles. The age, metallicity, and element abundance of the galaxies are estimated from single stellar-population models.
In spite of the available morphological classification, images show that PGC 1852 is a barred spiral which we do not further consider for mass modelling.

The barred galaxy PGC 1852 has a solar metallicity ( 0 dex) and strong overabundance ( 0.4 dex). Its age ( 7 Gyr) and SSP mass-to-light ratio ( 2.0 ) are smaller than those of the other two galaxies, in agreement with the later morphological type.

gmw_00011596.name: PGC 67207
gmw_00011596.overview: PGC 67207 is an early-type galaxy located in a low-density environment [arXiv:1611.03872]. Dynamical modelling indicates that the galaxy has a lower dark matter halo content compared to early-type galaxies found in high-density environments [arXiv:1611.03872].

sources.summary: The paper presents photometry, spectroscopy, stellar-population estimates, and dynamical modelling for PGC 67207, finding early-type structure and comparatively low halo dark-matter content in a low-density environment.

quotes.quote: New photometric and long-slit spectroscopic observations are presented for NGC 7113, PGC 1852, and PGC 67207 which are three bright galaxies residing in low-density environments. The surface-brightness distribution is analysed from the K_S band images taken with adaptive optics at the Gemini North Telescope and the ugriz band images from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey while the line-of-sight stellar velocity distribution and line-strength Lick indices inside the effective radius are measured along several position angles. The age, metallicity, and element abundance of the galaxies are estimated from single stellar-population models.

The structural parameters of the two early-type galaxies NGC 7113 and PGC 67207 are obtained from a two-dimensional photometric decomposition and the mass-to-light ratio of all the (luminous and dark) mass that follows the light is derived from orbit-based axisymmetric dynamical modelling together with the mass density of the dark matter halo. The dynamically derived mass that follows the light is about a factor of 2 larger than the stellar mass derived using stellar-population models with Kroupa initial mass function. Both galaxies have a lower content of halo dark matter with respect to early-type galaxies in high-density environments and in agreement with the predictions of semi-analytical models of galaxy formation.

gmw_00011597.name: FS90 110
gmw_00011597.overview: FS90 110 is a compact elliptical galaxy located in the Antlia cluster [arXiv:1109.6004]. It is connected to the galaxy NGC 3258 by a low-surface-brightness bridge, which confirms an ongoing interaction between the two systems [arXiv:1109.6004].

sources.summary: The paper confirms FS90 110 as a compact elliptical Antlia member, red and compact for its luminosity, offset from dwarf-galaxy scaling relations, and linked by a low-surface-brightness bridge to NGC 3258.

quotes.quote: The exceptions are two cE galaxies (FS90 110 and FS90 192) that are new members of Antlia and were originally considered just as ``possible" members, two galaxies originally classified as ``possible" and ``likely" dE members, which turned out to be an Antlia member (FS90 103) and a background systems (FS90 205), and a confirmed member but with a different (irregular) morphology (FS90 221).

Both cE galaxies clearly depart from the locus of constant effective radius but, at first glance, in a different manner. FS90 110 seems to extend the break defined by the brightest galaxies towards fainter magnitudes, but FS90 192 is located with the bulk of early-type dwarf galaxies defining what could be their low limiting r_ eff . If we calculate for their luminosities the corresponding _ eff on the locus of r_ eff = 1 kpc, we obtain that they differ from the measured values in _ eff =2.9 mag arcsec ^ 2 for FS90 110, and _ eff =2.7 mag arcsec ^ 2 for FS90 192. Therefore both cE galaxies depart from the trend defined by early-type dwarf galaxies in a similar manner, defining what could be a photometric criterion to identify candidate cE galaxies.

In Paper II we showed that one of them, FS90 110, displays a low surface brightness bridge that links it with NGC 3258, confirming the existence of an interaction between both galaxies. Surface brightness profiles displaying a break, as is the case of FS90 110 (see Paper II) would favour this interpretation choi02,MJ06 .

gmw_00011598.name: J0042+3150
gmw_00011598.overview: J0042+3150 is a giant radio galaxy located in a dense environment [arXiv:2201.12353]. It is hosted by the brightest cluster galaxy of the galaxy cluster WHL J004253.1+315028 [arXiv:2201.12353].

sources.summary: The catalog paper identifies J0042+3150 as the only giant radio galaxy in its sample in a dense environment, hosted by the brightest cluster galaxy of WHL J004253.1+315028.

quotes.quote: One GRG (J0042+3150) in our sample is present in the brightest galaxy cluster.
We find from our sample, only one GRG (J0042+3150) is placed in strong density environments. The host galaxy of GRG presents in the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in cluster WHL We12 .
For GRG-J0042+3150, the calculated galaxy cluster (WHL J004253.1+315028) parameters are r_ 200 =0.97, R_ L = 18.14, M_ 200 =0.61 10^ 14 M_ and N_ 200 = 8 respectively.

gmw_00011599.name: UGC 7774
gmw_00011599.overview: UGC 7774 is an edge-on galaxy that exhibits a striking warp in its structure [arXiv:2210.09383]. It has a single, disturbed companion galaxy located at a projected distance of approximately 232 kpc [arXiv:2210.09383].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 7774 as an edge-on galaxy with a striking warp and a distant disturbed companion that may be related to the warp.

quotes.quote: UGC 7774 is an edge-on galaxy with one of the most striking examples of a warp known (see Figure fig: ). We detect a single companion at a rather large distance from the galaxy (d _ proj 232 kpc). This companion does appear quite disturbed and hence it might be related to UGC 7774's warp however a detailed analysis would be required to further this idea. Its systemic velocity is aligned with the rotation in the western side of UGC 7774.

gmw_00011600.name: PMN J0948+0022
gmw_00011600.overview: PMN J0948+0022 is a narrow-line type I quasar [arXiv:0905.4558] that exhibits a blue optical continuum attributed to its accretion disk [arXiv:0905.4558]. The galaxy hosts a relativistic jet [arXiv:0905.4558], which produces hard X-ray emission and strongly variable radio and gamma-ray emissions [arXiv:0905.4558].

sources.summary: The paper reports Fermi/LAT gamma-ray emission from PMN J0948+0022, classifying it as a narrow-line type I quasar with variable radio and gamma emission, an accretion-disk continuum, and jet-powered X-rays.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery by the Large Area Telescope (LAT) onboard the Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope of high-energy ray emission from the peculiar quasar PMN J0948+0022 ( z=0.5846 ). The optical spectrum of this object exhibits rather narrow H (FWHM(H ) 1500 km s ^ 1 ), weak forbidden lines and is therefore classified as a narrow-line type I quasar. This class of objects is thought to have relatively small black hole mass and to accrete at high Eddington ratio. The radio loudness and variability of the compact radio core indicates the presence of a relativistic jet.

Both radio and ray emission (observed over 5-months) are strongly variable. The simultaneous optical and X-ray data from Swift show a blue continuum attributed to the accretion disk and a hard X-ray spectrum attributed to the jet.

gmw_00011601.name: Lock850.041b
gmw_00011601.overview: Lock850.041b is a distant source with a photometric redshift of z=2.22 [arXiv:0802.0497]. Its optical morphology exhibits an arc-like structure, and its photometry is well fit by a quasar template, suggesting it is likely a strongly lensed quasar [arXiv:0802.0497].

sources.summary: The paper describes Lock850.041b as an interesting secondary counterpart with QSO-like photometry, arc-like morphology, and likely strong lensing by a nearby elliptical galaxy.

quotes.quote: We draw particular attention to the interesting source Lock850.041b. The radio co-ordinates of this source correspond to the brightest but slightly less favourable of two radio sources within 8'' of the SCUBA centroid. ivison05 were unable to measure a spectroscopic redshift at the radio co-ordinates of Lock850.041b but did measure a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.69 for an elliptical galaxy lying 2'' to the south-west of the radio co-ordinates. We determine a photometric redshift of z=2.22 for the radio source and find that the photometry is well fit by a QSO SED template. Combined with the optical morphology which exhibits arc-like structure (the arcs being a result of a relatively bright extended host galaxy), these facts suggest that Lock850.041b is very likely a strongly lensed QSO. This supports the claim of chapman02 that the source is a plausible lens.

gmw_00011602.name: QSO B0014 + 810
gmw_00011602.overview: QSO B0014 + 810 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar [arXiv:2104.11094]. X-ray observations of the source reveal a high X-ray luminosity and a spectrum shaped by intrinsic radiative processes [arXiv:2104.11094].

sources.summary: The paper finds QSO B0014 + 810 is better described by intrinsic jet spectral curvature, with an EXP-model preference and folding energy near 0.7 keV.

quotes.quote: QSO B0014 + 810: study the XMM-Newton observation of 2001 August 23 and infer a photon index of 1.61, an X-ray luminosity of 5.94 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 and an excess absorption column density N_H of 1.82 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . re-analyze the XMM-Newton observation during 2001 and infer a harder photon index of 1.44 and a N_H of 6.00 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 , attributable to the screening of flaring backgrounds and improved calibration; the study finds a better fit with the broken power law model (including Galactic absorption) which provides an index _1 of 1.1 below the break energy E_b at 1.0 keV and _2 of 1.43 above E_b . This suggests a spectrum shaped by intrinsic radiative processes. study the XMM-Newton observation of 2001 August 23 and find a in the range of 1.49 - 1.77 and excess absorption N_H of < 0.54 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 with an inability to distinguish between excess absorption and intrinsic radiative processes; they also find a warm IGM with a temperature of > 6.9 10^6 K and number density of 10^ 7 cm ^ 3 in the context of the IGM contribution to the excess absorption along the line of sight. Our analysis of the X-ray spectra suggests a preference for the EXP model with a of 2.0 and E_ f of 0.7 keV.

gmw_00011603.name: PBC J1656.2 3303
gmw_00011603.overview: PBC J1656.2 3303 is a radio-loud blazar [arXiv:2104.11094]. The source was initially identified in a hard X-ray survey, and its X-ray spectrum has been modeled with both excess absorption and broken power-law fits [arXiv:2104.11094].

sources.summary: The paper treats PBC J1656.2 3303 as a mixed case: XMM-Newton favors an EXP model, Swift favors excess absorption, and better spectroscopy is needed.

quotes.quote: PBC J1656.2 3303 The source was identified in a Swift/BAT hard X-ray (14 - 200 keV) survey during 2004 December - 2005 September, with a reported preliminary photon index of 1.26 2006ATel..799....1O . The Swift/BAT based X-ray spectrum from 2006 June 09 is found to be fit well by a power law with excess absorption, yielding a photon index of 1.38 and an excess column density N_H of 2.21 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 2006ATel..835....1T . study the optical, X-ray and radio properties confirming its blazar nature; Swift/XRT observations on 2006 June 9 and 13 and Integral/IBIS observations between 2002 October and 2006 April result in a 0.7 - 200 keV continuum which is fit well by both a broken power law (including only Galactic absorption) with _1 of 0.86, _2 of 1.81, and E_b at 2.7 keV, and a power law including excess absorption with of 1.64, and N_H of 6.7 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . study the XMM-Newton observation from 2009 September 11, and NuSTAR and Swift/XRT based X-ray spectra from 2015 September 27, finding that the broken power law model (including Galactic absorption) provides a better fit, and yields indices _1 of 0.88, _2 of 1.19, and a break energy E_b at 1.44 - 2.24 keV; they also find a warm IGM with a temperature of 5.4 10^5 K and number density of 10^ 7 cm ^ 3 in the context of the IGM contribution to the excess absorption along the line of sight. Our analysis of the XMM-Newton spectrum favors the EXP model with a of 1.29 and a folding energy of 0.89 keV based. The analysis of the Swift spectrum however favors the PL model with a of 1.6 and an excess column density N_H of 15 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 . The mixed results necessitate new higher resolution X-ray spectroscopic observations.

gmw_00011604.name: PKS 2126 158
gmw_00011604.overview: PKS 2126 158 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2104.11094] that is also classified as a gigahertz-peaked spectrum source [arXiv:2104.11094]. Its X-ray spectrum shows mixed model preferences between XMM-Newton and Swift observations, leaving its excess absorption and intrinsic curvature unresolved [arXiv:2104.11094].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2126 158 as a GPS FSRQ with mixed XMM and Swift model preferences, making its absorption and intrinsic-curvature interpretation unresolved.

quotes.quote: In the remaining two FSRQs, PBC J1656.2 3303 and PKS 2126 158, the results are mixed. For PBC J1656.2 3303, the XMM-Newton spectrum is better fit with the EXP model while the Swift spectrum is better fit with the PL model, while the situation is reversed for PKS 2126 158, i.e. the XMM spectrum is better fit with the PL model while the Swift spectrum is better fit with the EXP model. The analysis is thus indicative of the difficulty in identifying sources particularly where the excess absorption scenario shapes their X-ray spectra.

PKS 2126 158: This FSRQ is also a GPS source and is of similar interest as PKS 1127 145 in the context of the synchrotron turnover originating from intrinsic jet based processes (SSA) or from the surrounding external environment (FFA), connected to the physical size of the GPS radio lobes and their dynamic evolution .

Our analysis of the XMM-Newton spectrum favors the PL model with a of 1.4 and an excess column density of 1.1 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 . The analysis of the Swift spectrum however favors the EXP model with a of 2.3 and a folding energy of 1.4 keV. The mixed results necessitate new higher resolution X-ray spectroscopic observations.

gmw_00011605.name: PKS 2149 306
gmw_00011605.overview: PKS 2149 306 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar [arXiv:2104.11094]. Its X-ray spectrum exhibits a spectral flattening and exponential roll-off below 1 keV, which is attributed to intrinsic radiative processes within its jet [arXiv:2104.11094].

sources.summary: The paper attributes PKS 2149 306 spectral flattening to intrinsic jet processes, consistent with a Swift EXP-model preference and previous roll-off interpretation.

quotes.quote: An intrinsically shaped particle energy distribution (and hence that of the produced photons) accelerated by the jet is found to shape the spectra (EXP model) in four of the FSRQs: RBS 315, PKS 1830 211, QSO B0014 + 810 and PKS 2149 306. For these, the spectral index is in the range 1.22 to 1.97, and the folding energy of the exponential roll-off E_f is in the range 0.356 keV to 1.127 keV (observer frame).

With these, the inferred break energy is in the range 0.04 - 1.42 (QSO B0014 + 810), 0.05 - 1.68 (RBS 315), 0.05 - 1.77 (PKS 1830 211) and 0.05 - 1.85 (PKS 2149 306). These are consistent with the current estimates for these sources (see Tables paratabxmm_2 and paratabswift ) and imply that the observed flattening is more likely from jet based intrinsic radiative processes.

The study of infers a spectral roll-off at energies < 1 keV from the Swift spectra during 2005, and attribute this to intrinsic processes in the jet owing to the onset of inverse Compton emission. Our analysis of the Swift spectrum suggests a preference for the EXP model with a of 1.59 and a folding energy of 0.51 keV. This is consistent with the inference of , with a consequent attribution of the spectral flattening to intrinsic jet based physical processes.

gmw_00011606.name: PRIMER-EXCELS-117560
gmw_00011606.overview: PRIMER-EXCELS-117560 is a massive, compact quiescent galaxy at a redshift of z = 4.62 [arXiv:2405.02242]. Its spectrum displays deep Balmer absorption lines, a strong 4000 Å break, and a high stellar velocity dispersion of 360 km/s [arXiv:2405.02242].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PRIMER-EXCELS-117560 as a z=4.62 massive quiescent galaxy with deep Balmer absorption, high stellar metallicity, compact size, and high velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: PRIMER-EXCELS-117560 (top panel) was selected as a robust candidate ( >95 per cent chance of being at z>2 and quiescent) by the process described in Section subsec:primer_passive . This galaxy was also selected as a non-robust candidate ( 50-95 per cent chance of being at z>2 and quiescent) in our earlier pre-JWST work Carnall2020 based on the catalogue, as well as having been selected independently by . PRIMER-EXCELS-109760 (second panel) was selected as a non-robust candidate (with 86 per cent probability of being at z>2 and quiescent) based on the PRIMER NIRCam data, having previously been classified as star-forming ( <50 per cent chance) in our earlier pre-JWST analysis. Both of the spectra for these objects exhibit a forest of extremely deep Balmer absorption features.

Both spectra exhibit the extremely deep Balmer absorption lines associated with 500 Myr old stellar populations, however PRIMER-EXCELS-117560 exhibits a much stronger 4000 \ break. Whilst the 4000 \ break is often used as an age indicator, it also has a well-known secondary dependence on stellar metallicity (e.g., Beverage2021 ). The Mg i triplet feature at 5170 \ is also far more pronounced in the spectrum of PRIMER-EXCELS-117560.

For PRIMER-EXCELS-117560, we obtain r_e=610 10 pc and n=4.7 0.1 . For PRIMER-EXCELS-109760, we obtain r_e=330 10 pc and n=5.2 0.2 . These statistical uncertainties are extremely small, as is common for galaxy size measurements (e.g., Ji2024 ). To gain some estimate of the systematic uncertainty, we apply the same fitting process to the F356W band, obtaining results consistent to within 10 per cent. These measurements place these galaxies significantly below the average size-mass relations for lower-redshift quiescent galaxies of the same stellar mass (e.g., 2.5 kpc at z 1.1 , or 4 kpc at z 0.7 ; Hamadouche2022 ). Our results are however broadly consistent with the 250-800 pc sizes measured for other photometrically selected z>4 quiescent galaxies by . Interestingly, the size of PRIMER-EXCELS-109760 is most similar to GS-9209, and both of these galaxies are smaller by at least a factor of 2 than the other 3 galaxies we analyse in this work. It is plausible that this difference could be in some way linked with the apparently substantially lower stellar metallicities of these systems. We calculate the stellar mass surface density within r_e for these galaxies, obtaining values of log _ 10 ( _ eff \ / \ M_ \ kpc ^ 2 ) = 10.63 0.03 and 11.23 0.04 for PRIMER-EXCELS-117560 and 109760 respectively. This places these objects amongst the most dense stellar systems in the Universe, and consistent with the inner regions of local elliptical galaxies (e.g., Hopkins2010 ). PRIMER-EXCELS-109760 appears to be particularly extreme, with a stellar mass surface density 0.5 dex in excess of the other galaxies in our sample, and even exceeding GS-9209, by 0.1 dex. Velocity dispersions As can be seen from our observed spectra shown in Fig. fig:spec2 , PRIMER-EXCELS-117560 has a significantly higher velocity dispersion than PRIMER-EXCELS-109760. After correction for instrumental dispersion (averaging 128 km s ^ 1 ) and the intrinsic dispersion of our stellar models ( 70 km s ^ 1 ), we obtain a stellar velocity dispersion, _*=360 20 km s ^ 1 for PRIMER-EXCELS-117560.

gmw_00011607.name: PRIMER-EXCELS-109760
gmw_00011607.overview: PRIMER-EXCELS-109760 is a compact quiescent galaxy at a redshift of z=4.62 [arXiv:2405.02242]. It formed early at z ~ 9.4, exhibiting low stellar metallicity, deep Balmer absorption, and strong Na D absorption that indicates cool interstellar gas [arXiv:2405.02242].

sources.summary: The paper presents PRIMER-EXCELS-109760 as a compact z=4.62 quiescent galaxy with deep Balmer absorption, low inferred metallicity, early formation, and strong Na D absorption.

quotes.quote: It is also interesting to note that PRIMER-EXCELS-109760 exhibits extremely deep Na i 5890 ,5896 \ (Na D) absorption, evidence for cool gas in the interstellar medium (e.g., Belli2023 ). Interestingly, this is also the galaxy in our sample with the highest continuum dust attenuation (as measured by A_V ; see Table table:galaxies ), which is known to be correlated with the Na D feature (e.g., Roberts-Borsani2019 ). There is however no clear evidence that this feature is blueshifted, which would indicate the gas is outflowing, as has been observed in young quiescent galaxies at lower redshift (e.g., Maltby2019 ).
For PRIMER-EXCELS-109760, we measure an earlier t_ form = 0.51 0.05 Gyr, equivalent to a formation redshift of z_ form = 9.4 0.7 .

In marked contrast to this, we recover a significantly lower stellar metallicity for PRIMER-EXCELS-109760 of log _ 10 (Z_*/ Z_ )=-0.41^ +0.06 _ 0.09 . This slightly puzzling result appears to be qualitatively well supported by a simple visual inspection of the two spectra in Fig. fig:spec2 . Both spectra exhibit the extremely deep Balmer absorption lines associated with 500 Myr old stellar populations, however PRIMER-EXCELS-117560 exhibits a much stronger 4000 \ break. Whilst the 4000 \ break is often used as an age indicator, it also has a well-known secondary dependence on stellar metallicity (e.g., Beverage2021 ). The Mg i triplet feature at 5170 \ is also far more pronounced in the spectrum of PRIMER-EXCELS-117560. Regardless of the precision of these measurements in the absence of enhanced models, it seems clear that these two galaxies exhibit very different stellar metallicities. Our result for PRIMER-EXCELS-117560 is broadly consistent with the stellar metallicities reported by for Heavy Metal survey Kriek2023 massive quiescent galaxies at z 1.4 and z 2.1 , and we also obtain very similar results for ZF-UDS-6496 and ZF-UDS-7329. The metallicity for PRIMER-EXCELS-109760 is much lower, though much more consistent with the low metallicity we measured for GS-9209 in . Whilst unexpected, this result is not without precedent, given that ultra-massive quiescent galaxies at z 2 have also been reported to exhibit a broad spread in stellar metallicity (e.g., Kriek2016, Jafariyazani2020 ). This result hints at significantly different evolutionary pathways for our two galaxies, despite their sharing several of the same basic properties (e.g., stellar mass) and having formed in relatively close proximity (see Section subsec:structure ). A potential explanation would be that PRIMER-EXCELS-109760 and GS-9209 are the product of recent mergers, meaning their stars formed in shallower potential wells, hence losing more of their metals to more-efficient outflows of enriched gas. Physical sizes We show PRIMER F277W cutout images for our two z=4.62 massive quiescent galaxies in Fig. fig:cutouts . We measure effective radii, r_e , and S ersic indices, n , following the process described in Section subsec:size_fitting . For PRIMER-EXCELS-117560, we obtain r_e=610 10 pc and n=4.7 0.1 . For PRIMER-EXCELS-109760, we obtain r_e=330 10 pc and n=5.2 0.2 .

gmw_00011608.name: ZF-UDS-6496
gmw_00011608.overview: ZF-UDS-6496 is an extreme post-starburst galaxy at a redshift of z=3.99 [arXiv:2405.02242]. It is a compact, quenched galaxy that formed during an intense, brief starburst event at z~5.5 [arXiv:2405.02242].

sources.summary: The paper describes ZF-UDS-6496 as a z=3.99 extreme post-starburst galaxy with high stellar mass and metallicity, a brief intense starburst, compact size, and high velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: ZF-UDS-6496: an extreme PSB at z=3.99 The third panel in Fig. fig:spec2 shows ZF-UDS-6496 (PRIMER-EXCELS-50789), which was selected from our PRIMER catalogue in Section subsec:primer_passive with a 99.98 per cent chance of being at z>2 and quiescent. This galaxy met virtually all of the criteria to be included in our pre-JWST analysis in , however it was removed due to our reduced chi-squared cut (this galaxy was also noted to be fitted with a relatively high chi-squared value by Schreiber2018 ). This galaxy was first spectroscopically observed with Keck/MOSFIRE by , who reported a non-detection, from which they inferred a lack of strong emission lines suggesting this galaxy was in fact quiescent.

In our medium-resolution data, this galaxy exhibits a very extreme PSB spectral shape, with deep and broad Balmer absorption lines. A summary of the physical properties we infer for this galaxy is given in Table table:galaxies . We again infer a mass of log _ 10 (M_*/ M_ ) 11 for this galaxy, as well as a very similar, relatively high stellar metallicity to PRIMER-EXCELS-117560 of log _ 10 (Z_*/ Z_ )=0.32^ +0.04 _ 0.05 . The SFH we infer for this galaxy is shown in Fig. fig:sfh . It can be seen that ZF-UDS-6496 is by far the youngest galaxy in our sample, having formed in an extremely intense, brief starburst event at z 5.5 .

From this F277W image, we measure an effective radius for ZF-UDS-6496 of r_e=730 10 pc. The stellar velocity dispersion we infer from our full spectral fitting is _*=370 10 km s ^ 1 , and we combine these measurements to infer a dynamical mass within r_e of log _ 10 (M_ dyn / M_ )=11.07 0.04 .

gmw_00011609.name: GRB081221 host galaxy
gmw_00011609.overview: The GRB081221 host galaxy is a high-redshift infrared galaxy [arXiv:2007.11874]. It has an estimated star formation rate of approximately 52 to 56 solar masses per year when informed by far-infrared detections [arXiv:2007.11874].

sources.summary: The paper finds Salpeter IMF fits the GRB081221 host better, with stellar mass consistent with earlier work but a lower, FIR-informed SFR.

quotes.quote: Our results show that Salpeter1955 IMF is better. In order to compare with previous work Perley2013a , we convert our stellar mass and SFR value from Salpeter1955 to Chabrier2003 by the factor from Madau2014 . The stellar mass is consistent with that in Perley2013a within the uncertainties. However, for the SFR values, our value is lower than previous work. When we transform our L_ IR to SFR through Kennicutt1998b , we obtained approximately SFR _ Kennicutt =52 3(M_ yr^ 1 ) which is very close to our estimation SFR_ SED =56(M_ yr^ 1 ) . The reason for the difference between our work and previous works is probably that no FIR detection was included in previous works, causing their SFR to be overestimated.

gmw_00011610.name: A2744-YD1
gmw_00011610.overview: A2744-YD1 is a high-redshift galaxy spectroscopically confirmed at a redshift of 7.8790 [arXiv:2305.04741]. It exhibits a coincident dust continuum, with an estimated dust mass of approximately 2.7 million solar masses after correcting for gravitational lensing magnification [arXiv:2305.04741].

sources.summary: The paper spectroscopically confirms A2744-YD1 at z=7.8790, detects coincident ALMA dust continuum, and estimates infrared luminosity and dust mass after lensing correction.

quotes.quote: In addition to the three known member galaxies, A2744-YD1, YD4, and the western component of YD7 (YD7-W), we serendipitously identified a new galaxy, A2744-s1, that was not recognized as a z 8 candidate in the previous works based on the HST and Spitzer data.

We obtained the spectroscopic redshifts ranging from 7.8733 to 7.8790 , corresponding to the maximum velocity offset of 152 10 km s ^ 1 . The line FWHM values are measured to be 118-183 km s ^ 1 after correction by the instrumental broadening (FWHM _ inst = 100 km s ^ 1 ; Jakobsen2022 ).

The dust continuum contours show a spatial coincidence in A2744-YD1, YD4, and YD7, within positional uncertainties, at a significance level >3 . The continuum flux densities are 83 34 , 60 25 , and 53 25 Jy in A2744-YD1, YD4, and YD7, respectively, without lensing magnification correction. In A2744-ZD1 and s1, we place a 3 upper limit on the flux density to be 20 Jy. We estimate the total infrared luminosity, L_ IR , by integrating the modified black-body radiation over 8-1000 . Because dust temperature and emissivity index values cannot be constrained with the current data, we assume a dust temperature of T_ d = 50 K and an emissivity index of _ d =1.5 (e.g., Bakx2021 ). We obtain L_ IR ( 1.4 0.5 ), ( 1.0 0.4 ), and ( 0.8 0.4 ) 10^ 11 \ for A2744-YD1, YD4, and YD7, respectively, after correcting for the cosmic microwave background effects ( da_Cunha2013 ). The dust mass is estimated to be M_ d = ( 2.7 1.1 ), ( 2.0 0.8 ), and ( 1.8 0.8 ) 10^ 6 , for A2744-YD1, YD4, and YD7, respectively, with a dust mass absorption coefficient = _ 0 ( / _ 0 )^ _ d , where _ 0 = 10 cm ^ 2 g ^ 1 at 250 \ ( hildebrand1983 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports A2744-s1 as a newly identified z=7.88 galaxy in the proto-cluster core, with no detected dust continuum and SED fitting from HST/JWST photometry.

quotes.quote: In addition to the three known member galaxies, A2744-YD1, YD4, and the western component of YD7 (YD7-W), we serendipitously identified a new galaxy, A2744-s1, that was not recognized as a z 8 candidate in the previous works based on the HST and Spitzer data.

We obtained the spectroscopic redshifts ranging from 7.8733 to 7.8790 , corresponding to the maximum velocity offset of 152 10 km s ^ 1 . The line FWHM values are measured to be 118-183 km s ^ 1 after correction by the instrumental broadening (FWHM _ inst = 100 km s ^ 1 ; Jakobsen2022 ).

In A2744-ZD1 and s1, we place a 3 upper limit on the flux density to be 20 Jy. We estimate the total infrared luminosity, L_ IR , by integrating the modified black-body radiation over 8-1000 . Because dust temperature and emissivity index values cannot be constrained with the current data, we assume a dust temperature of T_ d = 50 K and an emissivity index of _ d =1.5 (e.g., Bakx2021 ). We obtain L_ IR ( 1.4 0.5 ), ( 1.0 0.4 ), and ( 0.8 0.4 ) 10^ 11 \ for A2744-YD1, YD4, and YD7, respectively, after correcting for the cosmic microwave background effects ( da_Cunha2013 ). The dust mass is estimated to be M_ d = ( 2.7 1.1 ), ( 2.0 0.8 ), and ( 1.8 0.8 ) 10^ 6 , for A2744-YD1, YD4, and YD7, respectively, with a dust mass absorption coefficient = _ 0 ( / _ 0 )^ _ d , where _ 0 = 10 cm ^ 2 g ^ 1 at 250 \ ( hildebrand1983 ). Note that these physical quantities are corrected for the magnification factor of 2. To obtain physical properties of the galaxies, we performed SED fitting using photometry obtained from JWST and ALMA observations. We also use the ancillary HST data from the UNCOVER project ( Weaver2023 ) that include six filters, F435W, F606W, F814W, F125W, F140W, and F160W. Details of our methods are presented in Fudamoto2023 , and in the following we summarize essential items. We used the publicly available SED fitting code BAGPIPES Carnall2018 assuming delayed-tau star formation history (SFH). We adopt a dust attenuation law from Calzetti00 . For A2744-ZD1, we assume the redshift of z=7.88 . The \ line and dust continuum fluxes are included in the fitting process. For A2744-s1 and ZD1 in which dust continuum is not detected, the SED fitting uses only the HST and JWST photometry.

gmw_00011611.name: YD7
gmw_00011611.overview: YD7 is a high-redshift galaxy with a spectroscopically identified western component [arXiv:2305.04741]. Observations have detected dust continuum emission from the galaxy, indicating a dust mass of approximately 1.8 million solar masses [arXiv:2305.04741].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the western component of YD7 spectroscopically, detects ALMA dust continuum at YD7, and estimates its infrared luminosity and dust mass.

quotes.quote: In addition to the three known member galaxies, A2744-YD1, YD4, and the western component of YD7 (YD7-W), we serendipitously identified a new galaxy, A2744-s1, that was not recognized as a z 8 candidate in the previous works based on the HST and Spitzer data.

We obtained the spectroscopic redshifts ranging from 7.8733 to 7.8790 , corresponding to the maximum velocity offset of 152 10 km s ^ 1 . The line FWHM values are measured to be 118-183 km s ^ 1 after correction by the instrumental broadening (FWHM _ inst = 100 km s ^ 1 ; Jakobsen2022 ).

The dust continuum contours show a spatial coincidence in A2744-YD1, YD4, and YD7, within positional uncertainties, at a significance level >3 . The continuum flux densities are 83 34 , 60 25 , and 53 25 Jy in A2744-YD1, YD4, and YD7, respectively, without lensing magnification correction. In A2744-ZD1 and s1, we place a 3 upper limit on the flux density to be 20 Jy. We estimate the total infrared luminosity, L_ IR , by integrating the modified black-body radiation over 8-1000 . Because dust temperature and emissivity index values cannot be constrained with the current data, we assume a dust temperature of T_ d = 50 K and an emissivity index of _ d =1.5 (e.g., Bakx2021 ). We obtain L_ IR ( 1.4 0.5 ), ( 1.0 0.4 ), and ( 0.8 0.4 ) 10^ 11 \ for A2744-YD1, YD4, and YD7, respectively, after correcting for the cosmic microwave background effects ( da_Cunha2013 ). The dust mass is estimated to be M_ d = ( 2.7 1.1 ), ( 2.0 0.8 ), and ( 1.8 0.8 ) 10^ 6 , for A2744-YD1, YD4, and YD7, respectively, with a dust mass absorption coefficient = _ 0 ( / _ 0 )^ _ d , where _ 0 = 10 cm ^ 2 g ^ 1 at 250 \ ( hildebrand1983 ).

gmw_00011612.name: J-3608
gmw_00011612.overview: J-3608 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2401.07910]. It is a difficult radio target where supernova emission can rival the flux from its central black hole [arXiv:2401.07910].

sources.summary: The paper flags J-3608 as a difficult JADES radio target: ngVLA and SKA need longer exposures, and supernova emission can rival black-hole flux.

quotes.quote: Our estimates show that BH radio flux densities for most of the sources can be detected with ngVLA with integration times of 10 - 100 hr except for J-10013704 and J-3608. Radio emission from J-73488 and J-954 can be detected with integration times of only 1 hr at 1 - 10 GHz. On the other hand, SKA would require integration times of 100 hr or more at frequencies > 1 GHz to detect these sources except for J-10013704, J-3608 and J-62309, which require even longer times.
For J-3608, J-62309, J-77652 and J-10013704, the flux densities from SNe rival those of the BHs even at SFRs of 1 \ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00011613.name: BCG
aliases_external: RX J1720.1+2638 BCG / central radio galaxy
gmw_00011613.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of RX J1720.1 is a compact central radio galaxy [arXiv:1403.2820]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with a steep radio spectrum and lacks resolved large-scale jets or lobes [arXiv:1403.2820].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the RX J1720.1 BCG as a compact central radio galaxy with a steep active-radio-galaxy spectrum and no resolved large-scale jets or lobes.

quotes.quote: The central radio galaxy The radio source associated with the BCG (R.A. _ J2000 = 17h 20m 10.0s, Decl. _ J2000 =+26^ 37^ 32^ ; ) is unresolved in all existing observations of RX J1720.1. The VLA A-configuration data set at 4.86 GHz provides the highest angular resolution radio image ( 0^ .8 0^ .4 FWHM), which is presented in Fig. fig:mh_center (a), overlaid on the optical HST WFPC2 Hubble Space Telescope Wide-Field Planetary Camera 2. image. A single, compact component coincides with the optical peak of the galaxy; its beam-deconvolved size is <0^ .5 , which implies a linear size <1.4 kpc.

In these images, the point source at the BCG (white contours) is enshrouded by the innermost part of the larger-scale minihalo. No obvious extended features connected to the central point source, such as jets or lobes, are visible. The BCG radio properties are summarized in Table 3.

Radio spectra of the minihalo and BCG In Fig. fig:mh_sp (a), we show the radio spectra of the BCG (empty circles) and minihalo (filled circles) between 317 MHz and 8.44 GHz, based on the flux densities in Tables 3 and 4. The BCG spectrum can be fitted by a single power-law model over the entire frequency range, with a slope _ fit =0.83 , typical of an active radio galaxy e.g., 1992ARA&A..30..575C .

gmw_00011614.name: UDS-24561
gmw_00011614.overview: UDS-24561 is an X-ray luminous active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=3.21 [arXiv:2109.04493]. The galaxy exhibits a blue ultraviolet continuum slope and broad optical emission lines that likely originate from an active galactic nucleus-driven outflow [arXiv:2109.04493].

sources.summary: The paper reports UDS-24561 as an X-ray luminous z=3.21 AGN with extreme [O III]+H emission, a blue UV slope, and broad optical lines likely tracing an AGN-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: In this paper we report the detection of a X-ray luminous AGN at z=3.21 (UDS-24561) with extreme [ O III ] +H line emission (EW =1300 ) and a blue UV continuum slope ( =-2.34 ).

The [ O III ] doublets and H emission lines show extended profiles (Fig. fig:spectra ), and the [ O III ] 5007 line is best fitted by two Gaussians: a narrow component with FWHM =154 21 km / s and a broad component with FWHM =977 131 km / s (Fig. fig:oiii ). A broad line could be due to merger activity, emission from the broad line region of an AGN or AGN-driven outflows.

Although supernova feedback or shock-ionised outflows can also produce broad line emission, the FWHMs ( 300-600 km / s; e.g., Veilleux2005,Freeman2019,Matthee2021 ) are smaller than that of UDS-24561. On the other hand, the broad optical line velocity of UDS-24561 is in agreement with those of AGN outflows e.g., ForsterSchreiber2014,Genzel2014,Leung2017,Leung2019 . We estimate a lower limit to the broad [ O III ] 5007/ H ratio using the total flux of H (since we cannot accurately decouple the broad and narrow emission components for H ). The line ratio ( >9.3 at 3 ) is consistent with those of AGN e.g., Coil2015,Azadi2017 and greater than the maximum ratio predicted by starburst models e.g., Kauffmann2003,Kewley2013 . Thus we conclude that the broad optical lines of UDS-24561 likely originate from an AGN-driven outflow.

gmw_00011615.name: NB921-N-79144
gmw_00011615.overview: NB921-N-79144 is a massive Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 6.5 [arXiv:1703.10043]. Situated within a large-scale overdense region, it resides about 300 kpc from a companion galaxy and exhibits rapid star formation [arXiv:1703.10043].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NB921-N-79144 as one of two spectroscopically confirmed massive z=6.5 LAEs in an SXDS overdensity, about 300 kpc from its companion and forming stars rapidly.

quotes.quote: Our observations focus around two close, massive Lyman Alpha Emitters (LAEs) at redshift 6.5, discovered in the SXDS field within a large-scale overdense region ouchi2010 . The total GTC observing time in three medium band filters (F883w35, F913w25 and F941w33) is over 34 hours covering 7.0 8.5 arcmin ^2 (or 30,000 Mpc ^3 at z=6.5 ). In addition to the two spectroscopically confirmed LAEs in the field, we have identified 45 other LAE candidates.
The 2 LAEs are only 300 kpc apart, assuming they are relaxed, and yield star formation rates between 25-45 M_ /yr based on the estimation by ouchi2010 .
Within class-I LAE candidates, LAE-C-1-01 and LAE-C-1-02 are the 2 spectroscopically confirmed LAE at z 6.5, namely NB921-N-79144 and NB921-N-77765, respectively ouchi2010 .

gmw_00011616.name: NB921-N-77765
gmw_00011616.overview: NB921-N-77765 is a massive Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 6.5 [arXiv:1703.10043]. It resides within a large-scale overdense region, situated about 300 kiloparsecs from a companion galaxy and exhibiting rapid star formation [arXiv:1703.10043].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NB921-N-77765 as one of two spectroscopically confirmed massive z=6.5 LAEs in an SXDS overdensity, about 300 kpc from its companion and forming stars rapidly.

quotes.quote: Our observations focus around two close, massive Lyman Alpha Emitters (LAEs) at redshift 6.5, discovered in the SXDS field within a large-scale overdense region ouchi2010 . The total GTC observing time in three medium band filters (F883w35, F913w25 and F941w33) is over 34 hours covering 7.0 8.5 arcmin ^2 (or 30,000 Mpc ^3 at z=6.5 ). In addition to the two spectroscopically confirmed LAEs in the field, we have identified 45 other LAE candidates.
The 2 LAEs are only 300 kpc apart, assuming they are relaxed, and yield star formation rates between 25-45 M_ /yr based on the estimation by ouchi2010 .
Within class-I LAE candidates, LAE-C-1-01 and LAE-C-1-02 are the 2 spectroscopically confirmed LAE at z 6.5, namely NB921-N-79144 and NB921-N-77765, respectively ouchi2010 .

gmw_00011617.name: SDSS J111611.03+411820.9
gmw_00011617.overview: SDSS J111611.03+411820.9 is a foreground lens galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.25 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701537]. It strongly lenses a background quasar into two images separated by 13 arcseconds, and it has a measured light ellipticity of around 0.3 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701537].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J111611.03+411820.9 as the foreground lens galaxy in a wide-separation quasar lens, estimates its redshift, and models its mass and ellipticity.

quotes.quote: The quasars SDSS J111611.73+411821.5 and SDSS J111610.68+411814.4 are recognized as two images of the same object, being strongly lensed by the closer galaxy SDSS J111611.03+411820.9. The source is located at a redshift of z 3 , while the redshift of the lens galaxy is z 0.25 . The separation of the images is large, 13 arcsec. Commonly used models of the mass distribution for the lens galaxy with values of the parameters in the expected range describe the positions and fluxes of the images.

The mass of the lens galaxy, defined as all the mass contained inside the Einstein radius, is M_L = (5.3 0.1) 10^ 12 M_ in both models. The fitted ellipticity for the lens galaxy in the SIE model is smaller than the measured ellipticity for the light, which is around 0.3 in all the bands, as presented in Table  tab:lensgalaxy . The fitted shear in the SISS model is in the typical range for lens systems. The fitted position angles are not compatible with the measured angle for the light, shown in Table  tab:lensgalaxy , what means that some slight external perturbation may be present.

gmw_00011618.name: PID352
gmw_00011618.overview: PID352 is an intrinsically luminous, obscured quasar located at a redshift of approximately 1.6 [arXiv:1509.05413]. The galaxy hosts a high-velocity X-ray outflow with an iron emission and absorption line complex, which is capable of driving feedback into its host galaxy [arXiv:1509.05413].

sources.summary: PID352 is described as a luminous obscured quasar at z about 1.6 with iron emission and absorption features indicating a high-velocity X-ray outflow capable of host-galaxy feedback.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The resulting 2--10 keV position of PID352 is (ra,dec)=(03:32:42.46, 27:38:15.7; ranalli2013 ).

quotes.quote: Here we present ultra-deep \ and \ spectral data of an obscured ( 2 10^ 23  cm ^ 2 ), intrinsically luminous ( 4 10^ 44  erg s ^ 1 ) quasar (named PID352) at z 1.6 (derived from the \ spectral analysis) in the \ Deep Field-South. The source is characterized by an iron emission and absorption line complex at observed energies of E 2-3  keV. While the emission line is interpreted as being due to neutral iron (consistent with the presence of cold absorption), the absorption feature is due to highly ionized iron transitions (FeXXV, FeXXVI) with an outflowing velocity of 0.14^ +0.02 _ 0.06 , as derived from photoionization models. The mass outflow rate -- 2    yr ^ 1 - is similar to the source accretion rate, and the derived mechanical energy rate is 9.5 10^ 44   , corresponding to 9 of the source bolometric luminosity. PID352 represents one of the few cases where indications of \ outflowing gas have been observed at high redshift thus far.

For PID352 a mass outflow rate of 2   yr ^ 1 (range 0.3--6.8   yr ^ 1 ) is derived, which is consistent with the source accretion rate. We have also estimated that the mechanical power injected by the wind onto the host galaxy may be very high and potentially able to disrupt the bulge, provided that a large amount of the wind energy is efficiently transferred to the bulge gas on long ( Salpeter) timescales, which looks like an extreme scenario. In any case, the wind is likely to have a significant impact on the host galaxy.

gmw_00011619.name: SDSS J160200.05+211442.3
gmw_00011619.overview: SDSS J160200.05+211442.3 is a dwarf irregular galaxy that hosted the transient iPTF13z [arXiv:1703.09679]. It has an elongated, slightly bent shape with a physical size, irregular appearance, and absolute magnitude comparable to the Small Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:1703.09679].

sources.summary: SDSS J160200.05+211442.3 is presented as the irregular dwarf host of iPTF13z, with SMC-like luminosity, dimensions, physical size, and appearance.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: mcconnachie09 gives the position of the galaxy as = 16^h 02^m 0 1 , = 21 14 42 4 (J2000.0).

quotes.quote: Host galaxy iPTF13z was found in the irregular galaxy SDSS J160200.05+211442.3 (alternative name SDSSCGB 53789.4 ). mcconnachie09 gives the position of the galaxy as = 16^h 02^m 0 1 , = 21 14 42 4 (J2000.0).

For SDSS J160200.05+211442.3 (hereafter J1602), the absolute band magnitude is M_r = -16.99 . The galaxy is thus a dwarf whose absolute magnitude is comparable to that of the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC, with M_ V ^ SMC = -16.7 ; using magnitude and distance modulus from NED).

The apparent dimensions of J1602 (from SDSS DR 6, via NED) corresponds to 2.7 1.7 kpc. This is somewhat smaller than the dimensions of the SMC on the plane of the sky, which is around 4 2 kpc see e.g. jac16 . The SDSS image of J1602 (Fig. fig:finder13z inset) shows an elongated, slightly bent shape. The physical size and irregular appearance of this dwarf galaxy is also comparable to the SMC.

gmw_00011620.name: G3
aliases_external: G3 in the field of HE 0238-1904
gmw_00011620.overview: G3 is a dwarf galaxy located near a quasar nebula [arXiv:2309.00053]. The galaxy exhibits narrow nebular emission from its interstellar medium and displays a structure resembling a tidal tail, which suggests it may have undergone tidal stripping [arXiv:2309.00053].

sources.summary: G3 is a dwarf galaxy near the quasar nebula whose nearby emission is narrow, bright in O III knots, and possibly shaped like a tidal tail.

quotes.quote: The South East extension of the nebula is spatially coincident with galaxies G1, G3, G4, and G5.

In the case of G3 and G4, the line width of the nebular emission near the galaxies is significantly narrower than the more extended emission from nearby parts of the giant nebula, indicating that the galaxy line emission likely arises in the ISM of the two dwarfs .

The dwarf galaxies G3 and G5 show a black possible tidal-tail-like structure as shown in panels (e) and (h) of Figure fig:gas , suggesting that this part of the nebula might be created from tidal stripping.

gmw_00011621.name: G4
aliases_external: G4 in the field of HE 0238-1904
gmw_00011621.overview: G4 is a dwarf galaxy located within the southeast extension of a giant nebula [arXiv:2309.00053]. Narrow local line emission near the galaxy suggests that this emission likely originates from its interstellar medium [arXiv:2309.00053].

sources.summary: G4 is discussed as a dwarf galaxy in the nebular southeast extension, with narrow local line emission likely arising from the dwarfs ISM.

quotes.quote: The South East extension of the nebula is spatially coincident with galaxies G1, G3, G4, and G5.

This southern region is spatially coincident and potentially kinematically coincident with G1, G3, G4 and G5. black However, the continua of these galaxies are too faint to measure stellar absorption-based redshifts.

In the case of G3 and G4, the line width of the nebular emission near the galaxies is significantly narrower than the more extended emission from nearby parts of the giant nebula, indicating that the galaxy line emission likely arises in the ISM of the two dwarfs .

gmw_00011622.name: G5
aliases_external: G5 in the field of HE 0238-1904
gmw_00011622.overview: G5 is a dwarf galaxy located near high-surface-brightness knots of enhanced O III emission [arXiv:2309.00053]. The galaxy exhibits a possible tidal-tail-like structure, which suggests that this region of the surrounding nebula may have been created by tidal stripping [arXiv:2309.00053].

sources.summary: G5 is discussed as a dwarf galaxy near enhanced O III emission and high-surface-brightness knots, with possible tidal-tail-like structure from stripping.

quotes.quote: The majority of the nebula is O II dominated but the region South East of the quasar has greater O III emission, particularly, at a few O III knots near G1, G3 and G5.
The knots near G3 and G5 have the highest surface brightness in the nebula. black Furthermore, the bright region extending to the South of the brightest knot near G3 is reminiscent of a tidal tail.

The dwarf galaxies G3 and G5 show a black possible tidal-tail-like structure as shown in panels (e) and (h) of Figure fig:gas , suggesting that this part of the nebula might be created from tidal stripping.

gmw_00011623.name: J091305.0 005343
gmw_00011623.overview: J091305.0 005343 is a gravitational lens system with a lens galaxy that is likely a spiral Sa galaxy [arXiv:1311.5893]. This lens galaxy is highly elongated and lacks nearby perturbers, while the reconstructed background source is very extended and has a low magnification [arXiv:1311.5893].

sources.summary: The paper says J091305.0 005343 has a poorly constrained, elongated lens galaxy, low magnification from an extended source, and near-infrared/SMA agreement.

quotes.quote: J091305.0 005343 (ID130): The lens galaxy is relatively poorly constrained in this system. The best fit model is consistent with zero external shear (as might be expected from the lack of observed nearby perturbers) and the lens has one of the higher elongations found in the sample of = 1.34 . The best fit reconstructed source is very extended and as such, the magnification is low. The residual map shown in Figure recon shows some significant features towards the lens centre which is not surprising given the difficulty reported by N13 in removing the lens light.

The SMA data are in close agreement with the near-IR data which increases confidence in the GALFIT subtraction (see N13), although the near-IR data imply a slightly larger magnification than the SMA data. We make the caveat that this lens, as pointed out by N13, is a likely Sa galaxy.

gmw_00011624.name: J090302.9 014127
gmw_00011624.overview: J090302.9 014127 is a strong gravitational lens system with a relatively poorly constrained lens model [arXiv:1311.5893]. Residual features in the lensed image may be caused by spatially dependent extinction in the lensing galaxy or residual flux from the lens itself [arXiv:1311.5893].

sources.summary: The paper describes J090302.9 014127 as an acceptably fitted but poorly constrained lens with residual features, possible extinction or lens residuals, and a plausible reconstructed source.

quotes.quote: J090302.9 014127 (ID17): This is a relatively poorly constrained lens although the fit is acceptable. There are some minor features in the residual image which occur at 3 o'clock and 11 o'clock around the inner radius of the annulus as shown in Figure recon . N13 have modelled the flux in the lensed image by fitting individual GALFIT profiles to the different components. Two of these profiles lie on the inner radius of the annulus and these coincide with the locations of the residuals found in the lens modelling here.

One interpretation is therefore that spatially dependent extinction in the lensing galaxy affects the lensed image. This is consistent with the colour of the feature at 11 o'clock which N13 determine as having a significantly redder colour than the average colour of the other GALFIT profiles, but not with the feature at 3 o'clock which is consistent in colour. An alternative explanation might therefore be that the lensed image contains residual flux from the lensing galaxy itself which is highly possible given the complexity in removing the lens from this system.

gmw_00011625.name: galaxy L
gmw_00011625.overview: Galaxy L is an elliptical lensing galaxy situated between two lensed quasar images [arXiv:astro-ph/0201057]. It has an ellipticity of 0.50 and exhibits extended plumes to its northwest, which may trace a prior interaction with the nearby galaxy M [arXiv:astro-ph/0201057].

sources.summary: The paper identifies galaxy L as the main elliptical lens between the quasar images, measures its shape, and argues it belongs to the overdense lensing cluster or group.

quotes.quote: Note the elongated lensing galaxy L appearing between the two quasar images. Two other objects are also visible in the immediate vicinity of the lensed source (labelled galaxies M and N in Fig.  fig1 ). Although at the confidence limit after the deconvolution process, the extended plumes to the NW of the lensing galaxy L might trace a prior interaction with galaxy M. However we shall ignore this feature in the subsequent modelling. Table  tab1 summarizes the main properties of the quasar images and of the lensing galaxy.

As the lensing galaxy appears to be essentially elliptical, models are particularly sensitive to its orientation and ellipticity: hence particular attention was paid to the precise determination of these parameters. We used the Im2shape software (Bridle et al. 2002) to determine accurate values of the PA and of the ellipticity of the galaxies L and M. We measure an ellipticity of 0.50 0.02 for galaxy L, the ellipticity being defined as =(a^2-b^2)/(a^2+b^2) . Its major axis is oriented at PA =-23^o 7^o .

gmw_00011626.name: galaxy M
gmw_00011626.overview: Galaxy M is a nearly circular companion galaxy located 2.56 arcseconds to the northeast of a quasar image [arXiv:astro-ph/0201057]. It has an estimated photometric redshift of approximately 0.89 and contributes to the local gravitational lensing potential [arXiv:astro-ph/0201057].

sources.summary: The paper describes galaxy M as a nearby, nearly circular companion to the main lens whose photometric redshift and mass contribution affect the lens model.

quotes.quote: Galaxy M is located at 2.56 to the NE of the quasar image A and is likely to play some role in the lens modelling. It is almost circular ( =0.04 0.02, PA =-40^o 10^o ).
We obtain, for galaxy M, z _ phot =0 .89 ^ +0.02 _ 0.26 and z _ wm =0.57, and for the main lensing galaxy L, z _ phot 0 .88 ^ +0.02 _ 0.26 and z _ wm =0.71.

Model L+M Therefore, in a second step we consider that galaxy M (the nearest galaxy to galaxy L) contributes to the total lensing potential as well. Again the quantities _L , _M , _L , _M , and the positions of the two galaxies are fixed to their observed values within their respective error bars. In this case we recover the flux ratio, but not the geometrical configuration of the quasar images.

gmw_00011627.name: BCG2
gmw_00011627.overview: BCG2 is a brightest cluster galaxy located within the Abell 2142 cluster [arXiv:1711.07806]. It exhibits a high velocity relative to the cluster's mean velocity and is associated with an infalling or merged group [arXiv:1711.07806].

sources.summary: The paper treats BCG2 as a high-velocity brightest cluster galaxy associated with an infalling or merged group and with A2142 merger history.

quotes.quote: In Fig.  fig:a2142radec the location of the two brightest galaxies in the cluster (BCG1 and BCG2) is denoted with stars. The lower panel of Fig.  fig:a2142radec shows that the BCG2 has high velocity with respect to the mean velocity of the cluster see also 2000ApJ...541..542M, 2011ApJ...741..122O . This galaxy may be the brightest galaxy of merged group or cluster 2000ApJ...541..542M . Other possible members of the BCG2 group that have very close sky positions and similar velocities as the BCG2 are also indicated.
In A2142, the BCG2 is associated with the infalling group; the last major merger may be related to the third brightest galaxy, BCG3.

gmw_00011628.name: BCG
gmw_00011628.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of the A1213 cluster is a bright radio source [arXiv:2303.02528]. This galaxy is offset from the cluster's X-ray emission centroid and exhibits a statistically peculiar velocity relative to the cluster's mean velocity [arXiv:2303.02528].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the A1213 BCG as ID 422, a bright radio source offset from the X-ray centroid, with a statistically peculiar velocity that supports an unrelaxed cluster state.

quotes.quote: In determining the cluster center, we considered the position in right ascension (R.A.) and declination (Dec.) of the brightest cluster galaxy in our sample (BCG) [ R.A.= 11^ h 16^ m 22 70 , Dec.= +29 15 08 3 (J2000) ] .
The cluster galaxy population is dominated by the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) ID 422, which is 70 far from the centroid of the X-ray emission (see Sect. xray ). It is also a bright radio source.

The BCG has a lower velocity than the mean velocity of the cluster, and Fig. fighisto (inset) shows how much this velocity is peculiar within the velocity distribution. According to the Indicator test by Gebhardt Beers ( ), the BCG velocity is peculiar at the >99 .l.; the same result is obtained when considering the sample of galaxies within R_ 200 . For the BCG, we calculate |V_ rf / _ V,200 |=0.80 , placing A1213 in the upper range of |V_ rf / _ V | distribution, since only about 10 of the clusters have such a high value (see Fig. 8 of Lauer et al. lau14 ).

gmw_00011629.name: BCG1
gmw_00011629.overview: BCG1 is a brightest cluster galaxy located in the central clump of the Abell 2163 cluster [arXiv:1203.0229, arXiv:0712.2715]. The galaxy coincides with a peak of dark matter indicated by single-imaged arclets [arXiv:1203.0229], and its axis is aligned east-west with the cluster's large-scale structure [arXiv:0712.2715].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCG1 as Abell 2163's central brightest cluster galaxy, near the main weak-lensing mass peak, without strong lensing images but with reported arclets and an offset X-ray peak.

quotes.quote: In the field of Abell 2163, the main peak is detected at more than 8 and is centered close to the central galaxy BCG1. No strong lensing features or multiple images are identified around this galaxy, precluding any strong lensing model to explore the central mass distribution. However, a few single imaged arclets were reported around the BCG1 fort94 , confirming that a peak of dark matter coincides with the galaxy (Fig. fig:vltcolor ).

These maps are very different from the X-ray map govoni04,bourdin11 , which traces the gas distribution with the following characteristics: a single peaked distribution, with an E-W elongation and a peak center significantly off-center compared to BCG1.

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCG1 as one of two brightest cluster galaxies in A2163-A, near clump A1, with the BCG axes aligned east-west with the cluster large-scale structure.

quotes.quote: It is important to stress that the two Brightest Cluster Galaxies (BCG1 and BCG2 in Fig. fig:BCGs and Table Table_BCG ) are both located in the central clump A, but their positions do not coincide with the maxima of the density peaks. However, while BCG1 is near A1 (at bright and faint magnitudes), BCG2 has a significant offset to the west of A2. The axes of these two giant galaxies and the line joining them are aligned along the same direction of the large--scale cluster density distribution, i.e. the E--W direction (see Table Table_BCG ).

gmw_00011630.name: NGC 4703
gmw_00011630.overview: NGC 4703 is a galaxy that exhibits kinematic indications of a weak bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0606056]. Its structure is characterized by K-band secondary maxima and at least three distinct regions in its surface brightness profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0606056].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4703 as a likely contaminant of the control sample, citing weak-bar kinematic evidence, K-band secondary maxima, and at least three regions in its surface-brightness profile.

quotes.quote: For example, ldp00a classified the bulge of NGC 3957 as close to boxy-shaped, bf99 and cb04 both found kinematic indications of weak bars in NGC 3957 and NGC 4703, and cb04 also found indication of non-axisymmetric motions in NGC 7123. The current K band imaging also shows centered X features in NGC 3957 and NGC 7123 and secondary maxima in NGC 3957 and NGC 4703. All three galaxies show at least 3 regions in their surface brightness profile.

gmw_00011631.name: NGC 7123
gmw_00011631.overview: NGC 7123 is a galaxy that exhibits non-axisymmetric motions [arXiv:astro-ph/0606056]. Its K-band imaging reveals centered X-shaped features, and its surface brightness profile is characterized by at least three distinct regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0606056].

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 7123 shows non-axisymmetric motions, centered X features in the K-band imaging, and at least three surface-brightness profile regions, making it a possible B/PS control-sample contaminant.

quotes.quote: For example, ldp00a classified the bulge of NGC 3957 as close to boxy-shaped, bf99 and cb04 both found kinematic indications of weak bars in NGC 3957 and NGC 4703, and cb04 also found indication of non-axisymmetric motions in NGC 7123. The current K band imaging also shows centered X features in NGC 3957 and NGC 7123 and secondary maxima in NGC 3957 and NGC 4703. All three galaxies show at least 3 regions in their surface brightness profile.

gmw_00011632.name: ID=11372
gmw_00011632.overview: ID=11372 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 3.3 [arXiv:1703.00354]. It is considered a possible Lyman continuum emitter with an almost point-like morphology, though it lacks a confirmed active galactic nucleus classification [arXiv:1703.00354].

sources.summary: The paper treats ID=11372 as a marginal U-band LyC candidate with high relative escape fraction, point-like morphology, no confirmed AGN, possible faint contamination, and need for deeper data.

quotes.quote: The second object ID=11372, shown in Fig. obj11372ima , is at best marginally detected at 1.5 in the U band, and its relative escape fraction is f^ rel _ esc 60.3 . This galaxy has been originally studied by as the object C029 of the Westphal field. Its morphology in the HST V606 band is almost point-like, but no X-ray detection has been found for this galaxy, and thus an AGN classification for this object cannot be confirmed. Even for this object there is a hint in the HST image of a very faint blob close to the z 3.3 galaxy (at 0.1 arcsec towards the right), which can act as a possible faint contaminant, compatible with the marginal (1.5 sigma) detection in the U band. However, no firm conclusions can be drawn for this galaxy, and deeper data with LBC in the U band and HST in the V606 band are needed in order to understand its nature.

In Table table:gal , we mark the ID=16479 galaxy as possibly contaminated by a foreground object, while the galaxy ID=11372, where this hypothesis is weak, is considered as a possible LyC emitter, pending deeper observations.

gmw_00011633.name: ID=16479
gmw_00011633.overview: ID=16479 is a high-redshift galaxy located in the CANDELS/GOODS-North field [arXiv:1703.00354]. It was initially identified as a candidate Lyman continuum emitter at a redshift of 3.371, but it was later discarded due to contamination from a nearby foreground source [arXiv:1703.00354].

sources.summary: The paper treats ID=16479 as a candidate LyC emitter but finds nearby blended emission implies foreground contamination; it marks and later discards the object from the clean stacking sample.

quotes.quote: Figure obj16479ima shows the galaxy ID=16479 in the CANDELS/GOODS-North field in the U and R band by LBC and in the HST V606 filter (the closest band to the LBC-R one). At the right of the z=3.371 galaxy (identified by a green circle in the HST thumbnail) there is a faint source at 1.0 arcsec distance, which is clearly detected in the three bands. If we assume that the two sources are at the same redshift ( z=3.371 ) and compute their escape fraction, we find f^ rel _ esc =206 . If we evaluate the escape fraction only of the fainter galaxy to the right (outside the green circle) and assuming it is at z 3.3 , we end up with f^ rel _ esc =627 . In this case, similar to that already reported by and , the so-called ``local'' escape fraction (i.e. calculated in a small region showing LyC emission) is not compatible with the stellar population of a z 3.3 galaxy, indicating a clear sign of contamination by a lower-z interloper.

In Table table:gal , we mark the ID=16479 galaxy as possibly contaminated by a foreground object, while the galaxy ID=11372, where this hypothesis is weak, is considered as a possible LyC emitter, pending deeper observations.
Of this sub-sample, eight galaxies were discussed in detail in and an additional galaxy has been discarded from Table table:gal , i.e. ID=16479 in the CANDELS/GOODS-North field.

gmw_00011634.name: host galaxy of GRB 051210
gmw_00011634.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 051210 is a faint, extended object detected within the Swift X-ray Telescope error circle [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128]. Based on the lack of a clear Balmer or 4000 Angstrom break, the galaxy has a robust redshift lower limit of z ≥ 1.4 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint extended object as the putative GRB 051210 host, finds no brighter error-circle source, and uses spectroscopy to set a robust redshift lower limit.

quotes.quote: These observations revealed a faint, extended object within the /XRT error circle at RA= 22 00 40.93 , Dec= 57 36 47.1 (J2000; gcn4330 ). We obtained a deeper observation of this burst with the LDSS3 instrument on 2006 Jan. 05 UT for a total exposure time of 1950 s in band. We detect the same extended object and measure its brightness to be r_ AB =24.04 0.15 mag in comparison to the SDSS standard stars Feige 22 and G 162-66; see Figure fig:hosts . No other sources are detected in the error circle to a 3 limit of r_ AB >24.9 mag.

We detect weak continuum emission in each individual spectrum, but no clear emission or absorption features are detected in the combined spectrum. The lack of detectable [ O 2 ] 3727 emission (if the host is star forming) indicates z 1.55 , while the lack of a clear Balmer/4000 \ break (if the host is early-type) indicates z 1.4 ; we use the latter as a robust lower limit on the redshift.

gmw_00011635.name: host galaxy of GRB 060121
gmw_00011635.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 060121 is an extended object detected in optical and near-infrared observations [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128]. Its observed colors suggest a possible Lyman break at 610 nm, which may indicate a redshift of approximately 4 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 060121 host as an extended object coincident with the afterglow, measures its optical and near-infrared brightness, and argues its colors may indicate z about 4.

quotes.quote: In addition, Hubble Space Telescope (HST) observations with the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) and the Near Infra-red Camera and Multi-Object Spectrometer (NICMOS) revealed an extended object coincident with the position of the afterglow with F606W_ AB =27.0 0.3 mag and F160W_ AB =24.5 0.2 mag ltf+06 . We observed the position of the afterglow with GMOS on Gemini-North starting on 2006 Feb. 1.54 UT for a total exposure time of 1080 s in band and 1050 s in i band. We detect the galaxy noted by ltf+06 , and measure its brightness at r_ AB =26.2 0.3 mag and i_ AB >25.9 mag ( 3 ) relative to several nearby stars with SDSS photometry ( ceh+06 ; see Figure fig:hosts ).

We measure for the host galaxy F606W_ AB =27.2 0.3 mag and F160W_ AB =24.8 0.1 mag, consistent with the values given by ltf+06 . Images of the host are shown in Figure fig:060121 . We note that our detection of the host in band ( _ eff =630 nm) is about 1 mag brighter than the F606W flux ( _ eff =590 nm). This is possibly indicative of a Lyman break at 610 nm, or z 4 , in good agreement with the redshift estimated from the afterglow colors.

gmw_00011636.name: source B
gmw_00011636.overview: Source B is an extended galaxy located within the error circle of the gamma-ray burst GRB 060801 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128]. It lies at a redshift of 1.1304 and has a luminosity comparable to an L-star galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128].

sources.summary: The paper treats source B in the GRB 060801 field as an extended source, measures its photometry and spectrum, and assigns it redshift 1.1304 with roughly L-star luminosity.

quotes.quote: Only two of these sources are located within the revised XRT error circle with R=23.7 mag (source "B") and 24.6 mag (source "D") and extended morphologies gcn5392 . We obtained imaging observations of the burst with the Large Format Camera on the Hale 200-inch telescope starting 16.0 hr after the burst for a total exposure time of 1500 s in band. Photometry of the two extended sources relative to several nearby SDSS stars indicates r_ AB =23.20 0.11 mag and 24.1 0.3 mag, respectively, somewhat brighter than the magnitude quoted in the GCN circular.

We detect weak continuum emission and a single broad ( FWHM 11 ) emission line at a wavelength of 7943.19 , which we identify as the barely-resolved [ O 2 ] 3727 doublet at z=1.1304 (Figure fig:060801 ). We note that all things being equal, source D, which is a factor of two fainter, is likely to reside at an even higher redshift. At z=1.1304 , the putative host galaxy has an absolute magnitude, M_B 21 mag, or L_B L^* compared to the luminosity function of z 1.1 galaxies in the DEEP2 survey wfk+06 .

gmw_00011637.name: host galaxy of GRB 061006
gmw_00011637.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 061006 is a faint, extended object with a measured emission-line redshift of z=0.4377 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128]. At this redshift, the galaxy has a sub-L-star luminosity of approximately 0.1 L* [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128].

sources.summary: The paper finds a faint extended object coincident with GRB 061006, obtains photometry and spectroscopy, and measures emission-line redshift z=0.4377 and sub-L-star luminosity.

quotes.quote: We detect a faint, extended object coincident with the afterglow at RA= 07 24 07.75 , Dec= 79 11 55.3 (J2000) with an uncertainty of about 0.16'' relative to several nearby 2MASS stars (Figure fig:hosts ). Photometry of this object relative to USNO-B indicates r_ AB =24.18 0.09 mag (with a systematic uncertainty of 0.26 mag) and i_ AB =23.11 0.09 mag (with a systematic uncertainty of 0.30 mag). We note that there are no other significantly brighter galaxies within about 45'' of the afterglow position. In addition, we observed the position of this source with Persson's Auxiliary Nasmyth Infrared Camera (PANIC) on the Magellan/Baade 6.5-m telescope on 2006 Oct. 8.39 UT in Y band for a total of 960 s.

We detect weak continuum emission and several emission lines corresponding to [ O 2 ] 3727 , H , [ O 3 ] 4959 , and [ O 3 ] 5007 at z=0.4377 0.0002 (Figure fig:061006 ). At this redshift the putative host galaxy has an absolute magnitude, M_B 18.6 mag, or L_B 0.1L^* compared to the luminosity function of z 0.5 galaxies in the DEEP2 survey wfk+06 . In addition, the isotropic equivalent energy of the burst at this redshift is E_ , iso =(6.9 0.5) 10^ 50 erg.

gmw_00011638.name: host galaxy of GRB 061210
gmw_00011638.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 061210 is located at a redshift of approximately z = 0.4095 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128]. At this redshift, the galaxy has an absolute magnitude of M_B ~ -20.4 mag, which corresponds to a luminosity of about 1.5 L* [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the likely GRB 061210 host within the fading XRT source, gives its position and brightness, and measures z=0.4095 from multiple emission lines.

quotes.quote: The subsequent detection of two X-ray sources within the BAT error circle gcn5921 allowed us to propose candidate host galaxies, of which the brightest has r_ AB =21.00 0.02 mag and is located at RA= 09 38 05.36 , Dec= +15 37 18.8 (J2000) with an uncertainty of about 0.25'' relative to USNO-B (Figure fig:hosts ). The XRT source containing this galaxy eventually faded gcn5983 , confirming that it is likely the host galaxy of GRB 061210. We note that the nearest galaxies which are brighter than the putative host (by about 1.8 mag) are located about 45'' and 60'' away from the X-ray afterglow position.

We detect several emission lines corresponding to [ O 2 ] 3727 , H , [ O 3 ] 4959 , [ O 3 ] 5007 , and H at z=0.4095 0.0001 (Figure fig:061210 ). At this redshift the putative host galaxy has an absolute magnitude, M_B 20.4 mag, or L_B 1.5 L^* compared to the luminosity function of z 0.4 galaxies in the DEEP2 survey wfk+06 . In addition, the isotropic equivalent energy of the burst at this redshift is E_ , iso =(4.6 0.8) 10^ 50 erg.

gmw_00011639.name: host galaxy of GRB 061217
gmw_00011639.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 061217 is an extended object located at a redshift of 0.8270 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128]. It has a luminosity of approximately half-L-star and no brighter galaxies are detected within a radius of about 80 arcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0611128].

sources.summary: The paper reports an extended GRB 061217 host candidate with coordinates, no brighter nearby galaxy, and an [O II] redshift of 0.8270 with half-L-star luminosity.

quotes.quote: This led to the identification of an extended object with r_ AB =23.33 0.07 located at RA= 10 41 39.08 , Dec= 21 07 28.7 (J2000) with an uncertainty of about 0.28'' relative to USNO-B (Figure fig:hosts ). No brighter galaxies are detected within a radius of about 80'' . We obtained spectroscopic observations of the putative host with LDSS3 on 2006 Dec. 22.24 UT, for a total exposure time of 4100 s.

We detect a single bright and resolved emission line which we identify as the [ O 2 ] 3727 doublet at z=0.8270 (Figure fig:061217 ). At this redshift the putative host galaxy has an absolute magnitude, M_B 19.6 mag, or L_B 0.5 L^* compared to the luminosity function of z 0.8 galaxies in the DEEP2 survey wfk+06 . In addition, the isotropic equivalent energy of the burst at this redshift is E_ , iso =(8.3 1.4) 10^ 49 erg.

gmw_00011640.name: Triangulum II
gmw_00011640.overview: Triangulum II is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of the Milky Way [arXiv:2003.05260]. It is located at a heliocentric distance of approximately 30 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2003.05260] and follows a close-pericenter orbit with an estimated infall time of 7 billion years [arXiv:1804.10230].

sources.summary: The paper observes Triangulum II with MAGIC, reports no gamma-ray excess, estimates its dark-matter J factor and density profile, and sets WIMP annihilation limits.
