Those for which we fitted the HCN, HCO ^+ , and HNC line FWHMs suggest comparable line widths with those of ^ 12 CO (CO hereafter) in canameras18b , despite the large uncertainties. Line widths in PLCK G244.8+54.9 are large but remain consistent with the range of 580-- 750 km s ^ 1 of high S/N lines across the CO ladder ( J_ up =3 -11) For single Gaussian fits not listed in C18. , suggesting that these tracers probe highly turbulent gas reservoirs with similar spatial distributions. Emission lines detected with NOEMA and ALMA have higher S/N and better resolution than those observed with EMIR, and are well fitted by single Gaussians. They are overplotted in Figs. fig:noema and fig:alma with the mid- J CO transition from canameras18b with the highest S/N, shifted to the same frequency, rescaled arbitrarily, and rebinned to spectral resolutions of 100 and 40 km s ^ 1 , respectively (rightmost panels of Fig. fig:noema and bottom panel of Fig. fig:alma ). The widths and centroids of dense-gas and CO transitions are similar in most cases, in line with previous findings for high-redshift SMGs oteo17,bethermin18 . For instance, the profiles of HCO ^+ (5--4) and CO(4--3) in PLCK G092.5+42.9 match reasonably well.

PLCK G092.5+42.9 and PLCK G145.2+50.9 have comparably low HCN/CO and HNC/CO ratios in the J=5 -4 transition (Table tab:lineratios ) and their dense-gas SLEDs likely peak at lower J levels than those of CO. However, for PLCK G244.8+54.9, the J=4 -3 and J=5 -4 ratios are consistent within 1 , suggesting very excited SLEDs in this extreme source. Variations in the line ratios demonstrate the need to carefully account for the excitation of high-density tracer molecules in order to obtain a deep understanding of the gas phase directly fueling star formation. We emphasize that, since line emissions are not resolved in our dataset, these ratios are consistently probing source-averaged quantities. Figure fig:lineratios shows that, for each rotational level, line ratios in the three GEMS broadly agree with previous studies of lensed SMGs. In general, HCN/CO and HCO ^+ /CO for J_ up =4 and 5 are slightly higher than the stack of SPT SMGs spilker14 . Only upper limits for PLCK G092.5+42.9 and PLCK G145.2+50.9 approach the low HCN/CO(5--4) ratio of 0.013 in the stack. danielson11 and oteo17 obtain higher HCN/CO and HCO ^+ /CO ratios in the Cosmic Eyelash, SDP9, and SDP11 than for the GEMS; this is not surprising since these studies use J=3 -2 and J=1 -0 ratios, which, in low-redshift ULIRGs, are generally higher than for J 4 due to the rapid decrease of the dense-gas SLEDs at J 3 papadopoulos14 . The spatially-integrated HNC/CO ratio in PLCK G244.8+54.9 is remarkably similar to the SPT stack, and it drops in PLCK G092.5+42.9 and PLCK G145.2+50.9 closer to the value of 0.01 in SPT0551 50 uncorrected for the CN(4--3) contribution, bethermin18 .

sources.summary: PLCK G092.5+42.9 is one of the brightest GEMS, with a double-peaked CO SLED, blue and red kinematic components, and cool extended plus warmer compact gas phases.

quotes.quote: The SLEDs of PLCK G092.5+42.9 and PLCK G244.8+54.9, the two brightest GEMS with well-sampled SLEDs up to J_ up =10 -11 exhibit double peaks that strongly suggest the presence of two distinct gas excitation components. We will provide further evidence of these multiple gas phases using detailed excitation models in Sect. sec:coexc .

In PLCK G092.5+42.9, the measured fluxes of the blue component are nearly constant between J_ up =4 and J_ up =8 , implying that the variations of the global SLED are mainly driven by the red component.

The two spectral components in PLCK G092.5+42.9 also exhibit similar levels of excitation, including a cool and extended gas phase peaking at J_ up =3 -5 and a warmer and more compact phase producing a secondary peak at J_ up =7 -9. The red kinematic component more closely resembles the global SLED and strongly peaks at J_ up =4 -5, although the flux drop measured for the CO(6--5) transition is affected by large uncertainties on the component separation, and possibly by differential magnification effects (see discussion in Sect. sssec:diff ). Its properties are fully consistent with the best-fit parameters of Table tab:radout .

gmw_00011685.name: RID 122 - J033222.49-274805.4
gmw_00011685.overview: RID 122 - J033222.49-274805.4 is a radio source identified with a faint K-band galaxy [arXiv:0805.1522]. The galaxy is undetected in optical bands but is bright in the near-infrared, Spitzer IRAC bands, and X-rays [arXiv:0805.1522].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RID 122 with a K-band galaxy, links it to a Chandra source, and matches it to an ATCA radio source that was previously unidentified.

quotes.quote: RID 122 - J033222.49-274805.4 This radio source is identified with a K =22.8 galaxy located at 0.5 arcsec from the VLA position. This counterpart is not visible in the optical bands but is brigther in the NIR and in all the four IRAC/Spitzer bands. This object is also detected with Chandra ( XID =570 from giacconi02 ) and it was identified with a R =24.6 galaxy located 1.2 arcsec north-east of the X-ray position. We propose that this X-ray sources is identified with the same counterpart of the radio source. This radio source is also part of the ATCA 1.4 GHz catalogue presented by , ATCDFS J033222.36-274807.3, and the distance between the two radio positions is 2.6 arcsec.

gmw_00011686.name: RID 140 - J033228.83-274356.4
gmw_00011686.overview: RID 140 - J033228.83-274356.4 is a bright galaxy associated with an X-ray detection [arXiv:0805.1522]. The galaxy features a radio jet, with its southern component tentatively associated with a second X-ray source [arXiv:0805.1522].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RID 140 with a bright galaxy and X-ray detection, discusses a nearby ATCA source, and associates a second X-ray source with its southern radio-jet component.

quotes.quote: RID 140 - J033228.83-274356.4 This source is identified with an I 18.3 galaxy 0.6 arcsec from the central radio component (RID 140A). This identification is also supported by an X-ray detection (XID=103 from giacconi02 ). We note that reported a radio source, ATCDFS J033228.71-274402.3, 7 arcsec away from our radio position. This ATCA source remained unidentified in their catalogue, and we believe this was due to the less accurate position provided by the ATCA survey compared to our. Finally, another X-ray detection, XID=630 from , was tentatively identified with a R 23 galaxy, we suggest that is instead be associated with the south component of this radio jet (B).

gmw_00011687.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 93564 is a spectroscopically confirmed Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:1806.01741]. It is located at a redshift greater than 3.1 and is coincident with a compact F160W imaging region [arXiv:1806.01741].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 93564 as a spectroscopically confirmed Gold candidate, reports it as LBG01, and excludes it from composites because its redshift exceeds 3.1.

quotes.quote: Target IDs 86861, 93564, 90675 and 92616 are all spectroscopically confirmed at z 3.07 and are coincident with compact F160W regions, except for 92616 where there is no F160W imaging.
Objects 86861, 93564 and 94460 are reported respectively as AGN04, LBG01 and LAE06 in .
Only LAEs within the SSA22 protocluster at approximately z 3.1 were included and LAEs with z>3.1 , such as ID 93564 were excluded from the composites.

gmw_00011688.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 94460 is classified as a Lyman-alpha emitter [arXiv:1806.01741]. It has a very young inferred stellar age and heavily influences the stacked Lyman continuum flux of its sample [arXiv:1806.01741].

sources.summary: The paper places 94460 in the Silver LyC-detection sample, reports it as LAE06, notes a possible contaminating offset line, and says it strongly affects the stacked LyC flux.

quotes.quote: Objects 86861, 93564 and 94460 are reported respectively as AGN04, LBG01 and LAE06 in .

ID 94460 is placed in the Silver sample because, in addition to , and H all at z=3.07 , a spatially-offset emission line inconsistent with this redshift was found which may indicate a contaminating source.
The stacked Lyman continuum flux is heavily influenced by 94460, but this object is faint in IRAC and individually has a very young inferred age with moderate escape fraction.

gmw_00011689.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 104037 is classified as a Silver sample object due to its extended F160W emission [arXiv:1806.01741]. This fairly bright galaxy is inferred to be old and contributes significantly to the rest-frame optical emission of the composite Silver spectral energy distribution [arXiv:1806.01741].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 104037 as a Silver object with extended F160W emission, robust escape-fraction constraints, and strong contribution to the Silver composite rest-frame optical flux.

quotes.quote: IDs 104037 and 105937 fall into the Silver sample due to their extended F160W regions.

For other objects with good photometric constraints in the rest frame UV and at F160W , such as Silver Object 104037, the escape fraction constraints can change by less than 20 when the IRAC photometry is ignored.
The objects 104037 and 105397 contribute greatly to the rest-frame optical emission of the composite Silver SED, but these objects are fairly bright and one is inferred to be old.

gmw_00011690.name: NGC 3448
gmw_00011690.overview: NGC 3448 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits structural indications of giant magnetic ropes [arXiv:2004.14383]. These magnetic field vectors are oriented nearly parallel to vertical rotation-measure transition lines, making the galaxy a candidate for hosting giant magnetic ropes extending into its stellar halo [arXiv:2004.14383].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3448 as a possible giant-magnetic-rope candidate, with magnetic vectors roughly parallel to vertical rotation-measure transition lines.

quotes.quote: Only in NGC 3044 and possibly in NGC 3448 we observed vertical giant magnetic ropes (GMRs) similar to those detected recently in NGC 4631.

In two of them (NGC 3044 and NGC 3448) the magnetic field vectors are also oriented nearly in parallel with these lines. We conclude that NGC 3044 is another galaxy with GMR extending far into its halo. NGC 3448 can be considered as a candidate for GMR which needs to be confirmed by more sensitive polarization observations.
Also NGC 3448 shows indications of GMRs.

gmw_00011691.name: J1532-56
gmw_00011691.overview: J1532-56 is a face-on disk galaxy characterized by a very extended, ring-like structure [arXiv:2312.03545]. It features an exceptionally low column density and a large disk that exceeds the field of view of earlier observations [arXiv:2312.03545].

sources.summary: The paper reports that HIZOA galaxy J1532-56 is a face-on, very extended ring-like disk with low column density, too diffuse for robust SMGPS catalog parameterisation.

quotes.quote: Forty (out of 42) HIZOA galaxies in the GA-SMGPS data were recovered by SoFiA. The HIZOA galaxy J1532-56 at V 1400 , identified as a face-on, very extended, ring-like disk in the GA-SMGPS, has no entry in our catalog: the flux per voxel was too low to allow for a robust parameterisation. Earlier observations of this source with the Australian Telescope Compact Array ATCA; staveley-smith1998 reported on its exceptionally low column density and large disk.

Interestingly, the SMGPS data finds the disk to be even more extended than the ATCA FoV. We performed deeper observations with the MeerKAT 32k correlator for further investigations of the properties of this particular source, and results will be presented in a later study.

gmw_00011692.name: NGC 4189
gmw_00011692.overview: NGC 4189 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0505407]. It exhibits asymmetric ionized-gas velocity profiles along its minor axis, while its stellar component rotates slower than the gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0505407].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4189 as a barred spiral with asymmetric minor-axis gas motions and slower stellar minor-axis rotation.

quotes.quote: The asymmetric velocity profiles observed along the minor axis of NGC 4064 and NGC 4189 can be explained as due to the presence of a bar.

The ionized-gas velocity profile measured along the optical minor axis of NGC 4064 and NGC 4189 is strongly asymmetric. The gas velocity rises to a maximum and it remains almost constant out to the last observed point on the receding side. On the contrary, it drops to zero at large radii on the approaching side.

Non-zero velocities are measured along the minor axis of NGC 4064, NGC 4189, and NGC 4941 for the stellar component too. The stars rotate slower than gas and have a larger velocity dispersion ( 80 ) in NGC 4064 and NGC 4189.

gmw_00011693.name: PKS 0537 43
gmw_00011693.overview: PKS 0537 43 is an obscured quasar host galaxy that contains a highly massive central black hole [arXiv:1003.5563]. Far-infrared observations of the galaxy reveal that it also hosts an active starburst component [arXiv:1003.5563].

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 0537 43 as an obscured quasar host detected at far-infrared wavelengths, requiring an added starburst component and implying a very high black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: At 70 and 160 m, as said before, only the brightest R-band sources were observed; among them, only the two most luminous (in the optical band) were detected, PKS 0537 43 and GD 158 19, the latter being the source described in V09.
For PKS 0537 43 and G158 19, where data points at longer wavelengths are available, an additional starburst component is needed (green dot-dashed line in Fig. figure_sed b).
The inferred black hole masses are typically in the range 10^8-10^9 M _ , with three sources (PKS 0537 43, GD 158 19, Abell 2690 29) with higher masses (M _ BH 10^ 9.5 10^ 10.0 M _ ).

gmw_00011694.name: PKS 0537 111
gmw_00011694.overview: PKS 0537 111 is an obscured quasar hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1003.5563]. Its emission is dominated by reprocessed light, which is characterized by a high optical depth torus [arXiv:1003.5563].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0537 111 as an obscured quasar whose SED favors a high optical-depth torus, reprocessed AGN emission, and a column-density discrepancy between dust and X-ray estimates.

quotes.quote: In Fig. figure_sed_agn , the relative contributions of the thermal, direct and scattered light to the total AGN light are shown for two sources characterized by a low ( _ 9.7 =0.1, PKS 0537 123) and a high ( _ 9.7 =10, PKS 0537 111) optical depth, respectively. While for _ 9.7 =10, the AGN emission is dominated by the reprocessed emission in all the UV/optical/IR bands, for _ 9.7 = 0.1 the direct and scattered components account for the optical/UV AGN emission.

This is reflected also in the 16 best-fitting solutions, where only 2 sources (Mrk 0509 1 and PKS 0537 111) are fitted with =-0.5 , one with =-1.0 (PKS 0537 43), and the remaining ones with =0 (see Table table3 ).

Two sources have a significantly different N _H (by an order of magnitude) derived from the two methods, PKS 0537 111 and Mrk 509 01. These objects are those characterized by the highest optical depths ( _ 9.7 =6,10 , which are converted into N _H 5.3 10 ^ 23 cm ^ 2 and N _H 8.7 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , respectively).

gmw_00011695.overview: 20466 is a massive and luminous Type 1 active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=8.50 [arXiv:2308.11610]. Observed just 580 million years after the Big Bang, it exhibits a near point-like morphology and broad hydrogen emission originating from a broad-line region [arXiv:2308.11610].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 20466 as a z=8.50 Type 1 AGN with broad H emission, high ionization, a massive black hole, and a very high black-hole-to-host mass ratio.

quotes.quote: Initially identified as a potential high- z AGN candidate via the broad and medium band NIRCam photometry weaver23,labbe23_nat,labbe23 , UNCOVER ID: 20466 Corresponds to the MSA ID, taken from internal UNCOVER catalog v2.2.1 is located at R.A. = 3.640408 ^ , Dec = -30.386438 ^ . The target was observed for a total of 2.7 hours with the low-resolution PRISM on July 31 2023, as a part of the Multi-shutter Array (MSA) follow-up program of the UNCOVER JWST field, Abell 2744 bezanson22 .

The spectrum of 20466 shows a clear broad line component present in the H line. With a confidence level of 13.4 we estimate the FWHM of the broad profile to be 3440 km s ^ 1 . The adjacent [ OIII ] lines are well fit with a much narrower Gaussian ( 200 km s ^ 1 ), ruling out potential outflows. This leads us to conclude that broad H emission most likely originates within a BLR of an AGN. Furthermore, we note high ionization present in our source, as indicated by the extreme RO3 ratio as well as the ``OHNO" diagnostic.

Combining the above with a near point-like source morphology observed across all NIRCam bands leads us to conclude that 20466 is a massive and luminous Type - 1 AGN, observed just 580 Myr after the Big Bang.

gmw_00011696.name: SDSSJ101405.89 + 000620.3
gmw_00011696.overview: SDSSJ101405.89 + 000620.3 is a quasar classified as a Seyfert 1.8 galaxy [arXiv:1203.6071]. Its optical spectrum exhibits a strong, double-peaked emission line and excess emission likely contributed by host galaxy light [arXiv:1203.6071].

sources.summary: The paper classifies SDSSJ101405.89 + 000620.3 as a Seyfert 1.8 quasar with double-peaked optical emission and probable host-galaxy light contamination.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ101405.89 + 000620.3 . This quasar is classified as a Seyfert 1.8 by don05 . The optical spectrum exhibits a strong and double-peaked \ line but a weak \ line. The excess emission over the power-law around these two lines is likely to be contributed by host galaxy light.

gmw_00011697.name: SDSSJ141927.49 + 044513.8
gmw_00011697.overview: SDSSJ141927.49 + 044513.8 is classified as a BL Lac object [arXiv:1203.6071]. It exhibits a featureless ultraviolet spectrum that contains a few narrow absorption lines, which are likely caused by intervening material [arXiv:1203.6071].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSSJ141927.49 + 044513.8 as a BL Lac object with a featureless UV spectrum and narrow absorption lines likely from intervening material.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ141927.49 + 044513.8 . This object has a featureless UV spectrum with a few narrow absorption lines. It is a BL Lac object in the catalogs of col05 , plo08 and mas09 . The narrow absorption lines are most likely intervening.

gmw_00011698.name: F0398
gmw_00011698.overview: F0398 is an active galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z=2.55 [arXiv:1504.03771]. It is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley type II radio source and contains a slightly resolved VLBA source [arXiv:1504.03771].

sources.summary: The paper identifies F0398 as the only VLBA-detected IFRS with spectroscopic redshift, detects a slightly resolved VLBA source, and classifies it as an FR II radio source.

quotes.quote: The only VLBA-detected IFRS with spectroscopic redshift is F0398 at z=2.55 , showing an arcsec-scale 1.4 GHz integrated flux density of 62.9 mJy. This corresponds to a K corrected 1.4 GHz rest-frame luminosity of 2.3 10^ 27 W Hz ^ 1 , using the radio spectral index = -0.72 between 20 cm and 92 cm from Collier2014 . In our VLBA observations, the source---shown in Fig. fig:F0398 --is slightly resolved with a peak flux density of 13.7 mJy beam ^ 1 and an integrated flux density of 17.2 mJy, corresponding to a luminosity of 6.3 10^ 26 W Hz ^ 1 on scales smaller than 146 pc 43 pc . Based on this luminosity, F0398 can be classified as Fanaroff-Riley (FR; FanaroffRiley1974 ) type II. The source has a compactness of 0.283.

gmw_00011699.name: F0030
gmw_00011699.overview: F0030 is an infrared-faint radio galaxy that contains two spatially separated components detected in VLBA observations [arXiv:1504.03771]. These components have a linear separation of 1.7 to 3.8 kiloparsecs and exhibit complex, resolved morphologies [arXiv:1504.03771].

sources.summary: The paper highlights F0030 as an IFRS with two separated VLBA components, complex resolved morphology, kiloparsec-scale separation, and measured component spectral indices.

quotes.quote: A particularly interesting source is F0030 which has two spatially separated components in the VLBA map shown in Fig. fig:F0030 . The first, brighter component shows a mas-scale flux density of 21.8 mJy ( S/N = 55 ) and is spatially resolved with a complex morphology which is unique in our observations. The second component shows a flux density of 6.0 mJy ( S/N = 46 ) and is separated by 442.1^ +0.3 _ 0.3 mas. The linear distance between both components is between 1.7 kpc and 3.8 kpc at any redshift in the range 0.5 z 12 .

In order to obtain a rough spectral index of the components, we separately imaged the four lower-frequency basebands and the four higher-frequency basebands centred at 1.380 GHz and 1.508 GHz, respectively. Fluxes were measured as described in Sect. calibration_imaging . We obtained spectral indices of 1.2 1.2 and 1.2 0.6 for component 1 and 2, respectively.

gmw_00011700.name: F0257
gmw_00011700.overview: F0257 is an infrared-faint radio galaxy that consists of two individual components resolved on milliarcsecond scales [arXiv:1504.03771]. These components have a projected linear separation of 0.6 to 1.3 kiloparsecs and exhibit flat to inverted radio spectral indices [arXiv:1504.03771].

sources.summary: The paper describes F0257 as an IFRS resolved into two VLBA components with a sub-kiloparsec-to-kiloparsec projected separation and measured radio spectral indices.

quotes.quote: Source F0257---shown in Fig. fig:F0257 --consists of two individual components. While the brighter component shows a flux density of 5.7 mJy ( S/N = 77 ), the weaker component is 155.5^ +0.2 _ 0.2 mas distant and shows less than half of the other component's flux density (2.1 mJy beam ^ 1 , S/N = 36 ). This angular distance corresponds to a linear distance between 0.6 kpc and 1.3 kpc. We measured mas-scale radio spectral indices between 1.380 GHz and 1.508 GHz of 0.3 0.4 and 0.2 0.9 , respectively, for component 1 and 2. Collier2014 measured an arcsec-scale radio spectral index of 0.6 between 6 cm, 20 cm, and 92 cm.

gmw_00011701.name: KK196
gmw_00011701.overview: KK196 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located within 600 kpc of Centaurus A, positioned in the middle of its southern radio lobe [arXiv:1404.2424, arXiv:2308.13121]. The galaxy has a low current star formation rate and is composed mostly of stars older than 5 Gyr [arXiv:2308.13121].

sources.summary: The paper evaluates dwarf irregular galaxy KK 196 as a possible cause of the Centaurus A lobe filaments, but rejects it because its mass, velocity, and accretion radius are insufficient.

quotes.quote: Dwarf galaxy KK 196 (AM 1318--444) We consider explanation (iv) in Section sect:origin the least likely given the low mass of the dwarf irregular galaxy KK 196, its low relative velocity to NGC 5128, a combination of high temperature and low particle density of the giant lobe plasma, and the vertex and vortex morphology; moreover, we would expect a single wake. We first test this assertion by performing a simple check for a galaxy to have a Bondi-Hoyle wake : the accretion radius R_ acc should be larger than its size. The accretion radius is given by R_ acc = 2 G M_ gal /(v_ gal ^2 + c_ s ^2) , where M_ gal and v_ gal are the mass and the velocity of the traversing galaxy, and c_ s the local speed of sound ( BON52, SAK00 ). Adopting 15 arcsec as KK 196's angular radius JER00 we infer 1.8 10^ 21 cm for its physical size. The total baryonic mass of KK 196 is 5.9 10^7 M _ (based on the results from JER00 and WAR07 ), and its relative radial velocity to NGC 5128 is approximately 189 km s ^ 1 , so we estimate a relative 3D velocity of v_ gal 189 3 327 km s ^ 1 ( 0.001c ).

Hence, the accretion radius is at least 3 orders of magnitude smaller than KK196's size and no Bondi-Hoyle wake is expected. If the internal sound speed in the lobes is relativistic (as is required in any scenario where there is stochastic acceleration of ultra-high energy cosmic rays) then the dwarf galaxy has even less effect. Another possible manifestation of the galaxy-lobe interaction is a bow shock in front of the traversing galaxy and associated stripping of the galaxy ISM: then the filaments may be associated with a ram pressure-induced wake. This however requires KK 196's velocity to be in excess of the intralobe sound speed which is not the case (see above).

sources.summary: The paper lists KK196 as a Centaurus A dwarf irregular within 600 kpc, located in the southern radio lobe, with low current star formation and mostly old stars.

quotes.quote: Unlike the Milky Way satellites, extensive observational studies on star formation histories of Centaurus A satellites are not available, with the exception of KK197, ESO-269066, ESO-381018 Marakova+2007 and five dwarf irregulars KK182 (Cen6), ESO269-58, KK196 (AM1318-444), HIPASS J1348-37, ESO384-16 Crnojevic+2012 .

Two of the dwarf irregulars (KK196 and ESO269-58) studied are within 600 kpc (see Figure 6 of ). Positioned in the middle of Centaurus A's southern radio lobe, KK196 has a star formation rate of 0.0046 0.0004 M _ yr^ 1 and has formed more than 60 _ 30 ^ +20 of stars more than 5 Gyrs ago Crnojevic+2012 .

gmw_00011702.name: NGC 3362
gmw_00011702.overview: NGC 3362 is classified as a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:2007.02616]. Although it was undetected by NuSTAR, the galaxy was detected below 4 keV by XMM-Newton, and its fitted absorption is Compton-thick [arXiv:2007.02616].

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 3362 was undetected by NuSTAR but detected below 4 keV by XMM-Newton, and its fitted absorption is Compton-thick.

quotes.quote: NGC 3362: The source was not detected by . However, it was detected by \ below 4 keV. Thus we could not use neither the Pexmon nor the MYTorus configurations. We fitted the \ spectrum assuming an absorbed power law plus a scattered component and two Apec components. We were not able to constrain the power law photon index, hence we fixed it to 1.8 and 2.2.

gmw_00011703.name: UGC 8621
gmw_00011703.overview: UGC 8621 is a Seyfert galaxy and an X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2007.02616]. Although it was not detected by NuSTAR, observations below 6 keV by XMM-Newton suggest the galaxy has a Compton-thick column density [arXiv:2007.02616].

sources.summary: The paper reports UGC 8621 was not detected by NuSTAR but was detected below 6 keV by XMM-Newton, with the best-fit model suggesting Compton-thick absorption.

quotes.quote: UGC 8621: The source was not detected by . However, it was observed and detected by \ below 6 keV. Being unable to fit the spectrum in the Fe K range, we were neither able to use the Pexmon component, nor any of the MYTorus configurations. We fitted the spectrum assuming an absorbed power law plus a scattered component and a single Apec . We were not able to get any constrains on the photon index so we fixed it to 1.8.
The scattered fraction could not be constrained either, so we fixed it to C_ sc = 0.002 . The best-fit model suggests a CT column density with N_ H = 2.54_ 1.0 ^ +0.3 10^ 24 cm^ 2 .

gmw_00011704.name: NGC 5695
gmw_00011704.overview: NGC 5695 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:2007.02616]. X-ray observations of the galaxy confirm a Compton-thick line-of-sight column density [arXiv:2007.02616].

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 5695 with XMM-Newton and NuSTAR data, modeling the soft X-rays and confirming a Compton-thick line-of-sight column density.

quotes.quote: NGC 5695: We fit the spectra obtained by \ and . The \ spectra are background-dominated above 2 keV. We modeled the spectra assuming no variability. The soft X-rays are modeled invoking a single Apec component. The results from the four models confirm a CT LOS column density in this source.

gmw_00011705.name: NGC 5283
gmw_00011705.overview: NGC 5283 is a Seyfert galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2007.02616]. X-ray observations of the source reveal Compton-thin absorption along the line of sight, as well as variability in its intrinsic flux [arXiv:2007.02616].

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 5283 spectra with absorbed, scattered, and reprocessed emission components, finding consistent Compton-thin absorption and intrinsic-flux variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 5283: We fit the \ and \ spectra of this source. The spectra are fitted assuming an absorbed power law plus scattered component and reprocessed emission. All four models were applied and suggest consistent and Cth absorption in our LOS. We note that variability in the intrinsic flux can be seen between the two observations.

gmw_00011706.name: UM 146
gmw_00011706.overview: UM 146 is classified as an active galactic nucleus and a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:2007.02616]. X-ray observations of the source suggest the presence of Compton-thin absorption along the line of sight [arXiv:2007.02616].

sources.summary: The paper reports that UM 146 was detected with XRT only above 3 keV and that all four NuSTAR-based models suggest Compton-thin absorption.

quotes.quote: UM 146: The source was detected with XRT only above 3 keV (a total of 10 counts in 2 ks). Thus, we fit the \ FPMA/FPMB spectra only, assuming an absorbed power law plus reprocessed emission. All four models suggest a Cth column density in our LOS.

gmw_00011707.name: NGC 5674
gmw_00011707.overview: NGC 5674 is classified as a Seyfert 1.9 or Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0003298]. X-ray observations of the galaxy indicate Compton-thin absorption along the line of sight [arXiv:2007.02616], which may suggest partial visibility through its obscuring torus [arXiv:astro-ph/0003298].

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 5674 with Swift/XRT and NuSTAR spectra, using absorbed, scattered, and reprocessed components, with models suggesting Compton-thin absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 5674: We fit the /XRT and \ spectra of this source. The XRT spectrum was fitted above 1 keV. The spectra are fitted assuming an absorbed power law plus scattered component and a reprocessed emission. All four models were applied and suggested consistent and Cth absorption in our LOS.

sources.summary: NGC 5674 is presented as a large-PAH-equivalent-width Seyfert 1.9/2 where the measured X-ray absorbing column may indicate partial visibility through the torus.

quotes.quote: The next three largest PAH EW Sf2s are NGC 1667 , Mrk 673 and NGC 5674 . They have 7.7 m PAH EW of the order of 4.6 m, smaller than that of the normal galaxy NGC 701, but still a factor of 10 larger than Sf1's. NGC 1667 was observed with ASCA by Turner et al. ( ) who report luminosities of 0.8 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 and 2.6 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 over the 2--10 keV and 0.5--2 keV range, respectively, well within the range of inactive galaxies. Furthermore, according to these authors, 68 of the X-ray flux in this galaxy can be accounted for by a thermal component due to the integrated stellar emission from a starburst. Mrk 673 appears in Burstein et al. ( ) with an upper limit L_ x 41.2 to its 0.5--4.5 keV luminosity. Mrk 673 also coincides with the ROSAT PSPC source WGA J1417.3+2651 with a 0.5--2 keV luminosity L_ x = 40.8 , again consistent with a mostly stellar origin. Finally, NGC 5674 may represent the first Sf2 where one starts seeing through the torus, since its X-ray absorbing column could be measured with Ginga, 0.8 10^ 23 cm^ 2 (Smith Done ).

For instance, the sub-class of small PAH EW Sf2s include sources such as Mrk 3 and NGC 4507 which are bona-fide type 2 Seyferts. Conversely, NGC 5674 and Mrk 334 are classified as type 1.9 and 1.8 Seyfert galaxies whereas their 7.7 m PAH band EW is as large as 4.6 m and 2.6 m, respectively.

gmw_00011708.name: L3
gmw_00011708.overview: L3 is a luminous Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy with an extremely high equivalent width [arXiv:1903.03131]. Its properties are consistent with a dark-galaxy scenario, and it is located within five arcseconds of three other galaxies [arXiv:1903.03131].

sources.summary: The paper reports L3 as a luminous, continuum-faint Ly-alpha emitter consistent with a dark-galaxy scenario, likely illuminated by anisotropic UV radiation from L1.

quotes.quote: In the previous section, we reported on the discovery of L3, a luminous \ emitter with an extremely high \ equivalent width ( >312 ) and a FWHM of 350 . reported on a sample of sources with > 240 \ surrounding the ultra-luminous quasar HE0109 3518 which lack detectable continuum counterparts in deep broad-band imaging. They termed these sources ``dark galaxies'' and argued they were illuminated by the quasar as no star-formation could power such high W_ Ly , i.e. \ the observed \ is fluorescent radiation powered by the incident, ionizing photons. The properties of L3 are consistent with this dark-galaxy scenario, except that the observed UV emission from L1 is orders of magnitude lower than HE0109 3518.

The dark cloud discovered here is consistent with these properties. Note that there are three galaxies within 5 5 from L3 in the deep HST images, which we indicate in panel b of Fig. 2 as G1, G2, and G3. The broadband colors of G1 and G3 are consistent with Lyman break galaxies at z=3 , though with large uncertainties given the faintness of these galaxies.

gmw_00011709.name: IC 750
gmw_00011709.overview: IC 750 is a low-mass disc-maser galaxy [arXiv:2009.01104]. Very Long Baseline Array observations of its nucleus show evidence for an intermediate-mass black hole with a dynamical mass upper limit of 140,000 solar masses [arXiv:2009.01104].

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 750 as the motivating low-mass disc-maser galaxy, noting VLBA evidence for an intermediate-mass black hole and listing its stellar mass and secure-distance properties.

quotes.quote: However, new observations of maser emission in the low-mass galaxy IC 750 ( M_*=10^ 10.1 0.2  M_ ) with the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) find evidence for an IMBH in the nucleus, and measure a dynamical mass upper limit of M_ BH 1.4 10^5 M_ from emission within a 0.2 pc diameter disc Zaw20 . This mass falls 1--2 dex below that predicted by existing scaling relations, but it is unclear as of yet whether this is due to larger scatter at the low-mass end of the scaling relations, or because masers in low-mass galaxies trace BH masses systematically below the lines fit to massive galaxies, as seen in some simulations e.g., AnglesAlcazar17, Bower17, Dekel19 . A systematic search for additional disc masers in low-mass galaxies is necessary to distinguish between these scenarios.

In Table table:lowmass-masers and Figure fig:lowmass-masers , we have also included IC 750. It is currently the only disc maser in a low-mass galaxy that has been confirmed with VLBI observations Zaw20 , and motivated our search for maser emission in active dwarf galaxies. This galaxy was not in the samples used in Section subsec:lumandmass because it does not have a reliable distance in the NSA.

gmw_00011710.name: SDSS J114359.58+244251.7
gmw_00011710.overview: SDSS J114359.58+244251.7 is a galaxy that initially appeared to host a broad, blueshifted megamaser feature [arXiv:2009.01104]. However, separate polarization checks and follow-up observations confirmed that this feature was a false positive, establishing the galaxy as a non-detection for maser emission [arXiv:2009.01104].

sources.summary: The paper reports that SDSS J114359.58+244251.7 initially showed a possible maser feature, but polarization checks and follow-up observations established it as a non-detection.

quotes.quote: We devote particular attention to an initial false positive in the spectrum of SDSS J114359.58+244251.7 (RGG 18), in scans 58--61 of session AGBT19B 281 01. This spectrum initially appeared to have a broad ( 25 km s ^ 1 ) feature, blueshifted 300 km s ^ 1 from the systemic velocity of the host galaxy in its observed spectrum. However, upon inspection of each polarization separately, we found this feature was only detected in the left-handed polarization, with a maximum flux density of 18.2 mJy (after Hanning smoothing), while the right-handed polarization had a maximum flux density of 10.5 mJy, but no discernible emission features, corresponding to a difference of 3--4 . We show the spectrum for this galaxy resulting from the data reduction procedure in Section sec:data in the top panel of Figure fig:spectra . The remaining panels of Figure fig:spectra are also for SDSS J114359.58+244251.7, but separately show the spectra for each individual nod, and again for each individual polarization.

The source had a maximum of 10.7 Jy in its left-handed spectrum and 12.0 Jy in its right-handed spectrum, indicating that there is no real maser emission from SDSS J114359.58+244251.7. A follow-up 1-hour integration on the same source (project code AGBT20A 419) found no emission, so we assuredly classify it as a non-detection.

gmw_00011711.name: LBQS1009
gmw_00011711.overview: LBQS1009 is a robust lens galaxy located in a field or small group region [arXiv:1205.4880]. Gravitational lensing analyses of the system indicate that its lensing mass exceeds its stellar mass, requiring a cold dark matter component to reconcile the difference [arXiv:1205.4880].

sources.summary: The paper treats LBQS1009 as a robust field/group lens galaxy whose lensing mass exceeds stellar mass enough to require an added cold dark component in MOND/TeVeS.

quotes.quote: However, the other four robust candidates (LBQS1009, HE1104, B1600, HE2149) are located in field/group regions, so that a cold component (CDM) would be required even within the MOND/TeVeS framework. However, the other four galaxies (LBQS1009, HE1104, B1600, HE2149) are located in average density regions (either in the field or in small groups). Hence, for these systems the excess of lensing mass can only be reconciled if an additional, cold, component is added, i.e. CDM.

It was found that, for a range of parameterisations of the MOND and TeVeS theories significant quantities of dark matter are present for at least five galaxies: LBQS1009, HE1104, B1600, HE2149 and Q0957, irrespective of the stellar initial mass function adopted. Although it is noted that Q0957 lives in a high density region -- so that the lensing analysis may be probing scales larger than a typical galaxy halo -- still an additional set of four robust candidates are found (LBQS1009, HE1104, B1600 and HE2149) that pose a serious challenge to these theories.

gmw_00011712.name: J1442+5558
gmw_00011712.overview: J1442+5558 is classified as a mid-infrared outburst in a normal galaxy (MIRONG) and is a candidate turn-on active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2111.12729]. The galaxy exhibits sustained broad-line emission, emerging coronal lines, and N III emission [arXiv:2111.12729].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J1442+5558 as a MIRONG source with sustained broad-line emission, emerging coronal lines, N III emission, and a possible turn-on AGN interpretation.

quotes.quote: Except for J1442+5558 and J1003+0202, the strength of \ declines again in the subsequent observations although most of them have not returned back to the flux level of SDSS as of the latest epoch. Despite of their weakness, coronal lines clearly fade out after the first follow-up observation except for J1442+5558, whose coronal lines have just emerged in recent DBSP spectra while \ has been detected in the first DBSP. Particularly, 7 of the 10 CL sources present He ii 4686 , with two of them (J1442+5558 and J1513+3111) even with N iii 4640 emission.

Only J1442+5558 enters a long plateau phase in the light curve of , which has lasted for at least four years, and thus is a good candidate of a bona fide turn-on AGN. However, the integrated radiative energy to now for J1442+5558 is 1.1 10^ 52 erg, well within the range expected for TDEs.

gmw_00011713.overview: The post-starburst galaxy 8083-12703 exhibits a tidally disturbed optical structure [arXiv:2210.12199]. It features a star-forming center and a complex kinematic structure with a radius-dependent CO emission position angle, which may indicate a minor merger or interaction [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 8083-12703 is described as a post-starburst with tidally disturbed optical structure, changing CO kinematic position angle, star-forming center, and off-center shock-like ionization.

quotes.quote: At least four of the PSBs with extended gas appear to be tidally disturbed in the optical SDSS imaging: 8083-12703, 8655-3701, 9194-3702, and 9088-9102. This may be the result of imposing a global PA on a velocity field which has a changing PA with radius, as can be seen in the CO emission of 8083-12703. Three galaxies are dominated by star-forming spaxels in both diagrams: 8083-12703, 8085-6104, and 8086-3704.

Finally, the P-V diagram of 8083-12703 shows a complex kinematic structure with a radius-dependent kinematic position angle, which may be indicative of some disturbance such as a minor merger or interaction. hbt Radio and millimeter continuum detections can be associated with an AGN or a starburst e.g. best_radio_2005 . 8083-12703 is an rPSB with emission and no evidence of a central AGN but does have a star-forming center, indicating that the central, compact emission could be from a central starburst.

gmw_00011714.overview: The galaxy 8655-3701 is a ring post-starburst galaxy that exhibits a tidally disturbed morphology [arXiv:2210.12199]. It features a strong bar with post-starburst regions at its ends, a strongly star-forming nucleus, and widespread active galactic nucleus-like ionization [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 8655-3701 is presented as a ring post-starburst with tidally disturbed morphology, widespread AGN-like ionization, and a strong bar whose ends coincide with post-starburst regions.

quotes.quote: As mentioned in Section sec:sample , we include one ring PSB from the ALMaQUEST survey in our sample, 8655-3701. At least four of the PSBs with extended gas appear to be tidally disturbed in the optical SDSS imaging: 8083-12703, 8655-3701, 9194-3702, and 9088-9102. The spatial extent of the AGN ionization varies; 8655-3701 consists of almost entirely WHAN strong and weak AGN spaxels, whereas the other galaxies have a central core of WHAN strong AGN spaxels surrounded by a ring of weak AGN spaxels and then retired spaxels.
Only one of our galaxies has a strong bar, 8655-3701. While the nucleus of 8655-3701 is strongly star-forming, the ends of the bar are co-incident with post-starburst regions.

gmw_00011715.overview: The galaxy 9088-9102 is classified as an ongoing merger [arXiv:2210.12199]. It features extended central continuum emission likely driven by star formation, alongside shock-like off-center regions and separated carbon monoxide detections [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 9088-9102 is identified as an ongoing merger with separated CO detections, central AGN/composite ionization, shock-like off-center spaxels, and extended central continuum emission.

quotes.quote: 9088-9102 has many high significance CO line detections separated from the galaxy itself, likely due to a plethora of gas in a dense environment. Four of our PSBs are visually classified as either post-mergers (8083-12703, 8655-3701, and 9194-3702) by thorp_spatially_2019 , or on-going mergers (9088-9102). Mergers can also lead to shocks, which may be the case in 9088-9102 which is an on-going merger.

For each galaxy, this emission is unresolved and in the center of the galaxy except for 9088-9102, which has extended continuum emission in the center, and 9494-3701, which has continuum emission offset from the center. The extended nature of the emission in 9088-9102 also points towards a significant contribution from star-formation, as we would expect continuum emission heated by a central AGN to be highly compact.

gmw_00011716.overview: The post-starburst galaxy 9494-3701 features extended gas oriented perpendicular to its stellar disc [arXiv:2210.12199]. This galaxy exhibits a low molecular gas fraction, evidence of a molecular outflow ejecting gas from its center, and ionization dominated by hot low-mass evolved stars [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 9494-3701 has perpendicular extended gas, low molecular gas fraction, evidence for molecular outflow, HOLMES-like ionization, and possible past AGN activity.

quotes.quote: 9494-3701 has extended gas perpendicular to the disc of the galaxy, potentially indicative of gas expulsion or an outflow. One cPSB, 9494-3701, has a particularly low f_ H_2 , potentially indicating that a lack of molecular gas supply is contributing to quenching in this galaxy. We find evidence of an outflow ejecting molecular gas from the center in one of our PSBs, 9494-3701.

The WHAN diagram in Figure fig:overview_bpt shows that 9494-3701 is dominated by retired spaxels and is thus ionized primarily by HOLMES. However, given that the duty cycle of AGN is much shorter than the length of a post-starburst phase, it is possible that 9494-3701 previously hosted an AGN that has since shut off.

gmw_00011717.overview: 8080-3704 is a central post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:2210.12199]. Its central region is dominated by active galactic nucleus or composite ionization, and it exhibits asymmetric gas kinematics that may indicate a low-mass outflow [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 8080-3704 is classified as a central post-starburst by PCA maps, with asymmetric H-alpha kinematics, central AGN/composite ionization, and possible low-mass outflow.

quotes.quote: We compare our spaxel classification maps to the ring and central PSB classifications made by chen_post-starburst_2019 , and find consistent determinations by visual inspection with the exceptions of 8080-3704 and 9194-3702, where the PCA classification shows a central post-starburst morphology, but they are classified as rPSBs in chen_post-starburst_2019 . In the second category, all three galaxies have constant stellar Radon profiles but either have asymmetric H Radon profiles as in 8080-3704 and 8941-3701, or are kinematically misaligned as in 9494-3701. Finally, the remaining five galaxies have central regions dominated by BPT AGN or composite spaxels and WHAN strong AGN spaxels: 8655-3701, 8982-6104, 8080-3704, 9088-9102, and 9194-3702.
Other galaxies in our sample with misaligned or disturbed gas kinematics, such as 8080-3704, 8655-1902, and 8941-3701 may also have outflows with masses too small to be detected with our CO data.

gmw_00011718.overview: The galaxy 8941-3701 is a central post-starburst galaxy that has nearly quenched its star formation [arXiv:2210.12199]. Despite this quenching, it retains a sizable molecular gas reservoir and exhibits asymmetric H-alpha kinematics [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 8941-3701 is described as a central post-starburst with molecular gas, nearly quenched star formation, retired-spaxel ionization, asymmetric H-alpha kinematics, and possible outflow.

quotes.quote: In the second category, all three galaxies have constant stellar Radon profiles but either have asymmetric H Radon profiles as in 8080-3704 and 8941-3701, or are kinematically misaligned as in 9494-3701. In particular, 8655-1902 and 8941-3701 are two central PSBs with sizable molecular gas reservoirs that have nearly quenched their star-formation entirely. Five galaxies are dominated by retired spaxels in the WHAN diagram, corresponding to AGN/composite BPT spaxels: 7964-1902, 8081-3702, 8655-1902, 8941-3701, and 9494-3701.

Other galaxies in our sample with misaligned or disturbed gas kinematics, such as 8080-3704, 8655-1902, and 8941-3701 may also have outflows with masses too small to be detected with our CO data. In our sample, one of our cPSBs, 8941-3701, has a central post-starburst region and the outskirts of the galaxy are quiescent but not post-starburst, which is consistent with outside-in quenching.

gmw_00011719.overview: The post-starburst galaxy 8085-6104 features two distinct lobes of carbon monoxide gas [arXiv:2210.12199]. It displays aligned stellar and gas rotation, star-forming central spaxels, potential shock-like ionization, and signs of a weak stellar bar [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 8085-6104 is described as a post-starburst with two CO lobes, aligned stellar/gas rotation, star-forming central spaxels, off-center shock-like ionization, and weak-bar signs.

quotes.quote: We do not fit two galaxies: 8081-3702 lacks a robust CO detection, and 8085-6104 has two lobes of gas that cannot be adequately represented with a single Gaussian. Finally, the galaxies in the last category (8085-6104, 8086-3704, and 8982-6104) have symmetric stellar and H Radon profiles, as well as consistent CO, stellar, and H PAs. Three galaxies are dominated by star-forming spaxels in both diagrams: 8083-12703, 8085-6104, and 8086-3704.

The three galaxies without signs of kinematic disturbance (i.e. lacking asymmetrical stellar or H Radon profiles and having kinematically aligned CO, stars, and H ), 8085-6104, 8086-3704, and 8982-6104, all have inner bends in their stellar Radon profiles. stark_radon_2018 found that stellar bars are the primary driver of inner bends in stellar Radon profiles, so a weak bar contributing to star-formation suppression in these galaxies is possible. Finally, star-formation can drive outflows and shocks, as may be in the case of 8083-12703 and 8085-6104.

gmw_00011720.overview: The galaxy 8086-3704 is an older central post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:2210.12199]. It features aligned gas and stellar rotation [arXiv:2210.12199], star-forming central spaxels [arXiv:2210.12199], and weak-bar signatures that may contribute to star-formation suppression [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 8086-3704 is described as an older central post-starburst with aligned gas and stellar rotation, star-forming central spaxels, possible settled merger history, and weak-bar signatures.

quotes.quote: Finally, the galaxies in the last category (8085-6104, 8086-3704, and 8982-6104) have symmetric stellar and H Radon profiles, as well as consistent CO, stellar, and H PAs. Three galaxies are dominated by star-forming spaxels in both diagrams: 8083-12703, 8085-6104, and 8086-3704. While none of these galaxies currently show obvious signs of a merger, 8086-3704 is our oldest PSB with a burst time of 3.0^ +0.4 _ 0.5 Gyr, so it is possible that the gas and stars have settled since a previous merger.

The three galaxies without signs of kinematic disturbance (i.e. lacking asymmetrical stellar or H Radon profiles and having kinematically aligned CO, stars, and H ), 8085-6104, 8086-3704, and 8982-6104, all have inner bends in their stellar Radon profiles. stark_radon_2018 found that stellar bars are the primary driver of inner bends in stellar Radon profiles, so a weak bar contributing to star-formation suppression in these galaxies is possible. The two rPSBs without central AGN spaxels instead appear to be ionized by shocks in the outskirts of the galaxy with star-formation dominating the central ionization, similar to 8086-3704, a cPSB.

gmw_00011721.overview: The galaxy 8982-6104 is a ring post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:2210.12199] that displays kinematically aligned gas and stellar rotation [arXiv:2210.12199]. Its central region is dominated by active galactic nucleus or composite ionization [arXiv:2210.12199], and it contains an inner bend in its stellar Radon profile that may indicate a weak bar [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 8982-6104 is a ring post-starburst with possible AGN-contaminated central spectra, aligned gas and stellar rotation, central AGN/composite ionization, and off-center shock-like spaxels.

quotes.quote: Additionally, 8982-6104 is a ring PSB but the PCA classification was unable to accurately decompose the spectra of a number of central spaxels, potentially due to AGN contamination, or a strong starburst. Finally, the galaxies in the last category (8085-6104, 8086-3704, and 8982-6104) have symmetric stellar and H Radon profiles, as well as consistent CO, stellar, and H PAs. Finally, the remaining five galaxies have central regions dominated by BPT AGN or composite spaxels and WHAN strong AGN spaxels: 8655-3701, 8982-6104, 8080-3704, 9088-9102, and 9194-3702.

Three of these central AGN galaxies also have isolated islands of off-center WHAN strong AGN spaxels: 8982-6104, 9088-9102, and 9194-3702, which again may be ionized from shocks. The three galaxies without signs of kinematic disturbance (i.e. lacking asymmetrical stellar or H Radon profiles and having kinematically aligned CO, stars, and H ), 8085-6104, 8086-3704, and 8982-6104, all have inner bends in their stellar Radon profiles. stark_radon_2018 found that stellar bars are the primary driver of inner bends in stellar Radon profiles, so a weak bar contributing to star-formation suppression in these galaxies is possible.

gmw_00011722.overview: 8655-1902 is a central post-starburst galaxy that has nearly quenched its star formation [arXiv:2210.12199]. Despite this, it retains a sizable molecular gas reservoir and exhibits central ionization that may be driven by either a weak active galactic nucleus or retired stellar populations [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 8655-1902 is described as a central post-starburst with molecular gas, nearly quenched star formation, retired or weak-AGN ionization, and possible undetected outflow.

quotes.quote: In particular, 8655-1902 and 8941-3701 are two central PSBs with sizable molecular gas reservoirs that have nearly quenched their star-formation entirely. Five galaxies are dominated by retired spaxels in the WHAN diagram, corresponding to AGN/composite BPT spaxels: 7964-1902, 8081-3702, 8655-1902, 8941-3701, and 9494-3701. While 8655-1902 does have some central weak AGN spaxels, the diagram shows these spaxels are close to the border with the retired classification, making it difficult to differentiate between these two ionization sources.

Overall, we find that ionization in 4-5/9 our cPSBs is driven by HOLMES, 3-4/9 by AGN, and star-formation in 1/9, where 8655-1902 is an ambiguous case where HOLMES or a weak AGN could be contributing to central ionization. Other galaxies in our sample with misaligned or disturbed gas kinematics, such as 8080-3704, 8655-1902, and 8941-3701 may also have outflows with masses too small to be detected with our CO data.

gmw_00011723.overview: The galaxy 8939-3703 is identified as a star-forming interloper [arXiv:2210.12199]. Although it was selected via principal component analysis and exhibits deep Balmer absorption, spectral fitting reveals it lacks the recent starburst and quenching history of true post-starburst galaxies [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 8939-3703 is identified as a star-forming interloper in the ALMA sample rather than a true post-starburst, despite deep Balmer absorption and PCA selection.

quotes.quote: Our spectral fitting results (discussed in Section sec:fitting ) reveal that one galaxy we observe with ALMA, 8939-3703, is a star-forming interloper, despite deep Balmer absorption. Two galaxies in our final sample are not in the chen_post-starburst_2019 sample and are selected from the PCA method, 8086-3704, 9088-9102, and the star-forming interloper 8939-3703. All galaxies but one aforementioned interloper (8939-3703) exhibit clear histories of recent starburst and subsequent quenching from from their fitted SFHs, as displayed in Appendix app:SFH .

Different post-starburst selection methods result in samples with different properties including post-burst ages and AGN or shock presence, and no single selection will include all galaxies considered to be post-starburst. hbt As mentioned in Section sec:sample , our spectral fitting results of 8939-3703 indicate that this galaxy is a star-forming interloper. Figure fig:8939 shows the SDSS 3-color image, the PCA spaxel classifications, and the excess Balmer absorption of 8939-3703.

gmw_00011724.name: Yagi275
gmw_00011724.overview: Yagi275 is a strongly flattened ultra-diffuse galaxy located in a cluster environment [arXiv:1901.05489, arXiv:2309.15148]. It features a stellar population aged 6 to 8 billion years [arXiv:1901.05489], relatively high metallicity for its stellar mass [arXiv:2309.15148], and a dark matter fraction estimated between 50 and 80 percent [arXiv:1901.05489].

sources.summary: The paper describes this strongly flattened galaxy as lacking a radial-velocity gradient, having highly anisotropic orbits, relatively metal-rich intermediate-age stars, and 50-80 percent dark matter.

quotes.quote: J125929.89+274303.0 is a strongly flattened galaxy ( b/a=0.50 ) observed about 30 deg off the minor axis. We see no radial velocity gradient and dynamical modelling suggests highly anisotropic orbits. Its stars are more metal rich ( 0.6 dex) than most of the other galaxies in our sample, and the age of the stellar population is around 6--8 Gyr. This galaxy is included in the sample of FerreMateu+18 as Yagi 275 ; they report a somewhat higher metallicity value ( 0.37 dex).

For the two objects with high orbital anisotropy, J125929.89+274303.0 and J130026.26+272735.2 , the dark matter fraction estimate is 50 \ to 80 very similar to what was found in the three dE satellites of the Andromeda galaxy 2006MNRAS.369.1321D .

sources.summary: Yagi275 is a cluster UDG with low magnesium enhancement, late-infall/long-quenching behavior, relatively high metallicity for its stellar mass, and possible prior stripping.

quotes.quote: From these late-and-slow UDGs, the two located in clusters are the ones with the lowest enhancements ( 0.2 dex; PUDG-R24 and Yagi275). This is the case of PUDG-R24, Yagi275 from FM+18 FM+18 and Yagi90 from Ruiz-Lara2018 (see also Section section:phasespace ). 3 UDGs (PUDG-R24, Yagi275 and Yagi090) are located in this region, in agreement with their long quenching timescales and relatively young ages.

For example, Yagi275 and Yagi276 are two of the UDGs with the highest metallicities for their stellar mass. However, it would only work for Yagi275 if this UDG was already a stripped galaxy before infall, as it is considered to be a late infall from both its phase-space location and SFH.

gmw_00011725.name: Yagi276
gmw_00011725.overview: Yagi276 is a ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:2309.15148]. It is classified as a very-early-infall cluster member that exhibits a long quenching timescale and a high metallicity for its stellar mass [arXiv:2309.15148].

sources.summary: Yagi276 is a very-early-infall Coma UDG whose long quenching timescale resembles intermediate infallers and whose metallicity is high for its stellar mass.

quotes.quote: 9 UDGs (PUDG-R27, VCC 1287, Yagi358, Yagi093, Yagi098, Yagi276, Yagi392, Yagi418 and OGS1) are located in the `very early infall' region, with other 2 (DF07 and PUDG-R15) in the `early infall' region. They have quenching timescales compatible with their location with the exception of Yagi093, Yagi098, Yagi276 and Yagi418, which present very long quenching timescales that are more similar to those expected for intermediate infallers (timescales of 7.25 to 10 Gyr). For example, Yagi275 and Yagi276 are two of the UDGs with the highest metallicities for their stellar mass.
This scenario would be plausible for Yagi276, as it has long resided in the cluster (owing to its infall diagram), which is one of the requirements of this scenario.

gmw_00011726.name: PUDG-R27
gmw_00011726.overview: PUDG-R27 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Perseus cluster [arXiv:2309.15148]. It is situated in a very-early-infall region of the cluster and exhibits a stellar population distribution that matches that of globular clusters [arXiv:2309.15148].

sources.summary: PUDG-R27 is a Perseus UDG observed with KCWI, included by surface-brightness criteria, located in a very-early-infall region, and sharing GC-like abundance-space placement.

quotes.quote: These are the Coma cluster UDGs DFX1 and DF07, along with the Perseus cluster UDG PUDG-R27. PUDG-R27 ( R.A. = 49.93116, Dec. = 41.71326 in J2000) was observed on the nights of October 5th 2021 (Program W024; PI: Forbes), and January 5th and 29th 2022 (Program N195, PI: Romanowsky) using KCWI with the large slicer, BL grating and a 4550 central wavelength. Note that during the night 2022 January 29th, KCWI was slightly out of focus, which degraded the instrumental resolution by 15 , and affected 20 of our data for PUDG-R27.

All the UDGs from this work fulfill these criteria, with PUDG-R27 included because it lies within the surface brightness line with the highest M/L (which is in fact its own value; see Section section:agemet ). 9 UDGs (PUDG-R27, VCC 1287, Yagi358, Yagi093, Yagi098, Yagi276, Yagi392, Yagi418 and OGS1) are located in the `very early infall' region, with other 2 (DF07 and PUDG-R15) in the `early infall' region. Three UDGs (PUDG-R15, PUDG-R27 and DF07) match the GCs distribution, although in a region that also matches the dEs.

gmw_00011727.name: J1229 0307
gmw_00011727.overview: J1229 0307 is an active galactic nucleus with characteristics similar to narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxies [arXiv:1205.3224]. It is classified as an outlier quasar with narrow, asymmetric, and discrepant emission-line profiles [arXiv:1205.3224].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1229 0307 as an NLS1-like quasar outlier whose narrow, asymmetric and discrepant emission-line profiles make some black-hole mass estimates unreliable.

quotes.quote: The one apparent outlier with relatively narrow lines is SDSS J122925.95 030702.4, which was intentionally included for comparison because it has characteristics similar to narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxies.

Four of the quasars have \ line widths that are roughly consistent with those of \ (mean and standard deviation FWHM( )/FWHM( ) = 1.07 0.11 ), whereas the other three (J1229 0307, J1238 0056, and J1242 + 0249) are significantly broader, FWHM( )/FWHM( ) = 1.86 0.38 .

The most discrepant object is the NLS1 J1229 0307, confirming Vestergaard et al.'s (2011) suspicion that \ masses are especially unreliable for this class of AGNs, but the other two show no obvious warning signs as to why \ should misbehave.

gmw_00011728.name: NGC 3252
gmw_00011728.overview: NGC 3252 is a nearby star-forming spiral galaxy located approximately 20 Mpc away [arXiv:2108.12122]. It is identified as the host of the fast radio burst FRB 20181030A [arXiv:2108.12122, arXiv:2109.02663] and is notably deficient in atomic gas [arXiv:2109.02663].

sources.summary: The paper argues that NGC 3252 is the likely host of FRB 20181030A, describing it as a nearby star-forming spiral with measured metallicity, extinction, and star formation.

quotes.quote: Within the FRB localization region, we identify NGC 3252 as the most promising host, with an estimated chance coincidence probability < 2.5 10^ 3 . Moreover, we do not find any other galaxy with M _ < -15 AB mag within the localization region to the maximum estimated FRB redshift of 0.05. This rules out a dwarf host 5 times less luminous than any FRB host discovered to date. NGC 3252 is a star-forming spiral galaxy, and at a distance of 20 Mpc, it is one of the closest FRB hosts discovered thus far.

Physical properties of NGC 3252 Here we summarise major physical properties of NGC 3252. We obtained long-slit spectroscopy data from GTC, and its analysis is described in Appendix sec:longslit . From the integrated optical spectrum of the galaxy, we estimate the oxygen abundance 12+ (O/H) = 8.44 0.06 (or nebular metallicity (Z_ gas /Z_ )=-0.25 0.07 ), which is 60 of the solar value Asplund2009 . We also derive dust extinction at the V-band, A _ v = 1.3 0.2 (E(B V) =0.42 0.06 using R _ v =3.1 ), using H /H ratio (i.e. Balmer decrement), assuming the standard Milky Way extinction curve Cardelli1989 . Finally, using SFR(H ) = 7.9 10^ 42 M _ yr ^ 1 L(H /erg s ^ 1 ) kennicutt1994ApJ , we get SFR(H )=0.033 M _ yr ^ 1 using the extinction corrected total H luminosity of L(H ) = 4.12 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3252 as the FRB 181030A host, derives its atomic gas mass from 21 cm flux, and finds it deficient in atomic gas.

quotes.quote: These are NGC 3252 (UGC 5732; PGC 31278 2MASX J10342310+7345539), the host of FRB 181030A bhardwaj21b , M81 (NGC 3031), the host of FRB 200120E bhardwaj21,kirsten21,nimmo21,majid21 , and the Milky Way , the host of FRB 200428 bochenek20 . The hosts of GRB 980425 GRB 980425 , GRB 060505 060505 , and GRB 111005A 111005A have been detected with michalowski15hi,michalowski18grb,arabsalmani15b,arabsalmani19,lesniewska21 . masters14 reported the line flux of 5.89 0.14 Jy for . Using eq. 2 in devereux90 , this corresponds to the atomic gas mass =(3.90 0.09) 10^8 . I used the star formation rate (SFR) and stellar mass ( ) of this galaxy from bhardwaj21b .
It has an atomic gas mass lower by a factor of 5 than the expectation given its SFR. The scatter of this relation is a factor of 2.4, so can be considered deficient in atomic gas.

gmw_00011729.name: SDSS J0252+0039
gmw_00011729.overview: SDSS J0252+0039 is a galaxy-galaxy strong lens system [arXiv:2109.04704]. It was originally discovered in the SLACS survey and has been used to constrain the sub-galactic matter power spectrum [arXiv:2109.04704].

sources.summary: The paper compares fuzzy-dark-matter halo predictions with sub-galactic matter power constraints from the galaxy-galaxy strong lens SDSS J0252+0039 and discusses future ALMA sensitivity.

quotes.quote: 2018arXiv180305952B applied these methods to the real strong lens system SDSS J0252+0039 that is a galaxy-galaxy strong lens system found in SLACS survey 2009ApJ...705.1099A . They obtained constraints on the sub-galactic matter power spectrum and discussed the implication for the CDM model. We expect that this method can also be applied to the FDM model.

Using our formalism described in Sec.  sec:power-spectrum , we compare the sub-galactic matter power spectrum with a real observational data to constrain the range of FDM mass. We first use the current constraint on the sub-galactic matter power spectrum 2018arXiv180305952B that is obtained from the SLACS strong lens system SDSS J0252+0039 2009ApJ...705.1099A .

gmw_00011730.name: HELMS J004714.2+032454
gmw_00011730.overview: HELMS J004714.2+032454 is a strongly lensed source that exhibits a long, faint structure extending to the southeast [arXiv:1705.05413]. The galaxy contains a warm dust component and has an estimated star formation rate of approximately 220 solar masses per year [arXiv:1705.05413].

sources.summary: The paper describes HELMS J004714.2+032454 as a well-fit double-image lens whose source has a long faint extension, warm dust evidence, and an SFR of about 220 solar masses per year.

quotes.quote: HELMS J004714.2+032454 This is a double image system which is very well fit with a single power-law density profile and no external shear. The source exhibits a long faint structure extending to the south-east and this is readily seen in the lensed image. The SPIRE and ALMA photometry alone continues to rise towards shorter wavelengths, the peak of the SED being constrained purely by the PACS photometry.

The relatively high 100 m PACS flux is suggestive of a warmer dust component and this is reflected in a significantly better fit by the dual temperature SED compared to the single temperature template, although both SEDs give a comparable dust mass. De-magnifying the far-IR luminosity given in N16 using our magnification factor of 8.3 gives (L_ FIR /L_ )=12.1 0.1 , slightly less than our determination but consistent within the uncertainties. The luminosity implies a star formation rate of 220 60 M_ /yr. Given its dust mass range of 10^ 8.7 10^ 9.2 M_ , this places the source somewhere between having the characteristics of a high redshift SMG or lower redshift ULIRG and the bulk population of z<0.5 galaxies detected in H-ATLAS, according to rowlands14 .

gmw_00011731.name: HELMS J004723.6+015751
gmw_00011731.overview: HELMS J004723.6+015751 is a strongly lensed starburst galaxy at a redshift of z=1.44 [arXiv:1705.05413]. The source has a compact morphology with a faint northwest extension, and it features a dominant mass of cold dust [arXiv:1705.05413].

sources.summary: The paper models HELMS J004723.6+015751 as a sheared lens with a compact source, faint northwest extension, cold-dust-dominated SED, and elevated SFR-to-dust ratio.

quotes.quote: HELMS J004723.6+015751 This system is one of two in our sample which require external shear in the lens model, consistent with the location of a smaller external galaxy 10 arcsec to the south. The source shows a compact, relatively featureless morphology with the hint of an extended structure to the north west. The SPIRE and ALMA photometry of the source on their own indicate that the peak of the SED lies in the vicinity of the shortest wavelength data point at 250 m.

This is borne out by the inclusion of PACS photometry. As a result, the fitted dual temperature SED implies a dominant mass of cold dust at 26 K. The intrinsic source luminosity of (L_ FIR /L_ )=12.2 is in agreement with that measured by N16. The SFR of 230 60 M_ /yr for this z=1.44 source compared with its relatively low dust mass places it in the upper envelope of SFR to dust mass ratios spanned by SMGs and ULIRGs according to rowlands14 .

gmw_00011732.name: HELMS J001615.7+032435
gmw_00011732.overview: HELMS J001615.7+032435 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy that acts as a cusp-caustic gravitational lens system [arXiv:1705.05413]. The lensed source exhibits an extremely high star formation rate to dust mass ratio and has a far-infrared luminosity of 10^12.5 solar luminosities [arXiv:1705.05413].

sources.summary: The paper reports HELMS J001615.7+032435 as a cusp-caustic lens with low signal-to-noise, significant external shear, nearby lensing galaxies, and an extremely high SFR-to-dust-mass ratio.

quotes.quote: HELMS J001615.7+032435 The relatively low image signal-to-noise ratio in this cusp-caustic configuration lens results in an undetected counter-image which increases the modelling uncertainty for this system. Nevertheless, the most probable lens model is one with a significant external shear. This is consistent with several smaller nearby galaxies, mainly to the north-east, with colours similar to the lens which is, in turn, consistent with the larger Einstein radius of a group-scale lens. In light of this, we attempted a lens model that includes external convergence provided by a singular isothermal ellipsoid (SIE) mass model.

The ALMA and SPIRE photometry of the source in this lens system prefers an optically thick single temperature SED. However, with the inclusion of PACS fluxes, a marginally improved fit is obtained with a second weak but quite hot dust component, although the improvement in the fit is not significant given the additional SED parameters. The source has a luminosity of (L_ FIR /L_ )=12.5 which agrees with that of N16 who used an optically thin single component SED. The SFR to dust mass ratio of this source is extremely high, placing it nearly 3 above the mean in the distribution of ratios measured in the SMG/ULIRG population.

gmw_00011733.name: ESO 138--IG29
gmw_00011733.overview: ESO 138--IG29, also known as the Sacred Mushroom, is a young ring galaxy that was originally an S0 lenticular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0006084]. This ring system was formed less than 100 million years ago when the galaxy ESO 138--IG30 passed through its disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0006084].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 138--IG29, the Sacred Mushroom, as a young ring system formed by ESO138-IG30 passing through its disk, with models indicating it began as an S0 galaxy.

quotes.quote: ESO 138--IG29 Also known as the ``Sacred Mushroom'', this galaxy appears to be a young ring system formed by ESO138--IG30 as it passed through the disk of ESO138--IG29 less than 10 ^8 yrs ago (Wallin Struck--Marcell 1994). Optical imaging and dynamical models of this system have been carried out Wallin Struck--Marcell (1994) which suggest that ESO138--IG29 was originally an S0 galaxy. The radio contours at 4.79 GHz overlayed on the optical image of ESO 138--IG29 are shown in Figure fig_eso138 .

gmw_00011734.name: ESO 341--IG04
gmw_00011734.overview: ESO 341--IG04 is a galaxy in the very late stages of a merger that possesses a single nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0006084]. Although its optical appearance closely resembles an elliptical galaxy, it retains a large amount of neutral hydrogen gas, a tail or plume, and a southwest loop [arXiv:astro-ph/0006084].

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO 341--IG04 as a very late-stage merger with elliptical-like optical structure, substantial H I, one nucleus, a tail or plume, and a southwest loop.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The optical nucleus is at RA =20 ^h 41 ^m 14.3 ^s , Dec. = --38 ^ 11 ^ 40 ^ (J2000).

quotes.quote: ESO 341--IG04 This galaxy is probably at the very last stages of a merger. Its optical appearance is close to that of an elliptical galaxy (its surface brightness profile follows an ^ 1/4 law out to 5 effective radii), although it still contains a large amount of HI gas (Bergvall et al. 1989). It has one nucleus and one tail or plume, in addition to the prominent south--west loop. It appears to be more evolved than NGC 7252 but not quite at the end of the Keel Wu (1995) merger sequence. The optical nucleus is at RA =20 ^h 41 ^m 14.3 ^s , Dec. = --38 ^ 11 ^ 40 ^ (J2000).

gmw_00011735.name: RX J1420-A
gmw_00011735.overview: RX J1420-A is one of two candidate host galaxies identified within the error circle of the X-ray source RX J1420 [arXiv:1707.02986]. Its stellar surface mass density and velocity dispersion are consistent with other tidal disruption event hosts, making it the likely host galaxy [arXiv:1707.02986].

sources.summary: The paper treats RX J1420-A as one of two possible host galaxies for RX J1420 and argues its measured properties match the TDE-host sample, making it the likely actual host.

quotes.quote: RX J1420.4+5334, referred to here as RX J1420, was reported by 2000A&A...362L..25G as an X-ray source that varied by a factor of 150 in flux between template ROSAT observation and the ROSAT All-Sky Survey in 1990. Following A17, we treat this object as a likely TDE candidate. 2000A&A...362L..25G identified two possible host galaxies in optical images within the 10^ radius error circle of ROSAT's High Resolution Imager. Following 2000A&A...362L..25G , we label these hosts RX J1420-A and RX J1420-B. Both galaxies were later targeted by SDSS for spectroscopy, from which their global properties have been measured here.

Figure fig:compact shows that RX J1420-A has properties consistent with the rest of the TDE host galaxies in our sample, while RX J1420-B is a stark outlier. Thus, we propose that RX J1420-A is the actual host galaxy of this TDE and suggest that the galaxy properties we focus on here, namely stellar surface mass density and velocity dispersion, can be used to identify the host galaxies of other TDEs in similar cases.

gmw_00011736.name: SDSS J082853.50+010638.6
gmw_00011736.overview: SDSS J082853.50+010638.6 is a blue host galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.05298 [arXiv:1909.04545]. It is the host galaxy of the transient event SN2018kzr and exhibits narrow nebular emission lines [arXiv:1909.04545].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J082853.50+010638.6 as the blue host galaxy of SN2018kzr, using host emission lines to measure redshift and support difference-image photometry and host subtraction.

quotes.quote: It is coincident (0 6 offset) with the blue, g=20.5 , galaxy SDSS J082853.50+010638.6.
As the transient faded rapidly and was coincident with its host galaxy (Figure fig:SN2018kzr_host ), difference imaging was essential for all epochs which we carried out using hotpants Becker2015 .

The final spectrum taken was a VLT:Xshooter spectrum from MJD 58525.119 (+44.697 days) which showed narrow nebular emission lines from the host galaxy but no detectable flux from SN2018kzr. The \ doublet 3726.03, 3926.47 was resolved into two components and a double Gaussian with full width at half maximum FWHM =1.7 \ was fit to the profiles. The \ 5006.84 line was also detected and the mean of all three centroids gave z = 0.05298 0.00005 . For the cosmology we adopt, this equates to a luminosity distance of 236 Mpc.

gmw_00011737.name: AM 0702-601N
gmw_00011737.overview: AM 0702-601N is a luminous infrared galaxy that is interacting with its southern companion, AM 0702-601S [arXiv:2202.04019]. This interaction has produced expanded outer spiral arms and a tidal tail, and the galaxy hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2202.04019].

sources.summary: The paper describes AM 0702-601N as a LIRG interacting with AM 0702-601S, with expanded spiral arms, a tidal tail, a massive black hole, and a long-rising WISE transient.

quotes.quote: AM 0702-601N has coordinates RA= 07^ h 04^ m 05^ s .250 , Dec= 60^ 19 58 .43 and is located at a distance of 137.1 9.7 Mpc. Assuming this distance, the galaxy has log _ 10 (L _ IR /L_ )= 11.64. The galaxy has a distant companion, known as AM 0702-601S, which is separated 1.5 to the south, corresponding to a projected distance of 60 kpc. AM 0702-601N shows signs of interaction with this companion, through expanded outer spiral arms and a tidal tail.

Haan2011 find a black hole (BH) mass of log _ 10 ( M_ BH /M_ ) = 8.09 0.11 for this galaxy, based on the bulge luminosity and using the Marconi-Hunt relation Marconi2003 . The WISE light curves of AM 0702-601N show minor variation up until MJD = 57870d (27 April 2017).

gmw_00011738.name: NGC 5875
gmw_00011738.overview: NGC 5875 is an extended, face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2308.12991]. It is located at a distance of approximately 53.5 megaparsecs [arXiv:2308.12991].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5875 as the face-on spiral host of SN 2022oqm, giving its redshift, distance, host coordinates, large transient offset, and lack of nearby star formation.

quotes.quote: SN 2022oqm was detected 19.9 kpc from its host galaxy, the face-on spiral galaxy NGC 5875.
SN 2022oqm was found offset by 77.7'' ( 19.9 kpc) from the center of NGC 5875, an extended spiral galaxy at 53.5 1 Mpc Tully13 .

SN 2022oqm was discovered in the outskirts of a face-on, spiral galaxy, NGC 5875, shown in Figure fig:host_galaxy . Like most , SN 2022oqm is highly offset from the center of the galaxy, located 77.7 '' (19.9 kpc) from the optical centroid of the host. The host redshift is consistent with SN 2022oqm, showing SN 2022oqm is not a chance coincidence with the galaxy's location.

gmw_00011739.name: MATLAS-585
gmw_00011739.overview: MATLAS-585 is a ultra-diffuse dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2402.12033]. Spectroscopic and photometric analyses indicate that the galaxy has an estimated stellar age of approximately 9 Gyr [arXiv:2402.12033].

sources.summary: The paper compares MATLAS-585 Prospector results with VLT/MUSE spectroscopy, finding consistent age estimates but a less metal-poor population than the spectroscopic study.

quotes.quote: MATLAS-585 and MATLAS-2103 These two MATLAS UDGs were recently studied using VLT/MUSE data by . MATLAS-585 was found to have a metallicity of [ M/H ] = [ Fe/H ] = 1.88^ + 0.17 _ 0.13 dex and an age of 9.0^ + 2.9 _ 3.1 Gyr.
