With Prospector , we find that MATLAS-585 has a metallicity of [ M/H ] = 1.36^ + 0.27 _ 0.16 dex and an age of 8.91^ +3.44 _ 4.31 Gyr. Thus, our results in terms of age are consistent with those of , but they find a much more metal-poor population for MATLAS-585 than we do with Prospector .

gmw_00011740.name: MATLAS-2103
gmw_00011740.overview: MATLAS-2103 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2402.12033]. Spectroscopic and photometric analyses indicate that the galaxy has an old stellar population, with age estimates ranging from approximately 9.79 to 11.3 billion years [arXiv:2402.12033].

sources.summary: The paper compares MATLAS-2103 Prospector and VLT/MUSE stellar populations, finding consistent ages while its spectroscopy-based metallicity is more metal-poor than the SED fit.

quotes.quote: MATLAS-2103 was found by to have a metallicity of [ M/H ] = [ Fe/H ] = 1.69^ + 0.01 _ 0.22 dex and an age of 11.3^ + 2.2 _ 2.5 Gyr. In this study, we find that MATLAS-2103 has a metallicity of [ M/H ] = 1.40^ + 0.14 _ 0.13 dex and an age of 9.79^ + 2.81 _ 3.84 Gyr. Similar to the case of MATLAS-585, we find ages consistent with those of , but their stellar populations are more metal-poor than ours.

gmw_00011741.name: MATLAS-342
gmw_00011741.overview: MATLAS-342 is a low surface brightness ultra-diffuse dwarf galaxy candidate [arXiv:2402.12033]. Spectroscopic observations confirm that the galaxy is located at the same redshift as its massive neighbor [arXiv:2402.12033].

sources.summary: The paper selects MATLAS-342 for Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopy, observes it with the 1200B setup, shows a pPXF fit, and finds its redshift matches its massive neighbor.

quotes.quote: In this study, in addition to the SED fitting, we present spectroscopic follow-up of 3 MATLAS UDG candidates with Keck/DEIMOS (MATLAS-342, MATLAS-368 and MATLAS-1059) to recover their redshifts and probe their association with the group/massive neighbour. The spectroscopic observations and results are described in Appendix sec:appendix_deimos . We find that all three UDGs are at the same redshifts as their hosts, in agreement with the findings of .

MATLAS-342 and MATLAS-368 were observed with the blue-sensitive 1200 lines mm ^ 1 grating (1200B), GG400 filter, central wavelength 5850 and wavelength coverage of 4500 - 7200 for one hour, with individual exposures of 1800 seconds with a custom-designed 3 arcsec-wide longslit.

In Fig. fig:ppxf_matlas , we show one example of pPXF fit for the galaxy MATLAS-342. Results from the fits of all three galaxies are summarised in Table tab:ppxf_matlas . The results indicate that all three probed UDGs are at the same redshifts as their massive neighbours.

gmw_00011742.name: MATLAS-368
gmw_00011742.overview: MATLAS-368 is a ultra-diffuse dwarf galaxy candidate [arXiv:2402.12033]. Although it was initially thought to be nucleated, its central point source is actually a foreground star, and its recessional velocity was tentatively measured using low signal-to-noise galaxy light [arXiv:2402.12033].

sources.summary: The paper follows up MATLAS-368 with Keck/DEIMOS, finds its central point source is foreground, extracts low-S/N galaxy light, and tentatively measures a recessional velocity.

quotes.quote: In this study, in addition to the SED fitting, we present spectroscopic follow-up of 3 MATLAS UDG candidates with Keck/DEIMOS (MATLAS-342, MATLAS-368 and MATLAS-1059) to recover their redshifts and probe their association with the group/massive neighbour. The spectroscopic observations and results are described in Appendix sec:appendix_deimos . We find that all three UDGs are at the same redshifts as their hosts, in agreement with the findings of .

We note that MATLAS-368 was selected to be followed up because it was thought to be nucleated, which would facilitate recovering its velocity because of its brightness. However, the central point source was found to be at z=0 , thus likely being a foreground star. As an alternative, we studied the galaxy light coming from the outer parts of the slit, but this rendered low S/N data. The identification of the H line was possible, resulting in the recessional velocity shown in Table tab:ppxf_matlas .

gmw_00011743.name: MATLAS-1059
gmw_00011743.overview: MATLAS-1059 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy candidate [arXiv:2402.12033]. Spectroscopic observations show that the galaxy shares a redshift with its massive host, while imaging suggests it is being tidally disrupted by this neighbor [arXiv:2402.12033].

sources.summary: The paper follows up MATLAS-1059 with Keck/DEIMOS, finding its low-S/N spectrum and imaging support a host-matched redshift and possible tidal disruption.

quotes.quote: In this study, in addition to the SED fitting, we present spectroscopic follow-up of 3 MATLAS UDG candidates with Keck/DEIMOS (MATLAS-342, MATLAS-368 and MATLAS-1059) to recover their redshifts and probe their association with the group/massive neighbour. The spectroscopic observations and results are described in Appendix sec:appendix_deimos . We find that all three UDGs are at the same redshifts as their hosts, in agreement with the findings of .

The spectrum of the galaxy that was found to be closer than its host, MATLAS-1059, has very low signal-to-noise (3.8 ^ 1 ) due to its low exposure time, resulting in a much more uncertain velocity than the other studied UDGs. Even in this extreme case of low S/N, the recovered velocity is consistent with the one of the host. Additionally, from CFHT and DECaLS imaging, we can see that this UDG seems tidally disrupted by the host, further confirming its proximity to it.

gmw_00011744.name: IRAS 10190+1322
gmw_00011744.overview: IRAS 10190+1322 is an interacting ultraluminous infrared galaxy system composed of two galaxies with a projected nuclear separation of 6 kpc [arXiv:0704.3583]. The northeast galaxy is classified as a LINER, while the southwest galaxy is classified as an HII galaxy [arXiv:0704.3583].

sources.summary: The paper resolves IRAS 10190+1322 as an interacting ULIRG pair whose two galaxies have similar molecular gas reservoirs but different gas concentrations and possible future merger-driven starburst activity.

quotes.quote: We present the results of high resolution mapping of the CO(1--0) and CO(2--1) emission of the ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) IRAS 10190+1322, with the IRAM interferometer, down to an angular resolution of 0.3 . This object is composed of two interacting galaxies with a projected nuclear separation of 6 kpc, and was selected to analyze the physical and dynamical properties of the molecular gas in each galaxy in order to study the conditions that lead a galaxy pair to become ultraluminous in the infrared. With the exception of Arp 220, the closest ULIRG, this is the first time that the CO emission is morphologically and kinematically resolved in the two interacting galaxies of a ULIRG system.

From our CO(1--0) interferometric observations of IRAS 10190+1322 we conclude that the two galaxies of the system have similar CO(1--0) luminosities, i.e. similar gas reservoirs. The main difference between them is the more compact molecular gas distribution of the NE galaxy, which is a consequence of the initial configuration of the encounter see Murphy01 for a detailed discussion , being this galaxy the most affected by the interaction. The NE galaxy is classified as a LINER and the SW galaxy as a HII galaxy by Rupke02 . Therefore, contrary to the and conclusions, we find that the active galaxy does not have the larger molecular gas amount, but a higher concentration of gas in its nuclear region. Farrah03 obtained from starburst+AGN model fitting of IRAS 10190+1322 SED a total star formation rate of 195 M _ yr ^ 1 . At this rate the total molecular gas reservoir of the NE galaxy will be exhausted in 20 Myr, considering that the bulk of the star formation takes place in this galaxy (see Sect. Introduction and the discussion of our 2.8 mm continuum detection in Sect. Observations ). This period is shorter than the time needed for the two galaxies to coalesce or experience a second close passage ( 50 Myr), assuming a transversal velocity of the order of the measured relative radial velocity ( 140 km s ^ 1 , see Table Table ). The second encounter or final merger of the system may result in the concentration of the molecular gas in the central region of the SW galaxy and the restarting of the ULIRG phase with a new starbursting event.

gmw_00011745.name: NE galaxy
gmw_00011745.overview: The NE galaxy in IRAS 10190+1322 contains highly concentrated molecular gas that is coincident with its radio continuum core [arXiv:0704.3583]. This molecular gas is likely distributed in a circumnuclear disk or ring [arXiv:0704.3583].

sources.summary: The NE galaxy in IRAS 10190+1322 has compact molecular gas coincident with its radio core, a circumnuclear disk or ring, LINER classification, and stronger interaction-driven gas concentration.

quotes.quote: The CO distribution of the NE galaxy is highly concentrated (Figs. Maps a and Maps ). Given its compactness, it is not surprising that 100 of the CO(1--0) emission of this galaxy is recovered by the interferometer (Fig. Lines a). Very likely most of the CO(2--1) emission is also recovered, as the L'_ CO(2-1) /L'_ CO(1-0) luminosity ratio is 0.78, similar to the typical luminosity ratios found in normal spiral galaxies Braine93 . The emission is resolved in both transitions, but it is in the CO(2--1) intensity map, due to the smaller synthesized beam, that the structure of the galaxy is more clearly seen. The centroid of the molecular gas distribution of the NE galaxy coincides with its 15 GHz radio continuum core Nagar03 within 0.1 .

The deconvolved major axis of the CO emitting region (at zero intensity) is 1.7 kpc (1.2 ), similar to the sizes found in ULIRGs in advanced mergers Downes98 . From the ratio between the major and minor axis of the CO distribution we derive an inclination for the galaxy of i 40 . The symmetric CO(1--0) line profile and the resolved distribution of the CO(2--1) intensity map (Figs. Lines a and Maps ) hint that the molecular gas is distributed in a circumnuclear disk or ring.

gmw_00011746.name: SW galaxy
gmw_00011746.overview: The SW galaxy features an extended molecular gas disk with a major axis of 7.4 kiloparsecs [arXiv:0704.3583]. Its double-horned CO line profiles suggest that this molecular gas is distributed in a circumnuclear disk or ring [arXiv:0704.3583].

sources.summary: The SW galaxy has a more extended molecular gas disk than the NE component, with recovered CO emission, circumnuclear disk/ring kinematics, and a large dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: The molecular gas distribution of the SW galaxy is more extended than that of the NE galaxy, with a compact peak that remains detected even at the highest resolution. The deconvolved size of the major axis of the large CO(1--0) emitting region is 7.4 kpc. From the ratio between the major and minor axis of the CO distribution we derive an inclination for the galaxy of i 45 . In Fig. Lines b we compare the deduced single beam CO(1--0) spectrum of the SW galaxy with the CO(1--0) line emission detected by the PdBI.

The double horned CO(1--0) and CO(2--1) line profiles of the SW galaxy (Figs. Lines b and Lines d) hint that the molecular gas is distributed in a circumnuclear disk or ring. The position-velocity diagram along the mayor axis of the galaxy in the CO(1--0) transition is plotted in Fig. Slices b. The radial velocity increases as a function of the distance to the center up to R_ CO =0.8 and then remains constant up to R_ CO =2.6 (3.7 kpc). The terminal rotational velocity is 170 km s ^ 1 (cf. Figs. Lines b and Slices b), therefore we derive a dynamical mass of 5 10^ 10 M _ within this radius.

gmw_00011747.name: NGC 5092
gmw_00011747.overview: NGC 5092 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.023 that is dominated by a middle-aged stellar population of 2.5 billion years [arXiv:2003.09066]. Its nucleus hosted a multiwavelength transient flare, which is consistent with a stellar tidal disruption event by a massive black hole [arXiv:2003.09066].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5092 as the z=0.023 host of XMMSL1 J1319+2259, with nuclear X-ray, UV, optical, and infrared variability consistent with a TDE around a massive black hole.

quotes.quote: We present multiwavelength studies of a transient X-ray source, XMMSL1 J131952.3+225958, associated with the galaxy NGC 5092 at z=0.023 detected in the XMM-Newton SLew survey (XMMSL). The source brightened in the 0.2--2 keV band by a factor of >20 in 2005 as compared with previous flux limits and then faded by a factor of >200 as observed with XMM-Newton in 2013 and with Swift in 2018. At the flaring state, the X-ray spectrum can be modeled with a blackbody at a temperature of 60 eV and an overall luminosity of 1.5 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . A UV flare and optical flare were also detected with the Galaxy Evolution Explorer and the Sloan Digital Sky Survey, respectively, within several months of the X-ray flare, whose nonstellar UV--optical spectrum can be described with a blackbody at a temperature of (1-2) 10^4 K and a luminosity of (2-6) 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . Interestingly, mid-infrared monitoring observations of NGC 5092 with the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer 5--13 yr later show a continuous flux decline.

These dramatic variability properties, from the X-ray through UV and optical to infrared, appear to be orderly, suggestive of a stellar tidal disruption event (TDE) by a massive black hole, confirming the postulation by 2013ApJ...774...63K . This TDE candidate belongs to a rare sample with contemporaneous bright emission detected in the X-ray, UV, and optical, which are later echoed by dust-reprocessed light in the mid-infrared. The black hole has a mass of 5 10^ 7 M_ , residing in a galaxy that is dominated by a middle-aged stellar population of 2.5 Gyr.

gmw_00011748.name: Pisces B
gmw_00011748.overview: Pisces B is a gas-rich dwarf irregular galaxy with a mid-point velocity of 617 km/s [arXiv:1601.00494]. It exhibits slow rotation, but random motions provide most of its gravitational support, suggesting the galaxy is not yet in kinematic equilibrium [arXiv:1601.00494].

sources.summary: KAT-7 observations show Pisces B is a gas-rich dwarf irregular with slow rotation, random-motion support, a 617 velocity midpoint, and kinematics suggesting it is not yet in equilibrium.

quotes.quote: The small rotation velocities detected of 5 \ and 10 \ in Pisces A B respectively, and their \ content show that they are really dwarf irregular galaxies (dIrr). Despite that small rotation component, it is more the random motions 9-11 \ that provide most of the gravitational support, especially in the outer parts. The study of their kinematics, especially the strong gradients of random motions, suggest that those two dwarf galaxies are not yet in equilibrium.

Mid-point velocities (50 level) of 233 3 \ and 617 3 \ are found for Pisces A and Pisces B, respectively. These can be compared to 236 0.5 \ and 615 1 \ found by TOL15. The profile widths at the 50 levels are V _ A = 28 3 \ and V _ B = 41 3 , compared to V _ A = 22.5 1.3 \ and V _ B = 43 3 \ for TOL15.

gmw_00011749.overview: 0548 + 165 is a compact steep-spectrum quasar located at a redshift of 0.474 [arXiv:astro-ph/0203338]. On a milli-arcsecond scale, it features a radio jet that bends by 90 degrees and likely interacts with dense gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0203338].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0548 + 165 as a CSS quasar with asymmetric arcsecond structure, strong depolarization, high jet rotation measures, and a bent mas-scale jet likely interacting with dense gas.

quotes.quote: In arcsecond-scale images, 0548 + 165 shows an asymmetric structure with a strong unresolved component coincident with a quasar at redshift 0.474, and a faint secondary component 3 arcsec northward. Most of the polarized emission comes from the main component. This emission is strongly depolarized going from 15 to 5 GHz, with a rest-frame rotation measure of 1934 rad m ^ 2 (Mantovani et al., 1994). The magnetic field direction derived from the derotated electric vectors is aligned almost east-west, which is also the direction of the inner part of the milli-arcsecond jet.

The nucleus of 0548+165 is almost certainly component 1 (see Fig. 2). Using the flux density values derived from the 1.6-GHz observations by Mantovani et al. (1998) and from the present observations at 5 and 8.4 GHz (Table 3), a spectral index of 0.2 is found for component 1. The remaining components have steeper spectral indices. The projected linear size for 0548 + 165 from the nucleus to the region where the jet shows an 90^ bend is 330 pc.

gmw_00011750.overview: 1524 136 is a steep-spectrum compact steep spectrum (CSS) quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0203338]. Radio observations resolve the source into a compact double morphology with a jet-cloud interaction region located 475 parsecs from the nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0203338].

sources.summary: The paper presents 1524 136 as a CSS quasar with a steep spectrum, compact double radio morphology, high rest-frame rotation measure, and lobe/jet depolarization from an external screen.

quotes.quote: 1524 136 has a steep spectrum classified as C ^- ( i.e. inverted at low frequency) by Steppe et al. (1995) from observations made in the range 90 MHz--230 GHz. The Einstein 0.2--4.5 keV X-ray luminosity is 2.7 10^ 46 erg sec ^ 1 (Wilkes et al. 1994). Images from VLA observations of 1524 136 (Mantovani et al. 1994) show a rather compact object which is resolved into a double source, at 15 GHz. The separation between the two components is 330 mas. 1524 136 has a rest-frame RM= 840 rad m ^ 2 and does not depolarise between 15 and 5 GHz.

The region of jet-cloud interaction in the case of 1524 136 is situated 475 pc from the nucleus. The RM value changes going from component A to E making the astrophysical analysis more intriguing. The peak of polarized emission is found in region A.

gmw_00011751.name: z=2.3285 star-forming galaxy
gmw_00011751.overview: The z=2.3285 star-forming galaxy is a rotationally-supported disk galaxy with a star-formation rate of approximately 30 solar masses per year [arXiv:1306.0134]. It has a subsolar metallicity of about half the solar value and a maximum rotation velocity of 150 km/s [arXiv:1306.0134].

sources.summary: The paper describes a z=2.3285 star-forming galaxy near HE 2243 60 with disk-like kinematics, subsolar metallicity, and circumgalactic absorption interpreted as co-rotating accreting gas.

quotes.quote: The associated star-forming galaxy with redshift z=2.3285 is located just 26 kpc from the damped Lyman absorber (DLA) seen towards the quasar HE 2243 60 . The galaxy was detected in our z 2 SINFONI survey called the SINFONI \ Program for Line Emitters (SIMPLE) . Recent adaptive optics (AO) assisted SINFONI observations of this z=2.3285 star-forming galaxy (Fig. 1a) obtained at the Very Large Telescope (VLT) with 1 kpc ( 0.25 arc sec) resolution (table S1) allow us to map the emission kinematics with precision (Fig. 1b and figs S3 and S4). The kinematics reveal that this galaxy has physical properties (Table table:summary ) typical for rotationally-supported disks seen at that redshift .

For instance, the galaxy has a star-formation rate (SFR) of \ (where is the mass of the Sun), its maximum rotation velocity is 150 15 \ from 3D fitting (Fig. S4, Table S3), and its metallicity is about 1/2 solar ( [ ] =-0.35 0.1 dex) determined from a joint fit to all the major nebular emission lines ( , + \ and / ; figs. S1 and S2).

gmw_00011752.name: 7C 1756+6520
gmw_00011752.overview: 7C 1756+6520 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.4 [arXiv:0908.4106, arXiv:1010.1385]. It resides in an overdense region of red galaxies [arXiv:0908.4106] and has several close companion galaxies, including a mid-infrared selected active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0908.4106, arXiv:1010.1385].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 7C 1756+6520 as a z=1.48 radio galaxy in an overdense region with red galaxies, possible close companions, a red sequence, and excess mid-infrared AGN.

quotes.quote: In contrast, 7C 1756+6520 shows a significant overdensity of red galaxies within 2 Mpc of the radio galaxy, by a factor of 3.1 0.8 relative to the four MUSYC fields. At small separation ( < 6 ), this radio galaxy also has one z 1.4 evolved galaxy candidate, one z 1.4 star-forming galaxy candidate, and an AGN candidate (at indeterminate redshift). This is suggestive of several close-by companions.

Several concentrations of red galaxies are also noticed in the full 7C 1756+6520 field, forming a possible large-scale structure of evolved galaxies with a NW-SE orientation. We construct the color-magnitude diagram of red galaxies found near ( < 2 Mpc) 7C 1756+6520, and find a clear red sequence that is truncated at K_s 21.5 (AB). We also find an overdensity of mid-IR selected AGN in the surroundings of 7C 1756+6520.

sources.summary: The paper reports nearby objects around 7C 1756+6520, including a mid-infrared AGN and passive BzK galaxy, and estimates blended IRS/MIPS flux ratios.

quotes.quote: 7C 1756+6520 ( z=1.416 ,): galametz09 found three objects within 6 \ (48 kpc) of the radio galaxy, two of which were spectroscopically confirmed by galametz10 to be at the same redshift as the radio galaxy. One of them is a mid-IR selected AGN. Both this AGN and a passively evolving BzK selected (p BzK ) galaxy were individually detected with IRAC but only marginally resolved with IRS and MIPS. Tables table.photometry and table.binaryAGN provide photometry of both objects; as the HzRG and AGN are not fully spatially resolved in the IRS 16 m and MIPS 24 m images, we estimated the relative fluxes between the HzRG and AGN by summing the pixel values in a fixed circular 6 pixel radius aperture using the positions from the IRAC 3.6 m image (Fig. doubleAGN ) as centroids. The derived ratios of 50 /50 at 16 m and and 20 /80 at 24 m should be considered approximative.

gmw_00011753.name: MRC 2048 272
gmw_00011753.overview: MRC 2048 272 is a radio galaxy at a redshift of 2.060 that lacks a detected radio or X-ray core [arXiv:1010.1385]. It features two components separated by 21 kiloparsecs, with the western source detected at longer infrared wavelengths [arXiv:1010.1385].

sources.summary: The paper finds two components in MRC 2048 272, with no detected radio or X-ray core, IRAC detections for both, and longer-wavelength emission from the western source.

quotes.quote: MRC 2048 272 ( z=2.060 ): No radio or X-ray core was detected in this radio galaxy car97,ove05 . pen01 detected two sources, separated by 2 5 (21 kpc), in NICMOS H band imaging and considered the easternmost one as the most likely AGN host. This is consistent with the Ly halo, which extends for 5 \ along the radio axis ven07 . We detected both components in the IRAC 3.6 and 4.5 m images. However, at longer wavelengths only the western source is detected, suggesting it also contains an AGN.

gmw_00011754.name: 6C 0032+412
gmw_00011754.overview: 6C 0032+412 is a high-redshift radio galaxy [arXiv:1010.1385] classified as a type 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1010.1385]. Its spectral energy distribution requires unusually hot dust components, which may indicate a rare, unobscured line of sight through its dusty torus [arXiv:1010.1385].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 6C 0032+412 as an unusual type 2 radio-galaxy AGN requiring hotter dust components, possibly exposing a rare line of sight through its torus.

quotes.quote: 6C 0032+412 ( z=3.670 ): This HzRG is the only one where our standard 3-component dust model (using T=60 , 250, and 500--1500 K, see 3.2) could not fit the SED. First, we had to increase the temperature of the T=250 component to T=650 K. In addition, this is the only source where we required T=1500 K for the hottest dust component compared to a median T =800 K . A near-IR bump caused by a T 1500 K dust component has been seen in several quasars gallagher07,mor09,leipski10 and is generally associated with graphite dust near the sublimation temperature barvainis87 . The very high temperature necessarily locates the dust very close to the AGN.

6C 0032+412 is a bona-fide type 2 AGN as derived from its narrow-line UV and visible spectroscopy jarvis01 . The modest 2 radio core fraction (Table table.radiodata ) also does not indicate a line of sight close to that of quasars (see 4.3.1). A possible explanation would be that 6C 0032+412 presents a rare unobscured line of sight through the entire dusty torus all the way to the central regions near the AGN.

gmw_00011755.name: 6C 0140+326
gmw_00011755.overview: 6C 0140+326 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.41 [arXiv:1010.1385]. The galaxy is gravitationally lensed by a foreground ellipsoidal galaxy and is heavily contaminated by a foreground star [arXiv:1010.1385].

sources.summary: The survey describes 6C 0140+326 as a radio galaxy lensed and contaminated by a foreground galaxy and star, leaving only shallow upper limits for the radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: 6C 0140+326 ( z=4.41 ): rawlings96 argued that this radio galaxy is gravitationally lensed by a foreground ( z=0.927 ) galaxy 1 6 to the southeast. This foreground L_* galaxy has an ellipsoidal shape in both the Keck/NIRC K band image wvb98 and the HST /NICMOS F160W image lacy99 . Our Spitzer photometry at 3.6 and 4.5 m is dominated by strong a strong foreground star, while the 16 and 24 m photometry are dominated by mid-IR emission from the lensing galaxy. The radio galaxy itself appears undetected in all but the 5.8 or 8 m emission, where it is only marginally detected and contaminated by the foreground galaxy. We therefore provide only relatively shallow upper limits based on the detection of the foreground galaxy.

gmw_00011756.name: MRC 0324 228
gmw_00011756.overview: MRC 0324 228 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.894 [arXiv:1010.1385]. It features two potential host galaxies separated by 1.5 arcseconds, with a faint radio core tentatively detected in the southernmost source [arXiv:1010.1385].

sources.summary: The paper discusses two potential host galaxies for MRC 0324 228, unresolved Spitzer structure, a tentative radio core, and nearby sources that may contaminate MIPS photometry.

quotes.quote: MRC 0324 228 ( z=1.894 ): The NICMOS image of this radio galaxy pen01 reveals two potential host galaxies separated by 1 5 (12 kpc). Our Spitzer imaging does not resolve them as individual sources, though it does show an extended structure along the same north--south orientation. Our new radio data tentatively detected a faint radio core at 4.86 GHz, coinciding with the southernmost of these two identifications, which we have adopted as the host galaxy. There are also two nearby objects that may contaminate the MIPS photometry.

gmw_00011757.name: EOSL galaxy
gmw_00011757.overview: The EOSL galaxy is an edge-on lenticular galaxy located in the cluster MACSJ0416.1-2403 [arXiv:1409.1578]. It acts as a gravitational lens, with a modeled total mass of approximately 3e11 solar masses and a dark matter halo oriented nearly orthogonal to its stellar disk [arXiv:1409.1578].

sources.summary: The paper models a paper-local EOSL galaxy as an edge-on lenticular lens in MACSJ0416.1-2403, finding a 3e11 solar-mass total mass and a dark halo nearly orthogonal to its disk.

quotes.quote: We examine a well resolved 8 \ lensed image that is symmetrically bent in the middle by an edge-on lenticular galaxy, in the Hubble Frontier Field (HFF) data of MACSJ0416.1-20403. The lengthy image is generated primarily by the large tangential shear of the cluster with a local secondary deflection by the member galaxy out to a limiting radius of 18 kpc. The lensing lenticular galaxy is also well resolved and evidently lies nearly edge-on in projection. This fortuitous combination of a long arc intersecting an edge on galaxy provides us with an opportunity to place relatively strong constraints on the lensing effect of this galaxy. We can model the stellar lensing contribution using the observed pixels belonging to the galaxy, in 2D, and we add to this a standard parameterised dark halo component.

Irrespective of the detailed choice of parameters we obtain a combined total mass of 3 10^ 11 M_ . Depending on the dark halo parameters, the stellar contribution to this is limited to the range 5-15 10^ 10 M_ , or 20-50 of the total mass, in good agreement with the independent stellar mass computed from the photometry of 5 10^ 10 M_ for a Chabrier IMF, or 8 10^ 10 M_ for a Salpeter IMF. The major axis of the DM halo is constrained to be nearly orthogonal to the plane of the galaxy, within a range of 15^ , and with an ellipticity e 0.15 corresponding to an axis ratio a/=0.54 .

gmw_00011758.name: NGC 3913
gmw_00011758.overview: NGC 3913 is a blue dwarf galaxy with an extended morphology [arXiv:1903.04119]. It exhibits a very low star formation rate of 0.001 solar masses per year, making it unlikely to be a dwarf starburst galaxy [arXiv:1903.04119].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3913 as a blue dwarf galaxy close in velocity to the absorber, with extended SDSS morphology and a very low star formation rate.

quotes.quote: There is another dwarf galaxy, NGC 3913 , close by in velocity to the absorber at | v| = 88 and = 190 kpc ( 1.3R_ vir ). The g - = 0.542 color makes it a blue galaxy Blanton2003 with an extended morphology seen in SDSS. We derive a star formation rate of SFR = 0.001 M _ yr ^ 1 using the H luminosity which is very low for it to be a dwarf starburst galaxy Martin2003 .

gmw_00011759.name: IC 691
gmw_00011759.overview: IC 691 is a dwarf starburst galaxy with a star formation rate of approximately 0.24 solar masses per year [arXiv:1903.04119]. The galaxy has an estimated metallicity of 0.7 dex and hosts a starburst-driven outflow [arXiv:1903.04119].

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 691 as the closest galaxy to the absorber, a dwarf starburst whose outflow and metallicity may explain enrichment of the absorbing cloud.

quotes.quote: The galaxy at 33 kpc is IC 691 which is at 0.2R_ vir from the absorber. Keeney2006 have carried out a detailed analysis of this galaxy's association with the absorber. From the extinction corrected H luminosity, they infer a SFR 0.24 M _ yr ^ 1 for IC 691 which makes it a dwarf starburst system. Based on estimates for the wind velocity and the galaxy orientation, Keeney2006 attribute the incidence of the absorber to the starburst driven outflow from this dwarf galaxy. Keeney2006 obtain a metallicity of 0.7 dex for the galaxy, which is within the range of possible metallicities for the absorber given by the photoionization models.

gmw_00011760.name: GRB host galaxy
gmw_00011760.overview: The GRB host galaxy contains an absorption system resolved into three distinct components [arXiv:0911.3901]. Relative motion of the gas within the host galaxy drives the velocity offsets between these components, which exhibit a high silicon-to-iron ratio that may indicate dust depletion or active star formation [arXiv:0911.3901].

sources.summary: The paper resolves the GRB host absorption system into three components, fits column densities, and links their velocity offsets to host-galaxy gas; high [Si/Fe] may indicate silicon enhancement from star formation or iron depletion.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy system Most of the absorption features detected at the redshift of the GRB can be separated into three different components that we name I, II and III, with I having the highest redshift. We fit line profiles to the different components in order to determine the column density of each species. Due to the limited resolution and S/N, we fix the thermal b parameter to 5 km s ^ 1 and the turbulent b parameters to 20 km s ^ 1 (values that give the best global result for the fits), leaving only the column density as a free parameter.

The relative velocities between the 3 different components are due to relative motion of the gas within the host galaxy, in principle unrelated to the GRB itself. The large values of [ Si/Fe ] measured in the GRB host galaxy may be indicative of enhanced abundance of Si (produced by extensive star formation) or a higher depletion level of Fe (evidence of dust).

gmw_00011761.name: G1
aliases_external: bright SDSS galaxy near GRB 090313 afterglow (G1)
gmw_00011761.overview: G1 is a galaxy with a known redshift of z = 0.0235 [arXiv:0911.3901]. It is the brighter of two galaxies located near a specific afterglow, with a projected separation of 17.8 arcseconds [arXiv:0911.3901].

sources.summary: The paper describes G1 as the brighter galaxy near the afterglow, gives its redshift and projected separation, and reports no detected features matching its SDSS absorption lines.

quotes.quote: ber09 pointed out the presence of two galaxies near the afterglow (G1 and G2, see Fig.  FigAcq ). The brightest one (G1, =15.6) has a known redshift of z =0.0235 and is located at 17 8 from the afterglow, equivalent to 8.3 kpc projected at the redshift of the galaxy. We do not see any features at this redshift and, in particular, nothing is detected at the wavelength of the most prominent absorption features that can be seen in the SDSS spectrum of G1 aba09 , which are the Ca II 8500, 8544, 8664 and Na I 5891, 5897. Ca II features are not detected with 3- limits of EW _ rest < 0.6 and Na I with limits of EW _ rest < 1.2 .

gmw_00011762.name: J125848.94+281037.1
gmw_00011762.overview: J125848.94+281037.1 is a flattened ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:1901.05489]. It is a highly metal-poor system that is dark-matter dominated and shows indications of rotation along its major axis [arXiv:1901.05489].

sources.summary: The paper describes this Coma ultra-diffuse galaxy as flattened, very metal-poor, possibly rotating along the major axis, and dark-matter dominated within the modeled region.

quotes.quote: J125848.94+281037.1 is a flattened galaxy ( b/a=0.65 ) observed along the major axis. This is the most metal-poor object in our sample ( [ Fe/H ] =- 1.8 dex). There is a hint of major axis rotation from the three data points along the slit. We are able to constrain both orbital anisotropy (close to 0) and the dark matter content ( 90 ).

gmw_00011763.name: J125904.06+281422.4
gmw_00011763.overview: J125904.06+281422.4 is a flattened Coma galaxy observed nearly along its major axis [arXiv:1901.05489]. It exhibits a flat central velocity profile, weak rotation in its outskirts, and a high dark matter content of approximately 90 percent by mass [arXiv:1901.05489].

sources.summary: The paper describes this flattened Coma galaxy as observed near its major axis, with weak outer rotation, a flat central velocity profile, high anisotropy, and roughly 90 percent dark matter.

quotes.quote: J125904.06+281422.4 is a flattened galaxy ( b/a=0.56 ) observed almost along the major axis. There is a hint of rotation ( <10 km/s) in the outskirts and a flat velocity profile in the central region that drive the dynamical model towards higher anisotropy values with a dark matter content around 90 \ by mass.

The two galaxies with signs of major axis rotation, J125904.06+281422.4 and J125904.20+281507.7 likely have somewhat higher dark matter content, around 90 but further, deeper observations would be required for confirmation.

gmw_00011764.name: J125904.20+281507.7
gmw_00011764.overview: J125904.20+281507.7 is an approximately round, metal-poor dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1901.05489]. Its radial velocity profile does not show rotation, and the galaxy likely has a dark matter content of around 90 percent [arXiv:1901.05489].

sources.summary: The paper describes this galaxy as round and metal-poor, lacking significant radial-velocity rotation, fit by anisotropic orbits, and probably having about 90 percent dark matter.

quotes.quote: J125904.20+281507.7 is an approximately round ( b/a=0.82 ), metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] =- 1.5 dex) galaxy observed along the major axis. The radial velocity profile does not show significant rotation and can be fit with a moderate mass-to-light ratio with anisotropic orbits ( _z=0.5 ), assuming axial symmetry.

The two galaxies with signs of major axis rotation, J125904.06+281422.4 and J125904.20+281507.7 likely have somewhat higher dark matter content, around 90 but further, deeper observations would be required for confirmation.

gmw_00011765.name: J125937.23+274815.2
gmw_00011765.overview: J125937.23+274815.2 is a nearly round galaxy with a small effective radius of 0.9 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1901.05489]. Although it is located near the brighter galaxy GMP 3324, the two are not physically connected due to a radial velocity difference of nearly 1000 km/s [arXiv:1901.05489].

sources.summary: The paper describes this galaxy as nearly round, unusually compact for the sample, observed off the major axis, dynamically unconstrained in anisotropy, and physically separate from GMP 3324.

quotes.quote: J125937.23+274815.2 is a nearly round galaxy ( b/a=0.87 ) observed 18 deg off the major axis with the smallest effective radius (0.9 kpc) in our sample. We are unable to constrain the orbital anisotropy. This galaxy is not physically connected to the brighter GMP 3324 visible to the right as their radial velocities differ by almost 1000 km/s.

gmw_00011766.name: J130005.40+275333.0
gmw_00011766.overview: J130005.40+275333.0 is a round galaxy featuring a distinct, unresolved stellar nucleus [arXiv:1901.05489]. The galaxy hosts an intermediate-age, metal-rich stellar population, and its rotation cannot be modeled axisymmetrically, suggesting a triaxial system [arXiv:1901.05489].

sources.summary: The paper describes this round nucleated galaxy as having a possible metal-rich nucleus, rotation inconsistent with axisymmetric models, and triaxiality that may explain its structure.

quotes.quote: J130005.40+275333.0 is quite round ( b/a=0.9 ) with a distinct stellar nucleus unresolved both on Subaru and archival HST ACS images. The nuclear stellar population has the same age (3 Gyr) as the rest of the galaxy but may be more metal-rich ( [ Fe/H ] =-0.08 0.18 dex vs 0.30 0.22 dex). The rotation ( 20 5 km/s at 0.5 kpc from the center) cannot be fit by axisymmetric models with adequate M/L ratios. We suspect a triaxial system similar to some dwarf VCC 1545 , Chilingarian09 and giant NGC 4365 , 2001ApJ...548L..33D elliptical galaxies.

J130005.40+275333.0 is a special case where triaxiality may reconcile its significant stellar rotation with its round shape. The stellar population is intermediate-age (3 Gyr) and metal-rich ( 0.3 dex). The luminous nucleus ( m_R 22.44 mag; M_R 12.64 mag; red arrow in Fig. fjrmet ), is similar to the nuclei in many more luminous dE/dS0 galaxies in the Virgo cluster presented in Chilingarian09 .

gmw_00011767.name: J130026.26+272735.2
gmw_00011767.overview: J130026.26+272735.2 is a strongly flattened galaxy with a relatively young stellar population of 1.5 billion years [arXiv:1901.05489]. It lacks current star formation, and its stars likely occupy highly anisotropic orbits within a disk that has a dark matter fraction of 50 to 80 percent [arXiv:1901.05489].

sources.summary: The paper describes this as the most spatially extended sample galaxy, with a young non-star-forming population, strong flattening, high anisotropy, and 50-80 percent dark matter.

quotes.quote: J130026.26+272735.2 is the most spatially extended galaxy in our sample. Its slightly asymmetric shape explains the offset of the coordinates reported in Adami08 and used in our mask design so that the slit landed 1 ^ off the galaxy nucleus. We find a relatively young stellar population (1.5 Gyr) but no current star formation evidenced by the absence of emission lines. The absence of rotation with strong flattening ( b/a=0.44 ) suggest that stars in its disk have highly anisotropic orbits. This is best case in our sample where the _z - M/L degeneracy is broken by dynamical modeling.

For the two objects with high orbital anisotropy, J125929.89+274303.0 and J130026.26+272735.2 , the dark matter fraction estimate is 50 \ to 80 very similar to what was found in the three dE satellites of the Andromeda galaxy 2006MNRAS.369.1321D .

The two galaxies with younger stellar populations, J130026.26+272735.2 and J130038.63+272835.3 likely entered the central region of the Coma cluster quite recently with gas-rich disks that were stripped by the ram pressure of the hot intracluster gas 1972ApJ...176....1G .

gmw_00011768.name: J130028.34+274820.5
gmw_00011768.overview: J130028.34+274820.5 is a galaxy characterized by isotropic stellar orbits and a very high dynamical mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:1901.05489]. It exhibits a weak velocity gradient and S-shaped image fitting residuals, which may indicate a tidal interaction with a nearby brighter galaxy [arXiv:1901.05489].

sources.summary: The paper describes this galaxy as having a very high dynamical mass-to-light ratio, isotropic stellar orbits, a weak velocity gradient, and possible tidal interaction with a nearby brighter galaxy.

quotes.quote: J130028.34+274820.5 . Dynamical modelling yields a very high M/L ratio and isotropic stellar orbits similar to J125937.23+274815.2 . The data are consistent with a weak velocity gradient along the slit placed about 50 deg off the major axis. S-shaped image fitting residuals and the weak rotation may indicate tidal interaction with a nearby brighter galaxy ( J130028.98+274836.2 ) visible in the top left corner of the image cutout.

gmw_00011769.name: J130038.63+272835.3
gmw_00011769.overview: J130038.63+272835.3 is a strongly flattened galaxy with a relatively young stellar population of approximately 2 Gyr [arXiv:1901.05489]. It likely entered the central region of the Coma cluster recently, where its gas-rich disk was stripped by ram pressure [arXiv:1901.05489].

sources.summary: The paper describes this galaxy as young and strongly flattened, with a central velocity-dispersion bump that standard models cannot explain and could imply an overmassive black hole.

quotes.quote: J130038.63+272835.3 has a relatively young stellar population ( 2 Gyr) and a strongly flattened shape ( b/a=0.42 ). The central bump in the velocity dispersion profile ( 40 7 km/s compared to 15 10 km/s outside the peak) cannot be explained with standard dynamical modelling. If the bump is caused by a central black hole (dashed green line in Fig. udg_models ) a mass of 2.5 10^8 M_ would be required, 3 times the total stellar mass of the galaxy ( 7.5 10^7 M_ ).

The two galaxies with younger stellar populations, J130026.26+272735.2 and J130038.63+272835.3 likely entered the central region of the Coma cluster quite recently with gas-rich disks that were stripped by the ram pressure of the hot intracluster gas 1972ApJ...176....1G .

gmw_00011770.name: ESO 208-G021
gmw_00011770.overview: ESO 208-G021 is a disky galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0411148] that features a dust lane across its field of view [arXiv:1610.01174]. It hosts a nearly edge-on nuclear disk containing ionized gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0411148], and its nuclear dispersion peak is partly attributed to unresolved stellar rotation [arXiv:1610.01174].

sources.summary: ACS imaging shows ESO 208-G021 has an edge-on nuclear disk with ionized gas along its northern edge, faint clumps, consistent disk/bulge decomposition, and disky isophotes.

quotes.quote: ESO 208-G021 --- Fig. 1(a) : This galaxy possesses a nearly edge-on nuclear disk roughly 375 pc ( 7 ) in diameter and oriented along the major axis of the galaxy ( 110 ). The ionized gas is observed along the ``northern'' edge of the disk, suggesting that it is primarily confined to its interior. The gas appears diffuse with a few faint compact clumps. The most prominent is located at the geometrical center of the disk which coincides with the galaxy center.

Scorza et al. (1998) performed a photometric disk/bulge decomposition of ESO 208-G021 - our isophotal results are consistent with theirs. The B4 parameter indicates that the galaxy is very ``disky'' throughout most of its extent.

sources.summary: The paper notes a mismatch between ESO 208 G-21 stellar-rotation position angles, attributes it partly to a dust lane, and groups its nuclear dispersion peak with unresolved rotation.

quotes.quote: In ESO 208 G-21, the difference is 18 ^o . However, one should be aware that a stellar rotation detected with PCA Tomography is more affected by dust than the radial velocity maps. Tomograms associated with stellar structures in rotation are built in such a way that the result is a combination of the anti-correlation between the red and blue wings of the stellar absorption lines and the intensity of the stellar content of the galaxies (see paper I). Since ESO 208 G-21 has a dust lane across the FOV (paper III), this structure may have affected the measurement of the PA of the stellar rotation detected in the tomogram shown in paper I.
The nuclear peaks detected in ESO 208 G-21, IC 1459, IC 5181, and NGC 4546 should correspond, in part, to unresolved stellar rotation.

gmw_00011771.name: NGC 3078
gmw_00011771.overview: NGC 3078 is a galaxy characterized by strongly boxy isophotes [arXiv:astro-ph/0411148]. It features a nearly edge-on nuclear disk aligned with its major axis, as well as warm gas concentrated primarily on its nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0411148].

sources.summary: ACS imaging of NGC 3078 shows a nearly edge-on nuclear disk aligned with the major axis, boxy isophotes, and warm gas concentrated mainly on the nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 3078 --- Fig. 1(e) : An HST/WFPC2 F702W image of NGC 3078 is shown in Tran et al. (2001) and an isophotal analysis based on this image is presented in Rest et al. (2001). Our ACS images clearly show the nearly edge-on nuclear disk of diameter 180 pc ( 1 1 ) oriented along the major axis of the galaxy. The galaxy isophotes are strongly boxy, in accord with Rest et al. (2001).

The extraction of a reliable emission-line map is difficult because of the presence of the disk, but it appears that the warm gas is primarily concentrated on the nucleus, as reported by Trinchieri di Serego Alighieri (1991) with ground-based narrow-band imaging.

gmw_00011772.name: MRC 1138-26
gmw_00011772.overview: MRC 1138-26, also known as the Spiderweb radio galaxy, is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.16 [arXiv:1309.4223]. It is situated within a well-known protocluster that exhibits an overdensity of SPIRE 250-micron sources [arXiv:1309.4223].

sources.summary: The paper places a Herschel SPIRE overdensity near MRC 1138-26, identifying it with the Spiderweb protocluster/radio-galaxy field and noting possible association with the same large-scale structure.

quotes.quote: We report the serendipitous detection of a significant overdensity of SPIRE 250 \ sources in the vicinity of MRC 1138-26. We use an adaptive kernel density estimate (AKDE) to quantify the significance, including a comparison with other fields. The overdensity has a size of 3 5 4 and stands out at 5 with respect to the background estimate.

The target of the \ observations is a well known protocluster: MRC 1138-26, also known as the Spiderweb protocluster. Its existence was confirmed by reports of significant overdensities of Ly , H emitters and near-IR galaxy colours consistent with the 4000 \ break at z 2.2 , close to the redshift z=2.16 of the Spiderweb radio galaxy (e.g. pentericci00,kurk04a,kurk04b,miley06,venemans07 ). The SPIRE and PACS observations were used in a detailed analysis of the central massive star-forming radio galaxy seymour12 . Comprehensive details of the Herschel data processing, map-making and source detection, and catalogues of the \ observations, will be presented in S anchez-Portal et al. (in preparation). SPIRE map making The SPIRE observation ( obsid : 1342210877, on 07 Dec 2010) was executed in Large Map mode covering a field of 20 20 , centred on the radio galaxy (RA=11:h40:48.360, Dec=-26:29:10.60, J2000.0).

gmw_00011773.name: J0911+1333
gmw_00011773.overview: The active galactic nucleus J0911+1333 exhibits outflows traced by broad emission components with velocity dispersions four to six times larger than the stellar velocity dispersion [arXiv:1702.01900]. In contrast, the narrower emission components of the galaxy trace the host-galaxy gravitational kinematics [arXiv:1702.01900].

sources.summary: J0911+1333 is used as an example where broad-component velocity dispersions indicate AGN outflows, while narrower components trace host-galaxy gravitational kinematics.

quotes.quote: In J0911+1333, for example, the velocity dispersions of the broad component are a factor of 4 6 larger than the stellar velocity dispersion. Such large velocity dispersions indicate non-gravitational origin, i.e., AGN outflows. In comparison, the velocity dispersions of the narrow component are larger than the stellar velocity dispersion by a factor of 2 at the center, and become comparable to the stellar velocity dispersion at larger distance.

For example, J0911+1333 shows narrow components (blue dots) with larger velocity offsets compared to the broad components (red dot), while the velocities obtained from the total profile show a velocity in the middle of the velocities for narrow and broad component. The narrow components are located within 300 \ of the velocity offset, while the broad components show a blueshifted velocity offset from 500 \ to 100 .

gmw_00011774.name: J0952+1937
gmw_00011774.overview: J0952+1937 is an active galactic nucleus with extreme outflow energetics despite its relatively low bolometric luminosity [arXiv:1702.01900]. The host galaxy features a star-forming disk with ongoing star formation and a low electron density, suggesting its ionized gas has been swept out [arXiv:1702.01900].

sources.summary: J0952+1937 is one of two extreme AGNs with high outflow energetics, low electron density, high mass outflow rate, and an SF-type disk with ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, we find two AGNs (J0952+1937 and J1520+0757) with much higher energetics than the majority of AGNs in our sample. Although these two AGNs have relatively low bolometric luminosities ( L_ bol 10^ 42.7-43.0 ), their energy injection rate is 27--97 of L_ bol and the momentum flux is 228--449 L_ bol / .

In Sections int_energetics and origin , we find two AGNs with extreme energetics, while their bolometric luminosities are relatively low (J0952+1937 and J1520+0757). These two AGNs have the lowest electron density among the sample ( n_e =78 and 54 cm ^ 3 , respectively), implying that the ionized gas has possibly been swept out, which is consistent with their high mass outflow rate ( M _ out =17.7 and 9.2 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively).

On the other hand, J0952+1937 has a SF-type disk and harbors on-going star formation with log SSFR= 9.5 yr ^ 1 (see Figure fig:ssfr ). If the outflows in these AGNs are powerful enough to affect the star formation in the host galaxy, their different SSFRs are possibly due to different geometry of outflows with respect to the star-forming disk.

gmw_00011775.name: J1520+0757
gmw_00011775.overview: J1520+0757 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that resides in the green valley [arXiv:1702.01900]. It exhibits an extreme gas outflow with high energetics and low electron density, but has no detectable rotational features [arXiv:1702.01900].

sources.summary: J1520+0757 is an additional lower-luminosity IFU target and an extreme outflow object with high energetics, low electron density, no detectable rotation, and green-valley specific star formation.

quotes.quote: Note that we observed 2 additional targets (J1100+1124 and J1520+0757), which satisfy the selection criteria for gas kinematics but with a slightly lower [ ] luminosity ( L_ [ O III ] ,cor 10^ 41 ).

In Sections int_energetics and origin , we find two AGNs with extreme energetics, while their bolometric luminosities are relatively low (J0952+1937 and J1520+0757). These two AGNs have the lowest electron density among the sample ( n_e =78 and 54 cm ^ 3 , respectively), implying that the ionized gas has possibly been swept out, which is consistent with their high mass outflow rate ( M _ out =17.7 and 9.2 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively).

For example, J1520+0757 has no detectable rotational feature in , residing in the ``green valley'' with log SSFR= 10.9 yr ^ 1 . On the other hand, J0952+1937 has a SF-type disk and harbors on-going star formation with log SSFR= 9.5 yr ^ 1 (see Figure fig:ssfr ). If the outflows in these AGNs are powerful enough to affect the star formation in the host galaxy, their different SSFRs are possibly due to different geometry of outflows with respect to the star-forming disk.

gmw_00011776.name: C21434
gmw_00011776.overview: C21434 is a massive galaxy at a redshift of z=1.522 that was recently quenched [arXiv:1902.09564]. Its optical spectrum exhibits strong Balmer absorption lines, indicating that star formation ceased approximately 0.8 Gyr ago, while ALMA observations place a strict upper limit on its molecular gas mass [arXiv:1902.09564].

sources.summary: C21434 is presented as a massive recently quenched z=1.522 galaxy with strong Balmer absorption, compact HST morphology, and an ALMA non-detection implying very low molecular gas content.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA observations of CO(2--1) in a massive ( / =11.2 ), recently quenched galaxy at z=1.522 . The optical spectrum of this object shows strong Balmer absorption lines, which implies that star formation ceased 0.8 Gyr ago. We do not detect CO(2--1) line emission, placing an upper limit on the molecular H_2 gas mass of 1.1 10^ 10 .

The targeted galaxy, C21434, is selected from a sample of massive galaxies ( / 11 ) at z 1.5 in the NEWFIRM Medium Band Survey NMBS, whitaker:11 . The sample and full analysis is described in bezanson:13a , but we briefly summarize here. The NMBS survey provides extensive multi-wavelength photometry for this object from the UV to 24 , including medium-band near-IR filters that span the Balmer/ 4000 break at the redshift of the target (shown in the right panel of Figure fig:LRISspectrum ). A deep (18 hour) optical spectrum was taken in January and April 2010 using the LRIS spectrograph (main panel of Figure fig:LRISspectrum ) and high-resolution rest-frame optical imaging was obtained using the F160W filter on the HST-WFC3 Camera (Program HST-GO-12167, PI: Franx).

gmw_00011777.name: Carina dwarf spheroidal galaxy
gmw_00011777.overview: The Carina dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1607.05312] has a total stellar mass of approximately 1.60 million solar masses [arXiv:1607.05312]. Its star formation history consists of an old episode and an intermediate-age episode separated by a temporal gap, with the majority of its stellar mass formed during the older period [arXiv:1607.05312].

sources.summary: The paper derives Carina’s spatially resolved star-formation history, finding old and intermediate-age episodes separated by a gap, most stellar mass in the old population, and radial age-metallicity gradients.

quotes.quote: We present the spatially resolved star formation history (SFH) of the Carina dwarf spheroidal galaxy, obtained from deep, wide-field g, imaging and a metallicity distribution from the literature. Our photometry covers 2 deg ^2 , reaching up to 10 times the half-light radius of Carina with a completeness higher than 50 at g 24.5 , more than one magnitude fainter than the oldest turnoff. This is the first time a combination of depth and coverage of this quality has been used to derive the SFH of Carina, enabling us to trace its different populations with unprecedented accuracy.

We find that Carina's SFH consists of two episodes well separated by a star formation temporal gap. These episodes occurred at old ( >10 Gyr) and intermediate ( 2 - 8 Gyr) ages. Our measurements show that the old episode comprises the majority of the population, accounting for 54 5 of the stellar mass within 1.3 times the King tidal radius, while the total stellar mass derived for Carina is 1.60 0.09 10^ 6 M_ , and the stellar mass-to-light ratio 1.8 0.2 .

gmw_00011778.name: S6
gmw_00011778.overview: S6 is a double, non-thermal radio source that is extended along 45 arcseconds [arXiv:1603.07921]. Its shape and spectral index are consistent with those of a radio galaxy, and it could be the source of the flux detected by Fermi-LAT [arXiv:1603.07921].

sources.summary: The paper treats S6 as a double, nonthermal radio source whose shape and spectral index match a radio galaxy and which could account for the Fermi-LAT flux.

quotes.quote: S6, at 2' from WR 11, is a double source with non-thermal radiation.

The source S6 is double, extended along 45'' . If it is a galactic object (for instance, a microquasar), it should be detected as a stellar source too. The shape and the spectral index are consistent with those of a radio galaxy. Such object, for instance if 30-kpc long, will be at a distance of 100 Mpc, like other typical FRI or FRII galaxies.
S6 could be a radio galaxy, that radiates the flux detected by Fermi LAT. And S6 is, as WR 11, inside the maximum probability contour of the Fermi LAT source position.

gmw_00011779.name: 2MASX J08100010 4705059
gmw_00011779.overview: 2MASX J08100010 4705059 is a double-lobed galaxy identified based on its emission in the J, H, and K bands [arXiv:1603.07921]. The system is positionally coincident with a radio source and is also detected as an infrared source [arXiv:1603.07921].

sources.summary: The paper associates S7 with 2MASX J08100010 4705059, describing it as a double-lobed galaxy also detected as an IRAS source and coincident with a radio source.

quotes.quote: The source S7 correlates with the source IRAS 080484 4656, and the double galaxy 2MASX J08100010 4705059, 0.17 x 0.17 arcmin ^2 in size 2MASS 2006AJ....131.1163S . S7 shows a negative spectral index ( 0.9 0.3 ).
The search for counterparts of the radio sources detected in this work resulted in possible associations of S1 and S2 with YSO proplyds, S5 with the system WR 11 and S7 with a double galaxy.

The 2MASS catalog of extended sources XSC in 2006AJ....131.1163S suggests that 2MASX J08100010 4705059 is a double-lobed galaxy, based on the emission at J, K and H _ s bands. The object would also be detected as the source IRAS 08084 4656. They are positionally coincident with the radio source S7. There are many Fermi LAT sources identified with radio galaxies, with gamma-ray fluxes of the order of the one detected by 2015arXiv151003885P .

gmw_00011780.name: PKS 0226-559
gmw_00011780.overview: PKS 0226-559 is a blazar located at a redshift of z=2.464 [arXiv:1501.02765, arXiv:2401.14872]. It is a variable gamma-ray source that exhibits recurring flaring activity [arXiv:1501.02765], with its luminosity exceeding 10^49 erg/s during active periods [arXiv:2401.14872].

sources.summary: The paper links PKS 0226-559 to a newly detected variable gamma-ray region, identifies it as a z=2.464 blazar, and notes its source was established in 3FGL.

quotes.quote: Close inspection of the detected variable regions result in identification of 8 sources without previous known variability. Two of them are included in the second Fermi LAT source catalogue (FBQS J122424.1+243623 and GB6 J0043+3426) and one (3EG J1424+3734) was reported by EGRET and also was included in the First Fermi LAT source catalogue (1FGL), but is missing in the 2FGL. Possible identifications of five other sources are obtained using NED and SIMBAD databases (1RXS J161939.9+765515, PMN J2320-6447, PKS 0226-559, PKS J0030-0211, PMN J0225-2603). These new variable gamma-ray sources demonstrate recurring flaring activity with time scale weeks and have hard spectra. Their spectral energy distributions deviate significantly from a simple power-law shape and often peak around GeV.
One of the five, PKS 0226-559 (pix.11255), is a blazar with a redshift z=2.464 CGRABS .
Sources that were responsible for the variability in the pixels 11255, 11696, 11901 (PKS 0226-559,PKS B0027-024, PMN J0225-2603) are firmly estabisished in the new catalogue.

sources.summary: PKS 0226-559 is the most distant variable source in the sample, with several gamma-ray flaring periods, flux reaching 3.99e-7 photon cm-2 s-1, and luminosity above 1e49 erg/s.

quotes.quote: For example, the adaptively binned \ light curve of PKS 0226-559, the most distant source in our sample at a redshift z=2.464 showing variability, computed for energies above 239.66 MeV, is displayed in panel a) of Fig. strong_gray . From the start of \ observations until MJD 55504 (November 04, 2010), the \ emission of this source was in a low state being (4.89 0.71) 10^ 9 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . Subsequent periods of flaring activity occurred between MJD 55504.30-55584.50, 56153.18-57472.15, and MJD 58084.93-58664.52. During its most active state, between MJD 58084.93 and 58664.52, the maximum flux was (3.99 0.67) 10^ 7 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 observed on MJD 58507.69.

Also, the luminosity of the source significantly increased during these flaring periods. While the long-term averaged luminosity was (1.76 0.04) 10^ 48 erg s ^ 1 , the luminosity during flares exceeded 10^ 49 erg s ^ 1 . For example, the source luminosity was >10^ 49 erg s ^ 1 42 times, with the peak luminosity of (5.33 0.92) 10^ 49 erg s ^ 1 observed on MJD 58167.46.

gmw_00011781.name: PKS 1329-049
gmw_00011781.overview: PKS 1329-049 is a high-redshift blazar active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=2.15 [arXiv:2401.14872]. Although predominantly in its average gamma-ray emission state, it has exhibited elevated emission periods with luminosities exceeding 10^50 erg/s [arXiv:2401.14872].

sources.summary: PKS 1329-049 is usually near its average gamma-ray state but has an elevated state with peak flux 2.71e-6 and extreme-bright luminosities above 1e50 erg/s.

quotes.quote: The \ emission of PKS 1329-049, with a redshift of z=2.15 , is predominantly in its average emission state, as depicted in Fig. strong_gray panel f). However, it exhibited an elevated emission state during the period from MJD 55442.09 to 55507.87 when the peak flux reached (2.71 0.52) 10^ 6 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 observed on MJD 55445.32. In contrast, 4C+01.02, with a redshift of z=2.099 , experiences alternating periods of flaring activity, with the most intense \ flares observed after MJD 57000. The source entered an active \ emission state starting from MJD 59615.91, when the peak \ flux, measured above 171.79 MeV, reached (2.92 0.50) 10^ 6 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 on MJD 59663.19. The other two sources, depicted in panels ) and e) of Fig. strong_gray , also exhibit multiple flaring periods but with only modest increases in their \ flux. Specifically, the peak \ flux of S3 0458-02, measured above 202.55 MeV, was (8.32 1.41) 10^ 7 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 observed on MJD 57258.92. Similarly, for S4 0917+44, it was (4.27 0.75) 10^ 7 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 observed on MJD 57946.70. Especially profound is the luminosity increase in 4C+71.07, PKS 1329-049, and 4C+01.02 (see Fig. strong_gray ). For 4C+71.07, during the flaring periods in MJD 57227-57245.79 and MJD 57296.92-57338.47, the source luminosity exceeded 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 11 times. The highest luminosity of (2.03 0.36) 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 was observed on MJD 57335.21. PKS 1329-049 was in an extreme bright state on MJD 55445.23 and MJD 55468.18, with luminosities of (1.07 0.21) 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 and (1.39 0.26) 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 , respectively.

gmw_00011782.name: GQ 1309 + 2904
gmw_00011782.overview: GQ 1309 + 2904 is a low-ionization broad-absorption line quasar with a systemic redshift of z=2.66 [arXiv:1912.10649]. Its spectrum exhibits a red shape, very broad and deep absorption features, and extreme detached outflow velocities [arXiv:1912.10649].

sources.summary: The paper presents GQ 1309 + 2904 as a peculiar low-ionisation BAL quasar with red spectra, very broad absorption, z=2.66 evidence, and extreme detached outflow velocities.

quotes.quote: The object examined in this paper, designated GQ 1309 + 2904 with equatorial coordinates of the target RA = 13:09:23.91, Dec = + 29:04:51.7 (J 2000.0), was observed as part of a larger spectroscopic campaign of candidate quasars from this survey. We here present spectroscopic observations of the BAL quasar GQ 1309 + 2904, and discuss its systemic redshift and the implied properties of the broad absorption lines.

Line identification In Fig. fig:spectrum our spectra of GQ 1309 + 2904 are shown together with photometry from SDSS, UKIDSS and WISE. The GTC spectrum shows a red shape and the presence of very broad and deep absorption features, the three clearest being centred at around 6180 , 5150 , and 4640 . It is obvious that the object is not a star.

If we are right about the inferred systemic redshift of z=2.66, GQ 1309 + 2904 is hence an extreme example of a detached BAL quasar. For comparison, there are only 2--3 detached BAL quasars with minimum ejection velocities above 20,000 km s ^ 1 in the sample of more than 72 BALs studied by Lee1995 . Benn2005 have also reported a quasar with a C iv BAL detached by more than 20.000 km s ^ 1 . In the spectrum of GQ 1309 + 2904 there is also evidence for a weaker BAL component at a smaller velocity of about 6,000 km s ^ 1 seen both in C iv and Si iv .

gmw_00011783.name: NGC 6469
gmw_00011783.overview: NGC 6469 is a spiral galaxy characterized by a rotation curve that increases almost linearly before transitioning into a long, flat profile [arXiv:1202.2233]. This flat profile contains wiggles that are likely caused by a clumpy distribution of gas within the galaxy [arXiv:1202.2233].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6469 as a rotation-curve case where the observed flat profile is poorly matched and requires unrealistic halo parameters.

quotes.quote: NGC 6469. From an observational point of view, NGC 6469 can be considered a classical example of a galaxy with a rotation curve first increasing almost linearly and then setting in a long flat profile (although with some wiggles likely due to a clumpy gas distribution). On the contrary, the best fit curve poorly matches the rising part and shows a definitely increasing trend which does not follow the trend probed by the data. Moreover, we again find large concentrations and a virial mass, M_ vir = 9.92_ 1.25 ^ +1.02 , smaller than the disc mass.

While the theoretical best fit circular velocity provide a satisfactory agreement with the data for NGC 3621 and NGC 5055 (excluding the very inner points), it traces the data for NGC 2841 and NGC 6469 quite poorly. Moreover, in all cases, there is marked trend indicating a rising rotation curve in the outer regions, whereas an extrapolation of the data suggests a flat profile.

gmw_00011784.overview: The early-type disc galaxy 7443-12701 is located at a redshift of approximately 0.020 [arXiv:1611.00609]. It exhibits post-starburst spectral signatures, with its bulge containing younger, more metal-rich stellar populations than its disc due to more recent star formation events [arXiv:1611.00609].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 7443-12701 as an early-type MaNGA disc galaxy with post-starburst signatures, a younger metal-rich bulge, and recent bulge star formation.

quotes.quote: 7443-12701 has been classified as having a probability of 0.57 and 0.75 of being an early-type galaxy by Huertas-Company_2011 and Lintott_2011 respectively, and the single-fibre SDSS DR-12 data for this galaxy shows features indicative of a post-starburst spectrum. It lies at a redshift of z 0.020 Aihara_2011 , and was observed with a 127-fibre IFU with a field-of-view of 32.5 arcsec across. Figure fig:images shows that the galaxy was offset from the centre of the IFU, the purpose of which was to test the astrometric routines using the nearby star during commissioning.

In 7443-12701, the bulge appears to contain younger and more metal-rich stellar populations than the disc. Since these models only give estimates of the light-weighted ages of each component, these ages shouldn't be taken as a representation of the age of the component, but more as an indication of how long ago the final episode of star formation occurred and thus when the gas was stripped out or used up. In this galaxy, the light-weighted stellar populations suggest that the final episode of star formation occurred in the bulge region.

7443-12701 on the other hand shows more recent and extended star formation in both components. Within the disc, the star formation has slowly truncated since 8 Gyrs ago, whereas two star-formation events can be seen in the bulge at 5 and 1 Gyrs ago.

gmw_00011785.overview: 7443-9102 is an early-type lenticular galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.092 [arXiv:1611.00609]. Its bulge and disc contain stellar populations of similar ages, though the bulge exhibits a higher average metallicity than the disc [arXiv:1611.00609].

sources.summary: The paper presents 7443-9102 as a typical early-type/S0 MaNGA galaxy whose bulge and disc have similar ages but different metallicities.

quotes.quote: The second galaxy, 7443-9102, has been classified as an early-type galaxy with probabilities of 0.90 Huertas-Company_2011 and 0.79 Lintott_2011 . It lies at a redshift of z 0.092 Aihara_2011 , and was observed with the 91-fibre IFU with a field-of-view of 27.5 arcsec. This galaxy is a typical example of an observation of a Primary sample galaxy within MaNGA since it is centered within the IFU.

7443-9102 is a more typical example of a MaNGA observation for a galaxy in the Primary sample, and the same stellar populations analysis are presented in the lower panels of Figure fig:lw_stellar_pops . Even with the smaller field-of-view that cuts off the light from the outskirts of the galaxy, a good fit was achieved. The SSP model results for the radially-binned datacube before decomposition show a larger scatter than 7443-12701, particularly outside of 3 arcsec, which is most likely attributable to the smaller differences in the line strengths for older stellar populations.

As with 7443-12701, this galaxy is also likely to be an S0 galaxy, but the stellar populations show a very different trend. Due to the small differences in the H line strengths in older stellar populations, it can be assumed that the bulge and disc contain stellar populations of approximately similar ages. The decomposed bulge spectrum however shows a higher average metallicity than the disc spectrum.

gmw_00011786.name: UDS-07665
gmw_00011786.overview: UDS-07665 is a high-redshift galaxy characterized by a blue spectral energy distribution, which is consistent with an unreddened, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:2007.12197]. It features strong nebular emission lines that may require active galactic nucleus ionization [arXiv:2007.12197], alongside a triple-peaked Lyman-alpha profile [arXiv:2404.06569].

sources.summary: The paper presents UDS-07665 as a strong C III] and [O III]+H emitter near the AGN boundary, but with a blue SED consistent with young, metal-poor stellar populations.

quotes.quote: However we note that the two strongest C III ] emitters in our sample (EW _ CIII ] =18.7 \ for COSMOS-04432 and EW _ CIII ] =17.8 \ for UDS-07665) are situated very close to the AGN boundary of the Nakajima2018 diagnostic. For both sources, the [ O III ] line is also extremely prominent, with EW _ [ OIII ] +H =1608 \ (COSMOS-04432) and EW _ [ OIII ] +H =1800 \ (UDS-07665). These objects thus have nebular line properties that are very similar to what has been seen at z>6 , but their position in Figure fig:uv_ratio suggests that AGN ionization might be required to explain the line ratios. The blended C III ] doublets of both sources are narrower ( v=253 20 km/s) than the C III ] emission seen in type I AGNs ( v up to 1000 km/s; LeFevre2019 ) but similar to typical type II AGNs.

The data for UDS-07665 are shown in the upper left panel of Figure fig:sed . Its UV slope is blue ( =-2.2 ) and the near-infrared filters show the characteristic flux excess from strong [ O III ] +H emission. Such SEDs are very common at z>6 and are consistent with expectations for an unreddened metal poor galaxy dominated by very young stellar populations.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDS-07665 as a triple-peaked Ly emitter whose central component suggests direct escape, while blue and red peaks trace scattering gas.

quotes.quote: One object presents a more complex Ly profile (UDS-07665) with three peaks (Figure fig:lya_spec1 ), similar to that seen in the Sunburst arc Rivera-Thorsen2017 . The remaining 11 EELGs in our sample show single-peaked Ly lines. We first consider the velocity offset of the Ly peak redshift and the systemic redshift ( v_ peak ).

The Ly spectrum of UDS-07665 ( [ O III ] +H =1800 , f_ cen,Ly =0.38 ) provides an illustrative example. The Ly profile shows three components with a central peak close to the systemic, similar to that seen in some known LyC leakers (and strong Ly emitters) at z=0-4 , including the Sunburst Arc Rivera-Thorsen2017 , J1243+4646 Izotov2018 , and Ion3 Vanzella2018 . The central component in UDS-07665 has a peak velocity of v_ peak =-17 km s ^ 1 and narrow line width (FWHM =74 km s ^ 1 ). The central Ly component line width is comparable to that of the H emission line of UDS-07665 (FWHM =88 km s ^ 1 after subtracting the intrument resolution in quadrature) and also those of non-resonant rest-frame optical emission lines in similarly-selected galaxies e.g., Maseda2014,Tang2022 , as would be expected if it arises via direct escape without scattering.

The blue peak ( v=-405 km s ^ 1 ) and red peak ( v=+177 km s ^ 1 ) velocities are consistent with expectations for scattering through (and backscattering off of) dense outflowing neutral gas. Notably this implies that the separation between the red and blue peaks is large ( S_ peak =582 km s ^ 1 ), in spite of the presence of gas conditions that permit significant Ly transmission at line center.

gmw_00011787.name: UDS-11222
gmw_00011787.overview: UDS-11222 is a very low-mass galaxy at redshift z ~ 2-3 with a stellar mass of approximately 30 million solar masses [arXiv:2404.06569]. It exhibits a high specific star formation rate of 139 per gigayear, indicating that the galaxy is experiencing an active burst of star formation [arXiv:2404.06569].

sources.summary: The paper highlights UDS-11222 as a very low-mass, high-sSFR galaxy with unusually large Ly central flux for its optical-line equivalent width range.

quotes.quote: UDS-11222 ( [ O III ] +H EW =712 , Ly EW =48 ) has a Ly profile with f_ cen,Ly =0.39 , more than twice what is typical in this [ O III ] +H EW range. UDS-11222 stands out as having the lowest stellar mass ( 3 10^7 \ M_ for CSFH) in the entire z 2-3 sample presented in this paper. SED fitting results also show that UDS-11222 has the largest sSFR ( =139 Gyr ^ 1 ) among those with [ O III ] +H EW =400-1200 , suggesting it is experiencing a burst of star formation that is more typical of galaxies with [ O III ] +H EW >1200 .

gmw_00011788.name: UDS-08078
gmw_00011788.overview: UDS-08078 is a luminous emission line galaxy with an absolute magnitude of -21.7 [arXiv:2404.06569]. It exhibits a highly-redshifted Lyman-alpha profile where the emission escapes with a large peak velocity offset of 593 km/s [arXiv:2404.06569].

sources.summary: The paper notes UDS-08078 as a luminous EELG whose Ly emission escapes at unusually high velocity despite very large optical-line equivalent width.

quotes.quote: We find one source in this subset of our sample with a highly-redshifted Ly profile (UDS-08078), similar to the z 7 galaxies. In spite of its very large [ O III ] +H EW ( 1321 ), the Ly escapes with a large peak velocity offset of 593 km s ^ 1 and a wide FWHM of 414 km s ^ 1 , resulting in significant line flux at large velocities. The z 7 galaxies with large velocity offsets tend to be very luminous. UDS-08078 is similar, with an absolute magnitude (M _ UV =-21.7 ) that is significantly brighter than the median value in our sample.

gmw_00011789.name: UDS-27040
gmw_00011789.overview: UDS-27040 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy that exhibits a single central peak in its spectrum [arXiv:2404.06569]. This spectral profile suggests the galaxy may have young stars covered by a low-density, optically thick layer of neutral hydrogen [arXiv:2404.06569].

sources.summary: The paper describes UDS-27040 as a central-peaked Ly source whose unresolved or low-S/N profile still implies partial low-density neutral-hydrogen coverage.

quotes.quote: In one case (UDS-27040), we do not see clearly-defined and robustly-detected blue peak. It is possible that the blue and red peaks are unresolved in this system, leading to the appearance of a single central peak. This may be expected if young stars are uniformly covered by slowly-outflowing neutral hydrogen that has low column density yet is optically thick to Ly e.g., Verhamme2015 . It is also possible that the S/N of this system is too low to recover the blue peak. In either case, this spectrum still imply at least partial coverage by low column densities of neutral hydrogen.

gmw_00011790.name: GRB 980329 host
gmw_00011790.overview: The GRB 980329 host is a high-redshift starburst galaxy with an estimated photometric redshift of approximately 3.6 [arXiv:astro-ph/0204278]. It is a faint, multi-component system featuring several unresolved blue knots on top of a low surface brightness area [arXiv:astro-ph/0204278].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 980329 host as a faint, multi-component galaxy complex with blue knots and estimates its photometric redshift at about z 3.6.

quotes.quote: In the CL image several unresolved knots are seen on top of a low surface brightness area within an aperture of 0 5. The measured ABMAG in the CL-band within this aperture is 27.5 0.2 . In the LP image an extended object is seen, but the knots seen in the CL-band are not detected.

Using the Bayesian photometric redshift (BPZ) estimation software of and restricting z >1 due to absence of expected emission lines in spectra of the host, see Yost2002.1 we find z 3.6 with the best fitting SED corresponding to an Im galaxy type. Redshifts of z <1.2 and z >4.2 are excluded at the 95 confidence level (z >5 excluded at 99.99 level).

gmw_00011791.name: GRB 980519 host
gmw_00011791.overview: The GRB 980519 host galaxy consists of two very faint, extended objects that are likely in the process of merging [arXiv:astro-ph/0204278]. The optical afterglow of the burst is located in the outer regions of the northern component of this merging system [arXiv:astro-ph/0204278].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 980519 host as two very faint extended objects, likely merging galaxies, with the afterglow located at the outskirts of the northern component.

quotes.quote: At the OA position we detect two extended objects, clearly visible in Fig. fig:hosts2 . The photometry yields CL ABMAGs of 27.1 0.1 and 27.9 0.1 for the southern and northern component, respectively. The OA is located in the very outskirts of the northern component (S/N 9), where a faint blob coinciding with the position of the OA is detected (see also Table tab:results ). A host detection was reported by and at an estimated Cousins R VEGAMAG of 26.1 0.3 . This detection, however, consisted of the smeared sum of both objects.
