sources.summary: The paper describes 2215+020 as a z=3.572 quasar with an unusually extended pc-scale jet, sparse but comparable 5 GHz structure, and a bright region where the jet turns east to northeast.

quotes.quote: 2215+020 drinkwater1997 found an emission redshift of 3.572 for this quasar. lobanov2001 present a 1.6 GHz VSOP space-VLBI image of this source showing the jet extending to almost 80 mas ( 600 pc) with a particularly bright section between 45 and 60 mas. The extent of this jet is 4 times greater than in any other pc-scale jet observed for quasars with z>3 . Our 5 GHz image has similar resolution to the lobanov2001 image but is less sensitive to the extended jet emission hence, our image has a similar but sparser intensity distribution. We also detect the particularly bright region, where the jet changes direction from East to North-East on the plane of the sky.

gmw_00011842.name: dE1256
gmw_00011842.overview: dE1256 is a rare early-type dwarf galaxy located in a nearly isolated environment at the outskirts of the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2212.03430]. It features an outer tidal tail likely formed by the accretion of a smaller dwarf galaxy, which has doubled the host's overall size [arXiv:2212.03430].

sources.summary: The paper identifies dE1256 as a rare early-type dwarf with an outer tidal tail from accretion, a compact inner Sersic component, and size growth from a minor merger.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a rare early-type dwarf galaxy (dE), SDSS J125651.47+163024.2 (hereafter dE1256), possessing a tidal feature that was likely built up by accretion of an even smaller dwarf galaxy. dE1256 is located in a nearly isolated environment, at the outskirt of the Virgo cluster.

A detailed morphological examination reveals that the accreted stellar population is mainly deposited in the outer part of dE1256, where the tidal tail is most prominent. The inner part of dE1256 is perfectly modeled with a simple S ersic function of index n = 0.63 and half-light radius R _ h = 0.6 kpc, but in contrast, the entire galaxy has a size of R _ h = 1.2 kpc. The mass ratio between the host and the putative accreted dwarf galaxy is calculated to be 5:1, assuming that the observed two components, inner S ersic and outer tidal tail residual, represent the host's and accreted galaxy's stellar populations, respectively. We suggest that while the accretion contributes only 20 of the overall stellar population, the size of dE1256 grew by a factor of two via the accretion event.

gmw_00011843.name: Mrk 507
gmw_00011843.overview: Mrk 507 is classified as a narrow-line object [arXiv:astro-ph/0107528]. It is identified as a narrow-line outlier due to its large [OIII] spectral width [arXiv:astro-ph/0107528].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 507 as a narrow-line outlier with a large [OIII] width; it says HII subtraction and spectral resolution can make the NLR width significantly overestimated, so it excludes the object.

quotes.quote: It is clearly shown in Figures 1 and 2 that five NL objects, Mrk 507, Mrk 684, IRAS 15462-0450, MS 22102+1827, and PG 1404+226, having large [ OIII ] widths, deviate from other NL objects. Note that Mrk 507, Mrk 684, IRAS 15462-0450 are the three objects for which a narrow HII region contribution has been subtracted in the VVG sample. For spectra with a resolution of 3.4 used by VVG, the HII component may not be reliably separated from a narrow component of NLR with width 200---500 km s ^ 1 if an additional broad wing is present. Thus the widths of NLR in these three objects are likely to be significantly over-estimated.

gmw_00011844.name: MS 22102+1827
gmw_00011844.overview: MS 22102+1827 is classified as a narrow-line object [arXiv:astro-ph/0107528]. It is identified as an outlier in its class due to its large [OIII] emission line width [arXiv:astro-ph/0107528].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MS 22102+1827 as a narrow-line outlier with a large [OIII] width, notes poor-quality [OIII] profiles, and excludes it from the subsequent statistical analysis.

quotes.quote: It is clearly shown in Figures 1 and 2 that five NL objects, Mrk 507, Mrk 684, IRAS 15462-0450, MS 22102+1827, and PG 1404+226, having large [ OIII ] widths, deviate from other NL objects.

Only poor quality [ OIII ] profiles are available for MS 22102+1827 and PG 1404+226, and VVG mentioned that broad blueshifted [ OIII ] profiles should not be overlooked in PG 1404+226. These five objects will be excluded in the following statistic analysis.

gmw_00011845.name: RM 363
gmw_00011845.overview: RM 363 is a highly luminous quasar located at a redshift of z=2.635 [arXiv:1908.00027]. It has a bolometric luminosity of 10^47 erg/s and exhibits a multi-year observed-frame lag exceeding 1000 days despite its low typical variability amplitude [arXiv:1908.00027].

sources.summary: The paper presents RM 363 as a very luminous z=2.635 SDSS-RM quasar whose extended monitoring reveals an observed-frame lag exceeding 1000 days despite weak variability.

quotes.quote: Fig. \ fig:rm363 (top) presents such an example: we measure a \ lag in RM 363, one of the most luminous quasars in the SDSS-RM sample, having a bolometric luminosity of 10^ 47 erg s^ 1 at z=2.635 . The measured \ lag is more than 1000 days in the observed frame due to the 1+z time dilation. This example demonstrates the necessity of long-term monitoring for the detection of multi-year lags. In addition, the typical variability amplitude of this high-redshift quasar is only 10 (see Fig. \ fig:lc ), indicating that high S/N spectroscopy is required to detect the line variability.

gmw_00011846.name: RM 372
gmw_00011846.overview: RM 372 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=1.745 [arXiv:1908.00027]. Its light curves exhibit smooth variations over multi-year timescales, revealing an observed-frame lag of approximately 200 days [arXiv:1908.00027].

sources.summary: The paper presents RM 372 as a z=1.745 SDSS-RM quasar whose extended spectroscopy and PS1 monitoring recover a roughly 200-day observed-frame lag missed by shorter data.

quotes.quote: Fig. \ fig:rm363 (bottom) presents our RM analysis for RM 372 at z=1.745 . This source was analyzed in Paper I with the 4-year light curves, and a \ lag was not robustly detected due to insufficient light curve quality. With the addition of the 5 ^ th year of spectroscopy and the 4 years of PS1 monitoring, we were able to recover an observed-frame lag of 200 days. The \ line light curve is shifted by about 6 months from the continuum light curve, resulting in little overlap in the two light curves. The light curves have smooth variations over multi-year timescales, and the correlations between the continuum and line light curves are strongly suppressed on timescales longer and shorter than 6 months, allowing a time lag of about the length of the seasonal gap to be robustly detected.

gmw_00011847.name: RM 651
gmw_00011847.overview: RM 651 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=1.486 [arXiv:1908.00027]. It exhibits detected lags in three broad emission lines, although its broad-line region stratification remains unconfirmed due to large lag uncertainties [arXiv:1908.00027].

sources.summary: The paper presents RM 651 as a z=1.486 SDSS-RM quasar with detected lags in three broad lines, possible but unconfirmed BLR stratification, and weak variability in one line.

quotes.quote: Fig. \ fig:rm651 presents the analysis for RM 651 at z=1.486 , where lags are successfully detected for three different lines: , \ and . For , PrepSpec measures the line flux from the complex of , \ and , where \ dominates the flux. For this particular object, there is some evidence from the JAVELIN analysis that the \ lag < \ lag < \ lag, which is suggestive of BLR stratification.

However, the lag uncertainties are large, and the line widths measured from the rms spectrum do not differ significantly for these three lines (see Table tab:summary ). Therefore we cannot confirm BLR stratification for this example. The \ variability amplitude in this object is substantially lower than that of the continuum and the other two lines (see Fig.  fig:lc ).

gmw_00011848.name: ESO 381-G009
gmw_00011848.overview: ESO 381-G009 is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy that belongs to the tight triple system AM 1238-362 [arXiv:astro-ph/0411405]. Due to gravitational interactions within the system, the galaxy exhibits enhanced star formation activity that is concentrated on the side facing its companion [arXiv:astro-ph/0411405].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 381-G009 as a face-on spiral in AM 1238-362 with enhanced interaction-related star formation and an HII-region luminosity function sensitive to fitted range.

quotes.quote: AM 1238-362 is a fairly tight triple system composed by two nearly face-on (ESO 381-G009 and Tol 1238-36.4) and an edge-on (ESO 381-G006) spiral galaxies (Fig. AM_a ). A detailed study of the morphological and spectrophotometric properties of the two face-on galaxies has been presented in Temporin et al. (2003a), where the interaction signatures and star formation properties have been discussed in the framework of the evolutionary history of the system. The two galaxies exhibit enhanced star formation activity, and the radial and azimuthal distribution of their H emission show a tendency for both disks to concentrate the star formation activity on the side facing the companion (see Temporin et al. 2003a, Fig. 20).

For ESO 381-G009, the slope of the LF strongly depends on the choice of the fitted luminosity range. The steeper slope ( = 2.17 0.34) shown in Fig. AM_b , obtained for regions with (L _ H ) > 39.5, is in good agreement with the slope of the size distribution.

gmw_00011849.name: UGC 06324
gmw_00011849.overview: UGC 06324 is a dwarf lenticular galaxy located at the outskirts of the U376 group [arXiv:1401.4003]. Although it shows no obvious signatures of interaction in ultraviolet or optical images, its properties are well matched by simulations of a galaxy encounter [arXiv:1401.4003].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 06324 as a dwarf S0 on U376’s outskirts, best reproduced by an encounter simulation matching its SED, morphology, magnitude, age, and gas history.

quotes.quote: UGC 06324.       The global properties of this dwarf S0, at the outskirts of the U376 group (Figure dyn_a ), are well matched by a simulation of an encounter between two triaxial collapsing systems of equal mass and perpendicular spins. Its SED, from FUV to the K band, and morphology are shown in Figures figure1 and figure3 , respectively. Ant13 find no obvious signature of interaction in the FUV, NUV, or in SDSS band images of this galaxy. The B band absolute magnitude (Table table3 ) agrees well with the measured value in Table  table1 . The total mass involved is 2 10 ^ 11 M_ (Table table2 ), but that of the galaxy is about 10 times less (Table table3 ).

gmw_00011850.name: SDSS J101028.69+053212.5
gmw_00011850.overview: SDSS J101028.69+053212.5 is a spiral, star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1502.04949]. Located at a luminosity distance of approximately 247 Mpc [arXiv:1502.04949], it is the host galaxy of the supernova LSQ12btw [arXiv:1502.04949].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J101028.69+053212.5 as LSQ12btw’s spiral star-forming host, gives its redshift-derived distance, and notes no host-galaxy absorption in the supernova spectra.

quotes.quote: The former object was discovered on 2012 April 9.04 (UT dates are used throughout this paper), close to the nucleus of the spiral, star-forming galaxy SDSS J101028.69+053212.5 (see Fig. fig1 ). SDSS DR3 reports a redshift z = 0.05764 0.00006 . The spectroscopic data were obtained on 2004 September 28; http://www.sdss.org/dr3/products/spectra/getspectra.html. Adopting a Hubble constant H _0 = 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ( _ M = 0.27 and _ = 0.73), we infer a luminosity distance of about 247 16 Mpc ( = 36.97 0.15 mag). No sign of interstellar absorption within the host galaxy is visible in the SN spectra (see Sec. s_sp ), so we consider only the Galactic extinction component for LSQ12btw, A_g = 0.075 mag sch11 .

gmw_00011851.name: 2MASX J21355077-1832599
gmw_00011851.overview: 2MASX J21355077-1832599, also known as PGC 866755, is an emission-line galaxy [arXiv:1502.04949]. It has a measured redshift of z = 0.0603 and hosted the supernova LSQ13ccw [arXiv:1502.04949].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J21355077-1832599, also PGC 866755, as LSQ13ccw’s host galaxy and derives its redshift, luminosity distance, and line-of-sight absorption treatment.

quotes.quote: LSQ13ccw was discovered on 2013 September 4.03 near the galaxy 2MASX J21355077-1832599 (also known as PGC 866755; Fig. fig1 ). The redshift of the host galaxy was measured through the SN features and the detection of weak and narrow lines of [ O III ] 4959, 5007 from the galaxy. These give z = 0.0603 0.0002 and a luminosity distance of 259 17 Mpc ( = 37.07 0.14 mag). Since there is no clear spectroscopic evidence (see Sec. s_sp ) of host-galaxy absorption, we again adopt only the Milky Way component ( A_g = 0.163 mag) as total line-of-sight absorption to LSQ13ccw.

gmw_00011852.name: ESO379-G007
gmw_00011852.overview: ESO379-G007 is an isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located in the Centaurus A group [arXiv:0811.0329]. It features an off-center, slightly extended star-forming region and young main sequence stars situated west of its optical center [arXiv:0811.0329].

sources.summary: The paper reports off-center extended emission and young stars in ESO379-G007, discusses possible ram pressure, and notes that its isolation makes that explanation unlikely.

quotes.quote: ESO379-G007 An region in this dIrr galaxy is situated 10 (or 250 pc projected distance at D = 5.2 Mpc) west of the optical center and is slightly extended. Concordantly, the colour-magnitude diagram (CMD) for this object also reveals some young main sequence stars karachentsev2002a . The arc-shape of the emission and its position with respect to the optical center may suggest that ram pressure is operating. This however seems unlikely as this galaxy is one of the most isolated objects of the CenA group, situated at 1.3 0.1 Mpc (3D distance) from NGC5236 ( 2 0.8 Mpc from NGC5102).

gmw_00011853.name: ESO321-G014
gmw_00011853.overview: ESO321-G014 is an IABm dwarf galaxy located in a fairly isolated region of the Centaurus A group [arXiv:0811.0329]. It features an off-center point source, diffuse minor-axis emission, and stellar populations ranging from young main sequence stars to intermediate-age stars [arXiv:0811.0329].

sources.summary: The paper finds an off-center emission point source, diffuse minor-axis emission, young and intermediate-age stellar populations, and a fairly isolated Centaurus A group location.

quotes.quote: ESO321-G014 This IABm galaxy has an point source that is displaced from the center of the galaxy by about 15 or 230 pc projected distance, at D = 3.2 Mpc. It is accompanied by low level diffuse emission that extends along the minor axis of the galaxy. The CMD shows young main sequence and some upper-AGB stars --- signature of the presence of an intermediate age population, 1 Gyr and older --- are found karachentsev2002a . This galaxy is a fairly isolated object of the CenA group, situated at 640 150 kpc (3D) from NGC5102.

gmw_00011854.name: ESO381-G020
gmw_00011854.overview: ESO381-G020 is a relatively isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located in the outskirts of the CenA/M83 group [arXiv:1103.3707, arXiv:0811.0329]. It contains a high content of neutral gas and is currently experiencing a global starburst that began between 10 and 500 million years ago [arXiv:1103.3707].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO381-20 as an isolated outskirts dwarf with rich HI, 20000 CMD stars, active star-forming regions, a globular cluster, and a recent global starburst.

quotes.quote: The irregular galaxy ESO381-20 is located in the outskirts of the CenA/M83 group and is rather isolated (its tidal index is negative). Its distance as found by is 5.44 0.37 Mpc. Its deprojected distance from the closest massive neighbour (M83) is 1.1 0.1 Mpc. Its CMD (Fig. cmds ) contains 20000 stars and shows an old RGB, an intermediate-age luminous AGB and young MS, BL and RSG stars. This galaxy has a very high content of neutral gas ( M_ HI 2 10^ 8 M _ , see Tab. infogen ), which extends much further than the apparent optical galaxy.

ESO381-20 seems to have experienced a relatively high continuous star formation during its lifetime (with an average of 0.007 0.0048 M _ yr ^ 1 ), which then increased substantially from 10 to 500 Myrs ago. This increased star formation activity was more than twice the average rate (see also the parameter b_ 500 in Tab. ressfh ). Following the recent discussion by mcquinn09 , we may definitely say that this is a period of a global starburst for the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO381-G020 as a relatively isolated dwarf containing upper-AGB and young main-sequence stars, with several point sources and diffuse emission.

quotes.quote: ESO381-G020 This dwarf clearly contains both upper-AGB and young main sequence stars karachentsev2007 . It has several point sources and a considerable amount of diffuse emission. As for the two preceding galaxies, it is a relatively isolated object at 780 200 kpc (3D) from NGC5236 ( 2.1 0.8 Mpc from NGC5102).

gmw_00011855.name: ESO269-G037
gmw_00011855.overview: ESO269-G037 is classified as a dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:0811.0329]. It contains a low-mass neutral hydrogen cloud extending southeast of its optical center, which may be undergoing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:0811.0329].

sources.summary: The paper discusses ESO269-G037 as a dSph-classified dwarf with possible contamination, low H I in an offset cloud, possible ram-pressure stripping, and tentative central emission.

quotes.quote: ESO269-G037 Because of stellar crowding in the field of this galaxy it is hard to tell if the CMD shows young main sequence and upper-AGB stars or if these are the results of contamination. karachentsev2002a classifies this galaxy as a dSph. This object was detected in and contains M_ HI =4 10^5 in a cloud extending south-east of the optical centre. It is the only object of the CenA group where an mass below 10 ^7 was detected and it is believed to be presently losing its through ram pressure stripping bouchard2007 .

We have found some emission coinciding with the optical centre of the dwarf, which is also a high density region. There is also emission in two regions to the West and South-West of the optical center, surrounding foreground stars. After close inspection of the image, it seems that the different seeing conditions for the on-band and the off-band images have created artifacts around some foreground stars. The emission structure of the West and South-West features are indeed reminiscent of subtraction difficulties.

gmw_00011856.name: DDO6
gmw_00011856.overview: DDO6 is a drop-shaped dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0302045] classified as an IB(s)m system [arXiv:0811.0329]. It features mixed stellar populations, including a red giant branch [arXiv:astro-ph/0302045], young blue main-sequence stars [arXiv:0811.0329], and diffuse emission to its southwest [arXiv:0811.0329].

sources.summary: The paper reports diffuse emission southwest of DDO6, a measured H I mass, a CMD rich in upper-AGB and young main-sequence stars, and its NGC253 distance.

quotes.quote: DDO6 There is evidence for diffuse emission south-west of the center of this IB(s)m galaxy and cote1997 have found M_ HI = 1.2 10^7 . The CMD shows a large population of upper-AGB and young blue main sequence stars karachentsev2003 . This object is 660 300 kpc (3D) from NGC253.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 6 as a drop-shaped dIrr galaxy with mixed stellar populations, a prominent RGB population, and a TRGB distance of 3.34 Mpc.

quotes.quote: This dIrr galaxy of drop-like shape has a radial velocity of V_ LG = 348 km s ^ 1 . In Fig.2 the CMD shows the presence of mixed blue and red populations, in particular, a prominent population of RGB stars. We determined I(TRGB) to be 23 62 0 16 , yielding D = 3.34 0.24 Mpc.

gmw_00011857.name: AM0106-382
gmw_00011857.overview: AM0106-382 is an isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located in the Sculptor group [arXiv:0811.0329, arXiv:astro-ph/0301492]. The galaxy exhibits strong emission concentrated around four point sources and has a faint neutral hydrogen feature detected by the HIPASS survey [arXiv:0811.0329].

sources.summary: The paper describes AM0106-382 as isolated, with strong emission around four point sources, a prior H I non-detection limit, and a faint HIPASS H I feature.

quotes.quote: AM0106-382 This isolated galaxy is 2.4 1 Mpc (3D) from NGC7793. It has strong emission concentrated around four point sources. This dIm galaxy was not detected by cote1997 who placed an upper-limit of M_ HI < 1.7 10^7 (corrected to a distance of 6.1 Mpc). However, the HIPASS survey barnes2001 reveals a faint feature at V_ = 625 corresponding to M_ HI = 3 10^7 (if at 6.1 Mpc) We use M_ HI = 2.356 10^6 D^2 S_ HI .f., bouchard2005 .

sources.summary: The paper notes little prior literature for AM0106 382, gives adopted oxygen and nitrogen-to-oxygen abundances, and says these are its first published values.

quotes.quote: AM0106 382 Aside from the properties listed in Table table_gxylist , and the inclusion of this galaxy in the H I catalog by , there is little in the literature describing further this dwarf irregular in the Sculptor group. The adopted oxygen abundance is 12 + log(O/H) = 7.60 and the nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio is log(N/O) = 1.42 . These values are the first ever published for this galaxy.

gmw_00011858.name: IC 194
gmw_00011858.overview: IC 194 is a spiral galaxy viewed almost exactly edge-on [arXiv:1607.02274]. It features a weak optical disc warping with a barely detectable S-shape at its disc tips, and while it lacks nearby bright companions, a loose galaxy group is located 170 kpc away [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 194 as an almost exactly edge-on galaxy with weak optical disc warping and no nearby bright companions, though a loose group lies nearby.

quotes.quote: IC 194 (UGC 1542, RFGC 439, PGC 7812) is seen almost exactly edge-on. Optical disc warping is not strong: edge-on disc tips have a barely detectable S shape. This galaxy has no nearby bright companions, but a loose group of galaxies PPS2 136 with the close redshift is located at a projected distance of 170 kpc from IC 194 ( tb1998 ).

gmw_00011859.name: 2MFGC 6306
gmw_00011859.overview: 2MFGC 6306 is an edge-on spiral galaxy classified within the Galaxy Zoo project [arXiv:1607.02274]. Its stellar disc is asymmetric and warped, and the galaxy likely belongs to a group with three other close-redshift neighbors [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MFGC 6306 as an edge-on GalaxyZoo-classified system whose stellar disc is asymmetric and warped, probably sharing a group with three close-redshift galaxies.

quotes.quote: 2MFGC 6306 is classified within the framework of the GalaxyZoo project 2008MNRAS.389.1179L, 2011MNRAS.410..166L , with the weighted fraction of votes out of all responses that this galaxy is edge-on P=0.89 . Its stellar disc looks asymmetric and warped. 2MFGC-6306 probably forms a group with three other galaxies with close redshifts -- SDSS J075644.97+440536.7, CGCG 207-007, and 2MASSX J07563889+4407408.

gmw_00011860.name: SPRC-192
gmw_00011860.overview: SPRC-192 is an early-type spiral galaxy classified as related to polar-ring galaxies [arXiv:1607.02274]. It features a dust lane along its disc major axis surrounded by an inclined ring-like structure, and its morphology suggests a past accretion event or external perturbation [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper presents SPRC-192 as a polar-ring-related early-type spiral with an inclined ring-like structure, possible strong-warp appearance at higher inclination, and signs of past perturbation.

quotes.quote: SPRC-192 is classified as a related to polar-ring galaxies (PRGs) object 2011MNRAS.418..244M . It is an early-type spiral with the visible dust lane along the disc major axis surrounded by an inclined ring-like structure. Morphologically, it is similar to the well-known polar-ring galaxy NGC 660. Obviously, if this galaxy had a higher inclination, it would look like a strongly warped spiral. The galaxy has no nearby companions of comparable sizes, but its morphology may point to a profound external perturbation or an accretion event in the past.

gmw_00011861.name: UGC 4591
gmw_00011861.overview: UGC 4591 is an edge-on spiral galaxy that belongs to the WBL 193 galaxy group [arXiv:1607.02274]. The galaxy lacks a well-defined bulge and exhibits disk bending that begins at its half-radius [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 4591 as an edge-on spiral in WBL 193, says its disc bending starts at half-radius, and later notes no well-defined bulge.

quotes.quote: UGC 4591 (RFGC 1430, PGC 24674) is an edge-on spiral galaxy, a member of the group of three galaxies WBL 193 1999AJ....118.2014W (the brightest of them is the spiral galaxy IC 2394). The apparent disc bending starts at the half-radius of the galaxy.

Five galaxies (UGC 4591, MCG +06-22-041, UGC 5791, SDSS J140639.64+272242.4, SDSS J153538.63+464229.5) do not exhibit the presence of a well-defined bulge, and thus they were fitted to the single disc model.

gmw_00011862.name: MCG +06-22-041
gmw_00011862.overview: MCG +06-22-041 is a thin, late-type edge-on spiral galaxy that lacks a bulge [arXiv:1607.02274]. It may reside in a galaxy group, as suggested by the similar redshifts of nearby objects [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG +06-22-041 as a thin late-type edge-on spiral without a bulge, with nearby same-redshift objects suggesting group membership.

quotes.quote: MCG +06-22-041 (RFGC 1674, PGC 28776) at z=0.027 is a thin late-type spiral galaxy without any presence of a bulge. It was classified in the GalaxyZoo as an edge-on galaxy ( P=1.0 ). Similar redshifts of nearby objects (elliptical galaxy CGCG 182-048 and 2MASSX J09574267+3603307, both at z=0.027 ) suggest that MCG +06-22-041 may be in a group.

gmw_00011863.name: NGC 3160
gmw_00011863.overview: NGC 3160 is an edge-on spiral galaxy that belongs to the NRGb 78 galaxy cluster [arXiv:1607.02274]. The galaxy exhibits structural features including a strongly warped disc and a central X-shaped structure [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper calls NGC 3160 one of the most interesting sample objects, noting edge-on classification, strong disc warping, a central X-shaped structure, and cluster membership.

quotes.quote: NGC 3160 (UGC 5513, PGC 29830) is one of the most interesting objects in our sample. It was classified in the GalaxyZoo as an edge-on galaxy with P=0.838 . NGC 3160 shows several prominent features such as strong disc warping and a contrast X-shaped structure in the central part of the galaxy. NGC 3160 is a member of the cluster of galaxies NRGb 78, with the brightest elliptical galaxy NGC 3158 at the centre.

gmw_00011864.name: UGC 5791
gmw_00011864.overview: UGC 5791 is a nearby blue peculiar galaxy classified as a polar-ring-related object [arXiv:1607.02274]. It is a member of a galaxy pair with UGC 5798 and has a barely measurable inclination angle due to its peculiar shape [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 5791 as a nearby blue peculiar PRG-related galaxy with barely measurable inclination, pair membership, and no well-defined bulge in decomposition.

quotes.quote: UGC 5791 (SPRC-197, PGC 31697) is a blue peculiar galaxy for which S ^4 G ( s4g ) analysis has been done. It is the nearest galaxy in the sample. The inclination angle for this galaxy is barely measurable because of its peculiar shape. It is a pair member, together with the galaxy UGC 5798 1979ApJS...40..527P . UGC 5791 is classified as a PRG-related object 2011MNRAS.418..244M .

gmw_00011865.name: UGC 6882
gmw_00011865.overview: UGC 6882 is a flat galaxy related to polar ring galaxies [arXiv:1607.02274]. It is oriented nearly edge-on, though modeling suggests it is not purely edge-on and possesses an exponential non-edge-on disc [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 6882 as a PRG-related, nearly edge-on flat-galaxy-catalog object, then models it with a non-edge-on disc and flags possible false-warps.

quotes.quote: UGC 6882 (SPRC-204, PGC 37372) is another galaxy related to PRGs 2011MNRAS.418..244M , which is probably close to edge-on orientation according to the classification from the GalaxyZoo ( P=0.575 ). It also belongs to the 2MASS selected Flat Galaxy Catalog 2MFGC, 2004BSAO...57....5M . Some small nearby galaxies are seen in the SDSS image, although they do not have optical spectra, and, thus, estimated redshifts.

The galaxy UGC 6882 looks to be not exactly edge-on, therefore for this galaxy we used an exponential non-edge-on disc model ( eqExpDiskMu ) with the apparent disc flattening of 0.15 . However, we fitted the minor axis surface brightness distribution of this galaxy with the edge-on disc function and estimated its scale height to be 2.8 , or 1.87 kpc. Since this galaxy is not purely edge-on, we can assume that its vertical scale is somewhat overestimated, and the found value should be used as its upper limit.

gmw_00011866.name: SDSS J140639.64+272242.4
gmw_00011866.overview: SDSS J140639.64+272242.4 is a distant spiral galaxy with a small angular size [arXiv:1607.02274]. The galaxy lacks a well-defined bulge and has several nearby companion galaxies in projection [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J140639.64+272242.4 as a distant, small-angular-size sample galaxy with projected companions and no measured redshifts, lacking a well-defined bulge.

quotes.quote: SDSS J140639.64+272242.4 and SDSS J153538.63+464229.5 are the two most distant and small in angular size galaxies in our sample. Both galaxies have several nearby companions in projection, but, unfortunately, they do not have measured redshifts.

Five galaxies (UGC 4591, MCG +06-22-041, UGC 5791, SDSS J140639.64+272242.4, SDSS J153538.63+464229.5) do not exhibit the presence of a well-defined bulge, and thus they were fitted to the single disc model.

gmw_00011867.name: SDSS J153538.63+464229.5
gmw_00011867.overview: SDSS J153538.63+464229.5 is a distant spiral galaxy with a small angular size [arXiv:1607.02274]. It lacks a well-defined bulge, possesses several projected nearby companions, and exhibits a possible global warp that may be explained by inclined spiral arms [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J153538.63+464229.5 as a distant, small-angular-size sample galaxy with projected companions, no well-defined bulge, and a possible spiral-arm false warp.

quotes.quote: SDSS J140639.64+272242.4 and SDSS J153538.63+464229.5 are the two most distant and small in angular size galaxies in our sample. Both galaxies have several nearby companions in projection, but, unfortunately, they do not have measured redshifts.

Five galaxies (UGC 4591, MCG +06-22-041, UGC 5791, SDSS J140639.64+272242.4, SDSS J153538.63+464229.5) do not exhibit the presence of a well-defined bulge, and thus they were fitted to the single disc model.
global warp can be explained as inclined spiral arms (these galaxies are UGC 6882 and SDSS J153538.63+464229.5).

gmw_00011868.name: UGC 10716
gmw_00011868.overview: UGC 10716 is a late-type spiral galaxy viewed from an almost edge-on perspective [arXiv:1607.02274]. It forms a galaxy triplet with its neighbors UGC 10714 and SDSS J170726.32+301316.6 [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 10716 as an almost edge-on late-type spiral selected in edge-on catalogs and forming a triplet with two neighboring galaxies.

quotes.quote: UGC 10716 (RFGC 3242, PGC 59657) is a late-type spiral galaxy viewed almost edge-on ( P=0.933 ). It was selected in the RFGC catalogue and in the catalogue of edge-on galaxies created by . The galaxy forms a triplet with UGC 10714 and SDSS J170726.32+301316.6 ( ber2006 ).

gmw_00011869.name: UGC 12253
gmw_00011869.overview: UGC 12253 is a galaxy viewed in an almost exactly edge-on orientation [arXiv:1607.02274]. A sharp dust lane divides its main body, and an X-pattern at its center suggests a boxy or peanut-shaped bulge [arXiv:1607.02274].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 12253 as nearly exactly edge-on, with a sharp dust lane, possible boxy/peanut bulge, and likely triplet or group environment.

quotes.quote: UGC 12253 (RFGC 4028, PGC 70040) is seen in almost exactly edge-on orientation. The sharp dust lane divides the main body of UGC 12253 almost ideally in two halves. The X-pattern at the centre suggests that this galaxy has a boxy/peanut-shaped bulge, a possible evidence of the presence of a bar 2006MNRAS.370..753B . There are two nearby galaxies with close redshifts (PGC 070044 and SDSS J225556.61+124701.9) and, therefore, UGC 12253 can be a member of a triplet or a group of galaxies.

gmw_00011870.name: Source 1
gmw_00011870.overview: Source 1 is a distant or highly obscured radio galaxy with a complex radio morphology [arXiv:1807.09240]. It features a bright central core with no detected polarization, alongside two strongly polarized northern regions [arXiv:1807.09240].

sources.summary: Source 1 has complex radio morphology, two polarized northern regions near 20.5 rad m^-2, no detected polarization from the bright central core, and no clear SDSS counterpart.

quotes.quote: Source 1 (J133920+464115) has a complex radio morphology. We find two regions of strong polarization in the northern part, peaking at 20.4 0.1 rad m^ 2 and 20.6 0.1 rad m^ 2 , in agreement with what was found by mulcahy . Like mulcahy , who discussed the source as a radio galaxy as a core and a single lobe, we do not detect polarization from the bright ``core" at the center of the image. However, from the morphology of the high-resolution FIRST image it is not certain that the fainter features to the north are related to the ``core". There is no clear counterpart in SDSS, which suggests that the source(s) are distant or highly obscured.
The six detected sources appear to be at least partially resolved by FIRST, and have a morphology consistent with that of double-lobed radio galaxies (Fig. sources_fig ).

gmw_00011871.name: Source 2
gmw_00011871.overview: Source 2 is a double-lobed radio galaxy and is the brightest source in its sample [arXiv:1807.09240]. A quasar with a redshift of 0.502 is located between the lobes of the galaxy [arXiv:1807.09240].

sources.summary: Source 2 is described as the sample's brightest double-lobed radio galaxy, with a quasar between the lobes and LOFAR Faraday depth below earlier GHz-band RM values.

quotes.quote: Source 2 (4C+47.38; B3 1339+472) is a double-lobed radio galaxy (only partially resolved with LOFAR) and the brightest source in the sample. Between the lobes there is a quasar with redshift z=0.502 0.003 b3_vla . 2003A&A...406..579K derived an RM of 46.4 2.7 rad m ^ 2 from polarization measurements at 1.4, 2.7, 4.8 and 10.5 GHz, which is about twice as high as the Faraday depth that we measure in the LOFAR 150 MHz data. They also derived a spectral index of 1.01 between 408 MHz and 10.6 GHz.

gmw_00011872.name: Source 3
gmw_00011872.overview: Source 3 is a double-lobed radio galaxy with a morphology consisting of three distinct parts [arXiv:1807.09240]. Its middle component is visible in FIRST images and has an SDSS counterpart at a redshift of 0.68240 [arXiv:1807.09240].

sources.summary: Source 3 consists of three radio parts; its middle component appears in FIRST and has an SDSS counterpart at redshift 0.68240, while the detected sources resemble double-lobed radio galaxies.

quotes.quote: Source 3 (J132626+473741) consists of three parts. The middle component is not visible in this observation, but can be seen in the FIRST image. A counterpart to the middle component was observed by the SDSS, with a redshift of z=0.68240 0.000351 sdss_z .
The six detected sources appear to be at least partially resolved by FIRST, and have a morphology consistent with that of double-lobed radio galaxies (Fig. sources_fig ).

gmw_00011873.name: Source 4
gmw_00011873.overview: Source 4 is a double-lobed radio galaxy [arXiv:1807.09240]. It is partially resolved into a double source in FIRST and has an optical counterpart with a photometric redshift of 0.975 [arXiv:1807.09240].

sources.summary: Source 4 is partly resolved into a double source in FIRST, has an SDSS photometric-redshift counterpart, and belongs to the set with double-lobed radio-galaxy morphology.

quotes.quote: Source 4 (J133707+485801) is partly resolved as a double source in FIRST, but not by LOFAR. SDSS has an optical counterpart with a photometric redshift z=0.975 sdss_z_phot .
The six detected sources appear to be at least partially resolved by FIRST, and have a morphology consistent with that of double-lobed radio galaxies (Fig. sources_fig ).

gmw_00011874.name: Source 5
gmw_00011874.overview: Source 5, also known as B3 1330+451, is a radio galaxy [arXiv:1807.09240]. It is partially resolved by LOFAR and is resolved into four sources by FIRST, displaying a morphology consistent with a double-lobed radio galaxy [arXiv:1807.09240].

sources.summary: Source 5 was outside earlier imaging, is partially resolved by LOFAR and into four FIRST sources, with LOFAR polarization associated with the southwest part.

quotes.quote: Source 5 (B3 1330+451) was not observed by mulcahy , as it was outside their imaged field. It is only partially resolved by LOFAR. It is resolved into four sources by FIRST. The polarization detected by LOFAR is associated with the SW part.
The six detected sources appear to be at least partially resolved by FIRST, and have a morphology consistent with that of double-lobed radio galaxies (Fig. sources_fig ).

gmw_00011875.name: Source 6
gmw_00011875.overview: Source 6 is a double-lobed radio galaxy [arXiv:1807.09240]. It features two distinct polarized peaks in its northwest region, and both parts of the source have been detected in polarization at 1.4 GHz [arXiv:1807.09240].

sources.summary: Source 6 shows two northwest polarized peaks at 9.2 rad m^-2; both parts were detected at 1.4 GHz but not in earlier M51-field polarization work.

quotes.quote: Source 6 (J133613+490037) shows two distinct peaks in the northwest part, both at 9.2 0.1 rad m^ 2 . Both parts of the source were detected in polarization at 1.4 GHz, with the southeast at an RM of 10.7 16.5 rad m^ 2 and the northwest at 11.1 10.6 rad m^ 2 taylor . It was not detected in polarization by mulcahy .
The six detected sources appear to be at least partially resolved by FIRST, and have a morphology consistent with that of double-lobed radio galaxies (Fig. sources_fig ).

gmw_00011876.name: Source 7
gmw_00011876.overview: Source 7 is a double-lobed radio galaxy located behind the neutral hydrogen tidal tail of M51 [arXiv:1807.09240]. The galaxy features a faint radio core with an optical counterpart, as well as northern and southern radio components [arXiv:1807.09240].

sources.summary: Source 7 lies behind M51's H I tidal tail, is detected in polarization at several frequencies, and appears as a double-lobed radio galaxy with FIRST components and an SDSS optical counterpart.

quotes.quote: Source 7 lies at an angular distance of 12 ' from the center of M 51 (or 26.5 kpc, assuming a distance to M 51 of 7.6 Mpc, 2002ApJ...577...31C ). This source source is of special interest because of its detection in polarization at several frequencies and its location behind the prominent tidal tail of neutral hydrogen discovered by 1990AJ....100..387R and imaged more recently by The H i Nearby Galaxy Survey, THINGS 2008AJ....136.2563W . In the top right panel of Fig. figSource7 we show the THINGS H i integrated intensity image; the right square indicates the location of Source 7.

The source has the morphological appearance of a double-lobed radio galaxy. Three sources are listed in the FIRST catalog: the rather faint core (with an integrated flux density of about 4 mJy; FIRST J133045.1+470316), a northern component ( S_ 1.4 GHz 9 mJy; FIRST J133045.3+470324), and a brighter southern component ( S_ 1.4 GHz 11.8 mJy; FIRST J133045.0+470309) that is slightly more extended than the FIRST beam. The combined flux of those three components is in excellent agreement with the NVSS flux measurement of 24.8 1.2 mJy NVSS , indicating that no extended emission is lost. There is an optical counterpart to the radio core, SDSS J133045.13+470317.2, marked by a red cross on Fig. figSource7 , with a photometric redshift of z = 0.816 0.0432 (there is, however, a note in SDSS that the object's photometry may be unreliable).

gmw_00011877.name: I Zw 81
gmw_00011877.overview: I Zw 81 is a massive lenticular galaxy located inside the Bootes void [arXiv:2211.04043]. It features a bar, a ring, and a low surface brightness disk, hosting a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus alongside central star-forming activity [arXiv:2211.04043].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes I Zw 81 as a massive Bootes-void lenticular galaxy with a bar, ring, LSB disk, blue-cloud star formation, and a weak AGN unable to quench central activity.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The galaxy is centered at = 14h08m13.59s, = 48d51m44.74s and z = 0.052.

quotes.quote: We present a case study of a massive galaxy, I Zw 81, inside the Bootes void. The void galaxy is known to host an active galactic nuclei (AGN). Our detailed 2D decomposition of the surface brightness distribution in the Canada France Hawaii Telescope ( CFHT ) g- and -bands revealed multiple structural components such as a nuclear point source , a bar, a ring, and an inner exponential disk followed by an outer low surface brightness (LSB) disk. I Zw 81 turns out to be a disk-dominated galaxy with lenticular morphology.

The modelling of the multi-wavelength spectral energy distribution (SED) shows that the galaxy is star-forming (SF), and belongs to the blue cloud. We find that the optical (g ) color of the bar is bluer than the disks, and the far- and near-ultraviolet emission inside the galaxy observed with Ultraviolet Imaging Telescope (UVIT) onboard AstroSat is concentrated in the central few kpc region enclosing the bar. The strong bar might be playing a pivotal role in driving the gas inflow and causing SF activity in tandem with the minor merger-like interactions as evident from the deep CFHT data. The low-luminosity AGN is insufficient to quench the central SF.

gmw_00011878.name: GMP5975
gmw_00011878.overview: GMP5975, also known as NGC 4807, is a faint giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0504466]. Its outer regions are dominated by dark matter [arXiv:astro-ph/0504466], and its stellar orbits exhibit a striking depopulation of retrograde trajectories [arXiv:0709.0691].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4807 as a faint giant E2 elliptical with extended stellar kinematics and a dark-matter-dominated outer region, finding mild radial anisotropy and possible weak triaxiality or an outer component.

quotes.quote: Regularized orbit superpositions are applied to a first galaxy in our sample, NGC 4807, for which stellar kinematical observations extend to 3 r_ eff . The galaxy seems dark matter dominated outside > 2 r_ eff . Logarithmic dark matter potentials are consistent with the data, as well as NFW-profiles (Navarro, Frenk White 1996), mimicking logarithmic potentials over the observationally sampled radial range. In both cases, the derived stellar mass-to-light ratio agrees well with independently obtained mass-to-light ratios from stellar population analysis.

Kinematically, NGC 4807 is characterized by mild radial anisotropy outside > 0.5 r_ eff , becoming isotropic towards the center. Our orbit models hint at either a distinct stellar component or weak triaxiality in the outer parts of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents updated dynamical models for GMP5975 including diagonal-slit kinematics, confirming previous findings that its mass structure and stellar-orbit distribution are stable.

quotes.quote: GMP5975/NGC4807: Dynamical models for GMP5975, based on major and minor-axis kinematics, have already been presented in Tho05 . There, a striking depopulation of retrograde orbits was found. To check its significance we also determined kinematics along a diagonal slit. Here we present new models that include this additional kinematical data. Both, the mass structure and the distribution of stellar orbits did not change significantly.

gmw_00011879.name: ID 1514
gmw_00011879.overview: ID 1514 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate [arXiv:2207.11217, arXiv:2210.14915] that forms the bright, compact center of a red multi-object structure [arXiv:2207.11217]. This structure includes three nearby dropout companions [arXiv:2207.11217], and the galaxy itself exhibits a high estimated stellar mass [arXiv:2210.14915].

sources.summary: ID 1514 is a high-redshift candidate at RA/Dec given in the paper, described as the bright compact center of a red multi-object structure with nearby dropout companions.

quotes.quote: Among our final list of high- z candidates is ID 1514 which is located in the NIRCam module that is not centered on the SMACS0723 cluster. In our inspection of our candidates, we find that ID 1514 ( RA=110.61465 , DEC= -73.4774 ) is potentially a part of a wider structure located at high redshift. ID 1514 is the bright, compact centre component of the circled system shown in Figure fig:smacs_redcutout . Within close physical proximity, the target has three neighbouring objects with very red colours. The object immediately below it has a photometric redshift that is similar to ID 1514 at z_ phot =9.3 0.3 , the object immediately above is also a F090W dropout but was provided a redshift of 8.6 0.6 by our template fitting procedure.

The final object, located to the left, has a slight detection in F090W and a redshift of 6.15 0.4 . The exact nature of this structure is presently unknown, but since the majority of this structure drops out in the F090W band, it is warranting of a mention and further follow-up investigation is planned for the near future.

sources.summary: The paper treats ID 1514 as a massive JWST high-redshift galaxy candidate whose stellar mass exceeds the most massive TNG and EAGLE simulated galaxies at similar redshift.

quotes.quote: For example, Adams et al. identified the candidate ID 1514 with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 9.8 _ 0.2 ^ +0.2 located at z_ phot = 9.85_ 0.12 ^ +0.18 and Naidu et al. found even more distant candidates labeled as GL-z11 and GL-z13 with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 9.4_ 0.3 ^ +0.3 at z_ phot =10.9_ 0.4 ^ +0.5 and _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 9.0_ 0.4 ^ +0.3 at z_ phot = 13.1_ 0.7 ^ +0.8 , respectively.
For the TNG runs, ID 1514 located at z_ phot = 9.85_ 0.12 ^ +0.18 and ID 14924 at z_ phot = 9.92 are compared with simulated galaxies at z=10.00 .

The RefL0050N0752 and RefL0100N1504 snapshots contain galaxies reaching up to _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) 7.70 at z=15.13 and _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) 9.06 at z=9.99 , which is 79 and 5.5 times lower than the observed stellar mass of CEERS-1749 and ID 1514, respectively.

gmw_00011880.name: ESO 480-IG12
gmw_00011880.overview: ESO 480-IG12 is a galaxy with strong outer morphological distortions, featuring a long, narrow northern extension and several kiloparsec-scale southern plumes [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179]. Its velocity field is irregular with double line profiles, and its dynamics are dominated by dark matter [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179].

sources.summary: ESO 480-IG12 is described as morphologically distorted and asymmetric, with plumes, double line profiles, an irregular velocity field, and rotation-curve evidence for dark matter dominance.

quotes.quote: ESO 480-IG12 An intriguing finding with this galaxy is that it has strong outer morphological distortions (Fig. contig12 ) and that it is apparently aligned with a chain of galaxies in a background (uncatalogued) cluster. Spectra have been obtained for three of the galaxies with the smallest angular distance from ESO 480-IG12, which however are at much higher red-shift ( 30000 km/s), but it cannot be excluded that one of the galaxies apparently belonging to the cluster is in fact a low mass companion of ESO 480-IG12. The northern extension is long and narrow while the south one is broader and more diffuse. South of the centre, there are several kiloparsec scale plumes. In addition, there are several small faint blobs in the southern part.

The velocity field is irregular with double line profiles, almost over the entire galaxy, and two components have successfully been fitted in Paper I. The double lines could arise in a separate dynamical component or an outflow or expanding super bubble. The rotation curve and luminosity distribution suggest that dark matter dominates the dynamics (see Fig. massmod1st4 ).

gmw_00011881.name: ESO 338-IG04B
gmw_00011881.overview: ESO 338-IG04B is a physical companion galaxy located at a projected distance of 70 kpc from ESO 338-IG04 [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179]. It hosts an old underlying stellar population, exhibits a moderate star formation rate, and possesses a regular, unperturbed velocity field that supports the presence of a dark matter halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179].

sources.summary: ESO 338-IG04B is treated as a physical companion with moderate star formation, an old stellar population, regular kinematics, and evidence for a dark matter halo.

quotes.quote: ESO 338-IG04B (companion) This is a physical companion to ESO 338-IG04. The projected distance between the galaxies is 70 kpc. Although the colours are fairly blue, ESO 338-IG04B is not a BCG, and the star formation rate is moderate and does not imply a starburst (see Table 4). Photometry indicates an old underlying stellar population (Bergvall and Ostlin 2000).

The star formation in this galaxy could have been enhanced by the tidal drag from ESO 338-IG04, but the velocity field is regular and apparently unperturbed (Fig. contig04b ). The kinematics of this galaxy support the presence of a dark matter halo (see Fig. massmod1st4 ).

gmw_00011882.name: ESO 400-G43B
gmw_00011882.overview: ESO 400-G43B is a regular dwarf galaxy that acts as a physical companion to ESO 400-G43 [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179]. It features a regular rotation curve indicating the presence of a dark matter halo, alongside intense star formation activity that places it at the limit of being classified as a starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179].

sources.summary: ESO 400-G43B is described as a regular dwarf companion with a regular rotation curve, dark-halo evidence, central HII-region star formation, and probable interaction-enhanced activity.

quotes.quote: ESO 400-G43B (companion) This physical companion to ESO 400-G43 (Bergvall and J ors ater 1988) is a regular dwarf galaxy with a regular rotation curve (Figs. massmodlast4 and contg43b ). The shape of the rotation curve does not follow the light distribution which indicates the presence of a dark matter halo (Fig. massmodlast4 ). The properties of ESO 400-G43B are fairly similar to those of ESO 338-IG04B, but it has more intense star formation activity. The time scales for gas consumption and buildup of the observed stellar mass are both on the order of 3-4 Gyr. Thus, this galaxy is at the limit of being classified as a starburst galaxy.

gmw_00011883.name: J2135
gmw_00011883.overview: J2135 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a simple narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1909.13242]. Its X-ray spectrum is modeled with a broken power law and exhibits no high-energy complexity [arXiv:1909.13242].

sources.summary: J2135 is classified as simple; the authors model its unpublished XMM spectrum with a broken power law and find no high-energy complexity.

quotes.quote: J2135 (IRAS F21325-6237) A simple power-law fitting gives a ^2_ = 1.09 in the range of 2-12 keV, indicating that J2135 is a S-NLS1. Since no X-ray spectrum or modeling was published for this object, we reproduced its XMM-Newton spectrum in 0.2-12.0 keV using a broken power-law model, which yields a ^2 /d.o.f. = 354/322 = 1.10, as shown in Table j2135_parameters and Fig. j2135_fit . The broken power-law model gives photon indexes of _1 = 2.29 and _2 = 1.75 below and above a break energy of E_ break = 1.93 keV. No high-energy complexity is shown.

gmw_00011884.name: J2245
gmw_00011884.overview: J2245 is classified as a simple narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1909.13242]. Its X-ray spectrum can be modeled using a blackbody plus power-law component, showing no complex features at high energies [arXiv:1909.13242].

sources.summary: J2245 is classified as simple; the authors model its previously unanalyzed XMM spectrum with blackbody plus power law and find no high-energy complex feature.

quotes.quote: J2245 (IRAS F22423-4707) The spectral fitting with a simple power-law between 2-12 keV reaches a ^2_ = 0.94, suggesting that J2245 is a S-NLS1. This object has never been analyzed in the literature. Its XMM-Newton spectrum in the range of 0.2-12.0 keV can be reproduced by a blackbody plus power-law model, yielding a blackbody temperature of kT = 0.10 keV and a photon index of = 2.16. This model has a ^2 /d.o.f. = 189.4/157 = 1.21, as seen in Table j2245_parameters and Fig. j2245_fit . No complex feature is evident in the high-energy part of the spectrum.

gmw_00011885.name: UMa II
gmw_00011885.overview: UMa II is one of the faintest ultra-faint dwarf galaxies [arXiv:2001.01107]. Its elongated shape suggests the galaxy may be undergoing tidal disruption, and it contains four member RR Lyrae stars that classify it as an Oosterhoff I system [arXiv:2001.01107].

sources.summary: The paper identifies four Gaia RR Lyrae members in UMa II, all within its tidal radius, and classifies the dwarf's RR Lyrae population as Oosterhoff I.

quotes.quote: UMa II is one of the faintest among the SDSS UFDs. Its elongated shape may suggest it is undergoing tidal disruption. dallora12 found one RRL in UMa II but with doubts about its period. The period was revised in vivas16 using data from the Catalina Real Time Transient Survey (CRTS), finding that it was indeed different to the one suggested by dallora12 . Gaia finds this star with the same period as vivas16 Figure fig:UMaII shows a total of four RRLs of about the same magnitude (same color in the plot) in agreement with the proper motion of the spectroscopic members of UMa II. These stars have magnitudes between 18.18 < G < 18.37 . In the sky (right panel), those four stars (encircled in red) are all relatively close to the center of UMa II, at distances between 5 to 14 . Since the tidal radius of this galaxy has been estimated to be 59 8 munoz18 , we conclude these stars must be members. Thus, UMa II has a population of four RRLs, of which three are and one is . The mean period of the three is 0.515d, which is short for RRLs in UFDs. Based on the mean period, UMa II would be classified as an Oosterhoff (Oo) I system oosterhoff39 .

gmw_00011886.name: Coma Berenices
gmw_00011886.overview: Coma Berenices is a ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy with an absolute visual magnitude of -4.4 [arXiv:2001.01107]. It contains three known RR Lyrae variable stars, all of which lie within the galaxy's measured tidal radius [arXiv:2001.01107].

sources.summary: The paper finds the two known Coma Berenices RR Lyrae in Gaia plus one new member within the tidal radius, giving three total RR Lyrae.

quotes.quote: Coma Berenices (Figure fig:CB ) htb Coma Berenices (ComBer) is also a SDSS UFD with M_V=-4.4 . The morphology does not show signs of tidal disruption munoz18 . musella09 searched for RRLs in this galaxy and found two. We have found those two stars in Gaia, at distances of 2 and 8 from the center of ComBer. In our search we found one additional RRL in ComBer (Figure fig:CB ), which has similar proper motion and similar magnitude as the other members. The new RRL is located at 22 from the center of ComBer. Although it is much farther away than the others, it is still within the tidal radius of 26 8 measured by munoz18 . In total, ComBer then has three RRLs, two of which are and one is type . The periods of the two stars are quite similar, 0.670 d. The new star is labeled in Table tab:RRL as V4 since musella09 uses V3 for a short period variable detected in the field.

gmw_00011887.name: Sgr II
gmw_00011887.overview: Sgr II is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy with an absolute visual magnitude of -5.7 [arXiv:2001.01107]. It is believed to be a satellite of the disrupting Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal galaxy and contains five confirmed RR Lyrae variable stars, plus one extra-tidal member [arXiv:2001.01107].

sources.summary: The paper describes Sgr II as likely linked to Sagittarius dSph, recovers five RR Lyrae, and argues V4 is an extra-tidal RR Lyrae member.

quotes.quote: Sgr II is the third brightest galaxy in our sample with M_V=-5.7 . It is believed that Sgr II is a case of a satellite of a satellite since it has an orbit similar to the classical Sgr dSph longeard20 , which we know is disrupting. Sgr II was searched for RRLs by joo19 . They found six RRLs in the field, but associated only five of them to Sgr II because one of them, V4, was too far away from the center of the galaxy. Gaia recovered five out of the six stars in joo19 , including V4. As seen in Figure fig:SgrII , because of the similarity in magnitude and the agreement between the proper motions of V4 and the rest of the members of Sgr II, we believe V4 should be indeed an extra-tidal RRLs of Sgr II.

gmw_00011888.name: Tuc III
gmw_00011888.overview: Tuc III is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located relatively close to the Sun at a distance of 23 kpc [arXiv:2001.01107]. The system is currently disrupting, featuring clear tidal tails and six candidate RR Lyrae stars that may have been stripped from the galaxy [arXiv:2001.01107].

sources.summary: The paper highlights Tuc III as a disrupting UFD with tidal tails and six Gaia RR Lyrae candidates outside the galaxy, possibly fully stripped from it.

quotes.quote: Tuc III is known to be disrupting since it shows clear tidal tails that extend to a few degrees from the galaxy drlica15,shipp18,li18 . Tuc III is a relatively close system, at only 23 kpc from the Sun. Our search for Gaia RRLs in Tuc III resulted in none in the galaxy itself but six at distances between 60 and 110 from the center of Tuc III, and with a narrow range of magnitudes between 17.1-17.7 (Figure fig:TucIII ). Surprisingly, those star do not seem to follow the tidal tails but are distributed all around the galaxy. If radial velocities confirm the association of those RRLs to Tuc III, all the RRLs population in Tuc III would have been stripped off the galaxy.

gmw_00011889.name: Eri III
gmw_00011889.overview: Eri III is a distant, tiny dwarf galaxy that was discovered in the Dark Energy Survey [arXiv:2001.01107]. It has a half-light radius of 0.34 arcminutes and a tidal radius of 1.45 arcminutes [arXiv:2001.01107].

sources.summary: The paper describes Eri III as a tiny distant DES galaxy and identifies one far-out Gaia RR Lyrae candidate whose membership needs confirmation.

quotes.quote: Eri III is a distant, tiny galaxy discovered in DES by bechtol15 . Its half-light radius is only 0 34 and the tidal radius is 1 45 munoz18 . We found a single RRLs with the right magnitude for being a Eri III member. The star has a proper motion in agreement with other members. However, it is located at 45 from the center of Eri III, well outside its tidal radius. RRLs fainter than G 20.4 are rare in the Galactic Halo medina18 , and thus the chance that this is a Galactic star is low. The lightcurve of this RRLs is noisy since it is close to the faint limit of Gaia. Photometric confirmation is desirable.

gmw_00011890.name: PKS B1011 282
gmw_00011890.overview: PKS B1011 282 is a radio galaxy hosted by the 6dFGS source g1013297 283126 [arXiv:2004.13025]. It is suggested to be a double-double radio galaxy containing inner jets and a visible southern outer lobe [arXiv:2004.13025].

sources.summary: The paper confirms the 6dFGS host of PKS B1011 282 and suggests a double-double radio-galaxy morphology with inner jets and a southern outer lobe.

quotes.quote: At 4.7 GHz ReidR1999 ^ rkp1999note we see that the 6dFGS source, g1013297 283126, is indeed the host galaxy of G4Jy 829 (GLEAM J101329 283118). This source is also known as PKS B1011 282, and we suggest that it is a `double-double' radio-galaxy, with inner jets and only the southern outer-lobe seen in the 4.7-GHz image (Figure doubledoubleat5GHz ). The northern outer-lobe may be resolved out, or simply too faint to be detected at this high frequency.

gmw_00011891.name: ESO 499G37
gmw_00011891.overview: ESO 499G37 is a nearby late-type spiral galaxy located at a nominal distance of approximately 14 Mpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0610638]. It features a cluster-sized central structure that may represent a transition object between a galaxy bulge and a nuclear star cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0610638].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 499G37 as a nearby late-type galaxy whose cluster-sized central structure may bridge bulges and nuclear star clusters at very low mass.

quotes.quote: In ESO 499G37, the closest galaxy in our sample (at a nominal distance of only 14Mpc), the size/mass of the well-resolved "bulge" reaches the typical scales that are observed for the nuclear star clusters in the other more distant galaxies in the sample. In the latter galaxies, the nuclear clusters are found in addition to a bulge component; in ESO 499G37, the cluster-sized bulge is the only central structure detected in addition to the disk. It is an interesting question whether ESO 499G37 is a bulge-less disk with a nuclear star cluster (for which we resolve its spatial structure), or a disk galaxy with a small bulge and no central star cluster. In Paper 2 we show that, in terms of surface mass density versus stellar mass, the central structure in this galaxy can be considered as a "transition object" between bulges and nuclear star clusters. We have opted for including the cluster-sized "bulge" of ESO 499G37 in the current analysis, and treating this structure similarly to the other bulges.

gmw_00011892.name: XLSSC 116 BCG
gmw_00011892.overview: XLSSC 116 BCG is a brightest cluster galaxy that exhibits a high star formation rate and is not passive [arXiv:1601.00520]. It is associated with both radio and ultraviolet sources, displaying strong emission lines and a lower stellar mass than typical cD galaxies [arXiv:1601.00520].

sources.summary: The paper describes the XLSSC 116 BCG as a radio/UV-associated, actively star-forming brightest cluster galaxy with unusual properties for a cD-like cluster-dominating galaxy.

quotes.quote: First, we note that the BCG is not passive (best type: Sd, high SFR). This is in good agreement with the galaxy being a radio source (probable) or a UV source (possible): NVSS J021039-055637 and GALEXASC J021039.47-055645.5 are close to its position (respectively at 2 and 9 ). We also show the MUSE spectrum (described below) of the BCG in Fig. fig:SEDKs . The spectrum clearly shows strong emission lines. This object is clearly forming stars very actively.

The XLSSC 116 BCG has a stellar mass closer to the literature BCGs. Our ICL source seems to exhibit intermediate characteristics that are between an old and passive elliptical galaxy and a younger and more active object. The XLSSC 116 BCG does not seem to be a normal cD galaxy with a relatively low stellar mass and a high star formation rate.

gmw_00011893.name: SDSS J163033.90+401209.6
gmw_00011893.overview: SDSS J163033.90+401209.6 is a faint quasar located at a redshift of approximately 6.05 [arXiv:astro-ph/0301135]. Its spectrum displays strong, clearly separated Lyman-alpha and nitrogen V emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0301135].

sources.summary: The paper reports spectra of SDSS J1630+4012 showing clear Ly and NV emission, estimates z=6.05 from the NV peak, and describes it as a faint z>5.7 quasar.

quotes.quote: The discovery spectrum of SDSS J1630+4012 was obtained using DIS on the ARC 3.5m on 2 June 2002 with a total exposure time of 1800 sec under partly cloudy skies, resulting in very low S/N. A higher S/N spectrum was obtained with HET/LRS on 3 July 2002 with an exposure time of 3400 sec, and is shown in Figure 5(). Even with this moderate S/N and low resolution spectrum, strong Ly and NV emission lines are clearly visible and the two peaks are clearly separated. The total rest-frame equivalent width of the Ly +NV lines is 70 , and the FWHM of the NV line is 4000 \ km \ s^ 1 . Using the NV peak, we estimate the redshift of SDSS J1630+4012 to be z=6.05 0.03 .

gmw_00011894.name: HCG 89a
gmw_00011894.overview: HCG 89a features an arm-like structure with almost no emission in its center [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075]. A very bright knot on the southwestern side of its disk is a suspected tidal dwarf candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075], though the galaxy's outer velocity field remains largely symmetric [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075].

sources.summary: HCG 89a has an arm-like, center-deficient emission structure, a possible tidal-dwarf knot, and a velocity field whose outer disk remains largely symmetric.

quotes.quote: The monochromatic map and continuum image of HCG 89a show that the galaxy has an arm-like structure with almost no emission in its center. A very bright knot can be seen on the southwestern side of the disk of HCG 89a. This was noted by RHF1991, who suspected that this knot was a tidal dwarf candidate.

The brightest part of the H knot is very round and a velocity gradient of 60 km s^ 1 can be measured from one side to the other. This very bright giant H ii region will perhaps become a tidal dwarf galaxy. However, what we can say is that, in the present, its velocity gradient matches perfectly the grand design velocity field of the galaxy.
The velocity field of HCG 89a shows no major twisted isovelocities at large radii, which is confirmed by the symmetric RC for radii larger than 8 arcsec (4.6 kpc).

gmw_00011895.name: HCG 89x
gmw_00011895.overview: HCG 89x is an isolated emission-line system and a tidal dwarf candidate located south of the galaxy HCG 89a [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075]. It is weakly linked to HCG 89a by a stellar bridge and exhibits its own decoupled velocity gradient [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075].

sources.summary: HCG 89x is identified as an isolated emission-line system linked to HCG 89a, with its own velocity gradient and enough inferred mass to be a tidal dwarf candidate.

quotes.quote: At about 37 arcsec (21.3 kpc) south of HCG 89a, a bright emission region is visible in the H monochromatic map, we called HCG 89x on Fig. 7a. At very low intensity level (not shown here), our continuum image shows that this isolated region is in fact weakly linked (by a stellar bridge) to the main body of HCG 89a.

This giant H knot, which has a size around 7 arcsec (4 kpc), shows a clear velocity gradient of 84 km s^ 1 ( 42 km s^ 1 ) decoupled from the velocity field of the main body of the galaxy. This object is not in counter-rotation with respect to the main body (the position of its major axis is 80 ^ o while the one of the main body is 54 ^ o ). Moreover, it does not show any geometrical or velocity continuity with the main velocity field.

Both sides agree and reach a maximum rotational velocity around 70 km s^ 1 (for an inclination of 50 6 ^ o ). This provides a mass which is high enough for the galaxy to be self-graviting. In conclusion, the emission-line system south of galaxy HCG 89a is, in fact, an excellent tidal dwarf candidate.

gmw_00011896.name: HCG 89b
gmw_00011896.overview: HCG 89b is a barred spiral galaxy that exhibits a regular rotation curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075]. Its structure features large emission complexes, weak central emission, and a small ten-degree offset between its gas and stellar major-axis position angles [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075].

sources.summary: HCG 89b is described as a barred spiral with large emission complexes, weak central emission, a small gas-stellar axis offset, and a regular rotation curve.

quotes.quote: The monochromatic and continuum images of HCG 89b show well a spiral structure. In addition, the monochromatic image shows large emission complexes, the largest being a region to the northeast of the galaxy. As for HCG 89a, weak emission is visible in its center. The gas and stellar major-axis position angles of this galaxy differ by 10 ^o . HCG 89b was morphologically classified as a barred galaxy, an SBc hic93 and, indeed, a weak bar signature can be seen through the tilt of its central isovelocities.
The regular RC shows a continuous increase of the rotational velocity up to 130 km s^ 1 at a radius of 6 arcsec (3.5 kpc), after which a plateau is reached.

gmw_00011897.name: HCG 89c
gmw_00011897.overview: HCG 89c is a galaxy that forms a close pair with HCG 89d, separated by only 32 arcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075]. It is larger and has a higher maximum rotational velocity than its companion, featuring a clumpy monochromatic hydrogen-alpha map and peculiar outer isovelocities [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075].

sources.summary: HCG 89c is presented as the larger, faster member of the close HCG 89c/d pair, with a clumpy H map and peculiar outer isovelocities.

quotes.quote: HCG 89c and HCG 89d are separated by only 32" and were, therefore, imaged in one single exposure. HCG 89c is one and a half times larger than HCG 89d (maximum radius of 24 and 15 arcsec respectively) and it has a maximum rotational velocity almost three times as high as that for HCG 89d (180 and 70 km s^ 1 respectively). In both cases, the agreement between both sides of the RC is good in the central regions but diverge somewhat in the external regions. Both galaxies have a clumpy monochromatic map, but HCG 89d is brighter than HCG 89c in H , showing clearly three bright knots in emission. The velocity field of HCG 89c is regular in the inner part but the external isovelocities are peculiar.

gmw_00011898.name: HCG 89d
gmw_00011898.overview: HCG 89d is a galaxy that forms a close pair with the larger galaxy HCG 89c [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075]. It is brighter in H-alpha than its companion, featuring three bright emission knots and a southward extension that suggests a possible interaction [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075].

sources.summary: HCG 89d is the smaller, H-brighter member of the close pair with HCG 89c; its velocity field twists and extends southward, suggesting possible interaction.

quotes.quote: HCG 89c and HCG 89d are separated by only 32" and were, therefore, imaged in one single exposure. HCG 89c is one and a half times larger than HCG 89d (maximum radius of 24 and 15 arcsec respectively) and it has a maximum rotational velocity almost three times as high as that for HCG 89d (180 and 70 km s^ 1 respectively). In both cases, the agreement between both sides of the RC is good in the central regions but diverge somewhat in the external regions. Both galaxies have a clumpy monochromatic map, but HCG 89d is brighter than HCG 89c in H , showing clearly three bright knots in emission.

The velocity field of HCG 89d presents an U-shape twist around the major axis which can explain the peculiarity of the RC. Moreover, HCG 89d shows an extension to the south toward the direction of HCG 89c. This extension could maybe be explained by an interaction between the two galaxies even if the velocity difference is rather large between the extension of HCG 89d ( 8740 km s^ 1 ) and the northern part of the velocity field of HCG 89c ( 8920 km s^ 1 ).

gmw_00011899.name: HCG 100b
gmw_00011899.overview: HCG 100b is an irregular galaxy that exhibits a tail-like feature extending toward the southeast [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075]. The galaxy displays a disturbed velocity field and an asymmetric rotation curve that is inconsistent with a disk-like rotator [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075].

sources.summary: HCG 100b is reinterpreted as likely irregular, with a tail-like feature, disturbed velocity field, and a rotation curve inconsistent with a disk rotator.

quotes.quote: HCG 100b has been classified as an Sm galaxy by hic93 , but our data (and the velocity field in particular) suggests that this object is more likely irregular. The extension of the monochromatic image of this galaxy is 22", comparable to what was derived by vil98 . We can clearly see the central emission and the tail-like feature in the southeast direction. We also note, by comparing the monochromatic and continuum maps, that there is a difference of 25 ^o degrees between the PA of the two major axes. The velocity field appears to be disturbed, with a strong variation of the position angle along the radius.

In addition, some isovelosities are inconsistent with those measured over the whole map. The RC of HCG 100b is obviously asymmetric: there is no match between the two sides of the galaxy. In fact, the RC is so peculiar that it does not have an approaching side. The rotation of this object is not consistent with a disk-like rotator.

gmw_00011900.name: HCG 100c
gmw_00011900.overview: HCG 100c is a late-type barred spiral galaxy that exhibits strong central emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075]. Its velocity field reveals a bar-like structure, a varying major axis, and an asymmetric outer rotation curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075].

sources.summary: HCG 100c is a barred late-type spiral with strong central emission, bar-like velocity structure, varying major axis, and asymmetric outer rotation.

quotes.quote: HCG 100c is a late-type barred spiral galaxy. The monochromatic map shows strong central emission with an extension out to a radius of 35". vil98 show an H image of the central part of the galaxy only. The PA of the major axis obtained in the continuum map is slightly different from the monochromatic map (stellar and kinematic axes differ by 20 ^o ). The velocity map clearly shows the presence of the bar, with the central 5425 km s^ 1 isovelocity. The velocity field also shows the strong variation of the major axis along the radius and presents inconsistent isovelocities to the northeast.

The RC of HCG 100c shows pretty good agreement between both sides out to a radius of 13 arcsec (4.5 kpc). Further out, the receding side continues to rise out to 14 arcsec (4.9 kpc) and then reach a plateau. The approaching side has dropping velocities for radii between 13 arcsec to 20 arcsec (7 kpc). This drop corresponds to the inconsistent isovelocities to the northeast of the velocity field.

gmw_00011901.name: HCG 100d
gmw_00011901.overview: HCG 100d is an edge-on, late-type spiral galaxy that is a probable member of its group [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075]. Its monochromatic image reveals a clumpy structure, while its velocity field is fairly regular with a continuously rising rotation curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075].

sources.summary: HCG 100d is treated as an edge-on late-type spiral group member with clumpy emission, a mostly regular but radially varying velocity field, and a rising rotation curve.

quotes.quote: HCG 100d is a late-type edge-on spiral galaxy. No redshift information is available for this object but, due to the narrow FHWM of our interference filter (17 \ = 777 km s^ 1 ), the probability for this object to be part of the group is high. The monochromatic image of the galaxy shows a clumpy structure, consistent with the image produced by vil98 . The continuum map is regular, but with a knot to the northeast. The knot corresponds to an excess emission on the monochromatic map.

The velocity field if fairly regular but also shows a variation of the isovelocities along the radius. The RC of HCG 100d is continuously rising, both sides agree well within 8 arcsec (2.8 kpc) and further out the discrepancy between both sides increases with radius. The velocity amplitudes reach 240 km s^ 1 and 170 km s^ 1 for the approaching and receding sides respectively at a radius of 30 arcsec (10.4 kpc).

gmw_00011902.name: HCG 100x
gmw_00011902.overview: HCG 100x is an uncatalogued extragalactic emission object located 30 arcseconds east of the galaxy HCG 100b [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075]. It features a compact, intense nucleus and exhibits a non-axisymmetric velocity map with a highly discrepant rotation curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075].

sources.summary: HCG 100x is an uncatalogued extragalactic emission object east of HCG 100b with compact nuclear emission and a non-axisymmetric, discrepant rotation curve.

quotes.quote: HCG 100x is an extragalactic emission object located 30" east of HCG 100b. We did not find any mention of this object in the literature. This object has an H extension of 20" and a very compact and intense nucleus of 5" radius.

Line emission and continuum emission are concentrated in the nucleus but we observed a shift of 2 arcsec between both maxima. The velocity map is not axisymmetric, as can be seen also in the RC. The approaching and receding sides of the RC disagree completely.

gmw_00011903.name: ID2299
gmw_00011903.overview: ID2299 is a massive starburst galaxy at redshift z = 1.395 that is experiencing intense star-forming activity [arXiv:2101.04021]. Approximately 46% of its total molecular gas mass is decoupled from the galaxy, which is interpreted as a tidal ejection episode triggered by a merger [arXiv:2101.04021].

sources.summary: ID2299 is a z=1.395 massive starburst with intense compact star formation; about 46% of its molecular gas is decoupled and interpreted as merger-triggered tidal ejection.

quotes.quote: ID2299 is a starburst galaxy at z = 1.395 that has a stellar mass M _ = 9.4 1.3 10^ 10 M _ and is experiencing intense star-forming activity with a star formation rate SFR = 550 10 M _ yr ^ 1 . This is 5 higher than the average SFR of star-forming galaxies with comparable stellar mass at the same cosmic epoch .

Therefore, 46 13 of the total molecular gas mass is decoupled from the galaxy. This is a conservative estimate given the lower CO excitation in the broad component (Methods). Assuming that the gas in the broad component is associated to a feedback-driven wind (e.g., from AGN or star-formation), would translate its gas mass into an exceptional mass outflow rate M _ mol, OF = 25000 \ \ 8000 M _ yr ^ 1 . Consistently with the literature definitions (see Methods), this computation assumes that the outflow has a velocity v _ max, OF = 634 139 \ km s^ 1 and extends over R _ OF = 1.54 0.04 kpc corresponding to the radius over which the ALMA spectra are extracted. The velocity and spatial extent of the outflow imply a propagation timescale t _ OF = R _ OF / v _ max = 3 1 Myr (t _ OF 10 Myr and M _ mol, OF = 7100 \ \ 3600 M _ yr ^ 1 , if one were instead to use the most conservative average velocity shift v _ mol, b = 179 \ km s^ 1 ) . This suggests that such enormous quantity of gas (roughly half of the original galaxy ISM, mostly composed by molecular gas at these cosmic epochs ) is being expelled through a quasi-instantaneous event, not accumulating over the Gyr-long timescales of galaxy activity. This disruptive event, including a starbursting core and with half or more of the molecular gas mass being expelled, will eventually lead the galaxy to quench.
We thus conclude that the ID2299 observations can be explained by an exceptional tidal ejection episode triggered by the merger.
