gmw_00011904.name: SDSS J0812+1819
gmw_00011904.overview: SDSS J0812+1819 is a radio-loud type-1.9 quasar at a redshift of z=2.3794 [arXiv:2101.04688]. The system exhibits clumpy extended emission lines, as well as a compact unresolved outflow on scales under 1.9 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2101.04688].

sources.summary: SDSS J0812+1819 is a radio-loud type-2/type-1.9 quasar with clumpy extended emission; after model subtraction, the broad unresolved component is linked to a compact outflow.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0812+1819 (z=2.3794) This object was identified as a type-2 quasar candidate by alex13 based on its rest-frame UV properties. Follow-up NIR (rest-frame optical) spectroscopy by gree14b did not reveal a broad component in H but did reveal one in H ; hence this source can be classified as type 1.9 according to the standard optical line classification scheme. The object matches to an extended radio source in the FIRST survey beck95 consisting of two resolved radio sources centered on the quasar offset by 3 5 \ from the optical position and with an integrated flux density of 5.5 mJy. With this flux and the observed narrow , this object's radio emission is not consistent with being wind-induced zaka14, hwan18 , and we classify it as radio-loud. The morphology is linear and consistent with a lobe-dominated source at position angle 170 ^ East of North. Further, 33 GHz observations reveal a core with flux 0.23 mJy/beam and possible traces of optically-thin emission from the lobes alex17 .

After performing PSF subtraction on the IFS observations, we detect extended emission associated with two clumpy regions extended in the east-west direction in the , , and the \ emission lines. We detect no outflows on these spatial scales. After subtracting a model of the extended emission (similar to SDSSJ1652+1728, section sec:1652 ), we detect an unresolved component that consists of both narrow and broad emission components. The narrow emission comes from the extended-narrow line region of the quasar, while the broad component is associated with an outflow on scales <1.9 kpc.

gmw_00011905.name: SDSS J0826+0542
gmw_00011905.overview: SDSS J0826+0542 is an extremely luminous, extremely red quasar at a redshift of z=2.5767 [arXiv:2101.04688]. It is a marginally radio-loud, hard-spectrum X-ray source with a compact, broad-line outflow region and no detected extended emission [arXiv:2101.04688].

sources.summary: SDSS J0826+0542 is an ERQ and near-Compton-thick X-ray source; PSF-subtracted data show no extended emission but a compact broad-line outflow region.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0826+0542 (z=2.5767) SDSS J0826 + 0542 was selected from the SDSS as an ERQ ross15, hama17 , and just like for SDSS J1652 + 1728, and SDSS J2323 0100, follow-up NIR (rest-frame optical) observations obtained with Gemini GNIRS revealed high-velocity, broad and blue-shifted component in the \ emission line goul18a, perr19 . It is an extremely luminous, hard-spectrum X-ray source consistent with near-Compton-thick absorption along the line of sight goul18a . Just like SDSS J1652+1728, this source is marginally radio-loud hwan18 .

After performing PSF subtraction we detect no extended emission. We extract the spectrum from the point source. The spectrum is consistent with a broad \ emission line coming from an outflow region. The upper limit on the size of the outflow is 0.4 . We present the spectrum in Figure fig:spec_SDSS0826 .

gmw_00011906.name: NGC 3917
gmw_00011906.overview: NGC 3917 is classified as a low-surface-brightness spiral galaxy [arXiv:1602.00055]. Its observed rotation curves have been used to model and compare modified dark matter and cold dark matter density profiles [arXiv:1602.00055].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3917 as a typical low-surface-brightness spiral, comparing observed rotation curves with MDM and CDM predictions and showing model dark-matter density profiles for the galaxy.

quotes.quote: Rotation curves predicted by MDM for NGC 4217, a typical HSB galaxy, and NGC 3917, a typical LSB galaxy in the sample are shown in Fig. fig1.2 . (See Ref. 5 for the rotation curves for the other 28 galaxies.) In these figures, observed rotation curves are depicted as filled circles with error bars, and for the two curves at the bottom, the dotted and dash-dotted lines show the stellar and interstellar gas rotation curves, respectively. The solid lines and dashed lines are rotation curves predicted by MDM and the standard cold dark matter (CDM) paradigm respectively. For the CDM fits, we use the Navarro, Frenk White (NFW) density profile, employing three free parameters (one of which is the mass-to-light ratio.)

It is fair to say that both models fit the data well; We should point out that the rotation curves predicted by MDM and MOND have been found to be virtually indistinguishable over the range of observed radii and both employ only 1 free parameter. but we remind the readers that while the MDM fits use only 1 free parameter, for the CDM fits one needs to use 3 free parameters. Thus the MDM model is a more economical model than CDM in fitting data at the galactic scale. Since MDM employs only the minimal number of free parameters, the speaker (YJN) called it the ``minimal dark matter" model in his talk at MG14. But later he found out that the name had been used before for another model. His collaborators and he decided to change the name to ``modified dark matter". Shown in Fig. fig3.4 are the dark matter density profiles predicted by MDM (solid lines) and CDM (dashed lines) for the HSB galaxy NGC 4217 and the LSB galaxy NGC 3917 in the sample respectively.

gmw_00011907.name: NGC 3976
gmw_00011907.overview: NGC 3976 is an active galaxy that appears unabsorbed in soft X-rays [arXiv:0808.2385]. However, a hard excess in its spectrum suggests it hosts a heavily obscured nucleus, and no variability has been detected in its observations [arXiv:0808.2385].

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3976 resembles NGC 3486: it appears unabsorbed in soft X-rays, but a hard excess and absorbed component indicate a hidden obscured nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 3976 The XMM-Newton spectrum of NGC 3976 is very similar to that of NGC 3486 as it shows no intrinsic absorption, but a hard excess present above the simple power-law fit allows us to add a heavily absorbed power-law component. A strongly absorbed transmission component produces an overall better fit, suggesting that NGC 3976 is also hiding a heavily obscured nucleus beneath its apparently unabsorbed soft X-ray spectrum. Again, there is also no variability detected in this observation.

gmw_00011908.name: SDSS J002710.06-094435.3
gmw_00011908.overview: SDSS J002710.06-094435.3 is a broad-absorption line quasar that hosts a clumpy outflow [arXiv:1711.09047]. Its C IV and Si IV absorption troughs resolve into multiple narrow-absorption line doublets that exhibit coordinated variations over time [arXiv:1711.09047].

sources.summary: The paper focuses on SDSS J002710.06-094435.3, showing its C IV and Si IV BAL troughs resolve into multiple NAL doublets whose coordinated variations trace a clumpy quasar outflow.

quotes.quote: Utilizing the two-epoch observations of J002710.06-094435.3 (hereafter J0027-0944) from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS), we find that each of the C iv and Si iv BAL troughs contains at least four NAL doublets. By resolving the Si iv BAL into multiple NALs, we present the following main results and conclusions. First, all these NALs show coordinated variations between the two-epoch SDSS observations, suggesting that they all originate in the quasar outflow, and that their variations are due to global changes in the ionization condition of the absorbing gas. Secondly, a BAL consisting of a number of NAL components indicates that this type of BAL is basically the same as the intrinsic NAL, which tends to support the inclination model rather than the evolution model. Thirdly, although both the C iv and Si iv BALs originate from the same clumpy substructures of the outflow, they show different profile shapes: multiple absorption troughs for the Si iv BAL in a wider velocity range, while P-Cygni for the C iv BAL in a narrower velocity range.

gmw_00011909.name: J1333+1649
gmw_00011909.overview: The high-redshift galaxy J1333+1649 hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2008.01728] and exhibits extended ionized gas emission [arXiv:2008.01728]. This system features a spherically symmetric ionized outflow [arXiv:2008.01728] and a narrow, cool gas component with detected carbon absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381].

sources.summary: The paper treats Q1331 + 17 as a famous, well-studied damped system, with high-SNR measurements, detected CI absorption, and evidence for a narrow, cool gas component.

quotes.quote: Q1331 + 17, z = 1.776 This famous damped system which exhibits absorption ( ) has been studied by a number of authors (over 100 papers), yet never with the quality of data presented here (FWHM resolution 6 and SNR > 50). Table Q1331-clm lists the column densities for the metal-line transitions and Figure vpQ1331 plots their profiles. We assume HI = 21.176 0.041 ( ). The SNR is excellent throughout the entire spectrum yielding very accurate column density measurements. This is one of the very few systems where CI absorption is detected and Songaila et al. \ (1994) have used measurements of the CI profile to estimate the cosmic background temperature at z = 1.776 .

Consider the feature at v = +20 which is fully resolved in the CI profiles, barely resolved in the Zn ^+ profiles, and is unresolved in the stronger transitions even at 6 resolution. This suggest the gas in this component is at a temperature T < 6000 K. While this system may be atypical because it is one of the few damped systems to exhibit CI absorption, it is worth noting a majority of observed damped profiles may be the result of the superposition of many narrow components.

sources.summary: The paper says J1333+1649 has extended ionized emission, PSF-subtraction residuals, more spherical outflow maps, and one of the sample's highest escape fractions.

quotes.quote: From Fig. fig:astrometry1 , we see excess residuals at a level of >2 for 6 SUPER targets - X N 81 44, X N 115 23, cid 346, J1333+1649, J1549+1245 and S82X1905. In summary, from the joint analysis based on the COG and "PSF-subtraction" method we have identified seven sources from our sample which show extended emission. Further analysis on the spectra across the SINFONI FoV will therefore be restricted to the 7 targets, which show extended gas emission with at least one of the methods described above.

From the w_ 80 maps in Figs. fig:flux_velocity1 and fig:flux_velocity2 , it is clear that the ionized outflows detected in these AGN present a diversity of projected structures. In five sources (X N 81 44, X N 115 23, cid 346, S82X1905) there is a velocity gradient along a particular axis while for two objects (J1333+1649 and J1549+1245) the maps are more spherically symmetric. Some of the kinematic structures present in the w_ 80 maps could be consistent with a bi-conical outflow, where the inclination of the outflow with respect to the disk of the galaxy and the line-of-sight of the observer could explain the observed velocity maps: i.e. a high inclination of the outflow with respect to the line-of-sight will result in a clear velocity gradient along the outflow axis, with possibly both the blue-shifted and red-shifted part of the outflowing gas detected; a low inclination of the outflow with respect to the line of sight would produce a more uniform and spherically symmetric velocity field.
The highest escape fractions of 43 and 97 are observed in J1333+1649 and J1441+0454 which are also the targets with high bolometric luminosity.

gmw_00011910.name: J1441+0454
gmw_00011910.overview: J1441+0454 is a bright active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2008.01728]. It exhibits a highly blueshifted line blended with strong iron emission, as well as an exceptionally high escape fraction [arXiv:2008.01728].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1441+0454 as a bright AGN with absent expected emission, a highly blueshifted line, blended iron/H emission, and an exceptionally high escape fraction.

quotes.quote: Finally, J1441+0454 is well detected in , but the spectrum does not show the presence of emission at the expected observed wavelength. This object is one of the brightest AGN in our sample and the possible lack of emission at high bolometric luminosities has been previously reported in the literature e.g. WISSH quasars: bischetti17 . On the other hand, while the line fitting suggests that the spectrum around the expected location of is dominated by strong iron emission, it also found a significant highly blue-shifted ( 3000 km/s) line with FWHM 1956 km/s. Such extreme blue-shift has also been observed before in the literature in high-redshift Extremely Red Quasars ERQs, see perrotta19 . Due to the degeneracy between the iron component and the emission and the highly blended nature of the observed H emission (see Fig. fig:intspec_alltargets3 ) we will limit the analysis of the properties to the integrated spectum for this object and will not attempt to characterize the extended nature of the emission in the next sections.

The highest escape fractions of 43 and 97 are observed in J1333+1649 and J1441+0454 which are also the targets with high bolometric luminosity. Note that in J1441+0454, the exceptional high escape fraction is a result of the extremely blue-shifted profile which is also blended with the iron emission as explained in Sect. sect5.1 .

gmw_00011911.name: NGC 4410
gmw_00011911.overview: NGC 4410 is a disturbed, interacting galaxy classified as Sab [arXiv:astro-ph/0605453]. It contains two nuclei separated by about 20 arcseconds, with the more compact nucleus containing abundant molecular gas and acting as an active X-ray source [arXiv:astro-ph/0605453].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4410 as an active interacting double-nucleus galaxy with molecular gas, optical tails, surrounding X-ray sources, and aligned quasars and clusters interpreted as physically associated.

quotes.quote: The aim is to investigate the region of the sky around NGC4410/Mrk1325 for objects which are physically associated with this active, double nucleus galaxy. We use archived data to study the placement, brightness, X-ray properties and redshifts of objects within 60' of the bright, central galaxy. It is found that pairs of quasars are aligned across NGC 4410 which, if ejected from it, have equal and opposite ejection velocities and fall very close to the quantized Karlsson redshift peaks for quasars. X-ray sources and Abell galaxy clusters at higher redshifts appear elongated along directions away from NGC4410.

NGC 4410 is a disturbed galaxy classified as Sab interacting. It appears to contain two nuclei, 4410 A and B, separated by about 20 arcsec. A is more compact, an X-ray source and probably the source of the ultraviolet continuum which caused NGC 4410 to be catalogued as Markarian 1325 (Markarian et al. 1980). Radio measures by Beverly J. Smith (2000) show that A has abundant molecular gas.

gmw_00011912.name: G2
gmw_00011912.overview: The galaxy G2 is identified as one of the two brightest cluster galaxies in the galaxy cluster MACS 0416 [arXiv:1407.7866]. Its center is separated by a projected distance of approximately 30 kpc from the center of the cluster's southern dark-matter halo [arXiv:1407.7866].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G2 as one of MACS 0416's two brightest cluster galaxies and compares the galaxy luminosity centers with significantly offset dark-matter halo centers.

quotes.quote: There, we have marked the two brightest galaxies of the cluster, G1 and G2 (see also Table tab5 ), and the multiple image systems studied in this paper (see Section sec:lensmod:multsys ).

From Tables tab5 and tab3 and Figure fi22 , we remark that the reconstructed centers of two cluster dark-matter halos are significantly separated, more than 3 away, from the centers of the two brightest cluster galaxies. In detail, the northern halo is at a projected distance of approximately 50 kpc from G1 and a smaller projected distance of approximately 30 kpc separates the centers of the southern halo and G2. Interestingly, the northern halo is preferentially displaced towards the North-East direction, whereas the southern halo towards the South-West direction, resulting in a projected distance between the two cluster dark-matter halos of approximately 300 kpc. We have checked that (1) fixing the halo centers to those of the brightest cluster galaxies results in ^ 2 values that are approximately a factor of 4 higher than those obtained with the halo centers free to vary, and (2) increasing the F160W magnitude value of G1 by 0.3 mag, i.e. mimicking an overestimate of the galaxy luminosity due to a possible contamination from the intra cluster light, reduces the offset between the centers of the northern halo and G1 by only about 10 .

gmw_00011913.name: UGC 139
gmw_00011913.overview: UGC 139 is a large spiral galaxy classified as an SBc type [arXiv:1307.4899]. It exhibits a double-horn symmetric H I profile that is characteristic of ordered disk rotation [arXiv:1307.4899].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 139 as the nearby spiral dominating the H i line in the J0014-0044 field, causing that source to be excluded from further XMP analysis.

quotes.quote: J0014-0044 - The SDSS image (Fig. 1) shows a two-knot source next to a large spiral (SBc) galaxy (UGC 139). The NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ ) has flagged the brightest SDSS XMP knot as a Western H ii region of UGC 139. Indeed, SDSS spectroscopy of the bright knot and UGC 139 put them at a similar redshift of 0.01. Furthermore, the H i profile is double-horn symmetric, typical of ordered disk rotation, with a large line width ( 320 km s ^ 1 ; Table 2). Therefore, the H i line detected with Effelsberg is primarily associated with the spiral galaxy UGC 139 and the source has been excluded from the following analysis.

gmw_00011914.name: UGC2684
gmw_00011914.overview: UGC2684 is a nearby, low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy classified as an extremely metal-poor source [arXiv:1307.4899]. It is highly gas-rich compared to typical dwarf galaxies, exhibiting a low efficiency in converting its neutral hydrogen gas into stars [arXiv:1307.4899].

sources.summary: The paper highlights UGC2684 among the lowest-luminosity nearby XMPs, describing this low-surface-brightness subset as unusually gas-rich and inefficient at converting H i gas into stars.

quotes.quote: We call attention to the six sources with the lowest g band luminosity -- UGC2684, DD053, LeoA, SextansB, UGCA292 and GR8. These are all nearby (D < 6 Mpc), low-surface brightness galaxies, that also turn out to be XMPs.

We find an anti-correlation between the M _ H i /L _g ratio and the luminosity, whereby fainter XMPs are more gas-rich than brighter XMPs. The result suggests that the less luminous sources have converted a smaller fraction of their H i gas into stars. However, our sources deviate from the observed correlation found for dwarf galaxies (cyan line; bottom; Fig. 5; Staveley-Smith, Davies Kinman 1992) particularly at low luminosities, which correspond to the low-surface brightness, nearby galaxies UGC2684, DD053, LeoA, SextansB, UGCA292 and GR8. In this low luminosity regime, the XMP galaxies appear to be 10 to 100 times more gas-rich than typical dwarf galaxies.

gmw_00011915.name: J0301-0052
gmw_00011915.overview: J0301-0052 is a cometary, extremely metal-poor dwarf galaxy classified as a starburst system [arXiv:1307.4899]. It exhibits narrow emission lines typical of star-forming galaxies and displays a large neutral hydrogen gas velocity offset of approximately 100 km/s [arXiv:1307.4899].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0301-0052 as a cometary XMP with a large H i velocity offset, no nearby contaminant at similar redshift, and star-forming spectral classification.

quotes.quote: J0301-0052 - This cometary XMP (Table 3) has been observed with Effelsberg (Table 2) and shows a large H i gas offset ( 100 km s ^ 1 ; Table 5). We had previously discarded H i contamination (Sect. 2.2.2) by checking the SDSS spectroscopic or photometric redshifts of the neighbours and found no sources within 200 km s ^ 1 of the target. In addition, the nearest radio continuum source (NVSS) present in the field is about 1 away and has a flux of 3.2 mJy. The SDSS has classified this source as a starburst at a redshift of 0.0073 (Table 4). Indeed, the SDSS spectra of the source shows narrow emission lines, with weak [ O i ] and [ S ii ] , typical of a star-forming galaxy.

gmw_00011916.name: J2053+0039
gmw_00011916.overview: J2053+0039 is an extremely metal-poor dwarf galaxy with a two-knot morphology [arXiv:1307.4899]. It is associated with a nearby diffuse blue cloud to the northwest, and its neutral hydrogen data likely contains contributions from both of these components [arXiv:1307.4899].

sources.summary: The paper treats J2053+0039 as a two-knot XMP whose nearby blue cloud is likely associated with the main source, so the H i data probably include both components.

quotes.quote: J2053+0039 - This source (Fig. 1) is found in a SDSS field with nearby stars and small red objects, likely to be high redshift galaxies. However, there is a small blue cloud to the Northwest of the original XMP position (top right-hand corner; Fig. 1), with a SDSS photometric redshift of 0.17 0.12. The main XMP source has a spectroscopic redshift of 0.01. The large error in the photometric redshift, the close proximity, the diffuse structures and similar colours, has led us to associate these two sources with the XMP, whose morphology is classified as two-knot. The H i data will likely contain information from both these components.

gmw_00011917.name: ALH023002+004647
gmw_00011917.overview: ALH023002+004647 is a low-luminosity, high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately 5.41 [arXiv:1307.5117]. Its spectrum displays a very blue continuum along with broad emission lines, including Lyman-alpha, nitrogen V, and a silicon IV and oxygen IV complex [arXiv:1307.5117].

sources.summary: The paper reports ALH023002+004647 as a newly discovered low-luminosity QSO at z~5.41, with a blue high-redshift spectrum, broad emission features, and black-hole/bolometric luminosity estimates.

quotes.quote: As a result of the spectroscopic follow--up of one of the highest probability candidates, we report the discovery and basic properties of ALH023002+004647, a low luminosity and very high redshift QSO. The left panel of Fig. fig:High-z-QSO_spectra shows the ALH023002+004647 cutouts of the 23 ALHAMBRA filters plus a colour composite. A pilot program, containing ALH023002+004647, was approved and observed with the OSIRIS http://www.gtc.iac.es/en/pages/instrumentation/osiris.php spectrograph at the 10-meter GTC telescope located at the Observatorio del Roque de los Muchachos in La Palma.

The spectrum of ALH023002+004647 shows typical features of a high- z QSO spectrum with a a very blue continuum and prominent emission from broad Ly , N V , Ly +O VI and the Si IV +O IV ] complex. The continuum blue-wards of Ly is substantially reduced due to intervening clouds of neutral hydrogen present in the IGM at different redshifts along the line of sight (the Ly forest).

Table tab:QSO-params resumes the main characteristics of the identified QSO. Redshift We derived a preliminary spectroscopic redshift z=5.410 0.003 based on the position of the O I + Si II emission line 1302. The observed wavelength of Ly +O VI is compatible with this redshift.

gmw_00011918.name: RCS-01020502096
gmw_00011918.overview: RCS-01020502096 is a blue companion galaxy located northeast of the quasar ALH023002+004647 [arXiv:1307.5117]. It is a low-to-average mass galaxy that provides negligible gravitational lensing amplification to the nearby quasar [arXiv:1307.5117].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RCS-01020502096 as the NE companion near ALH023002+004647, a blue galaxy whose low-to-average mass makes its lensing amplification of the QSO negligible.

quotes.quote: The cutout images of the QSO (Fig. fig:High-z-QSO_spectra ) show two companions located 5 to the NE and 4 to the SW. According to the ALHAMBRA catalogue (Molino et al. 2013, in prep.), the NE source corresponds to an AB(I)=23.8 blue galaxy at photometric redshift z_ ph =2.25 whose spectrum peaks at 6500 , and the SW source corresponds to an AB(I)=25.2 blue galaxy at photometric redshift z_ ph =2.10 peaking at 5500 . The former has a counterpart in the NED database, the source RCS-01020502096 from the Red-Sequence Cluster Survey 2005ApJS..158..161H with a reported redshift of 0.249. However, the R band magnitude of this object ( R=23.6 ) puts it well below the limit that 2005ApJS..158..161H quote for good quality redshifts in their catalogue.

According to our data both sources correspond to low-to-average mass galaxies ( (M/M_ ) 10.5 10.8 ). Given the estimated masses for the companions and their distance to the QSO, they should provide negligible gravitational lensing amplification to the quasar image 2005ApJ...626..657W .

gmw_00011919.name: NGC 3318
gmw_00011919.overview: NGC 3318 is a nearby spiral galaxy classified as SAB(rs)b [arXiv:2008.06515]. Located at an estimated luminosity distance of approximately 33.0 Mpc, it has hosted multiple supernovae, including SN 2000cl, SN 2017ahn, and SN 2020aze [arXiv:2008.06515].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3318 as the nearby SAB(rs)b spiral host of SN 2017ahn, adopts a 33.0 Mpc distance, and analyzes the supernova's projected offset and local extinction.

quotes.quote: is located at RA =10 :37:17.45, Dec =-41 :37:05.27 [ J2000 ] , 21 75 E, 33 93 N from the center of its host galaxy, NGC 3318 (see Figure fig:17hFC ). NGC 3318 is a nearby ( D 38 Mpc https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ ) spiral galaxy SAB(rs)b; 1991rc3..book.....D , which already hosted the Type II SN 2000cl 2000IAUC.7432....1G , 15 47 W, 42 51 S away from the position of and the Type II SN 2020aze (24 44 W, 23 65 S from ; Ailawadhi et al. in preparation). In the following, we will assume a luminosity distance of 33.0 6.5 Mpc to NGC 3318, corresponding to a distance modulus of =32.59 0.43 mag (as derived by 2014MNRAS.444..527S using data obtained during the observational campaign Cosmicflows with Spitzer ; 2012AN....333..436C,2012ApJ...749..174C,2012ApJ...749...78T,2013AJ....146...86T ), placing at a projected distance of 6.4 kpc from the center of NGC 3318.

This value is in agreement with that found by 2015MNRAS.450..317C , resulting in a ``cosmicflows-3" luminosity distance D_L=40.76 Mpc (assuming _M=0.3 , _ =0.7 and H_0=70 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 ), corresponding to a distance modulus =33.05 mag https://cosmicflows.iap.fr , as well as that inferred using The Extragalactic Distance Database (EDD http://edd.ifa.hawaii.edu/CF3calculator/ ; see 2020AJ....159...67K ) based on the linear density field model of =32.73 0.21 mag . 2019MNRAS.488.5438G . For the foreground Galactic extinction we adopt the values reported by 2011ApJ...737..103S , corresponding to E(B-V)=0.068 mag , while to estimate the local extinction, we compared results obtained using different methods.

gmw_00011920.name: MG2016+112
gmw_00011920.overview: MG2016+112 is an early-type lens galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.004 [arXiv:astro-ph/0307281]. Its mass model reveals that luminous mass dominates the inner 10 kiloparsecs, while an extended dark matter halo dominates at larger radii [arXiv:astro-ph/0307281].

sources.summary: The review presents MG2016+112 as a z=1.004 lens galaxy whose joint lensing-dynamical mass model shows luminous central mass, an extended dark halo, and an almost isothermal total profile.

quotes.quote: The best fitting mass model for MG2016+112 at z=1.004 (Lawrence et al. \ 1984) is shown in the left panel of Fig. fig:MG2016 . Luminous mass dominates in the inner 10 kpc while a flatter dark matter halo contributes most of the mass at larger radii. No dark matter models, or constant total mass-to-light ratio models, are rejected at high confidence level. Remarkably, although none of the two components is a simple power law density profile, the total mass density profile follows very closely an ^ 2 singular ``isothermal'' profile (equivalent to a flat rotation curve for spiral galaxies).

gmw_00011921.name: SMM J02399--0136
gmw_00011921.overview: SMM J02399--0136 is a submillimeter galaxy that hosts a dusty active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1101.4027]. The system is a merger composed of a far-infrared-luminous starburst, a quasar host, and a faint third component [arXiv:1101.4027].

sources.summary: The paper describes SMM J02399--0136 as an early CO-detected SMG hosting a dusty AGN and merger components, with detected atomic-carbon emission and possible lensed, tentative higher-transition carbon emission.

quotes.quote: SMM J02399--0136 This source is one of the first SMGs (S _ 850 m =26 3 mJy, Ivison et al. \ 1998) ever detected in CO emission ( J =3, Frayer et al. \ 1998) J =3: CO(3--2) . It has subsequently been studied in detail in CO emission ( J =3: Genzel et al. \ 2003, J =1: Ivison et al. \ 2010a). Ivison et al. \ (1998) noted that this source hosts a dusty AGN; its QSO lines are discussed in Villar-Mart n et al. \ (1999) and Vernet Cimatti (2001). In the L_ FIR - L_ X-ray plane SMM J02399--0136 has the same luminosities as the Cloverleaf (Alexander et al. \ 2005a).

Ivison et al. \ (2010b) conclude that this source comprises a merger between a FIR--luminous starburst, a QSO host and a faint third component. For our analysis we adopt a FWZI of 1000 \ for SMM J02399--0136. The \ line is clearly detected in both individual channel maps (Figure 1) and the integrated line emission (Figure 2, left).

gmw_00011922.name: RX J0911+0551
gmw_00011922.overview: RX J0911+0551 is a bright submillimeter source [arXiv:1101.4027]. It exhibits a very narrow carbon monoxide emission line and contains detections of both atomic-carbon transitions [arXiv:1101.4027].

sources.summary: The paper describes RX J0911+0551 as a bright submillimeter source with tentative and follow-up CO measurements showing a narrow line, plus detections of both atomic-carbon transitions.

quotes.quote: RX J0911+0551 This source is bright in the submillimeter (S _ 850 m =26.7 1.4 mJy, Barvainis Ivison 2002) and CO has been tentatively detected in this source by Hainline et al. \ (2004, J =3). Follow--up observations by W11 ( J =3,5,7,8,9) show that the CO emission line is very narrow (FWHM 110 ). We detect both the \ and \ emission lines in this source (see Figure 8 and Table 3).

gmw_00011923.name: SMM J16359+6612
gmw_00011923.overview: SMM J16359+6612 is a triple-lensed submillimeter galaxy system [arXiv:1101.4027]. All three of its gravitationally lensed images are bright in the submillimeter and have been detected in CO emission [arXiv:1101.4027].

sources.summary: The paper describes SMM J16359+6612 as a triple-lensed submillimeter system with all three images bright and CO-detected, while these observations cover only the brightest image.

quotes.quote: SMM J16359+6612 This source is a triple--lensed system in which all three images are bright in the submillimeter (brightest image `B': S _ 850 m =17 2 mJy, Kneib et al. \ 2004). All images have subsequently been detected in CO emission (Sheth et al. \ 2004, J =3, Kneib et al. \ 2005, J =3). Kneib et al. \ (2004) have argued that the magnifications for the three images are 14 2, 22 2 and 9 2, respectively. Higher-- J CO transitions have been presented in Wei et al. \ (2005a).

The beams are 18 '' and 11 '' at our tuned frequencies at 2 mm and 1 mm respectively, i.e. only the brighest image (`B') is covered in our observations. In Figure 11 we show the \ (left) and the \ (right) spectra (see also Table 3).

gmw_00011924.name: PSS J2322+1944
gmw_00011924.overview: PSS J2322+1944 is a gravitationally lensed, high-redshift source that is bright in the submillimeter spectrum [arXiv:1101.4027]. It features strong CO emission, a molecular Einstein ring, and high-significance carbon-line detections [arXiv:1101.4027].

sources.summary: The paper presents PSS J2322+1944 as a lensed, bright submillimeter source with strong CO, a molecular Einstein ring, previous carbon detection, and a new high-significance carbon-line detection.

quotes.quote: PSS J2322+1944 PSS J2322+1944 is a lensed source ( =5.4 0.3, Riechers et al. \ 2008b) which is bright in the submillimeter (S _ 850 m =22.5 2.5 mJy, Isaak et al. \ 2002; S _ 1300 m =9.6 0.5 mJy, Omont et al. \ 2001). Strong CO emission has been reported by Cox et al. \ (2002, J =4, J =5) and resolved imaging has revealed a molecular Einstein ring in this source by Carilli et al. \ (2002b, 2003, J =2) and Riechers et al. \ (2008b, J =2). The \ line has been detected by Pety et al. \ (2004). We detect the \ line at high significance (Table 3, Figure 12 shows a wider bandwidth spectrum after combining the \ data with the CO(7--6) data presented in W11).

gmw_00011925.name: R.A. = 14^h43^m57^s , decl. = - 39^ 08'39'' , J2000
gmw_00011925.overview: R.A. = 14^h43^m57^s, decl. = - 39^ 08'39'', J2000 is a bright blazar classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1509.03972]. The source is a very-high-energy gamma-ray emitter that has shown no detected gamma-ray variability [arXiv:1509.03972].

sources.summary: The paper reports the discovery and multiwavelength modeling of a coordinate-identified BL Lac object, finding VHE emission, uncertain redshift, and a one-zone leptonic SED description.

quotes.quote: \ (R.A. = 14^h43^m57^s , decl. = - 39^ 08'39'' , J2000 ) is a bright blazar in the LAT energy regime and has a hard, well-constrained HE spectrum (spectral index: _ 2FGL = 1.77 0.06 ). Its distance was estimated as z=0.065 (6dF Galaxy Survey ), but this redshift value is not included anymore in the final version of the 6dF catalog due to poor spectral quality . Despite many measurements in different wavelength ranges, the redshift of \ remains unknown with the currently best constraint to be 0.14 < z < 2.2 .

In this paper, we reported on the \ discovery of the BL Lac object \ in 2012. The source was selected as VHE blazar candidate based on its hard high-energy spectrum measured by LAT, but is also listed in based on its infrared and X-ray properties. The average flux in the VHE regime was about 3 of the Crab Nebula flux and showed no significant gamma-ray variability. Multi-wavelength data, contemporaneous with the \ observations, were used to derive an SED which is well reproduced with a one-zone leptonic model with parameters typical for other VHE blazars.

gmw_00011926.name: UCD-FORS 2
gmw_00011926.overview: UCD-FORS 2 is an ultracompact dwarf galaxy with a Seersic half-light radius of approximately 34.68 parsecs [arXiv:1510.05010]. It exhibits a very blue color, strong Balmer lines, and three distinct tidal tail-like structures [arXiv:1510.05010].

sources.summary: The paper finds UCD-FORS 2 has multiple significant tidal-tail-like structures, blue color, strong Balmer lines, and ambiguous evidence for either stripped-nucleus or disrupted-cluster origins.

quotes.quote: The most striking features we found around UCD-FORS 2 (Y99025) as shown in figure fig:nr2 . We detect three clear tidal tail-like structures around this UCD. Two narrow tidal features emerge from the UCD towards the East and the North-West (NW). A smaller but brighter feature is visible on the northern side of the UCD. The NW tidal feature is above the 3 level for mainly all its extension with a small 5 peak in its middle.

This UCD-FORS 2 is also among those which have a size above the resolution limit, with a Sérsic half-light radius of r_ eff =34.68 0.76 pc , and a very blue (V-I) colour of 0.96 . This object has been pointed out before by for appearing to have two very faint features and potentially a faint envelope around it.They also found strong Balmer lines, which usually indicates the presence of a young stellar population. The alternative explanation of a very low metallicity is not supported because of its Washington colour that points to a metallicity of >-1.3 dex.

gmw_00011927.name: UCD-FORS 32
gmw_00011927.overview: UCD-FORS 32 is an ultracompact dwarf galaxy that exhibits a cored profile with a low Sersic index of 0.94 [arXiv:1510.05010]. Its structure is best fit by a double Sersic profile, which reveals a visible, faint envelope [arXiv:1510.05010].

sources.summary: The paper finds UCD-FORS 32 has the sample's lowest Sersic index, requires a double-Sersic profile, and shows a visible envelope with improved residuals.

quotes.quote: All these fits converged except for the one of UCD-FORS 32 (UCD6). Instead for this UCD a double Sérsic profile is the best fit to the data. Notably this is the object with the lowest Sérsic index of n=0.94 and thus the most cored profile consistent with the profile shape derived by .

The double profile fit is especially good for UCD-FORS 32, 45 and 81, for which a faint envelope is even visible from the images in figure fig:extended by eye. For illustrating the improvement in residuals for the double component fits, those three objects have been included in the last three panels of figure fig:surfprofiles and all of them show significant improvement of the residuals when using a K+S profile instead of a single Sérsic.

gmw_00011928.name: F336W-189
gmw_00011928.overview: F336W-189 is a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2206.10799] located at a redshift of z = 3.45 [arXiv:1612.06401]. It is identified as a Lyman continuum leaker candidate [arXiv:2206.10799] and exhibits a double-peaked Lyman-alpha line profile [arXiv:2206.10799].

sources.summary: The paper describes GC2 as a contaminated-sample galaxy with high-significance flux detection, relative brightness, K-band excess, significant UV detections at z=3.45, and a final contamination assessment.

quotes.quote: Fluxes for this sample are similarly crowded around zero, but there are two notable detections ( GC1 and GC2 ) with high ( 4 and 7.5 ) significance. Galaxies G1 and G2 from the clean sample are moderately faint ( i_ 775 27.3 mag), while GC1 and GC2 from the contaminated sample are relatively bright ( i_ 775 24.7 mag). Galaxies G2 and GC2 show an excess in the K band flux, suggesting that strong H + 4959,5007 emission lines enter the K band at their respective redshifts.

Galaxy GC2 (GOODS ID: J033240.30-274752.6) represents an interesting case. It is significantly detected both in the F336W and F275W image, and with less significance in the F225W images Rafelski2015 . This is surprising, as one would expect the signal to drop towards blue due to both the intrinsic galaxy emission properties and, on average, decreasing IGM transmission (see Fig. fig0 ). For example, at the redshift of this galaxy ( z = 3.45 ), the convolved IGM transmission with the F275W filter results in an average transmission of <T> 0.045 , approximately half of the convolved transmission through the F336W filter.
Based on the presented analysis we conclude that the GC2 source is contaminated.

sources.summary: The paper identifies F336W-189 as its strongest LyC-leaker candidate, with secure Ly emission, three LyC detections, moderate modeled escape, and dominant ionizing emissivity contribution.

quotes.quote: F336W-189: This galaxy was also identified by , and is our strongest candidate. It is bright, has a strong and unmistakable Lyman- emission line (see fig. fig:OOIspecs ) which determines its redshift beyond any reasonable doubt, and the MUSE and Photutils centroids are coincident to within 0 1 . It is detected in three bands in LyC, and its spectral energy distribution is textbook example of what we would expect a star-forming galaxy with a modest f_ esc ^ LyC to look like.

The absolute escape fraction is 36 22 and, after dust correction, we find a relative escape fraction of 325 . The Ly line profile is double-peaked with a peak separation of v 620 km/s. verhamme2015 found that a peak separation of v 300 km/s corresponds approximately to N_ HI 17 , at which point _ 900Å 1 , meaning that f_ esc ^ rel (LyC) for this galaxy is a good deal higher than expected from the peak separation (but as discussed above, the clumpy nature of dust may have inflated the relative escape fraction substantially and makes a comparison to theoretical values tricky). This galaxy also dominates the found value of _ 900 , contributing 83 of the total IGM-corrected ionizing flux in the searched volume.

gmw_00011929.name: F336W-554
gmw_00011929.overview: F336W-554 is a compact, high-redshift galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 2.87 [arXiv:2206.10799]. It is a young, lower-mass system that contains quite a lot of dust yet exhibits very high ionizing Lyman continuum flux [arXiv:2206.10799].

sources.summary: The paper treats F336W-554 as a compact high-redshift candidate with secure absorption-redshift evidence, strong LyC flux, high escape fractions, and LyC arising in its outskirts.

quotes.quote: F336W-554: This galaxy does not show any line emission, emission in Ly , but its spectroscopic redshift of 2.87 from I17 is reported with a confidence level of 3, the highest in that work, based on multiple absorption features. The two photometric redshifts from R15 are both slightly higher, at 2.97 and 2.97, also reported at a high confidence level. The morphology of this source seems point-like, or at least very compact, in F336W. It is situated at the Northern tip of a more extended feature seen only in the non-ionizing filters.

The fluxes measured in the rest-frame LyC for this object are very high. It is quite young and with (M_*) 8.8 in the lower mass end of our candidate list. It does however contain quite a lot of dust, which seems difficult to reconcile with such strong LyC emission. Indeed, while the absolute escape fraction is 67 , the relative escape fraction is measured to 876 .

gmw_00011930.name: F336W-1013
gmw_00011930.overview: F336W-1013 is a Lyman-alpha galaxy with a confirmed spectroscopic redshift of 2.98 [arXiv:2206.10799]. It has an ionizing escape fraction of 15 percent and is the second strongest contributor to the ionizing volume emissivity [arXiv:2206.10799].

sources.summary: The paper confirms F336W-1013 at z=2.98 from Ly emission, finds significant ionizing flux and moderate escape fraction, and identifies it as the second strongest contributor to ionizing emissivity.

quotes.quote: F336W-1013: This galaxy has a spectroscopic redshift from I17 of 2.98, which we have confirmed based on a Lyman line emission at 4840 Å. The photometric redshift methods consistently converge on a redshift 2 , which could be due to the assumption of a zero ionizing escape built-in to BAGPIPES . The galaxy was detected using F336W as detection filter. It has significant ionizing flux in F275W, and was also found in the source detection step, but fell for the color criterion and was rejected.

The galaxy has an ionizing escape fraction of a moderate 15 10 which, in combination with its redshift, leads to a strong IGM correction of the ionizing flux, making this galaxy the second strongest contributor to the ionizing volume emissivity _ 900 , delivering 10 of the total amount of photons. The relative escape fraction of f_ esc ^ rel = 450 is another example of a galaxy where the relative escape fraction indicates that the ionizing emission predominantly reaches us through lines of sight which are not typical for the general stellar population. Looking at the F275W cut-out of this galaxy in fig. fig:stamps , there is a brighter area to the W of the central object which, like the artifact next to F336W-1041, is not present in the non-PSF matched archival frames or the optical or IR frames.

gmw_00011931.name: F336W-1041
gmw_00011931.overview: F336W-1041 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy at a redshift of z=3.33 [arXiv:2206.10799]. It has a nearby companion galaxy at nearly the same redshift, indicating that the two form an interacting pair [arXiv:2206.10799].

sources.summary: The paper describes F336W-1041 as a z=3.33 Ly-emitting candidate with very high escape fractions, an unlikely but plausible clear line of sight, and a same-redshift companion.

quotes.quote: F336W-1041: This galaxy consistently converges on a photometric redshift of 2.0--2.2 both in our computations and in R15, but has a bright Ly emission line at redshift z=3.33 (see fig. fig:OOIspecs ), which is also its designated redshift in I17, with a reported confidence level of 2; we speculate that as in the case of F336W-1013, the built-in assumption of zero ionizing escape in BAGPIPES might have skewed its solution towards a lower redshift. There are no strong emission lines consistent with the location of the one observed which correspond to redshifts between 2.0 and 2.2. The high observed equivalent width of the emission feature limits which other lines it could conceivably be, as does the clearly asymmetrical line profile.

F336W-1041 has a relative escape fraction of 662 and an absolute escape fraction of just over, but consistent with, 100 . This means that not only must the line of sight to the LyC sources be completely free of internal stars and dust in the galaxy; also the LOS through the IGM must be essentially free of neutral Hydrogen. This is clearly a priori a very unlikely situation; however, as discussed above, our entire selection process is strongly favoring such a scenario among thousands of originally detected sources, and as argued above, the scenario is not implausible. The thumbnails in fig. fig:stamps show that the there is a nearby object to the NE of the galaxy. This companion has almost identical redshift in I17 (see Tab. tab_redshifts ), which we interpret as the two being an interacting pair, and which means that any cross contamination between the two stems from effectively the same redshift and thus will not give rise to false detection in LyC.

gmw_00011932.name: F275W-314
gmw_00011932.overview: F275W-314 is a galaxy with a solid spectroscopic redshift [arXiv:2206.10799]. It has a detection in the rest-frame Lyman continuum filter F225W, though deeper imaging is required to confirm this emission [arXiv:2206.10799].

sources.summary: The paper labels F275W-314 the lowest-redshift and least convincing candidate, but notes its spectroscopic redshift and LyC-filter detection require deeper imaging for confirmation.

quotes.quote: F275W-314: This is the lowest-redshift and perhaps also the least convincing object in the selection. However, it does have a solid spectroscopic redshift, a >1 detection in the rest-frame LyC filter F225W, and a flux in the Lyman edge straddling filter F275W which is difficult to reconcile with the inferred stellar population. However, this galaxy requires deeper imaging in F225W or a similar filter to be properly convincing.

gmw_00011933.name: F275W-2055
gmw_00011933.overview: F275W-2055 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:2206.10799] with an estimated photometric redshift of approximately z=2.33 [arXiv:2206.10799]. Its measured ultraviolet flux is inconsistent with models that assume no ionizing Lyman continuum escape [arXiv:2206.10799].

sources.summary: The paper presents F275W-2055 as a Tier 2 candidate lacking spectroscopy but with photometric redshifts, SED fits, and UV flux inconsistent with zero LyC escape.

quotes.quote: F275W-2055: This galaxy is not included in I17, and we found no strong emission lines or convincing absorption lines in the MUSE spectrum. Our initial BAGPIPES run yielded z=2.07 0.08 , while the two methods of R15 yielded z=2.43 and z=2.26 , respectively. However, in our initial SED modeling, we allowed metallicity to vary freely, which yielded an unbelievably high metallicity for this galaxy of Z 2 Z_ . In our second round of SED fitting, we constrained the metallicity to be equal to or below solar, which bumped up the inferred redshift to z=2.33 0.29 , better in line with the measurements of R15.

Even at the original z=2.07 , the measured flux in F275W is inconsistent with the best-fit model if we assume no LyC escape within 2 . An even lower redshift, or a significantly bluer UV spectrum, could bring the measured SED within consistency with an absence of ionizing escape. We note again that the redshift is also downward biased in the fitting process by the built-in assumption of no ionizing escape. When the UV filters have lower statistical weight due to the larger aperture size and the metallicity is constrained to sub-solar values, the fit settles on a redshift entirely consistent with those from R15.

gmw_00011934.name: F336W-606
gmw_00011934.overview: F336W-606 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a photometric redshift estimated between 2.41 and 2.73 [arXiv:2206.10799]. Morphologically, it is a complex object consisting of a bright, compact southwestern feature and multiple fainter northeastern clumps [arXiv:2206.10799].

sources.summary: The paper describes F336W-606 as a Tier 2 photometric-redshift candidate detected in all UV filters, morphologically complex, and interesting despite lacking spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: F336W-606: The second of two candidates which has no spectroscopic redshift. For this galaxy, the R15 photometric redshifts are 2.50 and 2.69. Our original BAGPIPES redshift is 2.41 which however shifts to z=2.73 when the metallicity is constrained as described above. The galaxy has SNR > 2 in all three UV filters, but only picked up by our detection and filtering procedure when using F336W as detection filter.

Morphologically, this is a complex object which consists of one major, bright, compact feature to the SW, and a collection of multiple fainter clumps to the NE (see Fig. fig:stamps ). These features are all treated as one feature in R15, while we have based our SED modeling on a small segment of the NE feature collection, and even in the 0 8 aperture, the two parts are separate. The combination of strong flux and consistent photometric redshifts make this an interesting candidate object, but the lack of a spectroscopic redshift is is a concern.

gmw_00011935.name: SMMJ00266 + 1708
gmw_00011935.overview: SMMJ00266 + 1708 is a high-redshift galaxy at a spectroscopic redshift of z=2.73 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816]. The galaxy is best fit by a starburst template combined with a weak active galactic nucleus, though an absorbed active galactic nucleus classification is also possible [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816].

sources.summary: The paper fits SMMJ00266 + 1708 with a PAH-plus-weak-AGN template, notes no clear 6.2 micrometer PAH feature, allows an absorbed AGN interpretation, and derives z=2.73.

quotes.quote: SMMJ00266 + 1708 The best fit for this source is the combination of M82 PAH template and weak AGN continuum (see Fig. fit ), but the ^2 test gives similar results also for the absorbed continuum NGC 4418 template (see Tab. fits ). The spectrum lacks an evident 6.2 m PAH feature, the wavelength of which still falls in the observed band. We proceed with the best fitting PAH plus continuum interpretation but note that a classification of this source as an absorbed AGN is clearly not excluded. The previous photometric redshift of z=2.7^ +2.3 _ 0.2 estimated by aretxaga03 is consistent with our spectroscopic value z=2.73 0.06 . frayer00 identify a faint ERO counterpart for this object and estimate a lensing magnification 2.4 0.5 , which we adopt for our analysis.

gmw_00011936.name: SMMJ09429 + 4659
gmw_00011936.overview: SMMJ09429 + 4659 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of approximately 2.38 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816]. Although its spectrum is well fitted by an M82-like template, intense radio emission suggests a radio contribution from an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SMMJ09429 + 4659 as a high-redshift source fit by an M82-like starburst spectrum, with radio evidence suggesting an AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: SMMJ09429 + 4659 This source is well fitted with the starburst-like M82 spectrum. No accurate redshift was previously known for this object. Using the radio-submm spectral index and the models from carilli00 , the estimated redshift is z=0.4 0.3 . The intense radio emission suggests, though, that there is a significant radio contribution from an AGN, even if we do not see AGN emission in the mid-infrared spectrum, making the value estimated from the radio-submm relation only a lower limit.

Assuming this galaxy follows the K-z relationship for powerful radio galaxies jarvis01 , it most likely lies at z 2 ledlow02 . Our spectroscopic value of z=2.38 0.06 confirms the high redshift of this object. The luminosity of this source (see Tab. sample ) is calculated by taking into account a lensing magnification factor of 1.3 cowie02 .

gmw_00011937.name: SMMJ09431 + 4700
gmw_00011937.overview: SMMJ09431 + 4700 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.36 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816]. The system is powered by both starburst activity and a heavily obscured or Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816].

sources.summary: The paper presents SMMJ09431 + 4700 as the sample's highest-redshift detected SMG, powered by starburst activity plus a powerful, heavily obscured or Compton-thick AGN.

quotes.quote: SMMJ09431 + 4700 We pointed IRS at the mm position neri03 of component H7 in the notation of ledlow02 , but note that component H6 is included in the observing aperture as well. This source has the highest redshift of the sample ( z=3.36 ), not accounting for the non-detected objects (see discussion below). It is well fitted with a PAH spectrum plus a strong linearly rising continuum, so we infer that it is powered by both starburst activity and a powerful AGN (see Fig. fit ). The optical spectrum of component H6 shows features of a weak AGN ledlow02 . The spectral properties, line widths and line ratios of this galaxy are very similar to those seen for narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxies (NLSy1s; crenshaw91 ).

XMM-Newton observations ledlow02 suggest that the intrinsic X-ray luminosity of the AGN is modest (L _ 2-10 keV 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ) , unless there is heavy obscuration. Comparison to the strong rest frame mid-IR continuum seen in the IRS spectrum suggests the latter is indeed the case: at L _ 2-10 keV / L _ (6 m ) 0.015 , this source falls more than an order of magnitude below the relation between unobscured 2-10 keV luminosity and 6 m continuum for local AGN lutz04 . SMMJ09431 + 4700 hosts a heavily obscured or Compton-thick AGN, the location of which we cannot firmly ascribe to component H6 or H7.

gmw_00011938.name: SMMJ10519 + 5723
gmw_00011938.overview: SMMJ10519 + 5723 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.67 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816]. The galaxy is primarily interpreted as being powered by starburst activity, supported by the tentative detection of a 6.2 micrometer PAH feature [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816].

sources.summary: The paper interprets SMMJ10519 + 5723 mainly as a starburst-powered SMG, while noting that an absorbed-continuum interpretation remains possible and confirming z about 2.67.

quotes.quote: SMMJ10519 + 5723 The lowest ^2 fit for this object is the one with the M82 starburst template, which is supported by the tentative detection of a 6.2 m PAH feature. Still, the ^2 is not much higher for the absorbed continuum template NGC 4418. We interpret this source as powered by starburst activity, consistent with optical spectroscopy chapman05 , but note that an absorbed continuum interpretation cannot be firmly excluded.

The spectroscopic redshift suggested for this source by chapman03 was z=3.699 . Later, egami04 and chapman05 indicated values of z=2.69 and z=2.686 respectively. Our fits give z=2.67 0.06 using the starburst template and z=2.64 0.06 using the obscured one. Both redshifts are in agreement with the latest optical results.

gmw_00011939.name: MMJ154127 + 6616
gmw_00011939.overview: MMJ154127 + 6616 is an infrared galaxy with a mid-infrared spectrum that resembles a starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816]. This spectrum exhibits well-defined polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) features at 6.2 and 7.7 micrometers, indicating that the source is powered by star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816].

sources.summary: The paper reprocesses MMJ154127 + 6616 and finds a starburst-like mid-infrared spectrum with clear PAH features, while an NGC4418-like fit is less favored.

quotes.quote: MMJ154127 + 6616 The mid-infrared spectrum of this source was already published by lutz05 . We reprocessed the data using a later version of the IRS pipeline (14.0.0). The new reduction further increases the similarity to a starburst spectrum with well defined 6.2 m and 7.7 m PAH features and is well fitted by a M82 spectrum plus a very weak flat continuum. The ^2 is not much worse adopting the NGC4418 template which better matches the emission at long wavelengths, but the clear presence of both PAH features strongly increases confidence that this source is powered by star formation.

gmw_00011940.name: SMMJ16369 + 4057
gmw_00011940.overview: SMMJ16369 + 4057 is an infrared starburst galaxy with a redshift of z=1.21 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816]. Its spectrum is well fitted by an M82-like template with a weak flat continuum, and optical spectroscopy reveals typical starburst lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816].

sources.summary: The paper gives SMMJ16369 + 4057 extra IRS coverage because of its low redshift, then fits it with an M82-like weak-continuum spectrum consistent with optical starburst lines.

quotes.quote: Because of its low redshift, SMMJ16369 + 4057 was observed in the LL2 range for 10 cycles of 120 s, as well as in the SL1 7.4-14.5 m module for 5 cycles of 240 s (2400 s total on-source integration time).

SMMJ16369 + 4057 This source has the lowest redshift of the sample ( z=1.21 ) and is well fitted with the M82 spectrum plus a very weak flat continuum. The optical spectroscopy of chapman05 detects typical starburst lines, consistent with our result.

gmw_00011941.name: SMMJ16371 + 4053
gmw_00011941.overview: SMMJ16371 + 4053 is a mid-infrared galaxy that displays clear polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon features and a flat continuum [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816]. The galaxy is powered mainly by starburst activity, although optical spectra indicate that an active galactic nucleus is probably also present [arXiv:astro-ph/0701816].

sources.summary: The paper reports clear PAH features and flat continuum in SMMJ16371 + 4053, concluding it is mainly starburst-powered while probably hosting an AGN.

quotes.quote: SMMJ16371 + 4053 The mid-infrared spectrum of this source shows clear 6.2 m and 7.7 m PAH features. A flat continuum is detected. The mid-infrared spectrum shows that it is powered mainly by starburst activity, even though an AGN is probably also present. AGN lines have been detected in the optical spectrum of chapman05 .

gmw_00011942.name: IC 2627
gmw_00011942.overview: IC 2627 is a southern spiral galaxy characterized by strong star formation [arXiv:1308.5545]. This star formation is highlighted by bright H-alpha knots distributed mostly along two arms, and the galaxy exhibits a very shallow metallicity gradient [arXiv:1308.5545].

sources.summary: IC 2627 is presented as a star-forming southern galaxy whose SN 1998A site lies on an outer-arm H II region with solar metallicity and a shallow gradient.

quotes.quote: IC 2627 is a southern object, characterized by strong star formation, as shown by the bright H knots that are mostly distributed along two arms. SN 1998A exploded in the outer region of one of these arms and was located on a bright H ii region (see Fig. sn98A , top panel). This galaxy also hosted the normal Type II SN 1994R, whose position we marked with a diamond. The galaxy was observed along the two directions defined by the host center and the two SNe (see the slits marked with VLT 98A and VLT 94R in Fig. sn98A , top panel).

The three different metallicity measurements at the exact position of SN 1998A converge to 8.62-8.68 dex, which is the highest value in our sample, consistent with solar abundance (8.69 0.05 dex; asplund09 ). The other measurements across the galaxy are all solar or super-solar, and overall the galaxy exhibits a very shallow metallicity gradient (Fig. sn98A , bottom panel).

gmw_00011943.name: 2MASX J05553978-6855381
gmw_00011943.overview: 2MASX J05553978-6855381 is a relatively distant galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.03 [arXiv:1308.5545]. The galaxy exhibits a super-solar central metallicity and hosted the supernova OGLE-2003-NOOS-005, which exploded near a bright H II region [arXiv:1308.5545].

sources.summary: The host 2MASX J05553978-6855381 is a relatively distant galaxy with a far-out SN site near an H II region, super-solar central metallicity, and moderately subsolar SN-site abundance.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J05553978-6855381 is a relatively distant galaxy (z = 0.03), which hosted OGLE-2003-NOOS-005. This SN exploded very far from the host center ( _ SN /R _ 25 = 0.94) and not on a H ii region, although close (2.44 kpc) to a bright one (top panels of Fig. ogle ). For this galaxy, we measured the extent of the axes directly on the B band image that we obtained at the VLT.

The N2 measurements reveal a super-solar central metallicity and a gradient that seems to flatten in the outer part of the galaxy (bottom panel of Fig. ogle ). We note that the H line measurements at the exact host center is made difficult by significant stellar absorption. At the SN distance, the oxygen abundance is moderately sub-solar ( 8.4 dex).

gmw_00011944.name: IC 1303
gmw_00011944.overview: IC 1303 is a spiral galaxy situated in a region of the sky crowded with foreground stars [arXiv:1308.5545]. The galaxy exhibits a super-solar central metallicity and a shallow metallicity gradient [arXiv:1308.5545].

sources.summary: IC 1303 is treated as a foreground-crowded spiral with an outer SN near an H II region, super-solar central metallicity, and roughly solar SN-site abundance.

quotes.quote: The spiral galaxy IC 1303 is placed in a region of the sky crowded with foreground stars. SN exploded in its outer part, close (1.16 kpc) to a relatively bright H ii region (see top panels of Fig. sn ). We measured the metallicity at the host center and at four other positions, revealing a super-solar central metallicity and a shallow gradient (see bottom panel of Fig. sn ). At the SN distance, the oxygen abundance is 12 + log(O/H) = 8.56 dex, which is consistent with solar metallicity within the errors.

gmw_00011945.name: NGC 5682
gmw_00011945.overview: NGC 5682 is a strongly tilted spiral galaxy with an inclination of 70 degrees [arXiv:1308.5545]. The galaxy features two arms characterized by star formation, and it hosted SN 2005ci near its center on a bright H II region [arXiv:1308.5545].

sources.summary: NGC 5682 is described as a strongly inclined, star-forming spiral where the SN lies near the center on an H II region with LMC-like metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC 5682 is a strongly tilted (i = 70 deg) galaxy, with two arms characterized by significant star formation. SN 2005ci is located quite close to the galaxy center ( _ SN /R _ 25 =0.44), on a bright H ii region (top panel of Fig. sn05ci ). We placed one slit along the SN-host center direction, and one along the major axis (see top panel of Fig. sn05ci ) The eight metallicity measurements that we obtained with two slits (see central panels of Fig. sn05ci ) show a steep gradient and a LMC-like metallicity at the SN position (bottom panel of Fig. sn05ci ). We have also included the N2 measurement at the center of the host from SDSS, finding a perfect match to our results.

gmw_00011946.name: NGC 5630
gmw_00011946.overview: NGC 5630 is a strongly tilted galaxy located at a low redshift [arXiv:1308.5545]. It exhibits active star formation concentrated primarily in its central region and its eastern portion [arXiv:1308.5545].

sources.summary: NGC 5630 is presented as a tilted low-redshift galaxy with central and eastern star formation; 16 measurements show subsolar central and lower outer metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC 5630 is a strongly tilted (i = 72 deg) galaxy at low redshift (z = 0.008). It shows star formation mainly in the central region and in the eastern part. The slit was first placed (Fig. sn05dp , central-left panel) at the SN position and on a reference star in the north of the galaxy. The second spectrum (Fig. sn05dp , central-right panel) was taken along the major axis of the galaxy, as shown in Fig. sn05dp (top panel).

A total of 16 metallicity measurements were obtained, revealing slightly sub-solar metallicity at the center and a lower, LMC metallicity in the outer parts. The SN, which is located at _ SN /R _ 25 = 0.63, exploded in an environment with 12 + log(O/H) = 8.27 0.07 dex (Fig. sn05dp , bottom panel).

gmw_00011947.name: NGC 4141
gmw_00011947.overview: NGC 4141 is a barred spiral galaxy that hosted the supernova SN 2009E [arXiv:1308.5545]. The galaxy exhibits a moderately sub-solar central metallicity that decreases further with distance from its center [arXiv:1308.5545].

sources.summary: NGC 4141 is identified as the barred spiral host of SN 2009E, with five metallicity measurements showing moderately subsolar central abundance and a declining gradient.

quotes.quote: NGC 4141 is the barred spiral galaxy that hosted SN 2009E. It was observed with the NOT, by placing the slit through the SN position and through a very bright H ii region closer to the host center (see the top panels of Fig. sn09E ). A total of five metallicity measurements were obtained, including that at the exact SN position. The galaxy has a moderately sub-solar central metallicity (as also found by SDSS) that further decreases with the distance from the center (see the bottom panel of Fig. sn09E ). At the SN position the value is lower than the LMC metallicity.

gmw_00011948.name: TN J0121+1320
gmw_00011948.overview: TN J0121+1320 is a high-redshift radio galaxy with a high star formation rate of 626 solar masses per year [arXiv:2309.00459]. It contains a substantial reservoir of molecular gas, with observations of its broad carbon line indicating rotationally supported cold gas [arXiv:2309.00459].

sources.summary: TN J0121+1320 is the sample's anomalous [C I] source, with a broad 600 km/s line, high SFR, inferred molecular gas, and evidence for rotationally supported cold gas.

quotes.quote: In terms of [ C i ] velocity dispersion, the most anomalous source in our sample is TN J0121+1320 which has a [ C I ] line-width of 600 km s ^ 1 and is located above the Main Sequence of star-forming galaxies, as seen in Fig. fig:SFR-M-star . Within our sample, this source also has the highest SFR measure (see Table table:alma-ci-detected-galaxy-properties ) which places it at the upper reach of the Main Sequence in Fig. 5 from Falkendal2019 as well. In this work, we have obtained a tentative 2 [ C I ] detection from which we infer a molecular gas mass of 2.60 10 M _ . Furthermore, the [ C I ] line dispersion and inferred H _2 mass are both consistent with the galaxy's position above the Main Sequence which is evidence that is still has sufficient fuel star-formation at a relatively high level ( 626 M _ yr ^ 1 ) compared to the other HzRGs within our sample.

Similarly, at the host galaxy of TN J0121+1320, the CO(4-3) line flux of 1.2 Jy km s ^ 1 from deBreuck2003 leads to an inferred mass of M_ H_2, CO(4-3) 7.0 10 M _ , when we assume a CO(4-3)/CO(1-0) ratio of 0.5 and an _ CO = 0.8 M _ / (K km s^ 1 pc^2) . This is a factor of 3 larger than the [ C I ] inferred mass of M_ H_2, [ C I ] = (2.60 1.46) 10 M _ .
In one of the galaxies, TN J0121+1320 ( SFR 626 M _ yr ^ 1 .), a [ C I ] line-width of 600 km s ^ 1 is measured and is indicative of cold gas that is rotationally supported.

gmw_00011949.name: NGC 622
gmw_00011949.overview: NGC 622 is an active Markarian galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.017 [arXiv:astro-ph/0501090]. Multiple quasars, including several with Karlsson-peak redshifts, have been identified in the general vicinity of this galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0501090].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 622 as an active Markarian parent galaxy whose nearby quasars are analyzed for Karlsson-peak redshift associations and geometric links to the galaxy.

quotes.quote: The field to be considered, which is in the vicinity of the active ( z=0.017 ) Markarian galaxy NGC 622 , was chosen for two reasons: firstly, prior to the SDSS , there were known to be Karlsson-peak quasars at z = 0.91 and 1.46 only 71'' and 73'' away (Arp 1981; 1987). secondly, in the intervening years, the SDSS survey has identified further quasars in the general vicinity of NGC 622 which have not been previously examined.
Table Table1 lists all catalogued quasars within 33' of NGC622 with the exception of two objects which we associate with UM341 .

Note: Fig. 4 does not show all the NGC622 objects, but does include two Karlsson-peak quasars (bottom z=1.46, 3.63 ) not listed in either table. These two are omitted because, although they are Karlsson peak objects in the frame of NGC622 (or in the frame of either of the other two z=0.017 objects in the field), they lie outside the chosen 33' radius circle associated with NGC622 .

gmw_00011950.name: UM341
gmw_00011950.overview: UM341 is a bright active Seyfert galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.399 [arXiv:astro-ph/0501090]. It is a putative parent galaxy for several nearby quasars, including two well-aligned pairs that appear across the central active galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0501090].

sources.summary: The paper presents UM341 as a bright active Seyfert and putative parent galaxy for nearby quasars whose redshifts and alignments are tested against Karlsson peaks.

quotes.quote: However, the problem is complicated by the fact that the general field which contains all these newly catalogued quasars also contains another bright ( V=16.6 mag ) active galaxy - the Seyfert UM341 ( z=0.399 ) - which is also a putative parent galaxy for any quasars in the vicinity. What we shall show is that all of the catalogued quasars within approximately 35 arcmins of either of these two bright objects is a Karlsson peak object relative to one or the other of them.
Table 2 lists all catalogued quasars which lie within 36' of UM341 . Fig. 4 shows a 40' radius field centered on UM341 , with all the SDSS objects included.

When looking just around UM 341 it is discovered that the inner four quasars form two rather well aligned pairs across the central Seyfert. They are marked in Fig. 5. It is seen that the deviations from the Karlsson peaks tend to be positive on one side of the pair and negative on the other. This is what would be expected if the pair were ejected with a radial component of approaching velocity on one side and receding on the other. Moreover the centering of the active galaxy is fairly good and the quantitative values of the z's are closely equal (e.g. +.05 and -.05 across one pair).

gmw_00011951.name: PGC0039483
gmw_00011951.overview: PGC0039483 is a dwarf spiral galaxy located in the direction of the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1103.5735]. Classified as an Scd-type galaxy, it features a short bar, weak flocculence, and open spiral arms [arXiv:1103.5735].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC0039483 as a rare dwarf spiral toward Virgo, with small diameter, faint magnitude, aliases, a short bar, weak flocculence, open arms, and Scd classification.

quotes.quote: We found two such objects using the criteria 2 , and g absolute magnitude fainter than -17: PGC0039483 and PGC0040705, with magnitudes 16.9 and 14.8 , and intrinsic D_ 25 values of 5.7 kpc and 1.9 kpc. Both galaxies are located in the direction of the Virgo cluster, with J2000 RA . and DEC . coordinates (184.499 ^ ,6.654 ^ ) and (186.635 ^ ,12.610 ^ ), but with significantly smaller recession velocities of 791 km/s and 193 km/s (corrected for virgocentric infall). Other names for PGC0039483 are NGC 4241 and IC 3115, whereas PGC0040705 is also named NGC 4413 (NED also provides NGC 4407, but it is not recognised in HyperLeda).

Both galaxies have a short bar ( \ = 2) and weak flocculence ( \ = 1). PGC0039483 has open arms ( \ = 3), whereas those of PGC0040705 have an intermediate curvature ( \ = 2). The well designed spiral arms and small B/T ratio of PGC0039483 result in its assigned Scd type in both the RC3 and the EFIGI catalogue.

gmw_00011952.name: J121326.03+543631.7
gmw_00011952.overview: J121326.03+543631.7 is a dwarf companion galaxy of NGC 4194, located approximately 91 kiloparsecs to the northwest [arXiv:0808.3883]. The galaxy has an atomic hydrogen mass of 70 million solar masses, and there is no detected gas bridge connecting it to its larger neighbor [arXiv:0808.3883].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J121326.03+543631.7 as a nearby dwarf companion of NGC 4194, with similar velocity, no detected bridge, and a smaller gas mass limiting its influence.

quotes.quote: We were also able to detect in the dwarf galaxy J121326.03+543631.7 at a distance of 8.06' (91 kpc) to the North-West ( = 12^h 13^m 26.38^s, = +54^ 36' 31'' ). Until now, it was not known that NGC 4194 has a companion: our measurements show that this dwarf has a central velocity of 2380 km \ s ^ 1 , which is only 120 km \ s ^ 1 lower than that of NGC 4194, thus probably indicating that the two objects are physically connected. The measured velocity is in good agreement with its optical velocity of 2436 km \ s ^ 1 (SDSS data release 2, 2004). We carefully checked our channel maps for an bridge, but even in the naturally weighted maps no connection is visible. Figure n4194chap_n4194hispec shows the integrated spectra of NGC 4194 and the nearby galaxy derived from the data cube.
With an line width of 60 km \ s^ 1 and an mass of rm 7 10^7 M_ this dwarf galaxy is significantly smaller than NGC 4194 and thus does not play an important role for the evolution of NGC 4194.

gmw_00011953.name: UGC 07049
gmw_00011953.overview: UGC 07049 is a late-type Sc spiral galaxy located near NGC 4061 [arXiv:1007.3286]. It exhibits an asymmetric, truncated gas distribution and strong star formation, likely caused by a combination of tidal and ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:1007.3286].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 07049 as a deficient late-type Sc spiral near NGC 4061, with asymmetric truncated gas and strong star formation requiring tidal plus ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: UGC 07049 is a late type spiral at an angular distance of 3.4 ^ from NGC4061. It has a radial velocity of 7551 km s ^ 1 and is of the morphological type Sc. As has been mentioned earlier, this galaxy shows significant deficiency. Additionally, it may be traveling through the X-ray emitting hot gas in the core of this group which makes it an interesting target in which to study how a hot IGM affects the neutral gas content of spiral galaxies.

observations reveal an asymmetric, truncated distribution of in spiral galaxy, UGC 07049, and the accompanying radio continuum emission reveals strong star formation. We examine the effectiveness of ram pressure stripping as a gas removal mechanism and find that it alone cannot account for the deficiency that is observed in UGC 07049 unless this galaxy has passed through the core of the group with a velocity of 800 . A combination of tidal and ram pressure stripping are necessary to produce the deficiency and asymmetry in this galaxy.

gmw_00011954.name: Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy
gmw_00011954.overview: The Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy is currently located near its orbital pericenter [arXiv:astro-ph/0405260]. It has a polar orbit that crossed the Magellanic plane approximately 190 million years ago, a passage that may have stripped its gas and terminated its star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0405260].

sources.summary: The paper measures Fornax proper motion, derives a low-eccentricity polar orbit near pericenter, and links a recent Magellanic-plane crossing to gas stripping and halted star formation.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: the center of Fornax is at (0,0), i.e., at = 2^h 40^m 04^s , = -34 31 , J2000, (Walcher et al. 2003)

quotes.quote: We have measured the absolute proper motion of the Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy from a combination of photographic plate material and HST WFPC2 data that provide a time baseline of up to 50 years. The extragalactic reference frame consists of 8 QSO images and 48 galaxies. The absolute proper motion is _ cos = 0.59 0.16 mas yr ^ 1 and _ = -0.15 0.16 mas yr ^ 1 . The corresponding orbit of Fornax is polar, with an eccentricity of 0.27, and a radial period of 4.5 Gyr. Fornax's current location is near pericenter.

According to our orbit determination, Fornax crossed the Magellanic plane 190 Myr ago, a time that coincides with the termination of the star-formation process in Fornax. We propose that ram-pressure stripping due to the passage of Fornax through a gaseous medium denser than the typical intragalactic medium left behind from the LMC may have caused the end of star formation in Fornax. The excess, anomalous clouds within the South Galactic pole region of the Magellanic Stream whose origin has long been debated in the literature as constituents of either the Magellanic Stream or of the extragalactic Sculptor group, are found to lie along the orbit of Fornax. We speculate that these clouds are stripped material from Fornax as the dwarf crossed the Magellanic Clouds' orbit.

gmw_00011955.name: 1RXS J022716.6+020154
gmw_00011955.overview: 1RXS J022716.6+020154 is a BL Lacertae object and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0410692]. Its host galaxy is detected at a redshift of z=0.457, exhibiting characteristic Ca II and G-band absorption features [arXiv:astro-ph/0410692].

sources.summary: The VLT spectrum detects host-galaxy Ca II and G-band absorption at z=0.457, matching a prior photometric redshift estimate based on the host fundamental plane.

quotes.quote: 1RXS J022716.6+020154 Previous spectra obtained by nass96 are featureless. Images taken by nilsson03 detected the host galaxy with m _ R =19.6. Our high S/N spectrum clearly exhibits absorption features of the host galaxy (Ca II and G band absorptions) at z=0.457 (corresponding to a host galaxy magnitude M _ R =-23.1). This agrees with the photometric estimate of the redshift (z 0.45) by nilsson03 based on the host galaxy position on the fundamental plane.

gmw_00011956.name: PKS 0306+102
gmw_00011956.overview: PKS 0306+102 is an active galactic nucleus located at a confirmed redshift of z=0.862 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410692]. Based on the equivalent width of its emission lines, the object exhibits an intermediate nature between a BL Lacertae object and a polarized quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0410692].

sources.summary: The paper confirms PKS 0306+102 at z=0.862 from multiple emission lines and classifies it as intermediate between a BL Lac and a polarized QSO.

quotes.quote: PKS 0306+102 On the basis of a single emission line identified with MgII 2798 , veron94 proposed a tentative redshift of 0.863. Our spectrum clearly shows this line together with CII ] 2326 , [ NeV ] 3426 , [ OII ] 3727 \ and [ NeIII ] 3869 . The spectrum shows also the NaI 5892 \ absorption feature from the ISM of our galaxy. This yields a firm redshift determination of z=0.862.
