sources.summary: The paper classifies IC 874 among its most reliable pseudobulges, noting its low Faber-Jackson and fundamental-plane placement and possible bar influence on bulge formation.

quotes.quote: We find that the bulges of IC 874, NGC 1380 and NGC 4340 are the most reliable pseudobulges in our sample. The bulges of NGC 1308 and NGC 4340 are more rotation-dominated than classical bulges and oblate spheroid models in the V_ max / _ 0 - diagram (e.g., Kormendy Kennicutt 2004). The bulge of IC 874 is a low- outlier in the FJ relation and it is outside the 3 band in the FP of elliptical galaxies and S0 bulges. Since IC 874, NGC 1308 and NGC 4340 host the strongest bars of the sample (Table tab:bar ) we could speculate that the formation of their bulges has been influenced by the bar.

gmw_00012017.name: C1591
gmw_00012017.overview: C1591 is a starbursting active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:1703.08183]. Its carbon monoxide emission is marginally resolved and displays a broad, double-peaked profile that suggests a rotating galaxy [arXiv:1703.08183].

sources.summary: The paper describes C1591 as a starbursting AGN host detected in CO, with a broad double-peaked profile suggesting rotation, marginally extended CO emission, and a dynamically measured gas mass.

quotes.quote: C92 and C1591, both MS outliers, are detected with a >5 and 4 significance respectively, while 226, in the CDFS field and a MS candidate, is detected with 3 significance.

C1591 has a very broad CO emission line profile with a FWHM of 616 71 km/s as shown in Fig. fig:spectra , middle panel. The CO emission from C1591 shows a double peaked profile suggesting a rotating galaxy. The flux map corresponding to the integrated CO profile is shown in Fig. maps , top right panel. The overlaid white and yellow contours are the emission from these two peaks, each of width 200 km/s. The top left panel of Fig. maps shows the HST-ACS image of C1591 with CO emission line contours of the entire profile overlaid suggesting a similar extension of CO emission compared to the UV/optical image.

For C1591, a starbursting AGN system, the observed CO emission is marginally resolved and we could use dynamical analysis as well to measure the gas mass. The gas mass measured using the dynamical analysis agrees within the errors with that estimated using _ CO =3.6 .

gmw_00012018.name: C92
gmw_00012018.overview: C92 is a starburst galaxy and active galactic nucleus host that is classified as a main sequence outlier [arXiv:1703.08183]. It exhibits high extinction and features a narrow, possibly double-peaked carbon monoxide emission line profile that remains unresolved by ALMA [arXiv:1703.08183].

sources.summary: The paper treats C92 as a starburst AGN host with significant CO detection, a narrow possibly double-peaked CO profile, unresolved ALMA emission, and high extinction.

quotes.quote: C92 and C1591, both MS outliers, are detected with a >5 and 4 significance respectively, while 226, in the CDFS field and a MS candidate, is detected with 3 significance.

The CO emission line width in C92 is narrower with respect to C1591 and with a high significance of detection. Therefore, we were able to get the spectrum in smaller bins 50 km/s wide (see Fig. fig:spectra ). A closer look suggests a double peaked CO emission profile in C92 as well and we constructed the flux maps with the overlaid contours, similar to the case of C1591. This is shown in the middle panel of Fig. maps . The HST map does not reveal a wider extent of molecular gas in the host galaxy compared to the UV/optical image and the CO map shows that the emission is not resolved with the synthesized ALMA beam.

An X-shooter spectrum of C92, which is also known as XID60053, is available and the results were published in brusa15 . This object has a high extinction ( A_ V 6 ) due to which the H_ and [ OIII ] emission lines are barely detected.

gmw_00012019.name: UGC 7346
gmw_00012019.overview: UGC 7346 is a dwarf galaxy located in the peripheral regions of the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2302.08516]. It exhibits diffuse streams or shells from a recent 1:5 dwarf-dwarf merger, which concentrated its globular clusters toward the center [arXiv:2302.08516].

sources.summary: The paper argues UGC 7346 is a Virgo dwarf caught early in nuclear star cluster formation after a 1:5 dwarf-dwarf merger that concentrated its globular clusters.

quotes.quote: We analysed the dwarf galaxy UGC 7346, located in the peripheral regions of the Virgo Cluster, to highlight a series of properties that indicate the formation of a NSC caught in its earlier stages. First, we report on remnants of a past interaction in the form of diffuse streams or shells, suggesting a recent merging of two dwarf galaxies with a 1:5 stellar mass ratio. Second, we identify a number of globular cluster (GC) candidates that are broadly compatible in colour with the main component that is both more extended and more massive. Strikingly, we find these GCs candidates to be highly concentrated towards the centre of the galaxy (R _ GC = 0.41 R _ e ). We suggest that the central concentration of the GCs is likely produced by the dynamical friction of this merger.
This would make UGC 7346 a unique case of a galaxy caught in the earlier stages of NSC formation.

gmw_00012020.name: W0126--0529
gmw_00012020.overview: W0126--0529 is a star-forming galaxy located at a revised redshift of z=0.8301 [arXiv:1911.09828]. Diagnostic BPT classification indicates that this object is a non-AGN galaxy, making it the lowest-redshift source in its studied sample [arXiv:1911.09828].

sources.summary: The paper revises W0126--0529 to z=0.8301, identifies its previous redshift as a single-feature misclassification, and treats it as the sample's lowest-redshift star-forming, BPT non-AGN galaxy.

quotes.quote: However, two differ drastically: quality ``A'' z=0.8301 for W0126--0529, previously z=2.937 , and quality ``B'' z=2.958 for W2042--3245 We have S/N=2.0 (Ly ), 1.8 ( C 4 ), and 4.2 ( Mg 2 ) from XSHOOTER, but with weak S/N, this is still classified as quality ``B''. , previously z=3.963 . The two mismatches occur for redshifts previously determined from Gemini/GMOS observed-frame 4000--6500 \ spectra Tsa15 . Both were quality ``B'' redshifts, derived by the detection, but uncertain identification, of a single feature. The broadband VLT spectra emphasizes the importance of multiple line detections for confident redshift determinations, although our quality ``B'' redshifts could still be biased by noise and we remove those sources from further analysis.

The Seyfert types shown in Table 6 range from type 1.5 (intermediate H to [ O 3 ] , 2/8), to type 1.8 and higher (weak H to [ O 3 ] , 6/8), apart from the undetermined type for W0126--0529 (which turns out not to be an AGN, Table 7) and a loosely determined type for W1719+0446 (type 1.2).

Combined, seven are BPT AGN, two are unconstrained (W0147--0923, W1719+0446), and the remaining object is star-forming (W0126--0529). The latter source, the lowest redshift galaxy in our sample, is not considered in the following when deducing and discussing AGN properties of our targets.

gmw_00012021.name: W1103--1826
gmw_00012021.overview: W1103--1826 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:1911.09828]. It is characterized by a narrow-component velocity-offset exception and exhibits blueshifted broad Balmer lines that are weaker than its broad [O III] emission [arXiv:1911.09828].

sources.summary: W1103--1826 is flagged as an [O III] narrow-component velocity-offset exception, and its broad Balmer lines are weaker than [O III] but blueshifted with a measured Balmer decrement.

quotes.quote: This assumption appears valid for our sample as most of the narrow Gaussian components have offsets within 100 \ to the systemic redshift (with exceptions for W1103--1826 and W2026+0716), for non- H 2 BPT sources with line S/N 5 (Table 3 and Figure 5).

The figure includes the 5/7 BPT non- H 2 Hot DOGs with significantly broad H ( _ H , broad/narrow >400 ) with S/N > 5 from Table 3 (W0114--0812, W1103--1826, W1136+4236, W2136--1631, W2216+0723). The normalized broad Balmer line components are weaker than the broad [ O 3 ] but are blueshifted for all five objects, with absolute values comparable to or smaller than the broad [ O 3 ] blueshift with a fraction of 0.53--1.23 times (median 0.65).

We check this by calculating the Balmer decrement of objects having S/N 5 in H and showing a broad component in both H /H (W1103--1826, W1136+4236, W2136--1631, W2216+0723). The values are 1.93 1.02, 4.28 0.87, 1.93 0.22, and 5.95 2.17 respectively, all comparable to or smaller than the narrow Balmer decrements in Table 8.

gmw_00012022.name: W1136+4236
gmw_00012022.overview: W1136+4236 is classified as a Hot Dust-Obscured Galaxy [arXiv:1911.09828]. It features broad, blueshifted Balmer line components and has an estimated electron density of approximately 1100 cm⁻³ [arXiv:1911.09828].

sources.summary: W1136+4236 has measured [S II] density diagnostics and belongs to the Hot DOG subset with broad, blueshifted Balmer components and a Balmer decrement comparable to the narrow-line value.

quotes.quote: For the objects on which both of the [ S 2 ] doublet lines are detected with S/N 3, namely W1136+4236 and W2136--1631, we measure the [ S 2 ] 6716 / [ S 2 ] 6731 ratios of 0.82 0.19 and 1.02 0.21 , respectively. Assuming a 10,000K temperature, the line ratios correspond to electron densities n_ e 1100 and 600 cm^ 3 , respectively Ost89 .

The figure includes the 5/7 BPT non- H 2 Hot DOGs with significantly broad H ( _ H , broad/narrow >400 ) with S/N > 5 from Table 3 (W0114--0812, W1103--1826, W1136+4236, W2136--1631, W2216+0723). The normalized broad Balmer line components are weaker than the broad [ O 3 ] but are blueshifted for all five objects, with absolute values comparable to or smaller than the broad [ O 3 ] blueshift with a fraction of 0.53--1.23 times (median 0.65).

We check this by calculating the Balmer decrement of objects having S/N 5 in H and showing a broad component in both H /H (W1103--1826, W1136+4236, W2136--1631, W2216+0723). The values are 1.93 1.02, 4.28 0.87, 1.93 0.22, and 5.95 2.17 respectively, all comparable to or smaller than the narrow Balmer decrements in Table 8.

gmw_00012023.name: W1719+0446
gmw_00012023.overview: W1719+0446 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy with a loosely determined Seyfert 1.2 classification [arXiv:1911.09828]. It exhibits significantly broad [O II] emission, which is consistent with either strong active galactic nucleus outflows or a highly disturbed interstellar medium [arXiv:1911.09828].

sources.summary: W1719+0446 has an uncertain type constrained using [O III] in place of undetected H, a loose type 1.2 classification, and significantly broad [O II] consistent with outflow or disturbed ISM.

quotes.quote: Ranges in the values are given taking into account the uncertainty in the H / [ O 3 ] ratio, 3-sigma upper limit on H flux ( [ O 3 ] instead of H for W1719+0446 due to complete undetection), and the absence of broad Balmer line detectability at S/N < 5.

The Seyfert types shown in Table 6 range from type 1.5 (intermediate H to [ O 3 ] , 2/8), to type 1.8 and higher (weak H to [ O 3 ] , 6/8), apart from the undetermined type for W0126--0529 (which turns out not to be an AGN, Table 7) and a loosely determined type for W1719+0446 (type 1.2).

In addition to the likelihood of outflows in the narrow Balmer line region for some Hot DOGs, we plot in the remaining panels of Figure 8 the 3/6 BPT non- H 2 sources with a significantly broad [ O 2 ] ( _ O II , broad/narrow >400 , W1719+0446, W2026+0716, W2216+0723) with S/N > 5. Additionally, 1/3 of the remaining (3/6) sources (W0114--0812) shows _ O II , broad/narrow >300 . This can either be explained by strong AGN outflows broadening the [ O 2 ] line profile, or the kinematics of the ISM in the star-forming region being highly disturbed by AGN outflows and/or galaxy merging (e.g., Dia16,Dia18 ). As [ O 2 ] is a forbidden line, a fraction of them being broad adds support for the presence of outflows, and hints for kpc scale, narrow line region origin for at least some the broadened Balmer lines.

gmw_00012024.name: W2136--1631
gmw_00012024.overview: W2136--1631 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy classified as a Hot DOG [arXiv:1911.09828]. It exhibits a broad, blueshifted hydrogen outflow, and its [S II] doublet lines indicate an electron density of approximately 600 cm⁻³ [arXiv:1911.09828].

sources.summary: W2136--1631 has [S II] density diagnostics, broad H in the Hot DOG outflow subset, and UV/optical Hydrogen and Mg 2 profiles indistinguishable from [O III].

quotes.quote: For the objects on which both of the [ S 2 ] doublet lines are detected with S/N 3, namely W1136+4236 and W2136--1631, we measure the [ S 2 ] 6716 / [ S 2 ] 6731 ratios of 0.82 0.19 and 1.02 0.21 , respectively. Assuming a 10,000K temperature, the line ratios correspond to electron densities n_ e 1100 and 600 cm^ 3 , respectively Ost89 .

The figure includes the 5/7 BPT non- H 2 Hot DOGs with significantly broad H ( _ H , broad/narrow >400 ) with S/N > 5 from Table 3 (W0114--0812, W1103--1826, W1136+4236, W2136--1631, W2216+0723). The normalized broad Balmer line components are weaker than the broad [ O 3 ] but are blueshifted for all five objects, with absolute values comparable to or smaller than the broad [ O 3 ] blueshift with a fraction of 0.53--1.23 times (median 0.65).

Although we are limited to a single object (W2136--1631) in Figure 8 covering all the major UV/optical broad emission lines, the Hydrogen lines (Ly , H , H ) and Mg 2 profiles are indistinguishable from [ O 3 ] , with C 4 showing a stronger broad/blueshifted wing component.

gmw_00012025.name: LRG 1440-0156
gmw_00012025.overview: LRG 1440-0156 is a luminous red galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.5 [arXiv:1809.09777]. The galaxy has negligible current star formation, yet it is surrounded by an extremely strong, cool circumgalactic medium [arXiv:1809.09777].

sources.summary: The paper presents LRG 1440-0156 as a z~0.5 luminous red galaxy with a 350 kpc sightline and extremely strong cool CGM absorption despite negligible recent star formation.

quotes.quote: Images from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) of LRG 1059+4039 and LRG 1440-0156 are shown in Figure fig:lrgs . Both LRGs are located at z 0.5 and have impact parameters of 30 kpc and 350 kpc, typical of the full sample.

The spectra shown in Figures fig:lines and fig:lines2 demonstrate the presence of extremely strong, cool CGM absorption-profiles surrounding LRG 1059+4039 and LRG 1440-0156. To provide context, we compare the measured C 3 977 EWs against the data for low- z L^* galaxies from the COS-Halos survey Figure fig:ewmst ; Werk13 . The C iii EWs of the two LRG CGM ( 1.8 \ and 1.2 \ ) clearly exceed the entire L^* galaxy distribution.

In our experiment, the targeted galaxies are `red and dead' with negligible current star-formation (SF) and no signature of SF activity over at least the past Gyr. Therefore, we rule out feedback from recent star-formation from LRGs as a viable origin for the observed CGM.

gmw_00012026.name: BG1501+3042
gmw_00012026.overview: BG1501+3042 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.645 [arXiv:1912.04033]. It is a strongly lensed system consisting of an interacting or merging galaxy pair with three detected lensed images [arXiv:1912.04033].

sources.summary: The paper confirms BG1501+3042 as a new lensed LAE with three detected images; lens modeling and source-plane structure suggest an interacting or merging galaxy pair.

quotes.quote: BG1501+3042 was initially selected by as a lensed LAE candidate by the detection of an asymmetric emission line at 4435 (likely Ly emission at z=2.649 ) in the BOSS spectrum of the z= 0.638 LRG SDSS J150114.60+304230.8.

Therefore, BG1501+3042 comprises three detected lensed images at the seeing-limited resolution. Such configuration is uncommon in galaxy-scale lensed systems, which typically present two or four lensed images see, however, other lensed configurations produced by groups or clusters of galaxies, e.g., oguri2008, dahle2013, shu2018 . To understand this system in more detail, we use the 420 s GTC band image ( 0.9^ FWHM) to perform the lens modeling for BG1501+3042.
One of these, BG1501+3042 at z=2.645 , is a new confirmed lensed LAE with three detected lensed images. This system is composed by a pair interacting/merging galaxies magnified by a factor of 3.2 .

gmw_00012027.name: PG1437+398
gmw_00012027.overview: PG1437+398 is a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2310.08989]. Radio observations reveal a polarized core-halo morphology with a flat spectral index and a kiloparsec-scale jet-like extension pointing south [arXiv:2310.08989].

sources.summary: The paper describes PG1437+398 as an HSP BL Lac with prior VLBI and LOFAR indications of structure; uGMRT imaging shows a polarized core-halo source with a kpc jet-like extension.

quotes.quote: PG1437+398 This object was identified as a BL Lac object by Bauer2000,Piranomonte2007 . It is an HSP blazar Paiano2017A . It belongs to the RGB sample of intermediate BL Lacs Laurent1999 . A possible parsec scale jet towards +45 is seen in the 8.3 GHz VLBI image in the Radio Fundamental Catalog http://astrogeo.org/rfc/ RFC; Petrov Kovalev, in preparation, as noted in Petrov2019 . The LOFAR image by Mooney2021 shows an unresolved structure with a possible extension towards the east.

Our 650 MHz uGMRT image (Figure fig7:Pg1437 ) shows a core-halo morphology with indications of a kpc scale jet-like extension in the southern direction. The core is polarized and the inferred B-fields there are perpendicular to the kpc jet axis. The spectral index image shows the core to be flat.

gmw_00012028.name: PG2254+075
gmw_00012028.overview: PG2254+075 is a radio-selected BL Lacertae object and a low-synchrotron-peaked blazar [arXiv:2310.08989]. Radio imaging of the source reveals a core-halo morphology, with polarization structures in the core and lobe that suggest a spine-sheath configuration [arXiv:2310.08989].

sources.summary: The paper presents PG2254+075/OY+091 as a radio-selected LSP BL Lac; uGMRT imaging shows core-halo morphology and a spine-sheath-like polarization structure.

quotes.quote: PG2254+075 a.k.a. OY +091 This is a radio-selected BL Lac belonging to the 1 Jy sample, as well as the MOJAVE sample. It is an LSP blazar Giroletti2004,Ajello2022 . The Fermi ray point source associated with this source is offset from its radio core by 6.235 Ajello2022 .

Our 650 MHz uGMRT (Figure fig9:PG2254 ) image shows a core-halo morphology. The core polarization shows B-fields perpendicular to the VLBI jet. The core and lobe show clearly differing polarization structures with the inferred B-fields in the lobes appearing to be largely tangential. This core-lobe polarization structure is reminiscent of a `spine-sheath structure' with the `sheath' displaying primarily toroidal magnetic fields see IIIZw2 in Silpa2021 . The spectral index image shows the core to be relatively flat with the spectra steepening in the halo/lobes.

gmw_00012029.name: JO45
gmw_00012029.overview: The galaxy JO45 exhibits a cloud-like resolved star formation rate-mass distribution rather than an elongated sequence [arXiv:1907.00976]. Its distribution median deviates significantly from the total sample relation and features an asymmetric residual tail [arXiv:1907.00976].

sources.summary: The paper describes JO45 as having a cloud-like resolved SFR-mass distribution, a median offset from the full-sample relation, and an asymmetric residual tail, supporting galaxy-to-galaxy variation.

quotes.quote: Some galaxies show quite elongated sequences (e.g. A3128 B 0148, P57486), while in some other cases a cloud rather than a sequence is observed (e.g. JO45, JO89).

In contrast, for some galaxies (e.g. JO45, JO41, JO205, P15703), the median of the distribution is significantly different, suggesting that the of the galaxy significantly deviates from the total one. In addition to the median values, also the shapes of the distributions give important information: while in some cases they are quite narrow (e.g. A970 B 0338, A3128 B 0148), in many other cases they are broad (e.g. P13384), show asymmetric tails (e.g. JO45) and are even double peaked (e.g. P20769).

gmw_00012030.name: P15703
gmw_00012030.overview: The galaxy P15703 exhibits a high-density bending in its star formation relation that remains present even when the bulge contribution is removed [arXiv:1907.00976]. Additionally, its median distribution significantly deviates from the total relation, and the galaxy lies well below the Kennicutt-Schmidt law [arXiv:1907.00976].

sources.summary: The paper identifies P15703 as showing a high-density bend independent of bulge masking, a median deviation from the total relation, and a position below the Kennicutt-Schmidt law.

quotes.quote: Few galaxies really show a flattening (A3376 B 0261, P669) or bending (P48157, P15703) of the relation at higher values. Note that this behaviour is not due to the presence of the bulge, as trends stay also when the contribution of the bulge is removed (plot not shown). Moretti et al. (in prep.) will provide a detailed description of these objects presenting anomalies in the central regions.

In contrast, for some galaxies (e.g. JO45, JO41, JO205, P15703), the median of the distribution is significantly different, suggesting that the of the galaxy significantly deviates from the total one.
Some galaxies lie on the Kennicutt-Schmidt law (even though with a large scatter - e.g. JO68), other lie well below it (e.g. P15703).

gmw_00012031.name: NGC 5567-cE2
gmw_00012031.overview: NGC 5567-cE2 is a massive compact elliptical galaxy currently in an early stage of tidal stripping [arXiv:1709.07012]. It is characterized by typically old stellar ages, sub-solar metallicity, and a history of recent, low-rate star formation [arXiv:1709.07012].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 5567-cE2 as a massive disrupted compact elliptical in an early stripping stage, with old ages, sub-solar metallicity, and recent low-level star formation.

quotes.quote: The most massive objects of our sample are two galaxies of the disrupted class (NGC 5567-cE2 and CZcE57), which also show the largest sizes in the sample. This could indicate that they are in an early stage of stripping and thus still have to experience structural changes, with further evolution towards smaller sizes and stellar masses. Despite having flagged unreliable sizes, such an early stage is still plausible even if considering a smaller size and thus a smaller stellar mass (see Figure 16).

We find that this is the case for CZcE44, CZcE57, CZcE95 and NGC 5567-cE2, which show typically old ages ( <t> = 11.0 Gyr) and sub-solar metallicities ( < [ Z/H ] > = 0.18 dex). Their early peaked SFHs with subsequent star forming episodes at a very low rate are compatible with having a progenitor of the ETG-like type.

gmw_00012032.name: CZcE57
gmw_00012032.overview: CZcE57 is one of the most massive disrupted compact elliptical galaxies [arXiv:1709.07012]. It is likely in an early stage of tidal stripping and hosts an old, sub-solar stellar population [arXiv:1709.07012].

sources.summary: The paper describes CZcE57 as one of the most massive disrupted compact ellipticals, probably in an early tidal-stripping stage with old, sub-solar stellar populations.

quotes.quote: The most massive objects of our sample are two galaxies of the disrupted class (NGC 5567-cE2 and CZcE57), which also show the largest sizes in the sample. This could indicate that they are in an early stage of stripping and thus still have to experience structural changes, with further evolution towards smaller sizes and stellar masses. Despite having flagged unreliable sizes, such an early stage is still plausible even if considering a smaller size and thus a smaller stellar mass (see Figure 16).

We find that this is the case for CZcE44, CZcE57, CZcE95 and NGC 5567-cE2, which show typically old ages ( <t> = 11.0 Gyr) and sub-solar metallicities ( < [ Z/H ] > = 0.18 dex). Their early peaked SFHs with subsequent star forming episodes at a very low rate are compatible with having a progenitor of the ETG-like type.

gmw_00012033.name: NGC 5567-cE1
gmw_00012033.overview: NGC 5567-cE1 is a low-mass compact elliptical galaxy that has recently begun undergoing a tidal stripping process [arXiv:1709.07012]. It exhibits a sub-solar metallicity and an extended star formation history that distinguishes it from other galaxies in its class [arXiv:1709.07012].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5567-cE1 as an unusual disrupted-class compact elliptical: sub-solar, extended-SFH, likely intrinsic low-mass ETG now beginning stripping.

quotes.quote: The exception is NGC 5567-cE1, which shows a sub-solar metallicity that is compatible with its stellar mass. While the progenitor would still be compatible with being a spiral-like one, such metallicity is more similar to what is seen for the rest of the disrupted galaxies -- that is, more representative of an early stage of tidal stripping.
NGC 5567-cE1 is the only galaxy in this class that shows a completely different SFH. This one resembles to the extended SFHs of the majority of the no host galaxies.

The finding of cEs that have just started being stripped further emphasizes the fact the even a galaxy with an intrinsic origin might be affected by a stripping process at some point of their life, like the low mass ETG NGC5567-cE1, which has just started its journey of being stripped.

gmw_00012034.name: J16+14
gmw_00012034.overview: J16+14 is a compact elliptical galaxy that lacks a host galaxy [arXiv:1709.07012]. It exhibits abnormally high metallicities and properties that are compatible with a runaway stripped origin [arXiv:1709.07012].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J16+14 as a no-host compact elliptical whose high metallicity, young age region, mass ratio, and other properties favor a runaway stripped origin.

quotes.quote: Similarly, the no host galaxy J16+14 presents abnormally high metallicities, pointing out that this would be, instead, a run away stripped galaxy.
Interestingly, the other galaxy with a large mass ratio is J16+14. This is the same no host galaxy that has shown properties more compatible with being a run-away case of a stripped galaxy.

Additionally, we found that the properties of J16+14, a galaxy without a host, are more compatible with having such stripped origin. This means that this galaxy could have been first stripped in a more dense environment and it was later ejected from it Chilingarian2015 .

gmw_00012035.name: NGC 3690W
gmw_00012035.overview: NGC 3690W is the highly disturbed western component of the interacting Arp 299 system and contains multiple nuclei [arXiv:1810.09897]. This irregular galaxy hosts a large population of 1,120 star cluster candidates, which exhibit brighter absolute magnitudes than those in the eastern component [arXiv:1810.09897].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3690W as Arp 299's disturbed western component, with multiple nuclei, 1120 cluster candidates, and brighter SSC magnitudes than the eastern component.

quotes.quote: The interacting system consists of a pair of two irregular galaxies: NGC 3690E NGC 3690E is commonly known as source A in the literature. in the eastern part, and NGC 3690W NGC 3690W is commonly known as sources B C. the western component. While the former is possibly the remnant of a retrograde spiral galaxy, the latter has a more disturbed morphology with multiple nuclei.

A number of 988 and 1120 star cluster candidates were recovered for NGC 3690E and NGC 3690W, respectively. With a relatively large SSC population, NGC 3690W also hosts candidates with brighter magnitudes than the ones in the eastern component. For instance, the star clusters of NGC 3690W have B band absolute magnitudes between 15.2 mag and 6.5 mag. This range becomes 13.1 M_B 6.5 mag in the field of NGC 3690E.

gmw_00012036.name: COS24563
gmw_00012036.overview: COS24563 is an extended star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1909.02572]. It is located slightly above the main sequence, with interstellar medium properties that suggest it may be progressively forming a compact core [arXiv:1909.02572].

sources.summary: The paper models COS24563 as an extended star-forming galaxy just above the main sequence, with ISM properties similar to GS30274 and possible compact-core growth.

quotes.quote: We found that one of our extended SFGs was observed in that survey, namely COS24563 (which corresponds to 18538 in 2018ApJ...869...27V ). We performed PDR modeling for this galaxy and also for GS30274 in 2017A&A...602A..11P for consistency in the methodology and to avoid systematics in the comparison.

COS24563 and GS30274 have similar properties as seen in Figure  fig:ism . They are both located in the intersection between normal SFGs and SBs ISM properties. COS24563 is placed at a MS slightly above the scatter of the MS, suggesting that the scatter at higher MS is linked to galaxies progressively forming compact cores, similar to the interpretation drawn from the CO excitation.

gmw_00012037.name: CGCG097-087N
gmw_00012037.overview: CGCG097-087N is a small companion galaxy that is experiencing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:1707.06241, arXiv:2202.03443]. This process has dragged ionized gas out of the galaxy into two distinct tails, while kinematics suggest a past tidal interaction with its larger neighbor [arXiv:1707.06241, arXiv:2202.03443].

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG 97087N as UGC 6697's smaller companion, stripped into double gas tails whose kinematics suggest ram pressure and possible interaction with UGC 6697.

quotes.quote: CGCG 97087N, a small companion that lies at few arcminutes in projection from UGC 6697, is also suffering from the hydrodynamic action of the ICM of the cluster. Along the whole extent of the tail we detect diffuse H emission and, to a lesser extent, H , [ OIII ] 5007 , and [ OI ] 6300 . By comparing the kinematics and distribution of gas and stars (as traced by the CaII triplet) for both galaxies, we separate the ionized gas, as traced by the H line, in a component still bound to the galaxy and a component that is stripped.

Fig. 87N_kin shows the velocity field of CGCG 97087N for its stellar and gaseous component separately. For the gaseous component, we plot the high velocity component since we detect only few spaxels in the low velocity component, representing a negligible percentage of the flux belonging to CGCG 97087N. The map shows the gas being dragged out of the galaxy by the ram pressure from NE to SW.

The ionized gas is stripped along two tails that are separated spatially and kinematically: the southern (S) tail and the northern (N) tail. Along the N tail we detect velocities that are very similar to the velocities of the stars (between -100 and 100 km s^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats CGCG097-087N as CGCG097-087's smaller companion, affected by both ram-pressure stripping and galaxy-galaxy tidal interaction with distinctive tail line ratios.

quotes.quote: Its smaller companion (CGCG097-087N) lies at 20 arcsec in the NE direction from CGCG097-087 and recent Subaru observations Yagi17 were highly suggestive of galaxy-galaxy interactions due to double gaseous tails connecting the two galaxies. CGCC097-087 and CGCG097-087N have a stellar mass of (M_*/ M_ ) = 10.13 and (M_*/ M_ ) = 8.78 respectively, computed from g- and i- band photometry following the colour calibrations by Zibetti09 . The analysis of this galaxy pair using the high sensitivity and spatial resolution of MUSE gave us unique new information about the hydrodynamic processes driving the stripping and the interaction of the two systems, and most importantly about the physics in the tails.
Furthermore, also CGCG097-087N is suffering RPS from the ICM of the cluster, even if kinematics indicators suggest also a past galaxy-galaxy interaction.

An outlier with respect to the other points is the CGCG097-087N, marked by a yellow triangle in the BPT diagrams. We notice in fact that this region shows an higher value compared to the other galaxies in all ratios on the x-axis of the BPT diagrams: [ NII ] /H , [ OI ] /H , and [ SII ] /H . This is probably related to the tidal interaction driven by its higher mass companion CGCG097-087, as already discussed in Consolandi17 .

gmw_00012038.name: NGC 2310
gmw_00012038.overview: NGC 2310 is a galaxy characterized by a box-shaped bulge and a narrow underlying stellar disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164]. Its bulge or bar structure is slightly rotated against the outer disk, and its radial profile is influenced by an inner ring-like feature [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164].

sources.summary: NGC 2310 is described as having a box-shaped bulge, a slightly rotated bulge/bar, complex radial profiles, a narrow underlying disk, and model residuals affected by an inner ring-like feature.

quotes.quote: NGC 2310: ( n2310 ) exhibits a box-shaped bulge . The bulge/bar is rotated 0.5 against the outer disk, therefore we find a slightly tilted residual. The radial profiles show a complex structure with inner, small, very bright nuclear bulge component, and further out a significant dip in the profile (caused by a possible dust ring or mimicked by a subsequent stellar ring), sitting on top of a bar/ring-like structure R 50 . There is a narrow disk-like structure R 82 , here assumed to be the main underlying disk, finally showing a straight decline into the noise (NE-side disturbed by nearby star-halo). The best fitting model overestimates the inner disk component due to inner ring-like feature.

gmw_00012039.name: ESO 311-012
gmw_00012039.overview: ESO 311-012 is a galaxy located close to the Galactic plane with many foreground stars superimposed [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164]. It features a box-shaped bulge and a radial profile that indicates a bar-like structure with a weak bar extending on both sides [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164].

sources.summary: The paper notes ESO 311-012 has a box-shaped bulge, lies close to the Galactic plane with foreground stars, and shows a bar-like radial structure plus weak-bar residuals.

quotes.quote: ESO 311-012: ( e311012 ) Box-shaped bulge . The galaxy position is close to the Galactic plane with many foreground stars overimposed. The bulge component is still visible on the minor axis high above the plane. The radial profile shows a bar-like structure at R 20 , followed by an exponential disk component out to a break at R 83 . The residual is a box-shaped bulge with the indication of a weak bar extending on both sides.

gmw_00012040.name: NGC 3564
gmw_00012040.overview: NGC 3564 is a bulge-dominated galaxy situated within a large, roughly spherical stellar envelope [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164]. This envelope was likely built up by a merger with a smaller satellite galaxy, which remains visible on the northeastern side [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164].

sources.summary: NGC 3564 is described as group-associated, bulge-dominated, embedded in a huge spherical stellar envelope, and likely affected by a smaller satellite merger visible in the structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3564: ( n3564 ) The galaxy is probably part of small group/cluster with at least two large, nearby physical companions (NGC 3568 and NGC 3557). Taking into account the low surface brightness part it is different from the other 0s. Out to _ V 23.4 it looks similar although with a more dominating bulge. The outer contours, on the other hand, reveal that NGC 3564 is sitting in a huge, roughly spherical stellar envelope, which has probably been built up by a merger with a smaller satellite galaxy still visible in this structure on the NE-side ( (R,z) 47 ,+24 , distance unknown). The radial profile shows an inner bulge-dominated part, followed by a bar-like plateau R 10 , sitting on top of an inner disk structure out to R 30 which is followed by a straight decline into noise.

gmw_00012041.name: NGC 4521
gmw_00012041.overview: NGC 4521 is characterized by an inner component that is rotated against its outer disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164]. Its radial profile reveals a bar- or ring-like structure on top of a two-slope disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164].

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4521 has a rotated inner component, bar/ring-like radial structure, a two-slope disk, and inconsistent thick/thin disk fitting despite multiple trials.

quotes.quote: NGC 4521: ( n4521 ) The inner component is 1 rotated against the outer disk. The radial profile shows a bar- or ring-like structure between R 20 out to z 5 ; on top of a two-slope disk, inner part out to a break radius of R 60 , followed by a steeper straight decline out to R 77 and a slightly upwards bending part at the very edge (perhaps flatfield problem). The only galaxy with inconsistent thick/thin disk fitting without obvious reason ( NGC 1596). All trials, such as changing vertical fitting function, have failed. When various thick disk models have been subtracted, the resulting thin disks always contributes too much light in the outer/higher z profiles dominated by the thick disk.

gmw_00012042.name: NGC 5047
gmw_00012042.overview: NGC 5047 is a galaxy featuring a peanut-shaped bulge and an inner disk that is slightly off-centered relative to its outer component [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164]. Its outer disk exhibits a subtle S-shaped warp, and the galaxy is surrounded by numerous companions associated with the foreground NGC 5044 group [arXiv:astro-ph/0404164].

sources.summary: NGC 5047 is described with a peanut-shaped bulge, nearby group companions, an S-shaped outer warp, an off-centered inner disk, and bar/ring-like radial profiles.

quotes.quote: NGC 5047: ( n5047 ) Peanut-shaped bulge . There are many companions around associated with the foreground NGC 5044 group of galaxies ( v 3600 ). Outer disk has a slightly s-shaped warp, on the SW-side towards N, on the NE-side towards S. The inner disk part is slightly off-centered to the outer component. The radial profiles show a bar-, or ring-like feature between R 30 out to z 3 , on top of a shoulder-like inner disk out to R 50 , followed by a straight decline out to _ V 25.0 .

gmw_00012043.name: Arp 299-A
gmw_00012043.overview: Arp 299-A is the A-nucleus of a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1802.03226]. It hosts a young, compact starburst that drives a two-sided, filamentary radio outflow extending up to 14 arcseconds [arXiv:1802.03226].

sources.summary: The paper presents sub-arcsecond LOFAR imaging of Arp 299-A and argues its two-sided filamentary radio structure is a young starburst-driven outflow rather than AGN-driven activity.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The peak of brightness of the image is located at R.A. = 11^h28^m33.487^s Dec. = +58 33'47.044'' (J2000.0)

quotes.quote: We report on the first sub-arcsecond (0.44 0.41 arcsec ^2 ) angular resolution image at 150 MHz of the A-nucleus in the luminous infrared galaxy Arp 299, from International Low Frequency Array (LOFAR) Telescope observations. The most remarkable finding is that of an intriguing two-sided, filamentary structure emanating from the A-nucleus, which we interpret as an outflow that extends up to at least 14 arcseconds from the A-nucleus in the N-S direction ( 5 kpc deprojected size) and accounts for almost 40 of the extended emission of the entire galaxy system.

We also discuss HST/NICMOS [ FeII ] 1.64 m and H _2 2.12 m images of Arp 299-A, which show similar features to those unveiled by our 150 MHz LOFAR observations, providing strong morphological support for the outflow scenario. Finally, we discuss unpublished Na I D spectra that confirm the outflow nature of this structure. From energetic arguments, we rule out the low-luminosity active galactic nucleus in Arp 299-A as a driver for the outflow. On the contrary, the powerful, compact starburst in the central regions of Arp 299-A provides plenty of mechanical energy to sustain an outflow, and we conclude that the intense supernova (SN) activity in the nuclear region of Arp 299-A is driving the observed outflow. We estimate that the starburst wind can support a mass-outflow rate in the range (11 - 63) M_ yr^ 1 at speeds of up to (370 - 890) km s^ 1 , and is relatively young, with an estimated kinematic age of (3 - 7) Myr.

gmw_00012044.name: BCG
gmw_00012044.overview: The BCG is an elliptical brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0308024]. It is offset from the nominal center of the cluster's X-ray isophotal contours, though it is associated with nearly circular soft-band X-ray emission and exhibits distorted nearby isophotes [arXiv:astro-ph/0308024].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the cluster brightest galaxy as an elliptical offset from the X-ray contour center, with distorted nearby isophotes and associated soft-band X-ray emission.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The elliptical BCG is at 04h 54m 10.8s, 03   00   52.4 ( x=4164 , y=3856 in the physical coordinates of the original Chandra image).

quotes.quote: We note that the nominal center of the X-ray isophotal contours is not located on the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG), identified by a diamond on Figure ms0451_adapt . The large galaxy to the south of the BCG is a foreground galaxy (John Stocke, private communication). The isophotes near the BCG seem to be distorted, perhaps suggesting that the BCG or a system centered on the BCG may be contributing to the surface brightness there. The elliptical BCG is at 04h 54m 10.8s, 03 00 52.4 ( x=4164 , y=3856 in the physical coordinates of the original Chandra image).

In the soft bandpass, the highest peak lies on the BCG, with a secondary peak to the east-south-east. The centroid is somewhat west of the primary peak, near 4h 54m 11.26s, 03 00 56 . In the mid-energy bandpass, the brightest peak is closer to the soft bandpass secondary peak, and a fainter peak is associated with the BCG. The centroid is near 4h 54m 11.32s, 03 00 56 , 6 west of the centroid in the soft band. In the hardest energy, there is no obvious peak near the BCG, and the centroid of this single-peaked distribution is close to the position of the primary peak in the mid-energy bandpass. The hard peak 4h 54m 11.48s, 03 00 55 . The soft X-ray emission near the BCG is nearly circular, embedded in a large-scale elliptical emission closer to that of the global cluster.

gmw_00012045.name: 3C 200
gmw_00012045.overview: The radio galaxy 3C 200 exhibits extended X-ray emission that is detectable out to 10 arcseconds from its peak [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606]. However, point-like emission dominates the core region within a 2.5-pixel radius [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606].

sources.summary: The paper reports extended X-ray emission around 3C 200 but finds point-like core emission dominates the spectral aperture; its core X-ray properties had not been previously described.

quotes.quote: The radial profile of 3C 200 shows that extended emission starts to dominate at 2 arcsec from the X-ray peak. However, only 2 counts contribute to the extended flux in the circular region used for the spectral analysis, and this is much less than any statistical error.

3C 200 : the source shows extended X-ray emission out to 10 arcsec, but point-like emission dominates within the 2.5 pixel radius of the circle used for the core spectral analysis. The X-ray properties of the core of this source have not been described previously in the literature.

gmw_00012046.name: 3C 228
gmw_00012046.overview: 3C 228 is a double radio galaxy [arXiv:0809.4645] located at a redshift of z=0.5524 [arXiv:1307.1727]. The source features a jet in its southern lobe and two highly polarized hotspots [arXiv:0809.4645, arXiv:1307.1727], which show no evidence of flux variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606].

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C228 as a polarized double radio galaxy observed through the corona, describing its hot-spot structure, polarization imaging, observing frequencies, and depolarization measurements.

quotes.quote: The radio source 3C228 was observed on August 16 and August 18, 2003. Results from these observations have been presented in , which shows a radio image of the source and describes its polarization characteristics (Figure 2 of ). 3C228 is a radio galaxy with a double structure, consisting of two highly polarized ``hot spots'' separated by about 46 arcseconds, corresponding to a linear separation of 33,000 km in the corona.

Simultaneous observations were made at frequencies of 1465 and 1665 MHz. The source was observed for a period of 8 hours, with the basic observational unit consisting of a scan of 15 minutes duration. A depolarization measurement was made for each scan at both frequencies of observation.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C228 as a z=0.5524 distributed, polarized radio galaxy whose northern/southern hotspots and jet/lobe structure make it useful for coronal Faraday-rotation observations.

quotes.quote: The source 3C228 is a radio galaxy with a redshift of z=0.5524 Spinrad:1985 . An image of this source is shown in Figure fig:3C228_Maps , the left image made from our VLA observations when the Sun was far from the source (see Section sec:Observation ). Figure fig:3C228_Maps shows that 3C228 represents a distributed, polarized source of radio waves, ideal for probing of the corona with Faraday rotation. The polarized emission is particularly strong in the northern hotspot at (J2000) RA = 09 h 50 m 11 1 and DEC = 14 20 25 , a jet in the southern lobe at (J2000) RA = 09 h 50 m 10 7 and DEC = 14 19 54 , and the southern hotspot at (J2000) RA = 09 h 50 m 10 5 and DEC = 14 19 39 . The stronger components, i.e. those with the largest value of the polarized intensity, were the northern and southern hotspots, with polarized intensities P=13 and 14 mJy/beam on the reference day, respectively.

sources.summary: Two screened Chandra exposures of 3C 228 produced consistent count rates, and the timing analysis found no evidence for flux variability.

quotes.quote: 3C 228 does not show any flux variability either, with both exposures having count rate of 0.023 0.005 s ^ 1 .

3C 228 : the source was observed in two separate exposures of 10 ks (Obs ID 2453) and 13 ks (Obs ID 2095), respectively, after flare screening. The shorter observation detected 281 net counts, while the longer has 365, consistent with no flux variation between the two.

gmw_00012047.name: 3C 263
gmw_00012047.overview: The astronomical source 3C 263 is a heavily piled up object [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606]. It features a jet that contributes to its overall emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606].

sources.summary: Because 3C 263 is heavily piled up, the authors use an annular extraction, exclude jet emission, and attribute a lower flux density to aperture and calibration differences.

quotes.quote: 3C 263 : the source is heavily piled up. We examined the spectrum extracted in several regions and we finally adopted the annular region of inner radius 3 pixels and outer radius 5 pixels as the least affected by pileup. The background spectrum was extracted in an annulus of inner and outer radii 9 and 12 pixels, respectively. We also excluded the emission from the jet using a rectangular region.

The power-law slope agrees with that found by mjh02 using the same data to within 1 errors. The 1-keV flux density differs significantly, with our value found to be 50 per cent lower, even after according a systematic error of 10 per cent to account for the PSF correction factor. This is likely to be due to a combination of a different region used (Hardcastle et al. used a circle of radius 5 pixels) and improvement in the calibration since the Hardcastle et al. analysis, or their use of a pileup model rather than fitting the spectrum in an annulus.

gmw_00012048.name: 3C 265
gmw_00012048.overview: The spectrum of the galaxy 3C 265 requires five distinct model components for an accurate fit [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606]. These components include Galactic absorption, a neutral iron line Gaussian, and both absorbed and unabsorbed power laws [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606].

sources.summary: Its spectrum requires five model components, including absorbed and unabsorbed power laws plus a neutral iron line; the absorbed slope matches prior detailed work.

quotes.quote: 3C 265 : the fit of this source spectrum requires 5 components. The model is composed of Galactic absorption, a power law, an absorbed power law and a Gaussian at the energy of the neutral iron line. bondi04 discussed the properties of this source in detail. We find that the slope of the absorbed power law is consistent with the result of bondi04 .

gmw_00012049.name: 3C 275.1
gmw_00012049.overview: The bright extragalactic radio source 3C 275.1 is a lobe-dominated quasar located at the center of a rich cluster of galaxies [arXiv:1501.00635, arXiv:astro-ph/9907034]. It exhibits extended X-ray emission likely arising from hot intracluster gas or lobe inverse-Compton emission [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034, arXiv:astro-ph/0511606].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 275.1 as a bright lobe-dominated quasar with resolved radio/hotspot structure and robust Fermi/LAT gamma-ray emission, measuring its flux, spectrum, and high gamma-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: 3C 275.1 is a well-studied bright extragalactic radio source (Bridle Perley 1984). It is identified as a quasar by optical spectroscope observation (Hintzen 1984). The source is the first quasar found at the centre of rich cluster of galaxies (Hintzen Stocke 1986).
Motivated by these facts, we conclude that the central significant ray excess is from 3C 275.1.

When preparing for this manuscript, we were informed that the Fermi /LAT collaboration reported the preliminary list of MAGN of 3LAC in the 5th Fermi Symposium, in which 3C 275.1 was included. The best-fit Power-law spectrum for 3C 275.1 is dN dE =(8.72 1.23) 10^ 13 ( E 809.24 MeV )^ (2.52 0.12) , and an integrated flux is (11.20 2.53) 10^ 9 photons cm^ 2 s^ 2 . At a redshift z=0.557 , the isotropy ray luminosity in the energy range of 0.1-100 GeV is (8.17 1.19) 10^ 45 erg s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The quasar 3C 275.1 is point-like after removing nearby NGC 4651 emission but shows additional extension likely from hot intracluster gas.

quotes.quote: 3C 275.1 Both HRI and PSPC data are available for this quasar. The PSPC data are confused by weak extended emission from the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 4651; when this is removed, the data show a point-like source. The longer HRI observation shows a profile slightly broader than the nominal PRF, characteristic of a source affected by aspect smearing. When the profile is fitted with a broadened PRF, there is some indication of additional extension on scales of 20--60 arcsec. Using the broadened PSF, we fit an additional model component, with results tabulated in Table profile .

As in 3C 215, the galaxy counts of Ellingson \ (1991) imply a rich cluster environment for this source, so it is likely that the extended emission we detect is real and due to hot intracluster gas. Hinten Romanishin (1986) suggest that the extended emission-line region around the source is due to a cooling flow, but the best-fitting model does not require cooling.

sources.summary: The core continuum is fit by a single Galactic-absorbed power law; the source has extended emission and only a weak, statistically unhelpful hint of neutral line emission.

quotes.quote: A single power law absorbed by Galactic absorption provides a good fit to the core continuum for 11 sources (3C 200, 3C 207, 3C 220.1, 3C 228, 3C 254, 3C 263, 3C 275.1, 3C 309.1, 3C 334, 3C 380, 3C 434.3), of which 8 are lobe-dominated or core-dominated quasars.

3C 275.1 : there is no evidence for X-ray emission from a resolved jet, but the source shows extended emission which has been interpreted as arising from a cluster-like atmosphere crfab03, mjh00 or from lobe inverse-Compton emission croston05 .
Hints of possible line emission are seen in 3C 207 (ionized, see also brunetti02 ) and 3C 275.1 (neutral), although the addition of a line does not technically improve these fits.

gmw_00012050.name: 3C 280
gmw_00012050.overview: 3C 280 is a well-studied radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606]. It features an extended emission-line nebula with a central peak and a loop around its eastern radio lobes, alongside a hard, absorbed core spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 280 as a well-studied radio galaxy with an extended emission-line nebula and a hard, absorbed core spectrum consistent with previous Chandra work.

quotes.quote: 3C 280 : this source is one of the best studied powerful radio galaxies. It has an extended emission-line (OII) nebula with a central peak and a loop around the eastern radio lobes (e.g., mccarthy87, ridgway04 ). We selected a background in the canonical annulus of inner radius 3.5 pixels and outer radius 6 pixels, but we also excluded the eastern and western regions corresponding to the X-ray lobes.

There are only 50 net source counts, but these are at energies above 1 keV, suggesting that we are seeing an absorbed power law. donahue03 , also using \ data, find a very flat spectral index, consistent with zero, when fitting between 0.3 and 8.0 keV. The 1-keV flux density we found is in agreement with their result.

gmw_00012051.name: 3C 330
gmw_00012051.overview: The radio galaxy 3C 330 exhibits extended X-ray emission that is aligned with its radio lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606]. Its core spectrum displays a broad, asymmetric line at a rest-frame energy of 6.7 keV, which may indicate the presence of ionized iron in the central region [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606].

sources.summary: The paper reports extended X-ray emission aligned with the radio lobes, agreement with previous core analysis, and a possible broad ionized iron line needing deeper data.

quotes.quote: 3C 330 : the source X-ray image shows the presence of some extended emission aligned with the radio lobes (see also croston05, mjh02 ). Core emission was analysed previously using \ data mjh02 and results are in good agreement despite the slightly different regions used for spectral analysis.

The core spectrum of 3C 330 displays a broad, asymmetric line at rest-frame energy 6.7 ^ +0.6 _ 0.3 keV. Although this may suggest the presence of ionized iron in the central region, deeper observations of this source are needed to establish the reality of the line.

gmw_00012052.name: 3C 427.1
gmw_00012052.overview: The radio galaxy 3C 427.1 has an X-ray spectrum dominated by hard emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606]. This spectrum is modeled with an absorbed power law and intrinsic absorption typical of other radio galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0511606].

sources.summary: The source has too few counts for conclusive fitting, but its hard-dominated spectrum is modeled with typical radio-galaxy absorption and constrains any soft component.

quotes.quote: 3C 427.1 : the statistics are not sufficient to perform conclusive spectral fitting. The spectrum is dominated by hard emission, with only 3 of the 15 counts found at energy below 2 keV. We fixed the parameters for an absorbed power law to typical values found for the other radio galaxies in the sample. We thus adopted a model composed of an absorbed power law of spectral index 1.6, and intrinsic absorption 1 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 . We also obtained an upper limit for the presence of an unabsorbed soft component.

gmw_00012053.name: SDSS J0827+5224
gmw_00012053.overview: SDSS J0827+5224 is a quasar lens candidate [arXiv:1110.5514]. Its spectrum reveals a background emission-line source that is aligned within 0.75 arcseconds of the foreground quasar but remains unresolved in ground-based imaging [arXiv:1110.5514].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J0827+5224 as a QSO lens candidate whose spectrum reveals a very close background emission-line source not resolved in ground imaging.

quotes.quote: An example is shown in Fig. fig:PCA_0827 , where the spectrum of SDSS J0827+5224 is modeled and subtracted, showing the residual lines from the lensed galaxy in the bottom panel.

No image deconvolution is possible for this object. The images obtained in the two optical bands (Fig. fig:j0827_image ) show no trace of any background object, in spite of the emission lines seen in the SDSS spectrum. These lines become striking in our Keck spectrum.

This may be due to photometric variations of the source between the SDSS and the Keck observations or maybe due to aperture effects. Indeed, the 1.5 \ slit width of LRIS is twice as narrow as the 3 \ fiber of the SDSS spectrograph. The increased brightness of the background emission lines in the Keck spectrum, as compared with the SDSS spectrum, therefore suggests that the emitting object must be very close to the foreground QSO on the plane of the sky, too close to the QSO to be seen in our ground-based images. From the Keck and SDSS data alone we conclude that we are in presence of two objects aligned with an accuracy of 0.75 \ (half the LRIS slit width) but the background object is not spatially resolved in the Keck images.

gmw_00012054.name: SDSS 1005+4016
gmw_00012054.overview: SDSS 1005+4016 is a face-on spiral galaxy hosting a quasar [arXiv:1110.5514]. Image deconvolution reveals a double nucleus [arXiv:1110.5514], and the system features a partial Einstein ring created by gravitational lensing [arXiv:1110.5514].

sources.summary: Keck imaging shows a face-on spiral QSO host with a double nucleus and an off-center elongated object later confirmed by HST as a partial lensed ring.

quotes.quote: The Keck images of \ show a face-on spiral galaxy with a bright nucleus. Image deconvolution reveals a double nucleus, as shown in Fig. fig:j1005_image . One of the two "nuclei" is compatible with being a point source (QSO) superposed with an extended component, i.e., the nucleus of the spiral galaxy. The other, located about 1 \ away from the QSO is elongated and not at the center of the spiral.

If it is lensed by the QSO+bulge of the galaxy, it does not show any counter image symmetric about the QSO. This object is either not lensed or forms a partial Einstein ring. The HST images presented below demonstrate that the latter hypothesis is correct.

gmw_00012055.overview: Galaxy 54829 is a compact galaxy that appears practically circular in GALFIT imaging [arXiv:1203.2623]. It lacks a measured rotation, and observations do not reach the flat part of its rotation curve [arXiv:1203.2623].

sources.summary: Galaxy 54829 is shown in GALFIT imaging, is one of the nearly circular compact objects, lacks a measured flat rotation curve, and is excluded from mean mass estimates.

quotes.quote: Figure 1 shows, for illustration, the galaxy images, together with the model from GALFIT and the corresponding residual for two of the galaxies (54829 and 321479). The rest of galaxies can be found in Appendix A. The best fit (lowest ^ 2 ) was visually inspected, to check its quality. The majority of the galaxies in our sample are elongated ( b/a 0.6), showing disk-like shapes. However, two of them (galaxies 54829 and 896687) are practically circular.

For the remaining four galaxies we cannot find a significant rotation and for some of them (54829, 685469 and 2434587), we do not reach the flat part of the rotation curve and, therefore, we cannot calculate V _ max .

The mass estimates for the galaxy 54829 are not taken into account to calculate mean masses, as it is the only one having a much lower (that contributes quadratically on the equation), which would strongly bias the mean values to lower masses. For consistency, we neither use this galaxy for the stellar mean masses.

gmw_00012056.overview: The galaxy 321479 is a rapidly rotating system that has been analyzed using GALFIT image modeling [arXiv:1203.2623]. It exhibits some of the largest radial velocities and highest velocity dispersions in its sample, alongside a large mass-fraction contribution from old stellar populations [arXiv:1203.2623].

sources.summary: Galaxy 321479 appears in the GALFIT image analysis, has among the largest velocities and dispersions, and shows one of the largest old-population mass-fraction effects.

quotes.quote: Figure 1 shows, for illustration, the galaxy images, together with the model from GALFIT and the corresponding residual for two of the galaxies (54829 and 321479). The rest of galaxies can be found in Appendix A.
We find that five of our galaxies are rapidly rotating (see Fig. 2a). The galaxies 890167, 321479 and 796740 show both the largest radial velocities and higher velocity dispersions ( ).

Indeed, the objects with the largest contribution from old stellar populations (321479, 796740 and 896687 and 2434587) are the ones showing the largest differences with respect to the masses derived by means of the SSP-equivalent parameters (by a factor 4).

gmw_00012057.overview: The massive compact galaxy 685469 is classified as a slow rotator [arXiv:1203.2623]. It is characterized by strong emission lines, an older stellar population age of 2.65 Gyr, and a high total metallicity [arXiv:1203.2623].

sources.summary: Galaxy 685469 lacks a secure maximum rotation velocity, falls in the slow-rotator regime, and is the oldest and most metal-rich object in the sample.

quotes.quote: For the remaining four galaxies we cannot find a significant rotation and for some of them (54829, 685469 and 2434587), we do not reach the flat part of the rotation curve and, therefore, we cannot calculate V _ max .

As it can be seen, the majority of the massive compact galaxies, without correcting them from inclination effects (big symbols) are fast rotators, except galaxies 685469 and 2434587 that fall in the slow rotators regime. However, if an inclination correction is applied, the latter galaxies practically reach the separating line.

The galaxy 685469 is not shown in these figures. Instead, its age and metallicity were derived from the full spectrum fitting with one SSP from Ulyss (see Sect 4.), due to the strong emission lines affecting the H _ o and Fe5015 features. This galaxy is very different from the rest, being the oldest one with 2.65 Gyr and with a total metallicity [ Z/H ] = 0.55.

gmw_00012058.overview: The massive compact galaxy 2434587 lacks a significantly measured flat rotation curve [arXiv:1203.2623]. After correcting for inclination effects, the galaxy lies near the boundary separating fast and slow rotators [arXiv:1203.2623].

sources.summary: Galaxy 2434587 lacks a measured flat rotation curve, lies near the slow-rotator boundary after inclination correction, and has broad possible old-population fractions.

quotes.quote: For the remaining four galaxies we cannot find a significant rotation and for some of them (54829, 685469 and 2434587), we do not reach the flat part of the rotation curve and, therefore, we cannot calculate V _ max .

As it can be seen, the majority of the massive compact galaxies, without correcting them from inclination effects (big symbols) are fast rotators, except galaxies 685469 and 2434587 that fall in the slow rotators regime. However, if an inclination correction is applied, the latter galaxies practically reach the separating line.

For example, for the galaxy 2434587 with mean SSP-age 2.24 Gyr, the amount of old population varies from 0.05 to 95 , while for the galaxies with less than 1.0 Gyr, only SSPs with more than 60 of old population are feasible.

gmw_00012059.overview: The galaxy 24143* is an ALMA-detected source that has been identified as a large disk [arXiv:2310.02500]. Visual inspection reveals that this disk galaxy is oriented in a more face-on configuration [arXiv:2310.02500].

sources.summary: The paper treats 24143* as an ALMA-detected galaxy requiring custom deblending; visual inspection identifies it as a more face-on large disk.

quotes.quote: NIR counterparts for all ALCS sources are identified. However, in two cases (UNCOVER IDs 9018, 24143; denoted throughout with an asterisk), visual inspection reveals the galaxies are shredded into multiple sources in the UNCOVER catalog as a result of the detection deblending thresholds, which were optimized to detect faint, high-redshift sources. As the photometry and segmentation maps are used for simultaneous modeling or masking of neighboring objects in the structural modeling (see Sec. sec:methods ), for these two objects we instead perform an alternative detection (using SEP; Barbary18 ) with less aggressive deblending parameters ( KERNEL=3.5 pixel FWHM Gaussian, MINAREA=3 pixels, THRESH=0.8 , DEBLEND NTHRESH=8 , DEBLEND CONT=0.0001 , CLEAN=Y , CLEAN PARAM=1.0 ), and use the resulting segmentation maps and photometric parameters (derived using the same photometric pipeline as except for the parameter changes) for structural modeling of these two galaxies.

Visual inspection (i.e., Figs. fig:sampleRGB fig:SW_LW_fits ) reveals galaxies that appear to be large disks, both edge-on (9018*, 16790, 26131, 43148; see also Kokorev23 for other analysis of 9018*/ID319, which Kokorev23 report may be in the early phases of a merger with the compact companion to the West given its undisturbed disk morphology) and more face-on (24143*; see also Wu23 ).

gmw_00012060.overview: The object 20.5+7-0.02 is classified as an uncertain Lyman-alpha emitter candidate [arXiv:1111.2354]. Multi-wavelength photometry and imaging of its field reveal the presence of multiple nearby objects [arXiv:1111.2354].

sources.summary: The paper treats 20.5+7-0.02 as an uncertain LAE candidate: its spectrum alone cannot rule out lower-redshift lines, poor seeing limits confirmation, and COSMOS imaging shows multiple nearby objects.

quotes.quote: One LAE candidate in Figure montage2 has not been discussed so far: 20.5+7-0.02. Like the two other uncertain candidates, we can not rule out \ or \ from the spectrum alone. (Although, we note that this object was observed under very poor seeing in March 2010, so future observations should confirm or rule out this LAE candidate.) However, the \ postage stamp contains multiple objects. We explore this further by examining the multi-wavelength photometry from the COSMOS survey.

gmw_00012061.name: 4C 18.68
gmw_00012061.overview: 4C 18.68 is an X-shaped radio galaxy [arXiv:2403.00580]. Its central active-lobe component exhibits a flat spectrum, while the wings show steeper spectra that are attributed to radiative and adiabatic particle cooling [arXiv:2403.00580].

sources.summary: The review cites 4C 18.68 as an unusual X-shaped source whose spectral analysis found a flat central active-lobe component and steeper wing spectra attributed to radiative and adiabatic particle cooling.

quotes.quote: One of the earliest works on spectral analysis of a rather unusual X-shaped source, 4C 18.68, was reported by Gower1982A , identifying the central active-lobe like component having a flat spectrum. The spectral values tended to be much steeper in the wing and showed a tendency to steepen towards the wing edges. This was due to the effect of radiative and adiabatic cooling effects operated on the particles e.g., Kardashev1962,Longair1973,Jaffe1973 and had been noticed in a number of extended sources by then.

gmw_00012062.overview: The object 0144 522 is a quasar with a bright host galaxy [arXiv:1802.04714]. Its radio jet extends to the east with a diffuse, one-sided morphology, and its inner jet is clearly detected in both X-ray and optical imaging [arXiv:1802.04714].

sources.summary: The note describes 0144 522 as a bright-host quasar with an eastward diffuse radio jet and a clear inner jet detection in X-rays and optical imaging.

quotes.quote: 0144 522 (PKS B0144 522) The radio emission from the jet extends 20 \ to the east, curving northeast while becoming significantly more diffuse and shows no hotspot, reminiscent of an FR I type morphology but one-sided. Simple galaxy modeling and subtraction (Fig. fig:0144 ) was done with ellipse fitting in IRAF because the host galaxy is so bright in the HST image. There is a clear detection of the inner 3 \ of the jet in both the X-ray and optical. The galaxy-subtracted HST data were analyzed separately using regions defined in Fig. fig:0144regions , with background regions comparably placed with regard to the core's diffraction spikes. Results are given in Table tab:0144 .

gmw_00012063.overview: The radio source 0508 220 is hosted by a bright elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1802.04714]. Its radio structure features a northwestern lobe and a southern structure that curves to the east, though Chandra observations show no detection [arXiv:1802.04714].

sources.summary: The note describes 0508 220 as having a curved southern radio structure, a northwestern lobe, no significant Chandra detection, and a bright elliptical host.

quotes.quote: 0508 220 (PKS B0508 220) The primary radio structure starts about 5 \ south of the core and curves to the east, ending 25 \ from the core. A lobe is found to the northwest extending about as far as the southern lobe. The Chandra data do not show a significant detection. There is a rather bright elliptical galaxy at the location of the core in the HST image so elliptical contours were fit in a manner similar to that for 0144 522. The result of the galaxy subtraction is shown in Fig. fig:0508 .

gmw_00012064.overview: The quasar 0707 + 476 features weak radio knots located to the east and west of its core [arXiv:1802.04714]. Its kiloparsec-scale jet extends toward the east, while VLBI imaging reveals a parsec-scale jet oriented to the northeast [arXiv:1802.04714].

sources.summary: The note describes 0707 + 476 as having weak east-west knots, an eastward kpc jet definition, a northeast pc-scale jet, and HST contamination from a bright star.

quotes.quote: 0707 + 476 (B3 0707 + 476) There are weak knots to the east and west of the core, with the west one being slightly farther at about 3 \ from the core. The jet continues beyond the east knot about 6 \ (apparent in the radio profile), so we somewhat arbitrarily define the jet direction to be toward the east. VLBI imaging shows a pc scale jet to the northeast 2004ApJ...609..539K but the apparent velocities given by 2004ApJ...609..539K are negative (but marginally significant) so we quote the absolute value and assume that the core location is not precisely known. The core lies quite close to a bright star in the HST image, avoiding its southern diffraction spike but the bright eastern knot lies practically on top of the spike so no jet features are apparent in the HST image.

gmw_00012065.overview: The quasar 1303 827 features extended radio emission consisting of a single southeast knot and a north-northwest knot pair [arXiv:1802.04714]. While no X-ray knots are detected by Chandra, the closer of the north-northwest knots shows faint, resolved optical flux in HST imaging [arXiv:1802.04714].

sources.summary: The note describes 1303 827 as having a southeast radio knot and a north-northwest knot pair, with no Chandra knots but faint resolved HST flux.

quotes.quote: 1303 827 (PKS 1302 82) The extended radio emission consists of a single knot at 6.5 \ southeast of the core and a knot pair about 16 \ north-northwest of the core. The X-ray jet searched was set to the SE to include the closest knot. No knots are detected by Chandra . In the HST image, there is faint, resolved flux from the closer of the NNW knot pair, shown in a inset in Fig. fig:optical .

gmw_00012066.name: GB6 J0043+3426
gmw_00012066.overview: GB6 J0043+3426 is a blazar located at a redshift of z=0.966 [arXiv:1501.02765]. It is a variable gamma-ray source that exhibits recurring flaring activity on week-long timescales and has a hard spectrum [arXiv:1501.02765].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GB6 J0043+3426 as one of eight previously unreported variable gamma-ray sources, a z=0.966 blazar with week-scale flares, hard spectra, and GeV-peaked SED behavior.

quotes.quote: Close inspection of the detected variable regions result in identification of 8 sources without previous known variability. Two of them are included in the second Fermi LAT source catalogue (FBQS J122424.1+243623 and GB6 J0043+3426) and one (3EG J1424+3734) was reported by EGRET and also was included in the First Fermi LAT source catalogue (1FGL), but is missing in the 2FGL. Possible identifications of five other sources are obtained using NED and SIMBAD databases (1RXS J161939.9+765515, PMN J2320-6447, PKS 0226-559, PKS J0030-0211, PMN J0225-2603). These new variable gamma-ray sources demonstrate recurring flaring activity with time scale weeks and have hard spectra. Their spectral energy distributions deviate significantly from a simple power-law shape and often peak around GeV.

Variability in these pixels was caused by 2FGL sources FBQS J122424.1+243623 (MS 1221.8+2452) and GB6 J0043+3426 correspondingly. The first one is BL Lac type object at the redshift z=0.218 that was recently observed also in the TeV energy range by the MAGIC collaboration MS1221 , the latter one is a blazar at the redshift z=0.966 .

gmw_00012067.name: 3EG J1424+3734
gmw_00012067.overview: 3EG J1424+3734 is a BL Lacertae type active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1501.02765]. The source exhibits long-term variability, recurring flares on a weekly timescale, and a hard spectral energy distribution that peaks around GeV [arXiv:1501.02765].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3EG J1424+3734 as a BL Lac variable gamma-ray source, reported by EGRET and 1FGL but absent from 2FGL, with week-scale flares and hard GeV-peaked spectra.

quotes.quote: Close inspection of the detected variable regions result in identification of 8 sources without previous known variability. Two of them are included in the second Fermi LAT source catalogue (FBQS J122424.1+243623 and GB6 J0043+3426) and one (3EG J1424+3734) was reported by EGRET and also was included in the First Fermi LAT source catalogue (1FGL), but is missing in the 2FGL. Possible identifications of five other sources are obtained using NED and SIMBAD databases (1RXS J161939.9+765515, PMN J2320-6447, PKS 0226-559, PKS J0030-0211, PMN J0225-2603). These new variable gamma-ray sources demonstrate recurring flaring activity with time scale weeks and have hard spectra. Their spectral energy distributions deviate significantly from a simple power-law shape and often peak around GeV.

One of the sources may be identified with a high confidence: the reported variability in the 431-th pixel was caused by the 3EG J1424+3734 BL Lac type object that was included into the 3rd EGRET catalogue 3EGRET and also into the first Fermi LAT catalogue (1FGL) 1FGL . However it is absent in the 2FGL catalogue and that clearly indicates its long-time variability.

gmw_00012068.name: G2
gmw_00012068.overview: G2 is a small satellite galaxy located in a lens plane near the lensed image G2/C [arXiv:1702.00406]. It acts as a perturber that suppresses the flux of the lensed image and contributes an enclosed mass of approximately 1.3 billion solar masses within its Einstein radius [arXiv:1702.00406].

sources.summary: G2 is modeled as a small satellite/perturber near the reddened G2/C image, suppressing image C and contributing about 1.3 10^10 solar masses within its Einstein radius.

quotes.quote: Models with just a single deflector predict a fourth image at the location of G2/C but considerably brighter and bluer. The addition of a small satellite galaxy ( R_ E 0.2^ ) in the lens plane near the position of G2/C suppresses the flux of the fourth image and can explain both the reddening and grey dimming.

The colours of image G2/C can be obtained by adding a standard reddening law using R_V=3.1 and E(B-V)=0.3 cmm89 to the SED of image B, but the overall magnitudes need an additional `grey' dimming of 0.8 mags; we also sum the small contribution of a putative galaxy G2 3.5mags fainter than G1, in order to better reproduce the zY- band fluxes.

When the perturber has non-null Einstein radius, its enclosed mass is M_ p (G2) 1.3 10^ 10 M_ . The contribution of a small galaxy with magnitudes m(G2)=m(G1)-2.5 _ 10 (M_ p (G2)/M_ p (G1)) is barely noticeable in gri bands, which in turn can be well reproduced by reddening and offsetting the SED of image B, and makes the zY- band magnitudes of image G2/C in complete agreement with the values measured from Section 2 (black line in fig. fig:taka ).

gmw_00012069.name: ESO 601- G 036
gmw_00012069.overview: ESO 601- G 036 is a galaxy identified as a proposed host candidate for the fast radio burst FRB 171020 [arXiv:1906.08749]. It is characterized by a low redshift and host dispersion measure estimates that are generally lower than those of FRB 121102 [arXiv:1906.08749].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 601- G 036 as the proposed FRB 171020 host candidate, with low redshift and host-DM estimates generally below those of FRB 121102.

quotes.quote: The one with the lowest redshift, ESO 601- G 036, is the galaxy candidate proposed by . It has DM_ host =41 pc cm ^ 3 and 54 pc cm ^ 3 for NE2001 and YMW16 models, respectively. For most possible redshifts, the derived DM_ host is much smaller than that of FRB 121102. Only if the host galaxy is intrinsically very faint (so they are much closer) could its DM_ host reach the lower limit of FRB 121102 DM_ host .

gmw_00012070.name: SDSS131705.58+552948.8
gmw_00012070.overview: SDSS131705.58+552948.8 is an edge-on disk galaxy characterized by a bulge [arXiv:1906.08749]. It has a stellar mass of 30 billion solar masses and a low star formation rate of 0.007 solar masses per year [arXiv:1906.08749].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS131705.58+552948.8 as an edge-on disk host candidate for FRB 180729.J1316+55, giving stellar mass, low SFR, and lower host-DM estimates than FRB 121102.

quotes.quote: The second one, SDSS131705.58+552948.8, is an edge-on disk galaxy with a significant bulge. SED fitting gives stellar mass M_*=3 10^ 10 M_ , SFR=0.007 M_ yr ^ 1 2015ApJS..219....8C . Its expected DM_ host are 20 pc cm ^ 3 and 28 pc cm ^ 3 for the NE2001 and YMW16 models, respectively. These two sources both have a smaller DM_ host than FRB 121102.

gmw_00012071.name: 2MASS J042221.4+734710.2
gmw_00012071.overview: 2MASS J042221.4+734710.2 is a galaxy with a determined photometric redshift [arXiv:1906.08749]. It has been evaluated as a potential host candidate for the fast radio burst FRB 180814.J0422+73 [arXiv:1906.08749].

sources.summary: The paper tests 2MASS J042221.4+734710.2 as an FRB 180814.J0422+73 host candidate, concluding it is unlikely if its photometric redshift is correct and would imply very small host DM.

quotes.quote: The DM_ host of FRB 180814.J0422+73 must be very small ( < 7 pc cm ^ 3 ), if its host is as bright as FRB 121102. In this case, the galaxy with redshift, 2MASS J042221.4+734710.2, is not the host, if its photometric redshift is correct. Even if we use the empirical z- DM_ IGM relation, the conclusion is similar. If 2MASS J042221.4+734710.2 is the host galaxy of FRB 180814.J0422+73, the estimated DM_ IGM =67 pc cm ^ 3 2014ApJ...783L..35D , indicating DM_ host 5 pc cm ^ 3 DM. If 2MASS J042221.4+734710.2 is not the host, the host galaxy should be at least three orders of magnitude fainter than the host of FRB 121102, that is, M_ > -14 mag.

gmw_00012072.name: SMDG0014598+023448
gmw_00012072.overview: SMDG0014598+023448 is a low surface brightness galaxy candidate that has been flagged for having an unrealistically large effective radius [arXiv:2109.03345].
