gmw_00012129.name: RXCJ 2308.3-0211
gmw_00012129.overview: RXCJ 2308.3-0211 is a fairly regular galaxy cluster containing a single brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:0804.0299]. The position of this central galaxy coincides with the cluster's X-ray emission peak and features diffuse stellar emission extending up to 100 kpc from its center [arXiv:0804.0299].

sources.summary: The paper describes the single BCG in RXCJ 2308.3-0211 as coincident with the X-ray peak, with diffuse light and a peculiar R-band profile possibly tracing unbound light.

quotes.quote: Diffuse stellar emission is robustly detected around all the seven BCGs in the three X-ray selected clusters, and its distribution and B-R colour down to 26 R- (observed frame) are investigated. The diffuse light around BCGs extends up to 100 kpc from their centres. At this distance, we expect that on average 30--40 of the detected emission (including galaxies) is due to the intracluster light (ICL), as from the statistical results obtained from 683 optically selected clusters of galaxies at 0.2 z 0.3 (Zibetti et al. 2005).

RXCJ 2308.3 0211 exhibits some correspondence between the distributions of diffuse light and X-ray emission (see Fig. FigXrayOpt3 ). In this fairly regular cluster, there is a single BCG and the X-ray emission peak coincides with its position. Thus, an alignment between the major axes of the BCG and the potential well of its parent cluster could be expected (Brown 1997; Dubinski 1998 and references therein). The ``peculiar'' R-band radial surface brightness profile of the BCG in RXCJ 2308.3 0211 (see Fig. SBprofs ) may witness the presence of diffuse light not bound to the BCG.

gmw_00012130.name: HCC 026
gmw_00012130.overview: HCC 026 is a dwarf galaxy located in a cluster center [arXiv:1205.5289]. It is mostly dissolved by the local tidal field, producing a redshifted tidal stream with tail stars that match the galaxy's low-metallicity population [arXiv:1205.5289].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HCC 026 as a dwarf galaxy producing a redshifted tidal stream; its tail stars match a low-metallicity population and the galaxy is mostly tidally dissolved.

quotes.quote: We have also discovered two tidal streams in the cluster center, emerging from the dwarf galaxy HCC 026 and from the S0 galaxy HCC 007. The HCC 026 stream is redshifted by 1200 \ with respect to NGC 3311 ( V_ N3311 3800 ), similarly as HCC 026 itself, a fraction of PNs in the off-centered envelope, and several other dwarf galaxies nearby. The stars in one of the HCC 026 tails are known to be consistent with the low-metallicity population of HCC 026, and our photometry shows that this galaxy is already mostly dissolved in the tidal field.

gmw_00012131.name: dm1647+21
gmw_00012131.overview: dm1647+21 is an isolated dwarf galaxy pair located at a redshift of approximately z=0.0090994 [arXiv:1708.02587]. The system consists of two interacting components that exhibit low metallicity and enhanced star formation triggered by their ongoing dynamical interaction [arXiv:1708.02587].

sources.summary: The paper treats dm1647+21 as an isolated dwarf-galaxy pair at z=0.0090994, with two SDSS components, enhanced star formation, low metallicity, and kinematic evidence for an ongoing interaction.

quotes.quote: The object of the present study, , is a dwarf pair at 16h47m10.96s +21d05m14.5s with a redshift, z=0.0090994 (D _L=39.6 Mpc and 0.189 kpc / ). The pair consists of the SDSS spectroscopic objects SDSS J164710.66+210514.5 and SDSS J164711.12+210514.8 which have a projected linear separation of 5 kpc and a velocity difference of 27 Stierwalt2015 . The system also meets the above isolation criteria from a massive galaxy.

Using the multi-band SDSS photometry, we compute a total stellar mass for the system of 9.0 10^ 7 calculated as described in Stierwalt2015 with a mass ratio between the two components of 4.6:1. Based on the SDSS spectroscopic measurements and an extrapolation to the entire galaxy the system has an estimated star formation rate of 0.15 Brinchmann2004 . The metallicity inferred for the system is Z =7.84 Tremonti2004 .

Based on the kinematic and morphological evidence, and a ``lookalike'' numerical simulation, we conclude is a pair of dynamically interacting dwarf galaxies. This dynamical interaction is the likely cause of the substantially enhanced star formation.

gmw_00012132.name: UGC 10549
gmw_00012132.overview: UGC 10549 is a dwarf companion galaxy with a stellar mass of 2.4 × 10⁸ solar masses [arXiv:1708.02587]. It is located near an interacting dwarf pair, suggesting it may belong to a dwarf-only group [arXiv:1708.02587].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 10549 as a similar-redshift companion near the dwarf pair, possibly part of a dwarf-only group and likely contributing single-dish gas flux.

quotes.quote: The pair has a nearby companion galaxy at a similar redshift, UGC 10549, which has a stellar mass of 2.4 10^ 8 from SDSS DR12, using the method Maraston2006 . UGC 10549 does not formally meet the association criterion for TNT pairs, with a projected separation to just over 50 kpc and thus was not initially identified as being associated. However, considering the proximity of this object to , this may be a potential dwarf-only group, similar to those found by Stierwalt2017 .

Single-dish observations of the system show a gas mass of 3.7 10^ 9 Stierwalt2015 , however this measurement likely includes associated with a nearby system that is within the 8 arcminute GBT beam but not part of the interacting pair (UGC 10549, z=0.009 , 50 kpc projected separation). The integrated spectrum shows a somewhat complex profile, with the velocity of the peak emission at 50 lower velocity than the redshifts of the two components of . This peak corresponds to the redshift of UGC 10549, suggesting a substantial amount of the single-dish flux is associated with that galaxy. In support of this interpretation of the single-dish profile, follow-up interferometric observations with the VLA reveal a clumpy distribution in and emission associated with UGC 10549 (S. Stierwalt et al. \ 2017 in preparation ).

gmw_00012133.name: NGC 5171
gmw_00012133.overview: NGC 5171 is the brightest galaxy in its group [arXiv:astro-ph/0408206]. It is linked to a cool central filament and is undergoing a tidal interaction with the less massive galaxy NGC 5176 [arXiv:astro-ph/0408206].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5171 as the brightest group galaxy, linking it to a cool central filament, tidal interaction with NGC 5176, and a hotter shocked region.

quotes.quote: This filament appears to join two galaxies: the brightest group galaxy NGC 5171, at the Southern end, and NGC 5176 at the Northern end.

If we assume that the two populations of galaxies, dominated by NGC 5171 and NGC 5179 respectively ( \ = 221 ), are in the process of merging, then it is probable that the bright central filament is the result of a tidal interaction between NGC 5171 and NGC 5176.

Our observed hot region is therefore consistent with an interaction in the direction of the bright central filament, where NGC 5171 is the more massive galaxy, and NGC 5176 belongs to a less massive infalling group.

gmw_00012134.name: NGC 5176
gmw_00012134.overview: NGC 5176 is a less massive galaxy belonging to an infalling group [arXiv:astro-ph/0408206]. It is located at the northern end of a bright central filament, where it is undergoing a tidal interaction with the more massive galaxy NGC 5171 [arXiv:astro-ph/0408206].

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 5176 at the northern end of the central filament, interpreting it as a less massive infalling-group galaxy tidally interacting with NGC 5171.

quotes.quote: This filament appears to join two galaxies: the brightest group galaxy NGC 5171, at the Southern end, and NGC 5176 at the Northern end.

If we assume that the two populations of galaxies, dominated by NGC 5171 and NGC 5179 respectively ( \ = 221 ), are in the process of merging, then it is probable that the bright central filament is the result of a tidal interaction between NGC 5171 and NGC 5176.

Our observed hot region is therefore consistent with an interaction in the direction of the bright central filament, where NGC 5171 is the more massive galaxy, and NGC 5176 belongs to a less massive infalling group.

gmw_00012135.name: IRAS F13443+0802
gmw_00012135.overview: IRAS F13443+0802 is a triple galaxy system containing a close northern-southern pair and a third galaxy to the southwest [arXiv:1805.00514]. The southeastern galaxy of this system hosts an active galactic nucleus and displays a Y-shaped outflow emerging from its highly inclined disk [arXiv:1805.00514].

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS F13443+0802 as a triple system whose SE galaxy hosts the dominant AGN and a Y-shaped [OIII] outflow in an inclined disk.

quotes.quote: IRAS F13443+0802 IRAS F13443+0802 is a spectacular triple system comprising a close pair with a nuclear separation of 5 arcsec ( 12 kpc) in the N-S direction, along with a third galaxy located 15 arcsec ( 35 kpc) to the SW of that pair. We will refer to the individual galaxies as the NE, SE, and SW components respectively (following the notation of Veilleux et al. 2002). The HST continuum image shows a highly disturbed morphology in the case of the NE component. Several clumps and condensations, along with dust features, are observed through the entire body of the galaxy, but are more prominent in the nuclear region and towards the south of this source. Overall, the NE component has the appearance of a distorted spiral galaxy observed close to face-on.

However, our most interesting finding for IRAS F13443+0802 is related to the morphology of the SE galaxy. The [ OIII ] emission from this galaxy has a peculiar ``Y'' morphology, centred on the region of brightest continuum emission along the disk, and bisected by the prominent dust lane. The overall morphologies of the continuum and [ OIII ] emission of the SE component suggest that the Y structure corresponds to the two sides of an emerging outflow in an edge-on or highly inclined system.

gmw_00012136.name: IRAS F15130-1958
gmw_00012136.overview: IRAS F15130-1958 is a galaxy featuring a complex, dusty nuclear morphology and a tidal tail that extends approximately 16 kiloparsecs to the southeast [arXiv:1805.00514]. Its nucleus hosts a compact structure of ionized gas emission associated with a warm outflow [arXiv:1805.00514].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F15130-1958 as having a complex dusty nucleus and tidal tail, with nearly all nuclear [OIII] flux coming from a compact outflow source.

quotes.quote: IRAS F15130-1958 The HST continuum image of IRAS F15130-1958 shows a complex nuclear morphology, with various dust features, as well as a tidal tail that extends 8 arcsec ( 16 kpc) to the SE of the galaxy. Only one prominent, nuclear condensation of continuum emission is observed in the image. Our [ OIII ] image of this source is dominated by a compact structure of ionized gas emission centred on the main nuclear continuum concentration. There is also some faint, diffuse emission to the south of the nucleus that extends to a maximum radius of 0.40 arsec (0.77 kpc). The spectroscopic aperture of RZ13 (1.5 2.4 arcsec aperture along PA358) encompass the entire compact [ OIII ] structure and the diffuse emission.

Therefore, in this case the entire [ OIII ] emission feature is blueshifted and associated with the warm outflow; there is no evidence for a narrow component at rest-frame wavelength defined by the stellar absorption features. Based on analysis of the narrow-band [ OIII ] image using circular aperture photometry, we find that the total flux in the compact nuclear source within an aperture of 3.5 pixel (0.175 arcsec) radius is 1.4 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . Following aperture correction by a factor of 1.22 sirianni05 , the total [ OIII ] flux associated with the compact nuclear source is 1.7 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

gmw_00012137.name: IRAS F16156+0146
gmw_00012137.overview: IRAS F16156+0146 is a merger system consisting of two galaxies separated by 3.5 arcseconds [arXiv:1805.00514]. The northwestern nucleus is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy and hosts an active galactic nucleus-induced outflow [arXiv:1805.00514].

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS F16156+0146 as a two-galaxy merger with clumpy [OIII] emission aligned between nuclei and an AGN-induced outflow from the NW nucleus.

quotes.quote: IRAS F16156+0146 The system is a merger between two galaxies with a nuclear separation of 3.5 arcsec ( 7.9 kpc) in the N-S direction. The HST continuum image of the source shows that the morphology of each of the two merging galaxies consist of a central condensation plus an irregular halo of diffuse emission. No prominent dust lanes and/or additional tidal features are visible in our continuum image of the source. However, the [ OIII ] image of the galaxy shows a series of clumps or condensations embedded in more diffuse emission. Relative to the NW nucleus, these clumps extend 0.8 arcsec ( 1.8 kpc) in the NW direction, and 1.9 arcsec ( 4.3 kpc) in the SE direction along PA 130.

This [ OIII ] structure extends almost the entire distance between the two merging galaxies, and its close alignment with the axis joining the nuclei suggests that it is a tidal feature. The brightest [ OIII ] clump coincides with the continuum nucleus of the NW source, which is classified as Sy2 at optical wavelengths veilleux99,yuan10 . RZ13 found evidence for an AGN-induced outflow in their 1.5 2.2 nuclear aperture centred on the NW nucleus.
